entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona: name all five scale anchors in rating legend
entries.plain: All five rating scale options now display their names in the legend, making it clearer what each choice means.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona minor cluster (2): role-delete warning, field clarity, AR errors
entries.plain: The system now warns you before deleting a role profile that candidates have already used, clarifies which project field is which in the voucher setup, and shows error messages in Arabic for Arabic-speaking test-takers.
entries.explanation:
- Warn (require explicit confirm) before deleting a role profile referenced by
  issued vouchers / completed sittings - deletion SET-NULLs the pin and silently
  strips the fit section from their reports.
- Disambiguate the voucher wizard's two project fields (cohort key vs the
  free-text project label note).
- Localise the known redeem errors for Arabic delegates.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona minor cluster: XP-13 payload, thresholds, a11y, badges, counts
entries.plain: Candidate scores are no longer visible in raw data, readiness results now align properly with fit categories, expired vouchers correctly show as expired instead of active, and private reports are now treated consistently.
entries.explanation:
- Strip the per-competency score profile from the non-staff submit response
  (XP-13 was client-side only; scores were readable in the network payload).
- Align readinessVerdict breakpoints to fitBand (80/60) so a 57% no longer
  shows 'Limited fit' next to 'Ready with Development'.
- Standard report route authenticates before peeking the session, removing the
  404-vs-403 existence oracle (now matches the lens routes).
- Admin voucher table shows an 'Expired' badge instead of green 'Active'.
- Client-portal Persona 'completed' uses a true head count, not a 100-row cap.
- Stable ORDER BY id on every paginated response fetch (no skip/dup).
- Likert grid: role=radiogroup + aria-labelledby + role=radio/aria-checked.
- Redeem hero no longer claims 'across the 41 competencies' for scoped vouchers.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona voucher lifecycle + runner resilience
entries.plain: Vouchers now check expiry when candidates start taking the test, prevent overwriting completed results, resume in-progress tests instead of starting over, and show a retry message if answers fail to save.
entries.explanation:
- Voucher take page re-checks status/expiry (not only at redeem) and blocks a
  dead code; startPersonaAction refuses a retake once a sitting is submitted so
  a completed recruiter-visible result can't be silently overwritten, and
  resumes an in-progress voucher session (restores saved answers) instead of
  minting a new empty one - closing the 'no resume' progress-loss gap.
- Autosave failures now re-queue the dropped batch, retry, and show a visible
  'couldn't save - retrying' banner instead of silently scoring missing answers.
- Retention purge anonymises persona_voucher_redemptions PII (name/email/company
  /IP/UA) past the 2-year window (rows kept for seat accounting).
- Wire the voucher wizard Language selector through to default_language (Arabic
  vouchers were silently issued as English).
- Gate the demo 'fill random answers' shortcut to non-production; honest intro
  copy per pinned item-format and per resume capability.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona scoring integrity: ipsative rollup, scoped coverage, cohort pagination
entries.plain: Scoring now uses the correct methodology across all reports, roles without answers no longer show as zero scores, and reports now show how many competencies were actually measured versus skipped.
entries.explanation:
- Route every report/list/cohort/intel surface through ipsative-aware
  selfScoreByCompetency/overallSelfScore instead of averaging raw 5/1/3
  forced-choice rows as if Likert (diverged from the canonical scorer).
- DARE + Leadership lenses no longer treat an unmeasured role/axis as a real
  0.00: rank/classify only measured groups, show 'n/a / not assessed', and
  disclose partial coverage. Standard report fit panel discloses N-of-M
  role-competency coverage; Leadership overall shows the real measured count
  instead of hardcoded 'all 41'.
- Range-paginate the response fetch on /ac/persona/cohort + /admin/cohorts
  (the ~1000-row API cap silently truncated means beyond ~4 sittings).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix cross-tenant PII leak: admin-gate Persona cohort page
entries.plain: Fixed a security issue where any logged-in user could see confidential candidate names and ratings from other organizations' cohort reports - now restricted to administrators only.
entries.explanation:
The cohort report page read submitted sessions across all orgs via the
service-role client with no role check; middleware only enforces auth, so
any authenticated non-admin could read cross-tenant taker names, client-org
names and self-ratings. Add the same admin gate the sibling pages use.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: VIFM EQ Framework v1.0 + Persona Emotional Intelligence Profile report
entries.plain: Persona now includes an Emotional Intelligence report showing four dimensions (self-awareness, self-management, social awareness, relationship management) based on 22 relevant questions out of the full assessment.
entries.explanation: Goleman four-quadrant EI lens (Self-Awareness / Self-Management / Social Awareness / Relationship Management) on the Persona sitting - the fourth report variant alongside the standard, Leadership, and DARE reports. Honest bounding: EI is a bounded construct, so only the 22 emotionally-loaded competencies map (3/5/3/11); the other 19 cognitive/commercial/executional competencies are explicitly out of scope and never scored. Judgment-calls documented (Emotional Regulation & Empathy -> Social Awareness; Sustainable Wellbeing -> Self-Awareness; Proactive Initiative -> Self-Management). Report: EQ index (quadrant-balanced), the canonical 2x2 Goleman grid with per-quadrant score bars, quadrant breakdown, and a priority-quadrant development focus with catalogue tips. Framework locked as v1.0 in docs/VIFM-EQ-Framework.md, published on /evidence; 'EQ' link on the Persona results page; admin/client-manager(own org) gated route. Explicitly NOT the ESCI/MSCEIT - stated in the report footer. Verified from a real full-coverage sitting (EQ 3.38; SA 3.58 / SM 3.30 / SocA 3.25 / RM 3.39).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: VIFM DARE Framework v1.0 + Persona DARE Decision-Role Profile report
entries.plain: Persona now includes a Decision-Role Profile report that maps competencies to decision-making roles (Decide, Advise, Recommend, Execute) to clarify where candidates naturally fit in organizational decisions.
entries.explanation: New decision-rights lens on the 41-competency spine (after McKinsey DARE / Bain RAPID): every competency maps to exactly one of Decide (8) / Advise (10) / Recommend (8) / Execute (15), locked as v1.0 in docs/VIFM-DARE-Framework.md and published on /evidence. Stage 1 implementation reads a Persona sitting (no new questions): role means, primary/dual/versatile profile resolution (dual within 0.25, versatile within 0.5 spread), a four-bar role chart with midpoint marker, role-by-role breakdown, and a two-move development focus (sharpen the primary role + build the weakest) with catalogue tips. Admin/client-manager(own org) gated route + DARE link on the Persona results page. Verified by rendering from a real full-coverage sitting (Versatile: R 3.50 / E 3.48 / A 3.28 / D 3.25). Instrument-agnostic by design - Reflect 360 (self-vs-others DARE gap) and AC (observed evidence, coverage-badged) are the staged follow-ups.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Leadership Report: section 3 starts on its own page
entries.plain: The Leadership Report now starts section 3 on a fresh page, making the first page a clean summary and improving readability when printed.
entries.explanation: The Leadership Potential Summary heading rendered at the bottom of page 1 with its elements flowing to page 2. The section now page-breaks first, making page 1 a clean executive page (summary + matrix) and page 2 open with section 3. Verified by rendering + per-page text extraction.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona reports: disjoint summary sets, footer reframe, strength deltas
entries.plain: The Leadership Report no longer lists the same competency as both a strength and a development area, and the footer now explains the report's value for development planning backed by established research.
entries.explanation:
Leadership Report: a competency can no longer appear under BOTH Dominant Tendencies and Development Areas (with few answered competencies the top-5/bottom-5 windows overlapped) - strengths take priority, development draws from the rest. Footer reworded from 'not a validated leadership typology or a selection tool' to a development-focused framing on the established Bass & Avolio model with the corroboration guidance kept (360 + interview before selection decisions; indicative until normed).

Role Fit: strength rows now show the surplus after the arrow (4.0 / 3.0 ^ +1.0), mirroring the deficit on gap rows - applied to the EN PDF, the Arabic HTML report, and both standalone results views (which also gain the arrows/deltas on gaps).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona voucher FK guard + Logica elements in Bespoke composer
entries.plain: Persona vouchers now check that the target role still exists before you create one, showing a clear message if it has been deleted; Bespoke assessments can now include specific Logica test types when building a custom package.
entries.explanation:
Persona: a hiring voucher's target role is now existence-checked server-side before insert, so a stale dropdown selection (role deleted/recreated in another tab) returns 'That target role no longer exists - refresh and pick a current role' instead of the raw persona_vouchers_target_role_profile_id_fkey violation. Applied to both the generate and email-delegates actions.

Bespoke composer: selecting Logica now reveals an elements picker (numerical/verbal/inductive/deductive chips, full battery default). The scope shows in the live preview + composed chips ('Logica - Inductive Reasoning') and each composed package links straight to /ac/cognitive/vouchers?subtests=... so scoped vouchers can be issued today via the real voucher system.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona Leadership matrix: fix Y-axis label alignment
entries.plain: Fixed a display bug where the Y-axis label on the Leadership matrix was positioned incorrectly on the page.
entries.explanation: React-PDF SVG ignored the 3-arg rotate(angle, cx, cy), so the 'Leadership (transformational)' label rotated around the origin and landed mid-chart. Switched to the deterministic G translate(x, yMid) + rotate(-90) idiom so it sits on the left Y-axis. Verified by rasterising the rendered PDF.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona Leadership Report: richer matrix + development tips page
entries.plain: The Leadership Report matrix is now clearer with gridlines and axis labels, and includes a new page of development tips for each low-scoring competency.
entries.explanation: Matrix redrawn fully in SVG: quadrant tints, integer gridlines, bold midpoint cross, 1-5 tick numbers, and rotated X (Management/transactional) + Y (Leadership/transformational) axis labels. New section 4 'Development Tips & Activities' lists 3 catalogue tips per development-area competency (the five lowest self-ratings); Details renumbered to 5. Model doc synced.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona: add Leadership Report (mgmt/leadership matrix) + model doc + 5 interpretation bands on the standard report
entries.plain: Persona now includes a Leadership Report showing how candidates balance management and leadership skills in a visual matrix, plus the standard report displays all five rating band meanings in English and Arabic.
entries.explanation: Leadership Report: 41-competency transactional/transformational mapping, 2x2 matrix, 4 sections, new admin-gated PDF route + results-page link + a published model brief on /evidence. Standard Persona report (EN + AR) now shows the 5 interpretation-band legend under the Overall Self-Rating; 'Critical Gap' renamed 'Critical Focus'.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Methodology briefs: add cited Reliability & Validity Evidence to all 10 assessments
entries.plain: All assessment methodology briefs now cite real published research on reliability and validity, showing honest current evidence without making up statistics.
entries.explanation: Real published method coefficients (Schmidt & Hunter, Gaugler, Roth, Barrick, Harris & Schaubroeck, McDaniel, Sackett 2022, etc.) plus VIFM's own reliability methods/thresholds with honest current status. No fabricated VIFM-specific coefficients.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona results: paginate response fetch (1000-row cap dropped recent sittings' scores + report links)
entries.plain: Persona results now load in smaller batches instead of all at once, so recent test scores and report links display reliably without timing out.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona vouchers: surface results-by-client + voucher activity (when issued, to whom, who completed)
entries.plain: Persona voucher admin can now see results organized by client and track when codes were issued, who received them, and who completed the test.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Rebuild shared voucher shell as the 3-step issue wizard
entries.plain: All four assessment types - Fluent, Logica, Persona, and ARC - now share the same three-step issuing wizard: enter details, choose delivery method, then generate or email codes.
entries.explanation: Fluent/Logica/Persona now use the same wizard as ARC: details+options, deliver to client or delegates, generate batch then email/copy to client, or upload/paste a delegate list and email each a link (generic emailVoucherLinksToDelegatesAction + voucher_to_delegate template).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Frame per-service voucher options in a dashed callout
entries.plain: Service-specific voucher options now appear in a highlighted box to make them easier to spot.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Unify admin voucher pages on one consistent style
entries.plain: All admin voucher pages now use the same consistent visual design and layout across all seven assessment types.
entries.explanation: Shared AdminVoucherIssuer shell for Fluent/Logica/Persona; Techno, ARC, Pre-Hire and Role Readiness restyled to the same Card/Table/Input/Button/Badge look while keeping their per-service controls.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona audit: clear remaining backlog (report explainers, prefill, readiness logs, RTL, Shadcn)
entries.plain: Persona results now show a caution when comparing small groups, include a plain-English legend explaining scores and thresholds, and no longer pre-fill personal data from URL links to prevent security risks.
entries.explanation:
Closes the lower-priority audit tail:
- Report view: small-comparison-group caution when norm n<100; "Reading the
  scores" legend (competency = average of 1-5 self-ratings; band thresholds; all
  self-report).
- Redeem page: only the voucher CODE is prefilled from the URL - name/email/company
  are no longer pre-fillable from query params (phishing-prefill vector closed).
- Readiness: warn when Reflect competencies can't map to AC (silent data loss) and
  when readiness falls back to Persona-self-only (no 360 evidence).
- Take page header now honours the voucher language (dir + Arabic welcome).
- Role designer: raw inputs/textarea -> Shadcn Input/Textarea (native selects kept).
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona audit Batch 2b/3: dedupe nav, emoji->lucide, brand colours, bilingual redeem
entries.plain: Persona interface removes duplicate navigation, replaces emoji icons with standard symbols, updates brand colors, and offers English and Arabic on the redeem form.
entries.explanation:
UI + remaining UX from the audit:
- Removed the duplicate "Design Target Roles" nav chip (it pointed at
  /ac/persona/roles which edits the same role_profiles as the shared
  "Design target roles" -> /admin/role-profiles designer; kept the canonical one).
- Completion screen emoji checkmark -> lucide CheckCircle2 (SOP: no emoji icons).
- Brand-colour fixes: stock blue-200/blue-50/blue-900 driver chips -> accent
  #5391D5 / #010131 in the report view + runner.
- Redeem form is now bilingual (EN/AR toggle + RTL + Arabic labels), matching the
  rest of the Persona surfaces.
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona audit Batch 2a: Likert anchors, time estimate, session-ownership guard
entries.plain: Persona test now displays the Likert scale clearly, shows the 15-20 minute time estimate, and prevents unauthorized access to test sessions.
entries.explanation:
Runner UX + the IDOR hardening (the audit's HIGH item) in one pass since both
touch the runner:
- Likert: visible scale legend (1 = Strongly disagree · 3 = Neither · 5 = Strongly
  agree, bilingual) + aria-label on each 1-5 button (the likertLabel existed but
  was only a title tooltip).
- "How it works" now states the ~15-20 min length + pause/resume.
- Session-ownership guard: savePersonaAnswersAction/submitPersonaAction validate
  that a voucher-linked session carries its matching redemption token (delegate
  can't write to another delegate's session); standalone/staff path unchanged.
- BackLink added to the candidate behavioural page.
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona audit Batch 1: voucher seat-release, RPC lockdown, idempotent submit
entries.plain: Persona voucher system now safely returns unused seats when redemption fails, restricts who can claim seats to prevent hacking, and lets takers resubmit without losing progress.
entries.explanation:
Server-side security/integrity fixes from the deep audit (no runner change):
- migration 00157: persona_voucher_release_seat (atomic hand-back) + lock EXECUTE
  on persona_voucher_claim/release to service_role only (was callable by anon via
  PostgREST - brute-force vector) + pinned search_path on both SECURITY DEFINER fns.
- redeemVoucher: release the claimed seat if the redemption insert fails, so a
  failed redeem no longer permanently burns a use (mirrors rr_release in 00156).
- submitAnonymousBehavioral: idempotency guard - only stamp submitted_at once
  (re-submit recomputes the same deterministic profile without re-marking).
- Persona report route: validate sessionId UUID shape (404 on malformed).
- Voucher email status badge "Sent" -> "Queued" with an "accepted, not delivered"
  tooltip (honest deliverability per the copy-link fallback).
migration 00157 applied + verified live. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona vouchers: don't block generation on competency coverage
entries.plain: Persona voucher generation no longer requires selecting specific competencies - leaving it blank creates a general-purpose link instead of raising an error.
entries.explanation:
Generating a seat-pool code kept erroring 'Select at least one competency'
when the coverage picker was empty/unset - blocking a general link. Now an
empty selection means the FULL bank (no gate): generate() + sendDelegates()
drop the hard competency check and send scopedCompetencyIds=[] (full bank)
when nothing is picked. Helper copy clarifies 'leave as-is for a general link'.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Landing: jewel-per-service modern scheme
entries.plain: The assessment menu now displays each service in its own distinct color scheme instead of using the same blue for most, making it easier to find what you need.
entries.explanation:
Switch the landing from the institutional (single navy/azure) treatment to a
distinct hue per service. Every card now uses its own tone (not just the four
single-purpose ones - the six dual-purpose diagnostics were previously all
blue), so the .launcher-card system dresses each with its own tinted surface,
gradient icon chip, corner glyph, top accent line and hover glow. Persona moves
off fuchsia (shared with Mentium) to a new cyan tone so all ten read distinctly;
the resting border is a soft tint of the hue. Hero eyebrow reverted to brand
azure (the gold signature belonged to the institutional direction).

Verified in preview (all ten hues distinct, no console errors); tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add "Design target roles" to every service landing's top nav
entries.plain: Every assessment now links to role design from its main page so you can define job requirements before running any test.
entries.explanation:
The target role is the common thread across every instrument - design it once,
assess against it with any service. Add a shared DesignTargetRolesLink (->
/admin/role-profiles) next to "All Services" on the standalone service heroes
(Fluent, Reason, Persona, ARC selection + main, Reflect, AI interview) and a
matching link in the All-Services launcher top bar (bilingual EN/AR).

Admin-area pages (Pre-Hire, Technical, AC) already reach the designer via the
sidebar's Role Profiles link, so they're unchanged.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Framework count: single source of truth (41 competencies, 9 clusters) + landing colours + ARC submit gate
entries.plain: The framework now correctly shows 41 competencies in 9 skill clusters - a single source of truth across all pages so the count never goes out of sync.
entries.explanation:
Competency count is 41 (migration 00100 added the 9th cluster "Customer &
Stakeholder Focus" + competencies 39-41); the DB and Persona bank already hold
41 - only the copy was stale at "38". Fixes:
- New src/lib/competencies/framework-meta.ts is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH
  (COMPETENCY_COUNT=41, FRAMEWORK_CLUSTER_COUNT=9, DOMAIN_COUNT=4 + labels),
  documented as mirroring the seeded tables.
- Services that DISPLAY the count import it: landing (persona copy, EN+AR),
  compare sheet (4 cells, EN+AR), AC roadmap stat card, evidence-map matrix.
- Stale "38" corrected to 41 across code comments, EN/AR locale strings, and
  CLAUDE.md (8 clusters -> 9). False-positives left alone (colours, fontSize,
  estimated_minutes, itemKey "38-x", marginTop).

Landing tiles (prior request):
- Dual-purpose tiles get dark-blue (#111232) borders; the four single-purpose
  portals each get a distinct hue with a matching tagline (Pre-Hire rose,
  Reflect 360 teal, Succession Readiness amber, VIFM Academy emerald).
- Talent Acquisition button: white text on the light-blue (#5391D5) fill.

ARC respondent form: Submit is dimmed/disabled until every required (scored)
question is answered, with a hint line. Wired via a CompletionContext so the
server-passed CompleteButton reads the form's answered state (optional open_text
reflections do not gate). tsc + lint clean; landing verified in-browser.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona PER-10: more most/least blocks + non-diluting forced-choice scoring
entries.plain: Persona's ranking section now includes more competency comparisons per test without making any single skill harder or easier to rate fairly.
entries.explanation:
buildIpsativeBlocks now draws up to IPSATIVE_STATEMENTS_PER_COMPETENCY (2) forward
statements per competency across rounds (round-robin), so the forced-choice
section has ~2x the blocks - a competency can be ranked in several blocks. Every
block is still four DIFFERENT competencies (verified no collision).

Crucially, the scoring is reworked so extra blocks don't dilute. A new shared
rollupSelfScores() (used by BOTH the anonymous + candidate paths) collapses a
competency's forced-choice signal to ONE value = 3 + (#most - #least) clamped
1-5, averaged with the normative values. So appearing in more blocks SHARPENS
the preference signal instead of dragging the average toward the mid (which the
old per-statement average of most=5/least=1/mid=3 would do as blocks multiply).
Unpicked statements carry no signal; a competency that appears but is never
picked stays neutral (3), so coverage is never lost.

Verified (pure test): 9-competency role -> 4 blocks (was ~2), 9/9 scored, score
spread 2.5-4.5 (no dilution-to-3), no same-competency block collisions.
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona: jump-to-unanswered (PER-9) + instant taker scoring (PER-11)
entries.plain: Persona now highlights unanswered questions so takers jump directly to them, and scores appear instantly after submission instead of waiting for AI insights to process.
entries.explanation:
PER-9: on the normative pages, a taker blocked at the final Continue/Submit
(must answer all) now gets a "Jump to the unanswered question" button that
switches to the right page, scrolls to the item and briefly highlights it -
instead of hunting page by page. Each item gets an anchor id.

PER-11: "scoring takes a long time" - submit built the full report (insights,
summary, course recommender, interview probes = 3-4 AI calls) for every taker.
With XP-13 the taker never sees the report, so we now build it only for staff;
a taker's submit runs just the deterministic scoring and returns near-instantly.
An admin builds the report on demand from the report route / cohort (insights are
cached on the session).

tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: XP-13 (Persona + ARC personal): hide results from the taker; staff-only
entries.plain: Persona and ARC results are now hidden from test-takers and shown only to staff, ensuring consistent privacy rules across all assessments.
entries.explanation:
Completes the cross-portal results-visibility change (Technical already conformed;
Fluent + Cognitive done).

Persona: submitPersonaAction returns isStaff; the standalone runner now shows the
report/result ONLY to staff - any non-staff taker (not just voucher delegates)
gets the "submitted" thank-you. The report PDF route was already admin-gated.

ARC personal: the results page + PDF route no longer key on the org
respondent_can_view_results flag - the taker (token, no session) always sees a
thank-you; only an authenticated admin/consultant sees results. The PDF route
allows the internal client-delivery header (collect-and-send) or staff; denies
token-only. The taker results email is dropped (it would only 403 the attachment
and link to a thank-you); the completion credential + send-to-client stay.

isStaffCaller() is the single gate across all portals. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona report: gap/strength arrows (red/green) + drop Reflect 360 under hiring
entries.plain: Persona reports now show colored arrows (green for strengths, red for gaps) instead of numbers, and hiring reports no longer mention Reflect 360 feedback.
entries.explanation:
PER-7: replace the bare "(-1.2)" gap deltas with directional arrows - a green
up-triangle on strengths (at/above target) and a red down-triangle + the gap
size on shortfalls. Applied to the web report view, the EN React-PDF, and the AR
Puppeteer HTML.

PER-8/12: the report already branches hiring vs development; this removes the
"Reflect 360" references from the HIRING captions (verdict line, screening
caveat, decision-integration block, methodology footer, and the AR
role-fit subtitle). Development keeps its "pair with a Reflect 360" guidance.
Hiring now reads "corroborate with a structured interview and work evidence."

tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Landing: two distinct-coloured level entries per service, no default highlight
entries.plain: The landing page now displays two clearly labeled, differently colored buttons per assessment - one for hiring and one for development - making it easier to choose the right path.
entries.explanation:
Refines the per-service purpose selector per feedback:
- Label each level with its family: "For selection" -> Talent Acquisition,
  "For development" -> Talent Development.
- Two DISTINCT colours (blue = selection, emerald = development), shown as two
  equal entries - neither is pre-highlighted/dimmed (the toggle-with-default is
  gone; each level is its own coloured direct-entry button to the right route
  incl. ARC/Technical lens + Persona purpose params).
- Single-purpose services show just their one coloured entry.

Verified live (authed): 10 cards, correct hrefs per level, no console errors,
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: SD-10: idempotent SDAIA pilot seeding script
entries.plain: A new setup script makes it simple to seed test batches for the Saudi Arabia pilot without accidentally creating duplicates.
entries.explanation:
scripts/seed-sdaia-pilots.ts ensures the SDAIA client org (saudi /
government, dual-written) and, on --apply, issues the three pilot voucher
batches: Pilot 1 Persona hiring (SDAIA HR role profile, 2 codes), Pilot 2
Technical L&D (node 2.6, 20 codes), Pilot 3 Persona development (2 codes).

Dry-run by default (prints the plan, issues nothing); re-runnable (skips any
batch whose label already exists). Reuses the app voucher libs so the issued
codes are identical to the admin UI. Verified in dry-run against live data:
org ensured, role profile + active L&D function both resolve.

Issuing live codes remains the operator's call (run with --apply). Pilots 1+3
are Persona codes, Pilot 2 is a Technical code - not ARC questionnaire codes.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: SD-9: Persona item-format chooser (presentation only)
entries.plain: Admins and test-takers can now choose how Persona questions are presented: traditional scale only, ranking only, or both formats.
entries.explanation:
An admin (voucher) or a standalone taker can now run the Persona as
'normative' (Likert only), 'ipsative' (most/least only), or 'both' (default,
unchanged). Migration 00140 adds item_format to behavioral_assessment_sessions
+ persona_vouchers (default 'both', tolerant peel-newest-first inserts).

Threaded through: createVoucherBatch + getVoucherScopeByRedemptionToken (read
item_format separately so a pending 00140 can't drop the existing scope),
createAnonymousBehavioralSession, startPersonaAction (voucher pin is server-
authoritative), the take page (pins the runner), the standalone runner (intro
chooser + phase machine skips the unused section + format-aware submit/back/
demo-fill), and the voucher issuance UI.

Scoping is unchanged: this is forced-choice PRESENTATION, not ipsative
MEASUREMENT - ipsative picks still map to pseudo-Likert pooled into the
per-competency mean. True ipsative scoring (Thurstonian IRT, MFC blocks,
norms) is explicitly deferred.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: SD-2: 9-box placement + Persona-only succession readiness tier
entries.plain: Persona alone can now determine succession readiness (without Reflect 360), and individuals are placed on the 9-box grid for talent planning.
entries.explanation:
Two gaps closed, both demo-critical for Pilot 3 (Persona-only succession):

1. Persona-as-driver tier. The readiness engine was 360-driven, so a
   Persona-only candidate (no 360) always returned insufficient_data.
   readiness-data.ts now falls back to the candidate's Persona self-ratings
   as the driver when no 360 exists, tagging evidenceSource='persona_self'
   so the report labels it as self-reported (corroborate with a 360).

2. 9-box on the individual report. computeReadiness now places the candidate
   on the AC talent-map 9-box (Performance = Results+People, Potential =
   Thinking+Self; reusing talent-map.ts scoreBand + NINE_BOX), with a domain
   field threaded through from the competency->cluster->domain join. Rendered
   as a highlighted 3x3 grid on the on-screen readiness page and the PDF, plus
   a "Self-reported (Persona)" tag on the headline tier. i18n EN+AR keys added.

No migration. Cohort 9-box (talent-map page) unchanged; this is the per-person
report. Tier stays the 4-band engine.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: SD-1: add Ready / Ready-with-Development / Not-Ready verdict to Persona hiring report
entries.plain: Persona hiring reports now include a clear Ready / Ready-with-Development / Not-Ready verdict based on job fit scores.
entries.explanation:
Additive to the existing fit % + band, keyed off fitPct (>=80 Ready,
55-79 Ready with Development, <55 Not Ready). readinessVerdict() in
persona-fit.ts; threaded through buildPersonaPdfData onto the hiring fit
payload; rendered on the EN React-PDF, the AR HTML report, and the on-screen
view. Labelled "indicative (self-report) - pair with a Reflect 360 for a
validated readiness verdict" to preserve the screening-signal guardrail.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Project cohort label: shared Persona + Cognitive cohort view (CAL-PER-406, CAL-TM-303)
entries.plain: Admins can now tag Persona and Cognitive test batches with a project name, and view combined results for both in one cohort report.
entries.explanation:
Add a project/cohort label (migration 00137) that rides voucher ->
redemption -> result for BOTH Persona and Cognitive, mirroring how
organization_id flows. An admin sets the label when issuing a voucher
batch; the new /admin/cohorts page lists projects and shows a combined
roster (Persona self-rating + Cognitive g side by side) matched across
the two instruments by email, with per-instrument summaries + report
links. Cross-linked from both instrument cohort pages + a Talent
Management sidebar entry.

Threaded newest-first peels keep both voucher inserts + the Persona
session insert tolerant of a pending 00137; the Cognitive route splits
the result UPDATE so a missing project_label column never drops the
org linkage.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Voucher email + redeem link + access code: Persona + Cognitive (CAL-PER-405, CAL-TA-205)
entries.plain: Admins can now email test-takers a one-click link to start Persona or Cognitive assessments, removing the need to manually share access codes.
entries.explanation:
Admins can now email a one-click redeem link (access code baked into the URL)
to delegates from both the Persona and Cognitive voucher tabs, mirroring the
shipped Technical Sandbox / ARA pattern.

- New src/lib/persona/email.ts + src/lib/cognitive/email.ts: bilingual EN/AR
  (RTL-aware) branded redeem-link emails via the Resend transport, guarded by
  resendConfigured().
- vouchers actions (persona + cognitive): emailVoucherDelegatesAction (one
  single-use voucher per delegate, inheriting batch scope, best-effort
  assigned_email stamp, builds /ac/<svc>/redeem?code=&email=, sends, returns
  per-delegate results) + emailExistingVoucherCodeAction for the per-row Email
  button. All admin-gated via requireRole(["admin"]); email validation +
  dedupe + 200/send cap; hiring still requires a target role.
- Redeem pages + forms (persona + cognitive): read ?email/?name/?company and
  prefill the form (code still prefills).
- Vouchers UI: "Email links to delegates" panel (email or email,name lines) +
  per-row Copy redeem link + Email link.
- Migration 00130: assigned_email on persona_vouchers + cognitive_vouchers
  (IF NOT EXISTS); all writes tolerant of it being unapplied.

Implemented by subagent, reviewed for auth-gating/tolerance/conventions; tsc
+ next lint clean. Email sending needs RESEND_API_KEY (else per-row "not
configured"). Apply migration 00130.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona fixes from adversarial review (A+B)
entries.plain: Fixed a bug where hiring assessments lost their role and target information on certain database setups, and improved Arabic strength-word recognition.
entries.explanation:
- behavioral.ts: build the session insert in migration order and peel the
  NEWEST migration's columns first (00129 email -> 00123 scope -> 00110
  purpose/seed -> core). The previous chain nested taker_email inside base,
  so on a DB at 00110+00123 but not 00129 a hiring run fell all the way to
  core and silently lost purpose/target/seed (rendered hiring as a no-role
  development report). Now an absent 00129 only drops email.
- persona-insights.ts: broaden PRAISE_AR to catch the قوة strength root in
  praise constructions (it previously missed قوة واضحة / قوة ذاتية / نقاط
  القوة, reintroducing the contradiction on AR reports); drop the absolute
  3.5 floor in isLow so at/above-target scores on low-target roles keep
  legitimate strength language; tighten PRAISE_EN 'master' to mastery/
  masterful (not the bare verb 'yet to master').
- persona-bands.ts + persona-report-view.tsx: the on-screen gap bar now
  uses inline hex (gapToneHex), not a Tailwind class - src/lib is outside
  the Tailwind content globs so bg-lime-500 would have been purged, leaving
  a slight-gap bar colourless. Removed the dead GAP_TONE_TW map.

Spot-checked regexes + isLow; tsc + next lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona report: score-consistent narrative + gap bars + count (CAL-PER-401/404/407)
entries.plain: Persona narratives now always match the displayed scores, bar charts show skill gaps clearly, and competency counts are visible throughout reports.
entries.explanation:
CAL-PER-401: the AI per-competency narrative can no longer contradict the
displayed score. Both system prompts gain a hard band rule (no mastery/
excellence language below the role target); a server-side post-check
discards an over-praising narrative for a clearly-below-target score (EN+AR
praise patterns) and falls back to the deterministic, score-consistent
text. Cached insights carry a version stamp (__v) so pre-guardrail
narratives regenerate on next render.

CAL-PER-404: per-competency bars are now coloured green->red by the
fractional gap to the role target (new personaGapTone / gapToneHex/Tw in
persona-bands.ts), on all three renderers (on-screen, EN PDF, AR HTML).
Uses the un-rounded gap, not getCompetencyGap. No target -> brand accent.

CAL-PER-407: the fit panel's priorities/strengths now show the top 5 to
match the 5-row action plan (internal top-6 kept for course recs / drivers
/ watch areas).

Spot-checked thresholds + praise regex; tsc + next lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona: lock purpose from landing tile + capture email (CAL-PER-402/403)
entries.plain: The hiring vs. development choice is now locked in from the landing button, and test-takers enter their email upfront for recruitment follow-up.
entries.explanation:
CAL-PER-402: the landing tile sets Persona's purpose - Talent Acquisition
-> hiring, Talent Management -> development. The runner reads ?purpose,
hides the purpose picker, and shows a read-only banner; the taker still
picks a target role. A bare /ac/persona (admin) keeps the free picker.

CAL-PER-403: capture the taker email on the intro - required for hiring
(selection/lead capture), optional for development - persisted via a new
behavioral_assessment_sessions.taker_email column (migration 00129).
createAnonymousBehavioralSession gains a tolerant email-strip fallback so
a pre-00129 DB still works.

Verified in preview: TA=hiring banner + required email, TM=development
banner + optional email, bare entry keeps picker, landing hrefs carry the
purpose. tsc + next lint clean. Apply migration 00129.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona development report: reframe to third-person (admin/manager-facing)
entries.plain: Development reports now address managers and HR teams directly rather than the test-taker, using language like 'Profile at a glance' instead of 'Your profile'.
entries.explanation:
The development report is read by the admin/hiring/line manager ABOUT the person,
not by the person. Reframed all second-person copy to third person across the
on-screen view, EN PDF and AR PDF: 'Your profile at a glance' -> 'Profile at a
glance', 'Your development plan' -> 'Development action plan', 'For your
development conversation' -> 'For the development conversation', 'Questions to ask
yourself' -> 'Self-reflection questions', captions 'how you see yourself' -> 'how
the person sees themselves'. The AI summary + per-competency insight prompts now
require third person ('the person/they'), and the deterministic summary fallback
(EN + AR) is third-person too.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona: gate both reports admin/client; VIFM Academy at end of dev report
entries.plain: Both hiring and development Persona reports are now restricted to admins only, and VIFM Academy course recommendations now appear at the end of development reports.
entries.explanation:
- Both the hiring and development reports are now admin/client only: the report
  PDF route requires admin auth for BOTH purposes (development was open before),
  and the on-screen result is hidden from any voucher candidate (both purposes),
  shown only on the admin standalone page.
- VIFM Academy programmes now render at the END of the development report (after
  the competency analysis and the development-plan scaffold), in the on-screen
  view, the EN PDF and the AR PDF. Verified: academy is the last section.
- Definitions + per-competency narratives remain in BOTH reports (every
  competency row), unchanged.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona development: always propose VIFM Academy courses for the gaps
entries.plain: Persona development reports now always recommend VIFM Academy courses to close skill gaps, even without a specific target role in mind.
entries.explanation:
Development now attaches to VIFM Academy even without a target role: it derives
the gaps from the lowest self-ratings (against an aspirational target of 4) and
proposes a ranked course plan to fill them, plus the holistic summary, planning
scaffold and coaching prompts. With a role bound, the existing path scores gaps
against the role target. Either way the development report carries a VIFM Academy
training plan. Verified: a no-role development run renders 6 recommended courses.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona: render full report sections on-screen (parity with PDF)
entries.plain: Persona on-screen results now show the same detailed sections as the PDF report, including interview guides and coaching prompts, so candidates and hiring teams see complete information in one place.
entries.explanation:
The on-screen result now renders the SAME PersonaPdfData the PDF uses, via a new
PersonaReportView - so the live view shows every sharpened section (interview
guide, decision-integration worksheet, role-critical markers, areas-to-verify,
holistic summary, planning scaffold, coaching prompts, overused-strength,
response-style, percentiles, course plan) instead of a lighter subset.
submitPersonaAction now returns the built report (buildPersonaPdfData), so the
on-screen result and the downloadable PDF can never diverge again. Falls back to
the lighter view if the build fails. EN/AR via lang.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona result: normalize Role fit title size
entries.plain: Persona result page now displays the role fit title in a consistent, normalized size for better readability.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona result: enlarge role-name subtitle under Role fit
entries.plain: The role name subtitle under role fit in Persona results is now larger and easier to read.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix Persona zero-report on mixed-answer save + enlarge/center Role fit title
entries.plain: Fixed a critical bug where Persona reports showed zero scores when answers were saved quickly - the system now correctly processes all answer types together.
entries.explanation:
Root cause of the 'generate report gave me zero' bug: when normative + ipsative
answers save in one batch (the demo 'fill all', or a fast Submit that flushes
both at once), the normative rows omitted item_type while ipsative rows set it.
supabase-js unions the columns and inserts NULL item_type for the normative
rows, violating the NOT NULL constraint, so the WHOLE upsert failed and nothing
persisted -> the session scored zero. Fix: always set item_type ('normative' by
default) so every batch is column-uniform. Verified: a fill-all + immediate
Submit now persists 205/205 responses and scores normally.

Also hardened the client: serialize autosave flushes (await any in-flight save
before scoring) and flush immediately after 'fill random', so a fast Submit
can never score before answers land.

Plus: the on-screen + PDF (EN/AR) 'Role fit' title is now much bigger and
centered, with the role name as a centered subtitle.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona: add demo 'Fill random answers' shortcut (gated by ?demo=1)
entries.plain: Demo presenters can now auto-fill all Persona questions in one click during live client presentations, skipping the 180+ manual clicks without affecting real candidate assessments.
entries.explanation:
A presenter can fill every normative item + ipsative block in one click during a
live client demo instead of clicking ~180 controls. Fills random valid answers,
queues them for autosave, and jumps to the Submit step. Gated behind ?demo=1 (or
dev) and shown only during the answering phases, so real candidates never see it
and a genuine sitting can't be tainted. Wired on both the standalone Persona page
and the voucher take page.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona hiring report: move interview guide to the end
entries.plain: The structured interview guide in Persona hiring reports now appears at the end after competency analysis, making it a convenient take-to-interview reference.
entries.explanation:
The structured interview guide (STAR probes) now renders after the per-competency
clusters, just before the closing caption, in both EN and AR - the take-into-the-
room artifact at the end. The decision-integration worksheet stays under the fit
panel.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona report: move development plan scaffold to the end
entries.plain: The development plan scaffold in Persona reports now appears at the end so the report flows from analysis to actionable next steps.
entries.explanation:
The 'Your development plan' fill-in scaffold now renders after the per-competency
clusters (just before the closing caption) in both the EN and AR reports, so the
report reads analysis first and ends with the take-away action plan.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona reports: fix 5 review findings (AR parity, norms FK)
entries.plain: Persona Arabic reports now correctly display all narratives and footers in Arabic instead of accidentally mixing in English text.
entries.explanation:
Adversarial review follow-ups:
- AR per-competency narratives: thread lang through the deterministic narrators
  (competencyNarrative/developmentNarrative gain Arabic branches) so the no-key/
  no-role fallback no longer leaks English into the Arabic report.
- AR report: add a per-page confidential + date footer (position:fixed repeats
  every page, shaped with the page font) mirroring the EN footer.
- AR development plan: match course codes on the language-stable English driver
  label (via recs) so the right VIFM code attaches in Arabic.
- AR percentages: keep digits + % together inside one dir=ltr run (numPct).
- 00128: add the missing FK persona_competency_norms.competency_id ->
  competencies(id) ON DELETE CASCADE (idempotent; aligns with sibling tables).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Persona hiring + development sample reports (EN + AR)
entries.plain: Sample Persona hiring and development reports in English and Arabic are now available for review and training.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona reports: hiring/development sharpening + norms + Arabic
entries.plain: Persona reports now include AI-generated interview guides, decision worksheets, competency markers, and response consistency analysis, with full Arabic language support.
entries.explanation:
Tier 1 (items 1-10) + Tier 2 (norms, Arabic):
- Hiring: structured interview guide (AI, grounded in lower-rated answers +
  deterministic fallback), decision-integration worksheet, role-critical SVG
  markers + inline self/target, areas-to-verify flag.
- Development: holistic opening narrative, development-planning scaffold,
  coaching/self-reflection prompts, overused-strength read.
- Shared: response-style/consistency indicator (advisory); Persona methodology
  brief + render route + in-report footer.
- Norms (00127): percentile context (normal-CDF) + seed script; tolerant.
- Arabic (?lang=ar): full RTL render via Chromium off a shared data builder;
  AI text in Arabic; cached under report_extras.ar.
- 00126 report_extras cache; all paths tolerant of 00126/00127 unapplied.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add sample Persona development report PDF for review
entries.plain: A sample Persona development report PDF is now available for internal review and quality assurance.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona development report: mirror hiring flow + Academy plan
entries.plain: Persona development reports now mirror the hiring flow, allowing candidates to pick a target role and receive an AI-powered growth plan with recommended VIFM Academy courses.
entries.explanation:
Development purpose now picks a target role + scope (same as hiring). The
report reframes as a development plan: AI growth narrative per competency
(grounded in the answer pattern) + a ranked VIFM Academy course plan from
the gaps. Shared on-screen + PDF; both cap at 6 courses. Admin Results view
shows role+alignment for development too. Hiring stays admin-gated.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona report: lazy-generate insights on view + PDF strengths
entries.plain: Persona reports now auto-generate missing insights when viewed and show candidate strengths alongside development areas, ensuring every report is complete and consistent.
entries.explanation:
- Report route lazy-generates the per-competency AI insights when a hiring
  session has none stored yet (legacy sittings, or a submit-time miss),
  then caches them - so every hiring report is insight-rich and the system
  is self-healing. Shared buildInsightCompetencies() keeps submit + report
  paths identical.
- PDF hiring panel now shows Biggest strengths (green) above the gaps,
  matching the on-screen report.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona hiring report: AI insights, strengths, paired cards, Persona(R)
entries.plain: Persona hiring reports now feature AI-generated insights tailored to each candidate's actual answers and the target role, plus a strengths section, replacing generic templates.
entries.explanation:
Reworks the hiring report from a templated read to a professional one:
- AI-GENERATED per-competency insight grounded in the candidate's actual
  item-level answers (which statements they rated high vs low) + the role
  target - varied per competency, not boilerplate. Generated once at
  submit (persona-insights.ts), stored on the session (migration 00125),
  reused by the on-screen result + the PDF. Deterministic fallback when
  no API key. Honest self-report framing throughout.
- Biggest STRENGTHS now shown alongside biggest gaps.
- Competency detail laid out as paired boxes (2-up grid); the self/target
  score is larger, centred, and GREEN at/above target, RED below.
- "Persona(R)" registered mark on the report name (runner header, voucher
  header, PDF banner).

Verified: tsc clean; PDF regenerates 200 for an existing hiring session.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add per-competency definitions + narrative to the Persona hiring report
entries.plain: Persona hiring reports now include a definition for each competency and a narrative explaining how the candidate compares to the role target, grounding hiring decisions in concrete evidence.
entries.explanation:
The hiring report now explains each competency, not just the fit score:
- A framework DEFINITION ("what it means") per competency.
- A deterministic NARRATIVE interpreting the candidate's self-rating
  against the role target (e.g. "self-rates below the role target
  (3.0 vs 4.0) - a development area; probe at interview"). Derived from
  the actual answers + target, not AI, so it is defensible for hiring.

- persona-fit.ts: competencyNarrative(self, target?) pure helper.
- competency-definitions.ts: shared loadCompetencyDefinitions().
- On-screen result (PersonaResult): a "Competency detail" section under
  the fit headline - per competency: self/target, definition, narrative.
  definitions threaded from both Persona pages.
- PDF report: each competency row now carries the narrative; the route
  builds a target map so the interpretation is target-aware for hiring.

Verified: PDF for a real SDAIA HR hiring session renders 200 / 9KB.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add 4-domain rollup to the Design Target Roles screen
entries.plain: Job descriptions now organize skills by four domains (Thinking, Results, People, Self) on the Design Target Roles screen, making it easier to see skill balance at a glance.
entries.explanation:
The JD-extracted competencies now group by DOMAIN (THINKING / RESULTS /
PEOPLE / SELF) -> area -> competency, with a domain rollup chip row
(e.g. "THINKING · 3"), using the real cluster->domain mapping from the
DB (competency_clusters.domain_id -> competency_domains). Domain colours
match the candidate-skills / JD-tally palette.

- actions.ts: extractRoleFromJdAction resolves each competency's domain
  via competencies.cluster_id -> competency_clusters -> competency_domains;
  DesignedCompetency gains domain + domainSort.
- role-designer.tsx: domain rollup chips + domain/area nested grouping.

Verified live: SDAIA JD -> THINKING 3 / RESULTS 2 / PEOPLE 3 / SELF 1.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add "Design Target Roles" (JD -> role profile) to Persona
entries.plain: Admins can now paste a job description and the system automatically maps it to skills and priorities, then save it as a reusable role template for hiring and assessments.
entries.explanation:
New admin screen at /ac/persona/roles (reached from a "Design Target
Roles" pill on the Persona landing): paste a job description, the AI
maps it to the VIFM competency framework (grouped by area), then set
weight / priority / target per competency and save it as a reusable
target role. Saved roles write role_profiles + role_profile_competencies
(with per-competency target_proficiency - the bit the /admin editor
omits) and are then selectable anywhere a role is picked: Persona hiring,
vouchers, and the engagement wizard.

- actions.ts: extractRoleFromJdAction (reuses the JD competency extractor;
  admin-gated) + saveTargetRoleAction (service client; tolerant of 00097).
- role-designer.tsx: JD intake -> editable competency list grouped by area
  with weight/priority/target -> save.
- page.tsx: admin-gated; lists existing roles + the designer.

Verified live: a sample SDAIA JD extracted 9 competencies across 7 areas.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add admin "completed Persona results" view (fit + report)
entries.plain: Admins now have a dedicated page to view all completed Persona assessments, including fit scores and one-click access to downloadable reports.
entries.explanation:
A dedicated admin page listing completed standalone Persona sittings
(voucher / self-served) with the data the hiring pilot needs: purpose,
target role, the computed role-FIT % + band (hiring), the overall
self-rating, and a one-click report PDF. This is how an admin retrieves
a voucher candidate's result now that the candidate-facing fit is hidden.

- src/lib/scoring/persona-results.ts: listPersonaResults() - loads
  submitted sessions + computes fit over the served role competencies
  (mirrors the report route); tolerant of 00110/00106 absent.
- /ac/persona/results: admin-gated table (getCurrentCaller=admin) with
  client filter + report links; complements the aggregate cohort page.
- "Results" pill added to the Persona landing nav.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Hide candidate-facing fit on Persona hiring (report is client-only)
entries.plain: Job candidates no longer see their fit scores after completing a Persona assessment - those results are now reserved for hiring managers and admins only.
entries.explanation:
For a hiring assessment the fit result is a client/admin deliverable,
not shown to the candidate:
- Voucher (candidate) hiring flow now ends on a completion confirmation
  instead of the fit/gaps screen. Development sittings still show the
  taker their growth read; the admin's own direct runs still show
  results (the admin is not the candidate).
- The report PDF route requires admin auth for a hiring session, so a
  candidate can't pull their own fit PDF even with the session id;
  development self-reads stay open.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add coverage override on standalone Persona (role vs full 41)
entries.plain: Admins can now toggle between assessing only role-specific skills or the full skill framework when running a Persona assessment directly, without changing how the score is calculated.
entries.explanation:
On the direct Persona runner, once a hiring role is selected the admin
can toggle competency coverage between the role's competencies (default,
scoped/shorter) and the full framework (all 41) - useful to demonstrate
the whole instrument to a client while still scoring fit against the
role. Default stays role-scoped; the voucher (pinned) path is unaffected.

Verified: SDAIA HR role -> 48 statements (role) / 164 (full profile).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Scope standalone Persona to the selected role's competencies
entries.plain: Persona assessments now use the selected role to determine which skills are tested, so the same role produces the same test length whether shared by link or run directly.
entries.explanation:
The role profile is now the single source of truth for scope on BOTH
paths. On the standalone /ac/persona runner, selecting a hiring role
narrows the served competencies to that role's set (mirroring the
voucher path) - so the same designed role yields the same scoped test
whether issued by voucher or run directly. The voucher (pinned) path is
left untouched (its competencies prop is already scoped upstream and may
carry an admin override). Also drops the optimistic "about 12-15 minutes"
copy; the statement count now reflects the scoped set.

Verified: selecting the SDAIA HR role drops Part 1 from 164 to 48 statements.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Harden Persona scoping + seed SDAIA HR role profile
entries.plain: Persona fit scores are now calculated consistently on screen and in downloadable reports, and candidates cannot manipulate assessment scope through technical workarounds.
entries.explanation:
Review fixes for the SD-1 scoping build:
- On-screen hiring fit now computes over the role competencies actually
  served (filter to measured), mirroring the PDF route - a scoped run no
  longer understates fit on screen or diverges from the downloaded PDF.
- saveBehavioralAnswers enforces the pinned competency scope server-side:
  out-of-scope answers are dropped at the write path, so a crafted call
  can't inject competencies and widen a pinned assessment (the UI filter
  alone was not load-bearing).
- Robust undefined-column detection (42703 read + PGRST204 write) drives
  the 00123/00110 strip-and-retry fallbacks, replacing message regexes
  that missed the PostgREST write-path error shape.

Plus migration 00124: a starter "SDAIA - HR / Talent Acquisition
Specialist" role profile (12 framework competencies, weighted +
prioritised + per-competency targets) so the Persona hiring picker has
a credible SDAIA-relevant target role for the TA pilot.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add admin-pinned competency scoping to Persona vouchers (SD-1)
entries.plain: Persona assessment links now lock in the skills to be tested and the purpose (hiring or development) at the time of issuance, so candidates cannot change the scope.
entries.explanation:
A Persona voucher now pins the assessment config at issuance: purpose
(hiring/development), a target role profile, and the competency scope.
The candidate redeems and just takes the pre-configured test - they
cannot change or widen it (scope is derived server-side from the
voucher, never trusted from the client). The runner serves only the
scoped competencies' items (full bank ~164; a subset ~4 x N), and the
hiring fit report computes over the served competencies only.

- Migration 00123: persona_vouchers.purpose + target_role_profile_id +
  scoped_competency_ids; behavioral_assessment_sessions.scoped_competency_ids
- vouchers lib/action: persist scope, tolerant of 00123 absent; a hiring
  voucher requires a target role (server-guarded)
- voucher issuance UI: purpose toggle + role picker (pre-fills the role's
  competencies) + per-cluster competency picker with Full-profile/Clear
- take page + runner: filter served competencies; lock the picker when
  pinned (read-only "Assessing for <role>")
- startPersonaAction: derive + override scope from the voucher server-side
- report: fit over measured role competencies only (no zero for unserved)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona: randomized + section-hidden flow, ipsative items, purpose-by-pillar
entries.plain: Persona questions now appear in random order with section labels hidden to reduce bias, and questions are shuffled differently for hiring versus development purposes.
entries.explanation:
Reworks the Persona (behavioural self-assessment) product surface per the
talent-acquisition vs talent-development split:

- Hide sections + randomize. Likert statements now present in a seeded-random
  flat order with cluster/section labels hidden (reduces section priming +
  ordering effects). The seed is stored on the session so a report can
  reconstruct exactly what the taker saw. Paginated 12/page.
- Two item types. Adds an IPSATIVE forced-choice section ("most / least like
  me" blocks of four, one forward statement per competency) alongside the
  NORMATIVE Likert section. Forced-choice maps to a 1-5 contribution
  (most=5, least=1, mid=3) and flows through the same per-competency rollup,
  so no scoring-engine change is needed; item_type/answer_data are stored for
  audit + reproducibility.
- Purpose-by-pillar. The intro asks what the run is for:
  - Hiring/selection -> a role FIT SCORE (weighted-gap vs a target role
    profile) + biggest gaps, with the screening-not-decision guardrail.
  - Development -> per-competency explanation + suggestions (band narrative +
    development tips on the rows that need them).
- Report. Persona PDF now carries competency DEFINITIONS, per-competency band
  narrative, development suggestions, and a hiring fit panel.

New pure libs: persona-format.ts (seeded shuffle, flatten, forced-choice
blocks), persona-fit.ts (weighted fit), persona-roles.ts (role loader).
Migration 00110 (write-only; user applies): behavioral_assessment_sessions +=
purpose/target_role_profile_id/randomization_seed; responses += item_type/
answer_data. All paths tolerant of 00110 not being applied (strip-and-retry).
The candidate-bound readiness "self" feed is intentionally unchanged.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix clipboard crash on Copy (NotAllowedError) + add fallback
entries.plain: The copy-link button on ARC assessment links no longer crashes in certain browsers or restricted environments and now falls back to alternative copy methods.
entries.explanation:
The ARC vouchers "Copy links" button fired navigator.clipboard.writeText
without awaiting/catching, so a denied write (e.g. an iframe lacking
clipboard-write permission, like the dev preview) surfaced as an
unhandled runtime error. Add a shared copyToClipboard() helper
(src/lib/utils/clipboard.ts): Clipboard API first, then a legacy
execCommand fallback that works in those contexts, throwing only if both
fail. Wire it into all four voucher screens (ARC fix + Cognitive/Fluent/
Persona). Verified: Copy no longer crashes and shows the copied state.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix React hydration mismatches from locale date/number formatting
entries.plain: Date and number formatting across assessment voucher pages now displays consistently in all browsers, eliminating display mismatches.
entries.explanation:
toLocaleDateString()/toLocaleString() with no fixed locale/timezone
render differently on the server vs the browser ("17/06/2026" vs
"6/17/2026"), tripping "Text content did not match" in client
components. Add a shared deterministic UTC formatter
(src/lib/utils/format-date.ts) and apply it across the four voucher
screens (ARC/Cognitive/Fluent/Persona), the notification bell, and the
Reflect IDP editor; pin the Pre-Hire client-report date to UTC and the
Evidence Map number format to en-US. (Washup uses a post-mount event
time, not SSR, so it's untouched.)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Admin nav: group self-serve instruments; expose retention surfaces
entries.plain: The admin menu now groups self-serve assessments (Cognitive, Persona, Fluent) and Pre-Hire into expandable sections showing all management options, improving navigation.
entries.explanation:
Promotes the self-serve instruments (Cognitive, Persona, Fluent) and
Pre-Hire from single sidebar links to expandable groups listing their
management surfaces (Overview / Cohort / Vouchers / Calibration /
Retention), matching the Technical + Assessment Center pattern. Closes
the Cognitive admin-console discoverability gap and makes the new
retention pages reachable from the sidebar. Adds shared svc* i18n keys
(EN/AR).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona to ARC bar: cohort report + retention admin
entries.plain: Persona now includes an organization-wide cohort report showing skill distributions and a data retention tool for purging old assessments after 24 months.
entries.explanation:
- Org-scoped cohort report at /ac/persona/cohort (overall self-rating +
  band distribution across the 38 competencies, per-run report links,
  client filter; standalone candidate_id-null runs only).
- Retention admin at /ac/persona/retention (24-month purge of standalone
  behavioral_assessment_sessions, typed confirmation, admin-gated;
  candidate-bound readiness sessions untouched).
- Cohort + Retention nav links on the Persona runner.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona: standalone voucher model
entries.plain: Persona now has its own independent voucher system, so admins can issue assessment links directly and track redemptions the same way as other platforms.
entries.explanation:
Give the Persona behavioural self-assessment its own independent voucher
system (mirrors Fluent/Cognitive):
- persona_vouchers + redemptions + claim RPC (migration 00106);
  behavioral_assessment_sessions gains organization_id + voucher_redemption_id
- admin console /ac/persona/vouchers; public redeem /ac/persona/redeem ->
  token runner /ac/persona/take/[token]; startPersonaAction stamps the session
  with the client org on a voucher run (best-effort, non-voucher path unchanged)
- middleware bypass for the delegate paths. Tolerant; inert until 00106 applied.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Refresh PENDING-ACTIONS: record 2026-06-16 work + new open items
entries.plain: Internal tracking updated to reflect recently completed work and pending tasks.
entries.explanation:
- Date bumped to 2026-06-16.
- New section G logs this session's shipped work (auth-doc fix, Persona
  scoping + bands, 9th cluster 00100, Succession Readiness combined wiring
  00099, Reflect 5 open questions 00101, voucher hub).
- Added open items: create ahmad.rashid admin, rotate the demo credential,
  fix the pre-existing ARA voucher-page hydration warning.
- Marked EMAIL_FROM on Render as done (confirmed by the 06-14 external send).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Scope Persona to agreed competencies + add interpretation bands
entries.plain: Persona assessments now evaluate only the competencies your organization agreed to assess for each role, making results more focused and relevant to your hiring needs.
entries.explanation:
- Candidate-bound Persona now evaluates only the engagement's agreed
  competencies (engagement_competencies), falling back to the full 38 when
  none are selected. The master framework is unchanged; each engagement
  draws its agreed subset. Standalone /ac/persona stays full-framework.
- Add a shared 5-band interpretation guide (Exceptional -> Critical Gap,
  bilingual) on the candidate submit screen, the standalone result, and the
  Persona PDF.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add readiness + Persona PDFs and candidate self-results view
entries.plain: Candidates can now download their Persona results as a PDF, and readiness reports are also available as downloadable PDFs for easier sharing and record-keeping.
entries.explanation:
Tier 2 deliverables from the walkthrough gap report:
- Succession Readiness report PDF (React-PDF) at
  /api/readiness/[engId]/[candId]/pdf + "Download PDF" on the report page.
- Persona self-profile PDF at /api/ac/persona/[sessionId]/report (works for
  anonymous and candidate-bound sessions) + "Download PDF" on the standalone
  result.
- Candidate self-results: the behavioral runner's submit screen now shows the
  candidate's own self-profile (per-cluster bars) + a PDF download, instead of
  just a thank-you.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add standalone Persona run + Cognitive candidate picker
entries.plain: Anyone can now take a quick Persona assessment with just their name, without needing to be linked to a specific job opening or role.
entries.explanation:
Make the two psychometric instruments symmetric - both get a name-only
quick run and an optional candidate binding:
- Persona: new /ac/persona standalone runner (anonymous, name only) that
  scores 152 self-ratings into a self-profile by competency cluster. Adds
  createAnonymousBehavioralSession + submitAnonymousBehavioral (returns
  the profile, no readiness rollup write). "Begin Persona assessment" CTA
  on the Psychometrics portal; candidate-bound path unchanged.
- Cognitive: optional engagement/candidate picker on the runner (blank =
  anonymous), so a result can bind to a candidate from the UI.
- Migration 00098 relaxes behavioral_assessment_sessions candidate/
  engagement to nullable + adds taker_name (idempotent).

services.label: AR Compass (ARC)
services.blurb: AI Readiness Compass - organisation + individual readiness.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC audit fixes (minors): weights, status lifecycle, audit rows, timer, regulatory
entries.plain: Fixed issues with AI Readiness assessments at the department or division level - weights can now be saved correctly and assessments can reach completion status.
entries.explanation:
- Pillar weight editor scopes read+validate+write to the IN-SCOPE pillars (4/6/8)
  instead of all 8, with the sum-to-100 rule + equal-split default over in-scope,
  so a Department/Division assessment's weights can actually be saved (WEIGHT-04).
- Org assessments gain Launch (draft->active) and Mark complete (active/frozen->
  completed) transitions - previously stuck at draft->frozen, so they never
  reached an end state (also why the year-on-year prior scan never saw them) -
  STATUS-08/09.
- Audit rows written on org hard-delete + sandbox purge (ORG-DELETE-02) to honour
  the immutable-log compliance requirement.
- Respondent time limit is now enforced server-side (with a grace buffer) in
  saveAraAnswer, not just by the client countdown (TIMER-05).
- Regulatory documents get Approve/Reject controls so they can leave 'review' -
  the lifecycle was rendered but unreachable (DEFER-01).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC audit fixes (majors): report scope, scoring, lifecycle, honest copy
entries.plain: Department and division AI Readiness reports now display only the relevant pillars instead of showing incomplete data, and practice-run disclaimers are now accurate.
entries.explanation:
- Org PDF (portrait + bilingual + radar) now shows ONLY in-scope pillars on a
  subset-stage run instead of zero-filling all 8 - a Department/Division org no
  longer reads as half-assessed in the client deliverable. Radar axis labels
  also localise to Arabic (PDF-17).
- Redeem 'practice run for development' disclaimer is now conditional on the
  voucher's is_practice flag; real (admin-issued) vouchers show accurate copy.
- Distortion anchor-deviation is leave-one-out (a faker's own extremes no longer
  dilute the population anchor on small ARC cohorts) - DIST-04.
- Reassessment carries pillars_in_scope + agentic/individual layers + tier so
  year-2 measures the same scope as year-1 - REASSESS-09 / AGN-REASSESS-01.
- Mode B deep-dive binds consultant_id to the issuer so they can open their own
  assessment under RLS - DEEPDIVE-03.
- ARA Graph email maps attachments to fileAttachment blocks (the results PDF was
  silently dropped) - EMAIL-17.
- Offline banner tells the honest truth (no local persistence; pause till online)
  instead of promising a sync that never happens - OFFLINE-02.
- Admin question list gains an activate/deactivate control + Inactive-draft badge
  so AI-authored questions can be made live - QCRUD-08.
- Year-on-year scopes the prior scan to the same major bank version at the query
  layer instead of truncating to 10 rows first - YOY-07.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Move ARC voucher add-client next to the client picker
entries.plain: The option to add a new client when issuing AI Readiness codes is now positioned next to the client selection field for easier access.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Capture ARC client contact in step 1, prefill step-3 email
entries.plain: When setting up AI Readiness assessments, you can now enter the client contact person's details upfront, and their email automatically populates when sending results.
entries.explanation: Step 1 adds client contact name/title/email; step 3 email-to-client is prefilled with the contact so the batch can go straight to them.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Rebuild ARC vouchers as a guided 3-step issue wizard
entries.plain: Issuing AI Readiness assessment codes now follows a simple 3-step wizard: select client and options, choose who receives codes, then generate and email them.
entries.explanation: Step 1 client + engagement label + ARC options; step 2 to-client vs to-delegates; step 3a generate batch then email/copy to client; step 3b upload/paste delegate emails then email each a link.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add email-codes-to-client option + collapsible email sections
entries.plain: All assessment code distributions now support sending one email with all codes to a client contact, and email sections can be collapsed on smaller screens.
entries.explanation: Shared VoucherClientEmailCard (one email, whole batch) on Fluent/Logica/Persona/ARC/Techno; delegate emailers now collapsible; mobile-responsive just-generated headers.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Unify ARC + Pre-Hire voucher forms: identity row + options callout
entries.plain: AI Readiness and Pre-Hire assessment code forms now use a consistent layout and design for better user experience.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC audit Batch 2/3: bilingual redeem, calibration-withheld note, rating anchor
entries.plain: The AI Readiness assessment redemption page now supports Arabic language, and shows clear notes when calibration checks cannot be completed due to insufficient responses.
entries.explanation:
- Redeem form is now bilingual (EN/AR toggle + RTL + Arabic labels), matching the
  rest of ARC; dropped the unused PII-prefill props.
- personal-analysis: when too few objective items (<4) were answered, surface a
  "calibration check withheld" note instead of silently omitting it, so the
  verdict isn't read as if a self-vs-objective alignment check had passed.
- Respondent rating anchor now reads "1 = Strongly disagree · 5 = Strongly agree"
  (was "1 = Low · 5 = High"), matching the intro's stated agreement scale (EN+AR,
  Arabic-Indic digits preserved for correct RTL BiDi).
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC audit Batch 1: voucher hardening + respondent integrity (migration 00158)
entries.plain: Fixed security vulnerabilities in AI Readiness code handling so that failed assessments don't waste seats and assessment codes cannot be manipulated through the URL.
entries.explanation:
- migration 00158: ara_voucher_release_seat (atomic hand-back) + lock EXECUTE on
  ara_voucher_claim/release to service_role only (was anon-callable via PostgREST)
  + pinned search_path + items_per_factor bounds CHECK (was client-side only).
- redeemVoucher: release the claimed seat on every post-claim provisioning
  failure (org/bank/assessment/respondent) so a failed redeem can't burn a seat.
- Redeem page: only the voucher code is honoured from the URL; name/email are no
  longer pre-fillable from query params (phishing vector); company stays
  server-derived from the voucher's client tag.
- markAraRespondentComplete: idempotency guard (re-submit doesn't re-stamp/
  re-score/re-fire credential+client-email) + completion-eligibility gate (a
  zero-answer respondent can't be marked complete).
migration 00158 applied + verified live. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Portal: ARC individual-readiness voucher track (individual + shared link)
entries.plain: The client portal now offers AI Readiness assessments as individual vouchers for personal readiness measurement, just like other assessment services.
entries.explanation:
ARC also measures an individual (personal AI readiness), so the portal's ARC
panel now offers it as a voucher exactly like the other code-based services:
individual single-use codes (one per recipient) or one shared link with N
seats - both drawing from the same ARC seat allocation. Reuses the existing
ARC voucher engine (createVoucherBatch -> ara_vouchers; redeem at /ara/redeem
mints an individual-stage run). New issueArcVouchers() lib fn +
clientIssueArcVouchersAction + ArcIndividualVoucher panel rendered above the
cohort invite. Department / Division / Organization stay on the invite flow.
Real (non-practice) runs; tier from service_config.assessment_tier (default
snapshot); release-on-failure seat accounting.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal: seat-service report guards + ARA org resolver
entries.plain: Added security checks to ensure clients can only access AI Readiness and Reflect results for their own organization's assessments.
entries.explanation:
- getClientAraOrgId(): resolve a client_manager's ara_organizations.id
  from their allocation (ARC/Reflect live in the ARA org store)
- requireAssessmentOwner (ARC), guardReflectEngagementAccess +
  canAccessReflect* (Reflect), prehire candidate report route: each gains
  an additive client_manager org-match branch; admin/consultant unchanged

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Guided demo: one shared rail per service, across all 10 services
entries.plain: Every assessment service now has its own guided demo walkthrough to help business users understand and explore the platform features.
entries.explanation:
