Generalise the ARC guided-demo into a single root-mounted, route-aware system so
every service has its own BD walkthrough (the user's pitch tool). A route->track
registry maps each service to its own label, brand accent and route-verified
roadmap; the root <GuidedDemo /> resolves the track for the current path, offers
a "Guided demo" launcher pill when idle on a service route, and runs that
service's rail (deep-linking the real screens, persisting across navigation)
when active. One component, ten tracks, no per-layout wiring or collisions.

Tracks: Assessment Center, Pre-Hire, Fluent, Techno, Logica, Persona (Talent
Acquisition) + AI Readiness, Reflect 360, Succession Readiness, VIFM Academy
(Talent Development). All step hrefs verified against real static pages (no
dynamic/token routes); end-user/anonymous flows excluded from the launcher.

ARC migrated onto the shared system (its bespoke rail/toggle/state files removed).

Verified on preview: /ara, /courses and /admin/prehire each show the correct
launcher + rail (label, accent, steps), stepping works, and the platform landing
stays clean. tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC guided-demo rail (BD trial): flip switch + step-by-step roadmap
entries.plain: AR Compass now includes a guided demo mode that walks consultants through the assessment step-by-step with a flip switch, making it easier to pitch to clients.
entries.explanation:
A presenter-driven guided walkthrough overlaying the REAL /ara portal for BD
pitches. A "Start guided demo" flip switch on the consultant dashboard turns it
on; a fixed bottom rail then walks the 5-step ARC roadmap (what it measures ->
engagement tiers -> set up an assessment -> take a free Personal Snapshot live ->
results + board-ready report), deep-linking into each real screen with Next/Back
and persisting across navigation.

- arc-demo-steps.ts (the roadmap) + arc-demo-state.ts (localStorage + same-tab
  CustomEvent so the switch, rail and Exit stay in sync).
- guided-demo-rail.tsx in the /ara layout; off by default (activates via the
  switch or ?demo=1), so real visitors never see it. Non-destructive - it only
  navigates and narrates.
- guided-demo-toggle.tsx flip switch on the consultant dashboard.

Verified on preview: switch -> rail (Step 1), Next advances + deep-links to
/ara/engage with the rail persisting. tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Audit cleanup: remaining minors + the Techno legacy-path gap
entries.plain: Draft reports are now blocked from being viewed by candidates and clients until officially released, and reports show a warning if they lack enough feedback from observers.
entries.explanation:
- AC report release gate: guardCandidateReportAccess now requires a RELEASED
  candidate_reports row for candidate + client callers (admins still preview any
  status), so a self-candidate or own-org client can't pull a draft report
  straight from /api/reports or the learning-plan route.
- AC report data-quality caveat: ReportData carries hasAssessorData + raterCount;
  both PDF renderers (EN React-PDF + AR HTML) show an amber caveat when the
  report rests on no observation data or a single assessor, so a thin/low-
  confidence report never reads as complete.
- ARC agentic tolerance: the agentic question query logs a clear warning (rather
  than failing silently) when migration 00041's column is absent on a fresh env;
  CLAUDE.md notes the migration list runs through 00144 (authoritative set under
  supabase/migrations/).
- Techno legacy mix-path: the mix-and-match start now catches TechGenerationError
  -> 503 and guards the placeholder deck in production, matching the function +
  domain paths (which already degraded gracefully).

Verified no change needed: Techno cut-scores (getCutScore returns correct
defaults; sandbox doesn't read the table) and custom-sitting non-certification
(intentional - a hand-picked subset is indicative, not a certified whole).

tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC TA: "Review a candidate" list on the selection landing
entries.plain: Talent acquisition staff can now review candidate results directly from AR Compass without navigating to the consultant dashboard.
entries.explanation:
Closes the TA loop: after a candidate completes, staff can come back to ARC TA
and open their results in one click, instead of routing through the consultant
dashboard. Adds a staff-only "Review a candidate" section listing the
acquisition-lensed personal deep-dives (engagement_stage='individual' +
talent_lens='acquisition') with name, issued date, status, and a "View results"
link to /ara/personal/results/[token] for completed ones, plus a "view all in
dashboard" link.

STAFF-GATED: /ara is a public, auth-bypassed route, so the component returns
null unless isStaffCaller() - a public visitor never sees candidate names or
result links (the results page is independently staff-gated too).

Verified: list renders 7 acquisition candidates, 4 completed with View-results
links. tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC robustness: bank-version fallback, pillar guard, PDF 503
entries.plain: AR Compass now handles legacy assessments correctly and shows clear warnings if assessment data is missing or incomplete.
entries.explanation:
Three majors from the service audit:
- Null question-bank fallback: loadQuestionsForRespondent now resolves the
  active bank version on demand when the assessment's question_bank_version_id
  is null (legacy rows), instead of silently serving zero questions.
  (createReassessmentFromPrior already pins the active version, so reassessments
  were fine; this covers legacy/other null rows.)
- Pillar-assignment validation: the loader logs a clear warning when pillar
  questions are wanted but zero in-scope pillars resolve (misconfig), and the
  respondent page now shows a "No questions available yet - contact your
  administrator" card instead of a blank form when nothing loads.
- Puppeteer PDF 503: both ARA PDF routes (org report + personal snapshot AR
  path) isolate the Chromium launch and return 503 with a retry message on a
  launch failure, rather than a bare 500 - so a renderer/infra hiccup reads as
  "try again", not "your assessment is broken".

tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: add the entry-points hub to the Talent Acquisition landing
entries.plain: The talent acquisition landing page now includes quick-access cards for the consultant dashboard, admin console, and complimentary snapshot.
entries.explanation:
The TA lens (/ara?lens=acquisition) rendered SelectionLanding, a focused
"assess a candidate" page that omitted the Consultant / VIFM Admin / Personal
snapshot action cards the TD (development) landing shows - so from TA you could
not reach the consultant dashboard, admin console, or the complimentary
snapshot. Add the same three entry cards to the TA landing (snapshot
acquisition-lensed), keeping its candidate-issue hero. TA now has parity with TD.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: store a real overall for individual-stage assessments (was null)
entries.plain: Individual-stage assessments now correctly display an overall score on staff dashboards instead of appearing blank.
entries.explanation:
recalculateAssessmentScores excludes individual_factor_id items from pillar
scoring (correct for the 8 pillars). But individual-stage assessments serve ONLY
factor items, so no pillar ever scored, anyPillarScored stayed false, and
overall_score was written null - leaving the consultant dashboard + org views
blank even though the taker's results page recomputed a real score from the same
responses (the Reflect-class "stored vs on-the-fly" split the audit flagged).

Fix: when no pillar scored but scored individual-factor responses exist, derive
the overall as the mean of the four factor means (the exact logic the personal
results page uses) and label it with the individual maturity stage. Gated on
no-pillar-scored, so Mode C (pillar + individual layer) is unaffected - its
pillars score, so the new branch never runs.

Verified (read-only) across the live individual-stage assessments: all currently
store null; the fix computes sensible 1-5 overalls (e.g. 3.06 Practising,
2.86 Emerging). tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC results: staff-only presentation toggle for both report lenses
entries.plain: Staff can now toggle between acquisition and development report formats on the results page to show clients the difference.
entries.explanation:
BD can now flip the ARC results page between the Talent Acquisition and Talent
Development framing of the SAME sitting (?present=acquisition|development) to
show a client the difference. Staff-only by construction: the taker never
reaches this render (they get the thank-you page), so the toggle is inherently
gated. The override never changes the stored lens - real sittings stay
single-lens (chosen at creation) and the candidate's own report is unchanged.
A "previewing the other framing" note shows when the displayed lens differs from
the sitting's actual lens. Verified: acquisition view = advisory band + no
courses; development view = priorities + course recs; toggle flips correctly.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Methodology briefs: downloadable PDF for every service + hub
entries.plain: All assessment services now have downloadable methodology briefs available in one central location.
entries.explanation:
Extends the ARC/Persona methodology-brief pattern to all 10 services. Adds the
3 missing briefs (Mentium, Pre-Hire, Academy - code-grounded, ~1700 words each)
so every service now has a brief doc, and a single generic route
/api/methodology/[service]/pdf that renders any service's docs/ markdown to a
branded PDF (current-brand eyebrow) via the existing methodologyBriefHtml. A
METHODOLOGY_BRIEFS registry is the single source of truth, surfaced as a
"Methodology briefs" section on the /evidence Research & validity hub (replaces
the dead "PDF export coming soon" stub).

Also strips em dashes from the 6 older briefs (AC/ARA/Fluent/Reflect/Succession/
Technical - 63 total) per SOP. Verified: all 10 PDFs render 200 (128-203KB),
hub lists all services, tsc + ESLint clean.

The legacy /api/ara/methodology/pdf and /api/ac/persona/methodology/pdf routes
are kept so existing report-page links keep working.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: overall score excludes unanswered factors (no longer drags the mean)
entries.plain: Overall scores now fairly represent completed sections by excluding unanswered questions from the calculation.
entries.explanation:
The personal overall score averaged all four factors; a factor with zero
answered items scored 0 and silently pulled the overall (and the maturity
stage) down. Average only the factors that have answers, on both the on-screen
results page and the PDF route. For a complete run this is unchanged (all four
answered); it only fixes partial / capped runs.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC vouchers: per-client question-count lever (length per voucher)
entries.plain: Vouchers now offer a length selector so clients can choose shorter assessments (24, 36, 48, or 60 questions) suited to their needs.
entries.explanation:
Adds an "Assessment length" selector on the vouchers page so a voucher can issue
the Personal ARC at a client-appropriate length: Full (60) / 48 / 36 / 24, i.e.
a cap on individual-layer questions per factor. The cap is stored on the voucher
(items_per_factor), copied to the provisioned assessment at redeem, and honoured
by the respondent loader - which keeps the objective (scenario/knowledge) items
first so a shorter run still supports the calibration / risk-flag reads.

- migration 00143: items_per_factor on ara_vouchers + ara_assessments (nullable).
- Tolerant: NULL/full length needs no migration; capped lengths require 00143.
- Threaded through createVoucherBatch, redeemVoucher, both voucher actions, types.

NOTE: apply migration 00143 for capped lengths to take effect (full works now).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC reports: prominent Talent Acquisition / Development badge at the top
entries.plain: Reports now display a clear colour-coded badge at the top showing whether it is an acquisition or development assessment.
entries.explanation:
The lens was shown only as a small eyebrow. Make it an unmistakable, colour-
coded badge at the very top of the report (acquisition = blue, development =
green) on all three surfaces: the on-screen results page, the EN React-PDF
hero, and the AR Puppeteer hero. Verified the on-screen badge renders and the
PDF still generates.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC vouchers: drop Practice snapshot; admin "Randomize answers" on any run
entries.plain: Vouchers now always issue the full 60-question assessment, and admins can demo any assessment by randomizing answers without needing a practice flag.
entries.explanation:
Per request - vouchers no longer offer the Practice snapshot; every code now
provisions the full 60-question Personal ARC (deep_dive, a real run). Removes
the Assessment-scope toggle + the scope plumbing from both voucher actions.

The staff demo shortcut is no longer gated on the sandbox flag: a signed-in
admin sees "Randomize answers" on ANY run (so a real Full-ARC voucher run can be
demoed), while the candidate - who has no session - still never sees it. The
action drops the sandbox gate (keeps the staff check), randomizes answers
(self-ratings 3-5, graded items a random option) instead of a fixed fill, and
the button now confirms before overwriting since it can touch real runs.

Verified: redeemed a Full-ARC code -> real 60-question run -> admin randomize
-> varied report (3.3-4.3 across factors). Vouchers page shows no scope toggle.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC reports: two genuinely different reports for acquisition vs development
entries.plain: Acquisition reports now show hiring advice and risk flags, while development reports show growth recommendations and learning paths.
entries.explanation:
The development lens was the generic snapshot with relabelled headers. Now each
lens produces a purpose-built report from the same answers:

Talent Acquisition (decision report): lead with an Advance / Review / Hold
advisory band + "human decides" guardrail; AI-specific risk flags (over-reliance
= high Working Practice + low Sense-Check, over-claiming, responsible-use);
verdict, calibration, interview probes, freshness stamp. No courses/coaching.

Talent Development (growth report, new buildDevelopmentAnalysis): non-uses
framing ("not a scorecard, not used for pay/promotion/hiring"), strengths-first,
constructive self-vs-objective read, 2-3 foundation-first sequenced priorities
each with why-now + a 70-20-10 first step + the AC competencies it builds,
reflect-&-commit (SMART), a manager GROW guide, and a re-measure cadence - plus
the existing course recommender. No verdict/cut-score/risk language (Feedback
Intervention Theory). Generic/null runs default to the development experience.

On-screen surface done + verified for both lenses (EN/AR). PDF surfaces next.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Docs: ARC report TA-vs-TD differentiation + enhancement plan
entries.plain: Documentation now explains how acquisition and development reports differ and outlines planned enhancements.
entries.explanation:
Deep-research output (6-agent workflow): how the ARC personal report should
differ for talent acquisition vs talent development, with a file-level,
prioritized backlog (quick wins on data we already compute vs bigger builds).
Also flags a real bug: the overall score is an unweighted mean where an
unanswered factor contributes 0, dragging the score down.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARA vouchers: let codes issue the full 60-question Personal ARC
entries.plain: Voucher codes now default to issuing the full 60-question assessment instead of the shorter practice version.
entries.explanation:
Vouchers were hardcoded to tier "snapshot" + practice, so a redeemed code
provisioned a 36-question sandbox run - while the selection landing issues
the full 60-question deep-dive. Both should give the complete ARC.

- Add an "Assessment scope" toggle on the vouchers page: Full ARC (60, real
  run) vs Practice snapshot (36, sandbox). Defaults to Full ARC.
- Thread scope through createVoucherBatchAction + emailVouchersToDelegatesAction
  (was hardcoded snapshot/isPractice:true).
- Bulk + seat-pool + email-to-list already supported, so one code per person
  or a single shared code the client distributes both now issue the full ARC.
- Fix stale 24/48 item counts in the respondent loader doc (actual 36/60).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Security: gate the demo "Simulate answers" shortcut to staff only
entries.plain: Demo answer shortcuts now only work for staff, preventing candidates from skipping the AI Readiness assessment.
entries.explanation:
The Simulate-answers banner was gated only on the assessment's sandbox flag,
so a candidate opening a demo token URL could see (and use) it to skip the
exam. It must only appear for the admin demoing to a client.

- Respond page now computes canSimulate = is_sandbox AND isStaffCaller(); the
  candidate (no session) never sees the banner
- Rename the form prop isSandbox -> canSimulate to make the intent explicit
- simulateAraAnswers server action now also requires staff (defence in depth)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Landing: animated stats, depth glyphs, scroll-reveal
entries.plain: The landing page now shows animated statistics and service cards reveal beautifully as you scroll.
entries.explanation:
- Hero gains an animated stat strip (10 services / 41 competencies / 100+
  programmes / 2 languages) and a "Two solution families" eyebrow
- Each service card renders its faint tone glyph (depth) and reveals on
  scroll with a stagger; CTAs bottom-align for even card heights
- Fix CountUp: reset the run-once guard on cleanup so a React StrictMode
  remount re-schedules the animation instead of sticking at 0

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARA: Personal-only selection lens with results analysis
entries.plain: You can now issue AI Readiness assessments for hiring purposes with results analysis tailored to selection decisions.
entries.explanation:
- /ara?lens=acquisition renders a Personal-only selection landing; the
  engage funnel redirects the acquisition lens there (no org tiers in the
  hiring path)
- Reuse the 60-item Personal deep-dive: issue to a candidate, keep the link
  private (optional email), or run it live; demo mode + simulate-answers
- Respondent flow: resume into saved progress, "Save & finish later", and a
  lens-aware back link to the ARC landing
- Deterministic candidate results analysis (verdict, self-vs-objective
  calibration, strengths, dev areas + probes, profile shape) on the results
  page and the EN/AR snapshot PDF
- Public ARA marketing routes (/ara, /ara/engage, /ara/roadmap) bypass auth
- Sidebar: add "Compare portals" under Platform

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire: design a role from a JD (reuse AC extractor) + drop region/sector jargon
entries.plain: Recruiters can now design Pre-Hire screening roles by pasting a job description instead of manually selecting from templates.
entries.explanation:
B2: the requisition form gains a "Design from a job description" panel beside the
role-profile picker. Paste a JD or upload a PDF/TXT and the SAME extractor the AC
engagement wizard uses (extractCompetenciesFromJd[File]Action ->
jd-competency-extractor) maps it to the behavioural 38 and pre-fills the quiz
competency set (turning the quiz stage on). So a recruiter can design the role
inline instead of only picking an existing role profile.

B1: removed the "(AI Readiness / Reflect)" parenthetical from the Region/Sector
labels in the shared Add Client dialog - internal jargon that read as noise when
adding a client for Pre-Hire. The fields stay (the ARA/Reflect org row needs
region/sector).

Verified live: requisition form renders the panel (paste + upload + extract);
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC personal report: AI-readiness wording, assessment type, legends under the score
entries.plain: AI Readiness reports now clearly show whether the assessment was for hiring or development, with improved wording and score legends.
entries.explanation:
Per client review of the individual AI Readiness report (PDF EN + AR + web
results page):
- A1: per-factor section header now reads "Where you stand on each AI readiness
  factor" (was "...VIFM factor").
- A3: the hero shows the assessment type beneath the score - Talent Acquisition /
  Talent Development (from the talent lens), or "General" when none.
- A2 + A4: both legends now sit directly under the overall score - the overall
  maturity-stage legend (Emerging/Practising/Embedded with ranges) AND the
  per-factor band legend (Opportunity/Developing/Strong). The band legend was
  previously buried on page 2 of the PDF; the stage legend was only a one-line
  current-stage hint.

Verified live: web results page renders all four; EN PDF 200/valid; AR HTML
renders for acquisition/development/null with the correct Arabic strings.
tsc clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC hiring: thread talent lens to the personal snapshot so the develop-courses panel is suppressed for selection
entries.plain: Hiring assessments now correctly hide development course recommendations from candidates' results.
entries.explanation:
The personal results page + snapshot PDF already hide the "Develop with VIFM
programmes" block under the acquisition (hiring) lens, but the lens was being
dropped: /ara and /ara/engage linked to /ara/personal/start WITHOUT forwarding
?lens=, so a "For Selection" entry produced a lens-less snapshot and the gate
never fired. Both pages now read searchParams.lens and forward it to the
snapshot (and engage) links. A hiring entry now yields talent_lens=acquisition,
which removes the development course panel; development/generic snapshots are
unchanged.

Verified: /ara?lens=acquisition -> /ara/personal/start?lens=acquisition;
development likewise; no-lens stays plain. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Landing: one box per service with a For selection / For development selector
entries.plain: Services used for both hiring and development now appear once with a selector, reducing duplicate boxes on the homepage.
entries.explanation:
Dual-purpose services (Assessment Center, AI Readiness, Technical, Cognitive,
Persona, Fluent) used to render TWICE - once in the Talent Acquisition column
and once in Talent Management - with the off-side purpose dimmed. They now
render ONCE as a single box with an interactive "For selection / For
development" segmented selector beneath; picking one updates the card's copy,
accent colour (blue=selection, emerald=development) and destination
(persona purpose / ARC + Technical lens param). Single-purpose services
(Pre-Hire, Reflect 360, Succession Readiness, VIFM Academy) show their one
purpose as a static label. Replaces the two-column duplicate layout with one
de-duplicated responsive grid. Verified: 10 unique cards, toggle re-renders
copy+tone, tsc + lint clean, no console errors.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC audit: GOV-05 retention 2y, NOTES-14 general notes, AUTHZ-04 docstring+guard
entries.plain: Data retention is now set to 2 years maximum to comply with government regulations.
entries.explanation:
SDAIA-relevant ARC audit findings (Saudi-government demo):

GOV-05 (PDPL): data retention dropped 3y -> 2y to match CLAUDE.md's documented
2-year max. New shared constant ARA_RETENTION_YEARS (src/lib/constants/
ara-retention.ts) consumed by the purge logic + the admin retention page, so
the value can't drift. (New client has no 2-3yr-old archived data, so the
tighter threshold is safe.)

NOTES-14: general (pillar_id NULL) include-in-report consultant notes were
collected under "_general" but never rendered in the bilingual report (only
per-pillar notes were) - they now render as an "Overall observations" bilingual
findings section, matching the single-language report.

AUTHZ-04: loadRespondentByToken docstring claimed it refused frozen/archived
but didn't (and must not - it also feeds the results/PDF view for completed
delegates). Corrected the docstring to match behaviour; added a defense-in-depth
frozen/archived guard to markAraRespondentComplete (saveAraAnswer already had
one), so a frozen run is locked at both write paths.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add ARC per-company voucher cohort insights
entries.plain: You can now see AI-readiness insights grouped by company for all employees who used a voucher code.
entries.explanation:
Aggregate AI-readiness across every delegate who redeemed a voucher
under the same company (grouped by the delegate-typed company_name,
case/whitespace-normalized).

- New lib src/lib/ara/company-cohort.ts: listCompanyCohorts() +
  computeCompanyCohortInsight(). Scores each delegate's four VIFM
  individual factors from ara_responses against the cross-version
  question map (delegates span many assessments/bank versions, so it
  cannot reuse the single-assessment workforce rollup), then rolls up
  cohort means, below-target counts, a maturity-band spread, and a
  population percentile vs the global snapshot norm pool. Tolerant of
  purged runs, missing factor seed, and un-applied tables.
- New admin page /ara/admin/vouchers/insights: company picker +
  per-company detail (completion funnel, cohort readiness with honest
  norms-accruing state, four factor cards, maturity spread, per-delegate
  table, CSV export, and gap-driven recommended VIFM training).
- Entry points on /ara/admin/vouchers: per-company "View" link in the
  Redemptions-by-company table + a "Cohort insights" header button.

Read-only, admin-gated (inherits the /ara/admin layout gate), no schema
change. Verified end-to-end against live data.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix five ARC delegate/voucher flow bugs
entries.plain: Five critical issues fixed: stray submit buttons, result access leaks, page layout breaks, expired voucher rejection, and legends on blank pages.
entries.explanation:
- #1 stray submit: gate Submit + optional sections behind QuestionsForm
  `started` so they no longer render on the pre-start intro screen
- #2 results leak: extract delegateCanSeeOwnResults; apply on results
  page + personal PDF route to match the completion-email R1 gate
- #3 legend orphan: move "How to read these scores" to the top of
  page 2 (EN + AR) so it never lands on its own blank page
- #4 voucher expiry: treat a date-only picker value as end-of-day UTC
  so a code is not rejected for the whole day it was picked for
- #5 department test empty: default pillar assignments on bulk-import
  and add a defensive in-scope-pillar fallback in the respondent loader

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: department/org respondents get the assessment's pillars by default (bug fix)
entries.plain: Team assessments now include the correct questions by default instead of requiring manual setup.
entries.explanation:
A respondent added to an org (department/division/enterprise) assessment
only received pillar questions if the consultant manually ticked pillar
checkboxes, which defaulted to UNCHECKED - so a respondent added without
ticking got zero questions: an empty 'department test not working'. Fix:
(1) the add-respondent pillar checkboxes are now pre-checked (defaultChecked);
(2) createAraRespondent defaults the pillar set to the assessment's resolved
in-scope pillars (getPillarsForAssessment - department=4 / division=6 /
enterprise=8, honouring a pillars_in_scope override) when the form passes
none, so a respondent can never be created with an empty pillar set.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC personal report: purpose-aware talent lens (R4-R7)
entries.plain: AI Readiness reports now adapt their content based on whether the assessment is for hiring or development.
entries.explanation:
Capture acquisition vs development lens from the launching pillar
(/ara?lens=) and persist on ara_assessments.talent_lens (00134).

R4 header label, R5 hide course recs under acquisition, R6 descriptive
(not coaching) narrative under acquisition, R7 drop 'Maps to VIFM AC'
lines under acquisition. NULL lens reproduces prior output exactly.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC R1: suppress delegate results when a client contact is configured
entries.plain: When a client contact is assigned, results go to the client instead of the candidate.
entries.explanation:
On respondent completion, the delegate's own deliverables (AI Readiness
credential + personal results email) now fire only for a SELF-SERVE run:
gated on respondentCanView AND no client contact email on the org. Once a
client contact is set (a delegated/client engagement), the delegate does
NOT receive their own results - the CLIENT receives them, either directly
(send_results_to_client per-completion email) or collected via the org
"Collect all results & email client" action (R10). Mode A anonymous
snapshots (no org -> clientEmail null) keep their results unchanged, as
does an explicit respondent_can_view_results=false. No migration (reuses
the 00108 results-visibility columns). tsc + next lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC admin-org: finish edit-client, delegates-completed count, collect-and-email (R8/R9/R10)
entries.plain: Admins can now edit client contact details and collect all team results to email to the client with a completion count.
entries.explanation:
R8: the org edit form already existed; close the gaps - add the 00108
results-delivery fields + a new client_contact_name to the AraOrganization
type, validate client_contact_email/name in createAraOrganizationSchema,
add the contact-name input to the edit form, and persist it via the
tolerant best-effort update. Migration 00131 adds ara_organizations
.client_contact_name (IF NOT EXISTS).

R9: "Delegates completed" N/M metric on the org list (batched, no N+1 -
one assessments query + one respondents query aggregated in JS) and on the
org detail.

R10: collectAndSendOrgResultsAction(orgId) - requireOrgAccess-gated; finds
completed delegates on the org's individual-stage / include_individual_layer
assessments; builds each personal results PDF via the token PDF route with
the server-only x-ara-internal header; sends ONE consolidated email to the
org client contact (ara_personal_results_to_client) with all PDFs attached.
New "Collect all results & email client" button on the org detail.

Subagent-built from the investigation map; reviewed the gated action + org
scoping; tsc + next lint clean. Apply migration 00131. (Graph email path
does not forward attachments - Resend does; pre-existing, unchanged.)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC snapshot: band definitions (R2) + move legend below factors (R3)
entries.plain: Score bands now include plain-English definitions and the legend moved below the scores for clarity.
entries.explanation:
R2: add definition_en/_ar to the three maturity stages (Emerging =
"building foundational habits", Practising = "applying AI with growing
judgment", Embedded = "AI is a confident, routine part of how you work")
in ara-individual-factors.ts, and render it beneath the band word on the
results page + EN PDF + AR PDF (single source of truth).

R3: move the "How to read these scores" legend (Opportunity / Developing
/ Strong) from above the per-factor breakdown to below it, in both the EN
React-PDF and the AR Puppeteer-HTML renderers (it does not exist on the
on-screen page).

tsc + next lint clean. No migration; pure rendering/content.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add registered mark (R) to all service report names
entries.plain: All VIFM Caliber reports now display the registered trademark symbol next to service names like 'AI Readiness Compass(R)' and 'Fluent(R)' to protect our brand.
entries.explanation:
Branding pass: the service/instrument name now carries the registered
mark on each report's title/banner/cover/footer/certificate-name, EN + AR.
Title surfaces only - not incidental body text, comments, or identifiers.

- AC: Assessment Center(R) (candidate report + learning plan, EN + AR)
- AI Readiness Compass(R) (personal snapshot EN/AR, fact sheet, methodology
  brief, ARA org report + i18n)
- Fluent(R) (certificate PDF + printable HTML route)
- Reflect 360(R) (participant + cohort + framework reports)
- Pre-Hire(R) (screening report)
- VIFM Psychometrics(R), Technical Assessment(R) (sandbox + cohort), Manager
  Development Report(R)

Persona was already marked. Naming convention respected (no "ARA Compass").

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: docs: backlog - SDAIA pilots (Priority 1) + ARC audit findings (section H)
entries.plain: Documentation updated to track upcoming Saudi Arabia AI initiatives and audit findings for the AI Readiness Compass.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: fix three audit FAILs (band-gap, weight deflation, agentic tier)
entries.plain: Fixed three scoring errors in AI Readiness Compass: scores now map to correct maturity bands, department and division results are no longer underreported, and agentic tier classifications are accurate.
entries.explanation:
FAIL-2 band-gap: maturity/overall band lookups now use a lower-threshold
rule (highest band whose min <= score) so a score in a former 0.1 gap (e.g.
3.95) resolves to its correct band instead of the lowest. Gauge label + arc
segments matched. Verified numerically across [1.0,5.0].

FAIL-3 deflation: scoring.ts renormalizes pillar weights over the SCORED
pillars so Department(4)/Division(6) overalls are true weighted means, not
deflated to ~50-75%. Enterprise unchanged. getPillarsForAssessment validator
no longer a no-op (validates against the canonical pillar ids).

FAIL-1 agentic tier: migration 00116 flips agentic items to layer=1 so they
are respondent-answerable; saveAraAnswer bypasses pillar-assignment for
agentic + individual items; the respondent form renders agentic dimension
sections; the Phase 2 guide query excludes agentic items.

Contamination guard (DERIVE-04): individual + agentic items reuse a storage
pillar_id, so exclude them from every pillar-grouped aggregator -
scoring.ts (pre-existing), compliance.ts, distortion.ts, and detectors.ts
(gaps + shadow-AI) - so enabling agentic saves can't pollute pillar means.

Plus report/detail transparency notes for the renormalized weights.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: harden regulatory compliance (sector binding, recalc, pillar scope)
entries.plain: AI Readiness Compass now correctly locks each assessment to its organization and industry sector so regulatory frameworks like Saudi banking requirements cannot be bypassed.
entries.explanation:
Four fixes surfaced by the SAMA CSF verification:
- A: assessment region/sector are now bound to the owning org - stamped from
  the org at creation + reassessment, re-stamped onto answer-free draft
  assessments on org edit, and backfilled by migration 00115. Keeps the
  assessment row authoritative so a mis-picked sector can't silently hide a
  sector-scoped framework (e.g. SAMA for Saudi banking).
- B: compliance recalculates in the background on respondent completion, and
  framework cards show 'Not yet calculated' instead of a blank '-'/0 state.
- C: compliance requirements mapped to out-of-scope pillars are no longer
  persisted or counted (recalc + summary + detail list), so the requirement
  total reflects what was actually measured.
- D: the AI regulatory-doc extractor emits 'advisory' (not 'informational',
  which isn't in the ara_severity enum), with a clamp in admin-actions so an
  off-spec severity can't reject the whole batch.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: scope SAMA CSF requirement rows to banking sector
entries.plain: Saudi regulatory requirements in AI Readiness Compass are now properly scoped to apply only to banking organizations, preventing confusion in other sectors.
entries.explanation:
The original 00113 requirement INSERT omitted applies_to_sectors, so the 6
SAU_SAMA rows inherited the table default ["all"]. The framework-level gate
already scopes SAMA to banking (harmless today), but this breaks the 00008
convention of propagating each sector-restricted framework's sector onto its
requirement rows. Fix the seed for fresh installs + backfill 00114 for DBs
that already applied the original 00113.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC audit: clear six remaining findings
entries.plain: AI Readiness Compass now displays Arabic help text in guides, adds workforce and agentic readiness sections to reports, includes Saudi regulatory framework, and sends respondent invitations automatically.
entries.explanation:
- Phase 2 guide tab now renders Arabic help_text_ar (AR column)
- Bilingual PDF gains Workforce + Agentic readiness sections (EN/AR)
- Add 9th Saudi regulatory framework (SAMA CSF) - migration 00113
- Document the respondent-form back-affordance exemption
- Mode B deep-dive auto-emails the respondent invitation + status line

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Close ARA consultant + ARA/Reflect admin IDOR (same class as Reflect)
entries.plain: Fixed security vulnerability where consultants and administrators could view AI Readiness Compass assessments and reports without proper authorization.
entries.explanation:
Re-running the scorecard, the live non-admin probe showed ARA had the same
hole Reflect did: /ara/consultant (dashboard) and /ara/consultant/assessments
/[id] (+ /report) read via the service-role client with no role gate or owner
check, and the /ara/admin + /reflect/admin consoles had no role gate.

- /ara/consultant/layout.tsx (new): consultant/admin role gate for the whole
  ARA consultant area.
- /ara/admin/layout.tsx + /reflect/admin/layout.tsx (new): admin-only gates.
- ARA dashboard query now scoped to the owning consultant (admins see all).
- ARA assessment detail + report pages enforce ownership (consultant must own).
- ARA report PDF route forwards the requester's cookies to Puppeteer so the
  now-gated SSR report page still renders (mirrors the Reflect PDF fix).

Verified live: Candidate -> 404 on /ara/consultant, /ara/admin/*, /reflect/admin;
Admin -> 200 on all. tsc clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Harden secret comparisons (timing-safe) + correct i18n comment
entries.plain: Strengthened security for secret access codes used in background jobs to prevent timing-based attacks that could expose credentials.
entries.explanation:
Follow-up to the QA audit's lower-severity items:

- Constant-time secret comparison. Added timingSafeStrEqual (node:crypto)
  and applied it to all three shared-secret checks: the x-ara-internal
  server-to-server header (personal-snapshot PDF route) and the two cron
  bearer gates (ARA retention purge, Reflect rater reminders). Replaces plain
  === on secrets; the helper also returns false for an empty/unset secret, so
  an unset CRON_SECRET can never be matched by an empty header. (Secret
  ROTATION remains an ops task - rotate CRON_SECRET in Render + GitHub Actions
  secrets periodically.)
- i18n: corrected the stale provider comment that claimed admin/login/assessor
  /client are forced LTR. They are intentionally locale-aware now - verified
  live that /admin under an `ar` cookie renders a coherent, fully-translated
  RTL layout (sidebar correctly flush-right), not the old "drift to centre"
  bug. No behaviour change; comment now matches the code.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: recalc scores once at submit, not on every auto-save
entries.plain: AI Readiness Compass assessments now save and calculate results faster by recalculating scores once at submission instead of repeatedly during answering.
entries.explanation:
Diagnosed the remaining submit slowness: saveAraAnswer awaited a full
recalculateAssessmentScores() on EVERY auto-save (a broad responses
query + 9 sequential pillar/overall upserts, ~150ms each). The final
submit flush fires 3-4 saves back-to-back, serialising 3-4 recalcs
(~400-800ms) right when the user clicks Submit. Move the recalc to run
exactly once in markAraRespondentComplete after all answers are
persisted. Totals aren't shown while answering, so this is invisible to
the respondent and keeps both saves and submit fast. Consultant-side
recalcs (consultant-actions) are unchanged.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC reports: add an Assessment Fact Sheet (item 6)
entries.plain: AI Readiness Compass reports now include a one-page fact sheet explaining what the assessment measures, how to interpret scores, and what it does not cover.
entries.explanation:
A one-page, bilingual fact sheet now appears in the reports: instrument,
what it measures, item format, how to read the score/bands, honest
limits, and a pointer to the methodology PDF. Single source of truth in
src/lib/reports/fact-sheet-content.ts (EN/AR, personal + org variants),
rendered on the personal snapshot (EN React-PDF + AR Puppeteer HTML) and
the org report (bilingual two-column + portrait EN/AR). Replaces the
thin "how we built this" blurb on the personal snapshot.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: client-level results visibility + send-to-client (item 5)
entries.plain: Organizations can now control whether respondents see their own AI Readiness Compass results and choose to receive copies of all assessment results by email.
entries.explanation:
Adds three client-setup prefs on ara_organizations (migration 00108,
apply separately): respondent_can_view_results (default true),
client_contact_email, send_results_to_client.

- Client setup UI: a "Results delivery" section on the org edit page.
- Gating (shared tolerant resolver getOrgResultsPrefs): when the client
  withholds results, the personal results page shows a neutral
  "shared with your organisation" notice, the personal PDF returns 403,
  and the delegate's results email + credential are skipped on
  completion.
- Send-to-client: on completion, the delegate's results PDF is emailed
  to the client contact (new ara_personal_results_to_client template).
  The completion task fetches the gated PDF with a server-only
  CRON_SECRET header to bypass the delegate gate.
- Anonymous Mode A (no org) and un-migrated DBs fall back to permissive
  (delegate sees results) so nothing regresses before 00108 is applied.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC org report: methodology points to the PDF, not the repo path
entries.plain: AI Readiness Compass reports now link to a downloadable methodology PDF instead of pointing to an internal repository path.
entries.explanation:
The consultant/org report referenced "docs/ARA-Methodology-Brief.md on
the VIFM platform repository". Repointed to the downloadable methodology
PDF (caliber.viftraining.com/api/ara/methodology/pdf) so no repo path
appears in a client-facing report.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC top bar: point the corner link to the Compass, not the AC
entries.plain: The navigation link in AI Readiness Compass now returns you to the Compass dashboard instead of accidentally sending you to the Assessment Center.
entries.explanation:
The top-right header link read "Assessment Center" -> /admin. On ARC
pages it now reads "AI Readiness Compass" -> /ara so it returns to the
Compass home rather than cross-linking into the Assessment Center.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC vouchers: bulk-import delegate emails from a CSV/TXT file
entries.plain: You can now upload a CSV or text file to bulk-import delegate email addresses for vouchers instead of typing them individually.
entries.explanation:
"Email codes to delegates" gains an "Upload list (CSV / TXT)" button.
It extracts every email-looking token from the file regardless of
column layout (handles an Excel-exported CSV with header/quoted cells),
dedupes against what's already in the box, and fills the textarea -
no more typing or copy-pasting 20 addresses one by one.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC methodology brief: downloadable PDF instead of a GitHub link
entries.plain: The AI Readiness Compass methodology guide is now available as a downloadable PDF instead of requiring access to an external GitHub link.
entries.explanation:
New public route /api/ara/methodology/pdf renders docs/ARA-Methodology-
Brief.md to a branded A4 PDF via the existing Puppeteer pipeline (read
at request time = single source of truth; tolerant fallback). Middleware
bypasses it (public, no PII). All four GitHub links repointed: the
cohort dashboard, the personal results page, and the EN + AR personal
snapshot PDFs.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: make respondent Submit return immediately
entries.plain: When respondents submit their AI Readiness assessment, they now see confirmation instantly instead of waiting for certificates and emails to finish sending.
entries.explanation:
Completion previously awaited credential issuance + results-PDF render
+ email + consultant notification sequentially (2-15s). These now run
as background tasks (Promise.allSettled, not awaited) so Submit returns
right after marking complete + revalidate. Render's long-lived Node
server keeps the background promises alive; each branch stays
best-effort.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC respondent form: label every question by response type
entries.plain: AI Readiness questions now display labels showing whether each question is a self-rating, scenario, or knowledge check, making it easier for respondents to understand what they're answering.
entries.explanation:
Self-rating items now carry a "Self-rating" / "تقييم ذاتي" chip,
matching the existing Scenario / Knowledge-check chips, so a respondent
can tell the three item types apart within a (mixed-type) factor or
pillar section.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC vouchers: attach a shareable redeem link to each code
entries.plain: Voucher codes now include a clickable shareable link that respondents can use to start their assessment immediately without needing to enter a code manually.
entries.explanation:
Generating a voucher now surfaces its full redeem link
(/ara/redeem?code=...) - per-code "Copy link", "Copy links", and a
code,link CSV in the generate result, plus a "Link" button on every
voucher-list row. The redeem page already prefills the code from
?code=, so a delegate clicks the link and starts. Link base uses the
live origin (caliber.viftraining.com in prod).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: issue a verifiable AI Readiness credential
entries.plain: Respondents who complete an AI Readiness assessment now receive an official verifiable credential they can share, just like certificates from other VIFM Caliber assessments.
entries.explanation:
Individuals who complete a personal AI-readiness assessment (Mode A/B/C)
now earn a verifiable credential, parallel to Fluent CEFR / Technical:
- issueAraReadinessCredential() computes overall + maturity stage exactly
  as the personal results page; idempotent per respondent, best-effort
- wired into markAraRespondentComplete; ai_readiness added to the
  credential type, verify page, and certificate PDF labels (EN/AR)
- migration 00102 widens the credential_type CHECK to admit ai_readiness

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: i18n: strings for portal invite + ARC client registration
entries.plain: Added translations for inviting users to the portal and registering new clients in both English and Arabic.
entries.explanation:
EN/AR keys for the engagement "Invite to portal" action and the ARC
Register Client form + connected-services badges.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: first-class client registration within the service
entries.plain: AI Readiness clients registered through the platform now work seamlessly with Assessment Center, Pre-Hire, and Reflect 360 - no longer siloed to just AI Readiness.
entries.explanation:
Make AI Readiness a fully standalone vertical (the template for other
services). Registering a client from inside ARC now routes through the
shared registry instead of writing only ara_organizations:
- registerAraClient action -> createClientOrganization dual-write, so the
  client is reusable in Assessment Center, Pre-Hire and Reflect 360
- registry accepts createdBy to preserve consultant RLS ownership
- richer Register Client form (industry/country/contact)
- org list shows a Connected services badge (All services vs ARC-only)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Docs: mark auth as enabled in CLAUDE.md
entries.plain: System documentation now correctly shows that authentication is enabled across the platform.
entries.explanation:
Companion to c1804bf - the CLAUDE.md auth-status corrections (tracked as
claude.md) were missed by a case-mismatched git add. Current Status, Tech
Stack, Auth Status, Important Notes and the ARA deferred list now reflect
AUTH_ENABLED=true (since 2026-06-13).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC intro: avoid '1 minutes' in the timed-limit copy
entries.plain: Fixed a grammatical error in the AI Readiness timer message that said '1 minutes' instead of '1 minute'.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Per-instance timers: ARC + Technical (countdown + auto-submit)
entries.plain: Consultants can now set time limits for individual AI Readiness and Technical assessments; respondents see a countdown and auto-submit when time expires.
entries.explanation: ARC: optional per-assessment time limit set in the consultant wizard (migration 00084); the respondent clock anchors to a persisted started_at so it survives resume, shows a countdown, and auto-submits on expiry. Technical: per-domain / per-function time limit set on the cut-score editors (stored in assessment_timers); the start API returns the deadline (a mix-and-match run sums the picked functions' limits, capped), and the runner counts down + auto-submits. Tolerant of the migrations being absent.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pending-actions: log ARC graded-types/recommender shipped + tag-hygiene TODO
entries.plain: Updated internal tracking for new AI Readiness question types and recommendation features that have been completed.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Recommender: scope gap threshold to AI Readiness Compass only
entries.plain: The recommendation system now correctly applies smart matching only to AI Readiness Compass, while other assessments use the standard matching approach.
entries.explanation:
Per clarification, both fixes are AI-Readiness-Compass-only. Revert the
minimum-gap threshold on the AC behavioural and Reflect 360 recommenders
(restore recommend-on-any-positive-gap there). The threshold + the AI-vertical
preference now apply ONLY to the ARC personal snapshot + org-pillar recommenders.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: graded situational-judgment + knowledge-check question types
entries.plain: AI Readiness now includes scenario and knowledge-check questions with correct answers that are scored, giving a more complete picture of AI readiness beyond self-assessment.
entries.explanation:
Adds two GRADED question types to the ARC individual/personal factors
(situational_judgment + knowledge_check) - performance-style items scored
against an expert key, complementing the existing self-report Likert items.

- Migrations 00080 (enum) + 00081 (12 pilot items, 2 SJT + 1 knowledge per
  factor, bilingual, validation anchors flagged ai_proposed for SME review).
- Respondent renderer: graded items render as single-select with a bilingual
  Scenario/Knowledge-check chip; answer key (score_map) stripped before the
  page reaches the browser; scoring re-fetches it server-side.
- calculateQuestionScore routes the new types through the score_map path; they
  fold into the individual-factor scores.
- distortion.ts excludes graded items from fake-detection signals.

Review fixes (from an adversarial multi-agent review + live test):
- Admin Edit/Add question dropdowns now include the graded types (Edit was
  silently flipping graded items to 'rating' on save - data corruption).
- Edit-page score-map preview no longer crashes on {value,label} options.
- i18n labels (EN/AR) for the two types.
- workforce-readiness + agentic-readiness rollups now score via the canonical
  calculateQuestionScore instead of an inline scorer that was dropping all
  rating items from the Mode C cohort numbers (pre-existing bug fixed).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: chore: remove ARC client-onboarding deck and NUPCO-derived icons (per request)
entries.plain: Removed outdated onboarding materials and icons from the system as requested.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(decks): restyle ARC client-onboarding deck to match the NUPCO proposal style
entries.plain: The AI Readiness client onboarding presentation has been redesigned with a modern professional look matching the latest visual standards.
entries.explanation:
Rebuilt in the VIFM NUPCO visual language: off-white slides with a dark title
band + accent underline, left accent rail, icon-in-circle cards, and the NUPCO
footer (section tag chip + running footer + page number). Reuses the VIFM icon
set (scripts/assets/arc-icons, extracted from the NUPCO deck). Same content
(voucher -> Compass journey), same palette + Open Sans. 14 slides.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(decks): generalized ARC client-onboarding deck (any client)
entries.plain: Created a reusable onboarding presentation template that any client can receive when they get access codes, explaining the AI Readiness assessment process from start to finish.
entries.explanation:
Reusable, client-agnostic version of the onboarding deck to hand any client
when issuing access codes: what the AI Readiness Compass is + the process of
completing the engagement (code -> one-click redeem -> ~10-min assessment ->
instant on-screen + emailed PDF results), the four factors, coordinator notes,
privacy, timeline, contact. Generalized from the KAFD deck (no client-specific
names or code counts). Builder script + generated .pptx.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: chore(decks): add generated ARC pitch deck (.pptx) artifact
entries.plain: The AI Readiness Compass presentation deck is now available as a downloadable file for sharing with clients.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(kafd): AI Readiness Compass onboarding deck (priority 2)
entries.plain: A new 14-slide onboarding presentation explains the AI Readiness Compass journey to clients, from code redemption through assessment to results.
entries.explanation:
14-slide client onboarding presentation for KAFD covering the voucher ->
individual AI Readiness Compass journey end to end (receive code, one-click
redeem, ~10-min assessment, instant on-screen + emailed PDF results), the four
factors, coordinator notes, privacy, timeline, and contact. VIFM brand,
reuses the patterns from build-arc-pitch-deck.js. Builder script +
generated .pptx committed. Tracks KAFD as priority 2 in PENDING-ACTIONS.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(ara): attach results PDF to the completion email (Phase 2.3)
entries.plain: Assessment results now arrive as an email attachment, so participants get their PDF report directly in their inbox when they finish.
entries.explanation:
On individual-respondent completion, markAraRespondentComplete now fetches the
personal results PDF and attaches it to the ara_personal_results_link email
(best effort; falls back to the in-email PDF link if generation fails). With
the Resend transport, delegates receive their results on screen AND by email
with the PDF attached - completing the end-to-end client journey.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(ara): email codes to delegates + one-click redeem (Phase 2.2)
entries.plain: Administrators can email assessment codes to participants, who simply click a link and confirm their details to start the assessment.
entries.explanation:
- Voucher screen: 'Email codes to delegates' - paste emails, generates one
  single-use code per delegate and emails each a one-click link via Resend
  (code baked into ?code=, region/client inherited from the tagged org).
- Redeem page prefills code (read-only), email, and company (from the
  voucher's client) from the URL, so the delegate just confirms their name
  and starts. Manual entry still works when no code is in the URL.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(email): Resend transport for app email (voucher invites + ARA results)
entries.plain: Email delivery now uses a dedicated service, making assessment invites and results emails more reliable and professional.
entries.explanation:
Adds src/lib/integrations/resend.ts (Resend HTTP API, attachments supported)
and routes sendAraEmail through it first when RESEND_API_KEY is set, falling
back to Microsoft Graph, then console mock. Enables actually sending app email
(results, invites) via the same Resend account used for Supabase SMTP.

Env: RESEND_API_KEY + EMAIL_FROM (use onboarding@resend.dev until the domain
is verified, then noreply@viftraining.com).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(ara): vouchers - per-company insights, region from client, back link; SOP no em dashes
entries.plain: Administrators see a breakdown of how many employees at each company have started and completed the assessment, plus a downloadable report.
entries.explanation:
- Redemptions-by-company table on the vouchers screen (delegates / started /
  completed / completion% / last redeemed) + CSV export. Aggregated server-side
  from ara_voucher_redemptions joined to respondent completion.
- Region now inherits from the tagged client org: server derives region (and
  client_name) from ara_organizations when a client is selected; the UI locks
  the region control and shows "(from client)". Editable only when untagged.
- createClientOrgAction / org list now carry region so a newly-added client
  applies its region immediately.
- Back link (history-aware) added to the vouchers screen.
- SOP: replaced em dashes with hyphens across the voucher feature; recorded
  the no-em-dash + back-link-on-every-screen conventions in claude.md.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(ara): add-client inline on the vouchers screen
entries.plain: Administrators can add a new company client directly from the assessment code screen without switching pages.
entries.explanation:
Admins can create a client org (ara_organizations) without leaving
/ara/admin/vouchers. New createClientOrgAction (admin-gated; name + region +
sector enums) inserts the org; the client list + selection are now stateful
so a newly-added client appears in the dropdown and auto-selects for the
batch being generated.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(ara): add Vouchers tile to the ARA admin hub
entries.plain: The assessment code management page is now linked from the main admin dashboard for easier navigation.
entries.explanation:
Surfaces /ara/admin/vouchers on the /ara/admin landing (was direct-URL
only). EN/AR labels added. Discoverability only — page is already
admin-gated. Marks migration 00075 applied + nav done in PENDING-ACTIONS.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(ara): ARC voucher system — practice-access codes for AI Readiness Compass
entries.plain: Administrators can generate reusable codes that let employees take the AI Readiness assessment without needing a login account.
entries.explanation:
Admin generates redeemable codes; a delegate redeems one (no account) to
take a practice (sandbox) individual Compass run.

- Migration 00075: ara_vouchers + ara_voucher_redemptions + ara_voucher_claim
  RPC (atomic seat-pool claim so concurrent redemptions cannot oversell).
- src/lib/ara/vouchers.ts: generateVoucherCode (VIFM-ARC-XXXX-XXXX, unambiguous
  charset), createVoucherBatch, redeemVoucher (mirrors the consultant deep-dive
  provisioning recipe; snapshot tier; is_sandbox=true; pins active question bank).
- Admin UI /ara/admin/vouchers (admin-gated): batch generate, seat pool
  (uses-per-code), client-org tag, label, expiry, copy/CSV, disable/enable.
- Public redeem /ara/redeem (auth-bypassed in middleware): code + name + email +
  COMPANY (required, for future per-company insights) → /ara/respond/{token}.

Decisions applied: snapshot-only grant, seat pool, sandbox/practice, admin-only
generation, client-org tagging, company captured at redemption.

Build green; /ara/redeem + /ara/admin/vouchers render 200. NOTE: migration 00075
must be applied to Supabase before use (tracked in PENDING-ACTIONS.md §E).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Show all six bulk-generate buttons on the Evidence Map
entries.plain: All six assessment types can now be bulk-generated from the validation dashboard, instead of only two.
entries.explanation:
The Evidence & Validity Map only rendered the AC and ARC bulk-generate
buttons (show={["ac","arc"]}); the four adapter-driven instruments
(Fluent, Technical, Reflect, Psychometrics) only had buttons on their
individual consoles. Now the map surfaces all six so every instrument
can be drafted from the one hub.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Add Evidence Map 'Path to Green' plan
entries.plain: A plan outlines which validation gaps can be closed immediately, which need sign-offs, and which require research studies.
entries.explanation:
Takes stock of every remaining non-green cell on the Evidence & Validity
Map (84 cells: 23 documented, 13 live, 16 partial, 21 missing, 11 n/a)
and buckets each by what it actually needs:

1. Verify research anchors (do now — turns 7 cells green, no waiting)
2. Closeable with a document/sign-off (face-validity reviews, finish Psy brief)
3. Automatic once data accrues (already-wired live cells + thresholds)
4. Needs a real empirical study (CFA, test-retest, DIF, criterion, norms)
5. Honest reclassification candidates (ARC criterion → n/a, by design)

Deliberately does not claim a uniformly-green map: the empirical cells
stay red until the science is done, which is itself the credibility
signal. docs/Evidence-Map-Path-to-Green.md.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Add one-tap bulk evidence drafting (AC + ARC) for phone/web use
entries.plain: Evidence descriptions for assessments can now be generated from the web interface without needing technical tools.
entries.explanation:
Adds web-triggered bulk draft generation so evidence can be populated
from the deployed site (e.g. a phone) without the CLI script:

- generateAllCompetencyEvidence (AC) + generateAllQuestionEvidence (ARC)
  server actions, admin-gated. Each processes a small batch (6) per call
  and returns how many remain, so a hosted serverless request never
  times out making dozens/hundreds of AI calls. Reuses the per-item
  suggesters (saved as ai_proposed, never verified). Guards on
  ANTHROPIC_API_KEY with a clear message.
- BulkEvidenceButtons client component auto-loops the batched action with
  live progress until done; surfaced on /admin/ac-evidence (AC) and
  /admin/evidence-map (AC + ARC).

Typecheck + lint clean.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Add batch evidence-backfill script (AC + ARC)
entries.plain: A background process can automatically generate draft evidence descriptions for all assessment questions at once.
entries.explanation:
scripts/backfill-evidence.ts walks the AC competencies and the active
ARC question bank, calls the per-construct AI evidence suggester for any
construct with no evidence, and saves results as review_status=
'ai_proposed'. Hallucination guard preserved: nothing is written as
verified — a human still signs off in the admin UI before anything
reaches a client deliverable.

Idempotent (never overwrites verified/edited/rejected; --refresh redoes
ai_proposed). Flags: --ac, --arc, --limit N, --refresh. Runs in the
user's environment via tsx (needs SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY +
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env.local).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Rebrand ARA to ARC (AI Readiness Compass) in user-facing text
entries.plain: The 'AI Readiness Assessment' module is now branded as 'AI Readiness Compass' throughout the platform and all emails.
entries.explanation:
Rename the AI Readiness module's user-visible branding from ARA /
"AI Readiness Assessment" to ARC / "AI Readiness Compass":
- EN/AR locale strings: acronym ARA -> ARC (28 strings)
- Email templates: "AI Readiness Assessment" -> "AI Readiness Compass"
  (EN + AR + bilingual), "VIFM ARA" signature -> "VIFM ARC"
- Org service-picker label -> "AI Readiness Compass (org)"
- Clients page badge "ARA: not linked" -> "ARC: not linked"

Routes, code identifiers, and database (ara_* tables/policies) are
intentionally unchanged.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01L5dfkK7BmU5WjmRjtsQKpk

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: i18n the Guided Start wizard (start namespace, EN/AR)
entries.plain: The setup wizard now displays in both English and Arabic, making it accessible to a wider range of users.
entries.explanation:
Replaces the wizard's English literals with a full start.* i18n namespace (~185
keys per locale) and wires guided-start.tsx through t() — the English const maps
are gone, each sub-component uses useTranslation, all logic unchanged.

Covers the chrome (fork, stepper, steps, nav), goals/contexts/depths,
requirement/rationale/construct/instrument labels, the module menu, and the four
inline create forms (Pre-Hire / ARA / Reflect / AC). Verified that all 90 static
keys + 95 dynamic combinations resolve in both EN and AR (a key typo renders as
raw text, which tsc can't catch). AR is AI-generated, flagged for native review.

tsc + next lint + EN/AR locale parity (3969) clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Restore ESLint config and fix the issues it surfaces
entries.plain: Fixed code quality checks so the system builds correctly and catches errors during deployment.
entries.explanation:
Adds the missing .eslintrc.json (next/core-web-vitals) so `next lint` runs
non-interactively and `next build` lints again. Fixes the 9 errors + 1 warning
it flagged: escape JSX apostrophes (react/no-unescaped-entities) in the ARA
respond page, recommended-courses panel, and the candidate + personal-snapshot
PDF reports; drop a stale @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any disable directive
in fade-in (the rule isn't registered by this config); and document the
intentional dep omission on the wash-up Realtime effect. tsc + lint both clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix consent submission RLS rejection via service-role write
entries.plain: Fixed a security issue where admins could not submit consent forms on behalf of candidates.
entries.explanation:
The consent_records INSERT policy only permits a candidate inserting their
own row, but POST /api/consent/[id] also serves the admin "view as candidate"
flow and used the RLS-aware client - so an admin caller hit "new row violates
row-level security policy". Route the candidate validation, idempotency check,
insert, and status update through the service client (the getUser() guard still
requires an authenticated caller), matching the Academy/ARA write pattern.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Document Reflect 360, Fluent, CBI, Academy + Credentials in CLAUDE.md
entries.plain: Updated internal documentation to cover all assessment modules and platform features.
entries.explanation:
- Remove the stale "Undocumented modules" pointer now that each is covered
- Add sections: Reflect 360, Fluent, AI Conversational Assessor (CBI),
  VIFM Academy, VIFM Credentials + Verify
- Refresh Project Structure tree and migration list (00028-00049)
- Add ARA pillar-scope override, agentic-AI readiness tier, and public
  course quote-requests subsections

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Translate the AI Readiness console to Arabic (RTL)
entries.plain: AI Readiness assessment dashboards and reports now fully support Arabic language with right-to-left display.
entries.explanation:
Wires the ARA consultant + admin consoles into the i18n system with
Arabic copy: the consultant dashboard, assessment creation, the
assessment-detail view (all tabs), personal deep-dive, the admin console
(organizations, question bank + authoring, regulatory, retention,
sandbox), the cohort view, and the report charts (gantt, investment
matrix, compliance). Report components that were already bilingual were
left untouched. ~760 new EN/AR keys (2019 total at parity); tsc clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Techno audit fixes: certification integrity, lifecycle, PDFs, UX
entries.plain: Technical Certification now requires a minimum number of assessment items to award credentials and prevents duplicate certifications from multiple test attempts.
entries.explanation:
Adversarial audit of the Technical Certification module (11 findings, 0
critical). App-layer fixes, no migration:

Majors
- Certified function/mix assembly now enforces an overall min-item floor
  (8) so a single-skill selection can no longer certify + mint a public
  credential from 2 items; below the floor drops to indicative.
- Program participant path: one certified sitting per seat. Start refuses
  a retake once a result exists; issuance dedupes the credential per
  participant+scope so a token can't farm multiple verifiable credentials.
- Retention purge now includes technical_sandbox_voucher_redemptions so
  redeemer PII (name/email/company/ip/user_agent) is removed in-window.
- Both technical cohort PDF routes await document.fonts.ready so Arabic
  shapes before capture (no tofu glyphs).

Minors
- Participant access token rejected once the program is completed/archived.
- Function cohort query filters on the program's own function_key (a mix or
  other-function run no longer surfaces as this function's score).
- Result screen labels "Certified" + green footer only when passedCut; a
  non-passing certified sitting is no longer labelled certified.
- Instructions screen discloses the time limit before Start.
- Server-side time-limit backstop: deadline stamped on the session at start;
  /score rejects a grossly late submission (client countdown unchanged).
- En-dash replaced with a hyphen in the taker-facing band-range string.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Wrap Techno voucher issuance in the 3-step wizard
entries.plain: Technical Certification voucher issuance now uses a guided step-by-step process to set up and distribute tests.
entries.explanation: Keeps the function picker, custom test-builder, CSV export and function column; adds Setup/Delivery/Issue steps + send-to-delegates (upload/paste + email each).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Unify Techno voucher form: identity fields + Techno options callout
entries.plain: Technical Certification voucher forms now have a consistent design with clear labeling.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Voucher consolidation: bilingual redeem for Pre-Hire + Techno (Phase 2 done)
entries.plain: Voucher redemption now works the same way across Pre-Hire and Technical Certification assessments in both English and Arabic.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Techno: copy redeem link (not code) + save reusable custom assessments
entries.plain: Users can now copy a ready-to-share redemption link instead of a code, and save custom test designs for reuse.
entries.explanation:
- Copy link, not code: the voucher result panels and the custom-builder
  issued rows now copy the redeem LINK (code pre-filled) instead of the
  bare code; the dropped "Copy code"/"Copy codes" buttons are gone and the
  builder's link copy gained the secure-context execCommand fallback.
- Save custom assessments for reuse: a built custom (pick-and-choose)
  design can be named and saved, then loaded/updated/deleted from a
  "Saved assessments" picker - in BOTH the custom builder and the vouchers
  "Custom (shorter trial)" scope. Loading rehydrates skills/tasks/weight
  (Set-safe) and waits for the builder data before applying the selection.
- New table technical_custom_assessments (migration 00166, admin-only RLS
  mirroring the vouchers table) + a server-only lib + admin-guarded
  save/list/delete actions; all reads/writes tolerate an un-applied 00166.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Techno vouchers: copyable redeem link + custom-built test scope
entries.plain: Technical Certification now offers copyable redemption links and the ability to create custom shorter tests from a function's skills.
entries.explanation:
- Shared "many seats" and single-code results now surface a copyable
  redeem LINK (code pre-filled) so a candidate starts without typing the
  code; add a per-row "Copy link" action in the Existing vouchers table.
- Add a "Custom (shorter trial)" test scope: pick a subset of the
  function's skills and hands-on tasks inline, carried to the redeemed
  sitting via customConfig (indicative, no credential).
- Robust clipboard copy with an execCommand fallback so the buttons work
  over plain HTTP / LAN, not just secure contexts.
- Add getCustomBuilderDataAction to load skills/tasks for the picker.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Techno audit: credential UNIQUE backstop (00164) + sandbox UX polish
entries.plain: Technical Certification database now prevents accidental duplicate credential issuance and improved sandbox interface clarity.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Techno audit: harden technical sandbox voucher RPCs (migration 00160)
entries.plain: Technical Certification sandbox voucher system now has hardened security controls to prevent unauthorized access.
entries.explanation:
The claim was already atomic and a release RPC already existed (00078); 00160
adds the SECURITY DEFINER hardening the other instruments got - pinned
search_path + EXECUTE locked to service_role on both technical_sandbox_voucher_
claim/release (were callable by anon via PostgREST). Applied + verified live.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Techno audit: close credential-replay race + cross-candidate substitution
entries.plain: Fixed critical security issues where credentials could be issued multiple times or assigned to the wrong candidate.
entries.explanation:
The two security-critical fixes on the only credential-issuing instrument:
- loadSession now claims the single-use session ATOMICALLY (UPDATE consumed_at
  null->now ... WHERE consumed_at IS NULL AND not-expired, RETURNING) instead of
  a SELECT-then-UPDATE. Two concurrent /score calls can no longer both pass the
  unconsumed check and both issue a credential (replay / double-issuance closed);
  TTL is enforced in the same atomic step.
- A SESSION run now binds the result + credential to the session's own
  candidate_id/engagement_id (set at start), not the request body - a leaked
  session id can no longer mint a credential under another identity. The
  untrusted items path (indicative-only, no credential) still uses the body.
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Mobile + copy polish: ARC GCC wording, Techno mobile layout, no h-swipe
entries.plain: Updated messaging for GCC regions, improved mobile layout, and fixed page scrolling issues on phones.
entries.explanation:
- ARC landing tile: "calibrated for Saudi Arabia, UAE, and the wider GCC" (EN + AR).
- Techno framework overview: skill-block rows stack on mobile (source text dropped
  the shrink-0 right column that bled off-screen); side-by-side on sm+.
- Global: html/body overflow-x: clip so a stray wide element can't make the whole
  page pan sideways on phones (clip keeps sticky + inner scroll working).
- Techno overview: relabel the sandbox-editor quick action "Review & edit sandbox
  tasks" so it's distinct from the "Preview the sandbox" launch button.

Verified at 390px: zero horizontal overflow; the previously-bleeding rows now sit
inside the viewport. tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Rename the Technical Assessment portal to "Techno" (user-facing)
entries.plain: The Technical Assessment portal is now called 'Techno' throughout the platform for consistency with other assessment names.
entries.explanation:
Brand rename like Cognitive->Reason: every user-facing display name of the
Technical Assessment portal is now "Techno" (Arabic "تكنو") - sidebar nav,
admin hub + landing service cards (EN+AR), portal-comparison, page titles +
heroes, tech-sandbox/runner/redeem/vouchers/results/answers/custom/client-report
back-links + headings, voucher hub label, delegate emails, cohort + sandbox
report titles, settings timer label, i18n values (techAssessment/techCertification).

Deliberately kept (50 renamed / 105 kept, found by a discovery workflow): the
/tech-assessment + /tech-sandbox routes, tech_assessment_*/technical_* tables,
all TS identifiers/types, code comments, and the generic descriptor "technical
proficiency / skills / domains / certification" (what it measures). Verified:
tsc + JSON clean; sidebar + hub render "Techno"; no "Technical Assessment" left
in the UI.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical: structured scoring editor for sandbox tasks
entries.plain: Admins can now use a structured form to define correct answers and scoring rules for hands-on tasks, instead of writing raw code.
entries.explanation:
- Replace the raw master_solution / checkpoints JSON textareas with a typed
  editor: engine-aware master answer (cell map / field map / SQL query) and a
  per-kind checkpoint list (cell_value / is_array_formula / logic_value /
  sql_result_match) with target/field/expected/tolerance/weight fields
- Strict server-side checkpoint schema (zod discriminated union) + unique-id
  check so a malformed checkpoint can't reach the scorer
- Non-standard shapes (e.g. python) fall back to raw JSON; label_ar preserved

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical: sandbox-task content editor on the review page
entries.plain: Admins can now edit task content (instructions, time limits, correct answers, scoring) directly on the review page without leaving it.
entries.explanation:
- "Edit content" toggle per task expands an inline editor for the prompt,
  instructions, description, name (EN/AR), framework ref, time limit, and
  the master answer / expected output + scoring checkpoints + engine config
  (JSON), alongside the existing approve/reject status controls
- New admin-gated updateBlockContentAction validates + parses the JSON
  fields before writing; updateBlockContent persists via service role
- listBlocksForReview now returns the editable content per block

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical Assessment: section nav on landing, numbered actions
entries.plain: The Technical Assessment menu on the admin dashboard is now cleaner, with key links moved to a top navigation bar.
entries.explanation:
- Move the Overview / Voucher codes / Model answers / Retention links from
  the sidebar's Technical Assessment group onto a top section bar on the
  /admin/tech-sandbox landing; the sidebar now carries a single Technical
  Assessment link per pillar
- Number the quick-action cards 1-4
- Remove the "Back to admin" link (the multi-portal admin home is gone)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical custom builder: assign via the standard voucher system (carry the design)
entries.plain: Custom technical assessments now use the same voucher-code system as other assessments, making assignment and sharing easier.
entries.explanation:
