Replaces the custom builder's bespoke "Assign the assessment" delegate roster
(which issued one direct session link each) with the same voucher system the rest
of the platform uses (ARC / Persona / Fluent / Cognitive). Each delegate now gets
a voucher code + one-click redeem link; an empty roster yields one shareable
anonymous code.

The custom design (selected skills + hands-on blocks + title) is carried on the
voucher via a new custom_config jsonb column (migration 00141) and applied at
redemption, so a redeemed code provisions the SAME custom sitting - not the
function default. generateVoucherBatch + generateVouchersAction thread
customConfig; redeemVoucher reads it (progressive, tolerant of pending 00141 /
00136 / 00085) and passes isCustom + skills + blocks + title to createSession.

Migration 00141 must be applied for the design to persist; until then the path
degrades to the function default (no crash). The old direct-session action is
left unused but in place.

tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical landing: promote the four actions into a titled "Quick actions" card grid
entries.plain: The four main actions on the Technical Assessment landing page are now displayed as easy-to-spot cards instead of hidden text links.
entries.explanation:
The inline text links (Build a custom assessment / View completed results /
Review sandbox tasks / View model answers) were easy to miss. They are now a
titled "Quick actions" section of four icon + title + one-line cards, matching
the page's card hierarchy. Also dropped two em dashes from comments.

Verified live. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical client report: PDF export (P4) - finishes the aggregation feature
entries.plain: Admins can now download technical assessment results as PDF, JSON, or CSV reports for clients and stakeholders.
entries.explanation:
- src/lib/reports/tech-client-report.tsx: React-PDF document for the
  ClientReportPayload (company banner, company overall funnel + skill-profile
  table + insight columns, then per-project sections, band legend). English,
  Helvetica - mirrors the per-session tech-sandbox-report styling.
- Route moved to route.tsx and gains format=pdf (renderToBuffer) alongside the
  existing json (default) + csv. Admin-gated, force-dynamic.
- Admin page now offers PDF / JSON / CSV download buttons.

Verified live (authed): PDF 200 application/pdf ~14KB valid %PDF-; JSON 200
(vifm, 3 projects, 6 insights); CSV 200. tsc + lint clean. This closes the
Technical client-report aggregation feature (P1-P4 all shipped).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical client report: Company -> Project aggregation + insights + payload
entries.plain: Technical results from multiple assessment types can now be combined into one company-level report showing overall skill profiles and gaps.
entries.explanation:
A modular mechanism that aggregates individual technical results across portals
into a hierarchical client report.

- src/lib/reports/tech-aggregation/: types, sources (portal adapters -
  Sandbox primary via technical_sandbox_sessions, MCQ via tech_assessment_results,
  normalised into one RawTechResult), metrics (pure participation / per-domain
  avg-high-low profiles / gap-vs-baseline), insights (pure deterministic
  strengths / systemic vulnerabilities / training recs), aggregate (orchestrator
  -> ClientReportPayload, wires recommendCoursesForTechnical for course-backed recs).
- Company key = normalized organization_name; project key = voucher batch (else
  per-function "direct"); domain = function (sandbox) / domain_key (MCQ).
  Baseline default 70% (Decision 3). Tolerant of either portal being absent.
- Admin page /admin/tech-sandbox/client-report (picker + company/project detail:
  funnel, skill-profile table with gap badges, grouped insight cards) + JSON/CSV
  export at /api/tech-sandbox/client-report.

Verified live: VIFM -> 4 invited/3 completed (75%), 3 domains/3 projects,
gaps detected, and the engine produced "Targeted Accounts Payable (AP)
training" (the spec's AP<70% example). tsc + lint clean. PDF export deferred.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: SD-7: technical report - per-band capability meanings + FP&A pillar definitions
entries.plain: Technical assessment reports now clearly explain what each proficiency band means and include descriptions for all skill categories.
entries.explanation:
Ask (c): the proficiency-bands legend now states what each band MEANS, not
just the numeric threshold. proficiencyTierMeaning(tier, locale) is the single
source of truth, rendered on the tech-sandbox PDF legend and the on-screen
admin results page.

Ask (a): migration 00139 authors the three FP&A (node 1.7) pillar definitions
(they were inserted before the description columns existed in 00118), so the
FP&A report explains each category. L&D 2.6 already had definitions (00118);
the seven inactive 00086 finance functions stay NULL (not in the demo).

Asks (b) candidate narrative + the bands legend were already shipped (TECH-6).
Ask (d) in-assessment hints deferred (the report already carries rich
explanatory text; hints are a guided-mode feature, not demo-critical).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical lens: catch PGRST204 + carry lens through voucher path
entries.plain: Fixed a critical issue where technical assessments could fail if assigned via voucher codes with certain talent groups.
entries.explanation:
Review fixes for TECH-5:

1. createSession's insert peel ladder relied on isMissingSchemaError,
which only matched 42P01/42703 - but a missing column on a PostgREST
INSERT returns PGRST204. A pending 00135 would hard-500 every technical
sitting. isMissingSchemaError now also matches PGRST204 (consistent with
the codebase-wide strip-and-retry sites).

2. The voucher/bulk-delegate path provisions the session at redemption,
so it dropped the talent lens - acquisition voucher cohorts fell back to
development framing. The lens now rides the voucher row (00136) and is
copied onto the session on redeem; threaded through both issuance UIs.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical: design-first custom builder + dev-lens course block (TECH-1, TECH-5)
entries.plain: The custom assessment builder now works in two steps: first design the assessment, then assign it to candidates with individual links.
entries.explanation:
TECH-1: the custom builder is now a two-step flow - design the
assessment (name/title + knowledge skills + hands-on tasks) first,
then confirm and assign the client + multiple delegates, one private
link each. New createCustomSandboxSessionsAction issues one session
per delegate from a shared design.

TECH-5: the technical report carries a talent lens (00135) captured
from the launching pillar. Under development (and the NULL default,
which has always been a development read) the report adds a 'Recommended
VIFM Academy programmes' block driven by the function's domain and the
candidate's weakest assessed area; under acquisition the block is
suppressed. recommendCoursesForTechnical ranks vertical-matched courses
by level-appropriateness. Rendered on the on-screen report + the PDF.

Also fixes two pre-existing em dashes in the technical report PDF.
Migration 00135 adds technical_sandbox_sessions.assessment_title +
talent_lens (tolerant, newest-first peel preserves is_custom).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical report: per-block development narrative + tips (TECH-6)
entries.plain: Technical assessment reports now include personalized development tips for each task where a candidate struggled.
entries.explanation:
Each per-subcategory block card showed only MISS/PASS checkpoint lines and
no prose. Add a 2-3 sentence development note per block that has >=1 missed
checkpoint: explain the missed competency (from the block description +
framework ref + the exact missed checkpoint labels) then 1-2 concrete
development tips. Blocks with no misses (strengths) get no note.

- New src/lib/ai/tech-block-notes.ts: one batched AI call (getAIClient) keyed
  by block id, prompt-injection-guarded (missed labels treated as DATA ONLY),
  EN+AR, with a deterministic per-block fallback when no key / on failure.
- ReportBlock gains developmentNoteEn/Ar; getSessionReport builds contexts for
  missed blocks, reads cache, generates the uncached, best-effort persists.
- Migration 00133: technical_sandbox_sessions.block_development_notes jsonb
  cache (keyed by skill_block_id) so screen + PDF stay identical and AI isn't
  re-run each view; tolerant of it being unapplied (generate in-memory).
- Rendered under each block's checkpoints on the on-screen results page + the
  PDF (EN; tech PDF is EN-only).

Subagent-built; reviewed the generator + fallback; tsc + next lint clean.
Verified live: AI path grounded in real framework refs, fallback path, and
00133-absent tolerance. Apply migration 00133.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical report + voucher fixes (TECH-2/3/4)
entries.plain: Technical reports now show unique development advice per skill area and display the proficiency band meanings prominently at the top.
entries.explanation:
TECH-2: the per-section "Development focus" no longer repeats. techDevFocus
now takes a DevFocusContext (category name + score% + band + weakest
sub-area + the actual missed checkpoint labels) and produces a distinct,
data-grounded line per section - the lead (name + score + band) alone makes
two basic sections read differently; advanced sections still carry none.

TECH-3: surface the proficiency-band legend (Basic < 60, Intermediate
60-84, Advanced >= 85) at the TOP of the report (PDF + on-screen), not just
the footer; fixed an en-dash in the footer copy.

TECH-4: named delegates moved from Option 2 to Option 1. Option 2 (client
self-distributes) is now count-only: anonymous single-use codes OR one
shared seat-pool code (no delegate box). Option 1 (direct issue) gains the
"Issue to named delegates" paste/CSV -> one code+link each via the existing
generateVouchersAction({delegates}). Verified live.

Subagent-built; reviewed TECH-2 logic; tsc + next lint clean. (Tech PDF is
EN-only per React-PDF; AR dev-focus strings produced but not rendered there,
matching existing behaviour.)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add exposure control to certified MCQ function bank
entries.plain: Admins can now control how many questions from a certified question bank appear on each candidate's test, reducing repetition across candidates.
entries.explanation:
The certified assembler already draws a random subset per skill per
sitting (shuffle + slice), but the approved pool equalled the draw
(~4 each), so every candidate saw the identical form. Make the
per-sitting draw configurable and surface pool-vs-draw guidance so
SMEs build a surplus pool that yields varying forms.

- Migration 00122: technical_function_cut_scores.draw_per_skill
  (nullable; falls back to DEFAULT_DRAW_PER_SKILL=4)
- getFunctionCutScore: tolerant wide/narrow select (loads pass/floor
  even when 00122 is unapplied); FunctionCutScore gains drawPerSkill
- buildCertifiedFunctionTest draws Math.max(floor, cut.drawPerSkill)
- setFunctionCutScoreAction persists draw_per_skill, tolerant of 00122 absent
- cert-workbench: per-sitting-draw input, target-pool-per-skill input
  feeding Draft-all, exposure legend, per-skill thin/good badges
- EN + AR i18n keys

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add custom pick-and-choose technical assessment builder
entries.plain: Admins can now build custom technical assessments by cherry-picking specific skills and tasks from a full function.
entries.explanation:
Admins can assemble a narrower technical sitting from one function -
a subset of its knowledge (MCQ) skills and/or hands-on tasks - at
/admin/tech-sandbox/custom. Custom sittings are indicative (no
credential) by design, enforced server-side in submitSession.

Also gate the certified combined credential on SME-approved sandbox
tasks (review_status='approved'), not just approved MCQ items.

- Migration 00121: is_custom + selected_skills + selected_block_ids
- buildMcqTestForFunction narrows MCQ to chosen skills (intersection;
  empty intersection means no knowledge section, never the full set)
- loadBlocks honors an explicit block selection, incl. empty [] as a
  knowledge-only sitting (NULL stays "all" for legacy/full sittings)
- createSession fails loud if 00121 is absent for a custom sitting so
  the is_custom no-credential flag can never be silently dropped
- runner handles knowledge-only sittings (no hands-on tasks)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical: sandbox task SME-approval workflow + review console
entries.plain: Technical sandbox tasks now go through a formal review and approval process, like other test items, so L&D can control which tasks are live.
entries.explanation:
Performance sandbox tasks now have the same draft -> in_review -> approved ->
rejected/retired lifecycle as the MCQ items (migration 00120 adds review_status
+ reviewer columns to technical_skill_blocks, orthogonal to the active/inactive
runtime status; backfills seeded blocks to approved). New admin-gated review
console at /admin/tech-sandbox/sandbox-blocks (per-function, grouped by pillar,
per-pillar approved count + per-block approve/reject/retire with reviewer
stamp), linked from the Technical Assessment command page. Service:
listBlocksForReview + setBlockReviewStatus (tolerant of 00120 not applied).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical: close results-access review items (dead code + hardening)
entries.plain: Removed unused code paths that could have emailed candidates their technical assessment scores - candidates should not see these results.
entries.explanation:
Adversarial review confirmed the candidate cannot reach results via any path and
the admin view is correctly gated (no IDOR, no regression). Cleared the two
low-severity items it flagged:
- TS-1: delete the orphaned emailSessionResults wrapper (results-email.tsx) that
  would have emailed the candidate their scored PDF if re-wired; the generic
  emailResults() in email.ts is kept for a future client-recipient send. Removed
  the now-dead buildInitialResult helper from the candidate page (no longer
  imports getSessionReport). Updated the stale PENDING-ACTIONS note.
- TS-2: the report PDF route hard-fails a synthetic dev-admin in production, so a
  misconfigured auth-off prod deploy can't serve the scored PDF on the candidate
  token (local dev auth-off still works).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical: admin/client on-screen results view (development report)
entries.plain: Managers and VIFM admins can now view detailed technical assessment results on-screen, including development guidance and detailed breakdowns by skill area.
entries.explanation:
The explanations now render on-screen for the reviewer (manager / VIFM admin),
not the candidate: a new admin-gated results surface under
/admin/tech-sandbox/results - a completed-sittings list + a per-sitting
development report (purpose note, narrative, overall band, knowledge-by-
subcategory, per-category definitions + development focus + bands, per-
subcategory definitions + bands + checkpoints) + the admin-gated PDF download.
listSubmittedSessions() backs the list; both pages requireRole(admin) (and sit
under the admin-gated layout). Linked from the Technical Assessment command page.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical: hide results from the candidate (client/admin-only)
entries.plain: Candidates no longer see their technical assessment scores after completing the test - results are for managers and L&D leaders only.
entries.explanation:
The technical assessment is a development instrument whose report is read by the
client / VIFM admin, NOT the candidate. So the candidate no longer sees results:
- runner shows a completion confirmation on submit (no scores), never the Results panel
- page.tsx never seeds the candidate runner with results on reload
- the submit response carries only { submitted } - no score, no combined, no keyed test
- the candidate results email is removed
- the report PDF route now requires admin (it sits under the /api/tech-sandbox/*
  middleware auth-bypass, so the candidate's token could otherwise fetch it)
Scoring still runs server-side; delivery moves to the client/admin (admin results
view + admin-gated PDF). Auto-email to a client recipient is a follow-on.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical: frame the manager report as a development report
entries.plain: The technical assessment report now emphasizes development and learning priorities rather than selection, with guidance tailored to each skill level.
entries.explanation:
The report is read by a third party (manager / L&D lead), not the candidate,
and is for development - not selection. So: a development-purpose statement in
the header, a development-framed narrative (relative strength + where to
prioritise development), and a per-category 'Development focus' line (band-aware
guidance for basic/intermediate categories; advanced = a strength, no focus).
Definitions + per-subcategory results + narrative remain.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical: explanatory manager report + deeper per-subcategory coverage
entries.plain: The manager-facing technical report now explains what each section tested and shows results broken down by individual skill areas for better clarity.
entries.explanation:
Manager-facing technical report now EXPLAINS what each category + subcategory
tested and reads more completely:
- per-category (pillar) + per-subcategory (skill block) plain-English
  definitions in the PDF (migration 00118 adds pillar description columns +
  authors the 3 L&D category definitions; block descriptions already existed
  but were dropped - now threaded through getSessionReport)
- a 'Knowledge by subcategory' section: the MCQ knowledge section is
  recomputed per subcategory from the stored keyed test, so every knowledge
  subcategory gets a band (not just the hands-on tasks)
- a plain-language candidate 'Performance summary' narrative
- items-per-subcategory raised 4 -> 5 for a more reliable per-subcategory read
loadBlocks tolerates the 00118 columns being unapplied (renders names-only).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix AI model id: claude-sonnet-4-20250514 -> claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929
entries.plain: Fixed AI model configuration so all AI-powered features (assessments, report writing, course suggestions) now work properly instead of falling back to static content.
entries.explanation:
Every AI feature (Fluent/Cognitive generation, Technical assessment, quiz gen,
CBI, report writing, course/JD extraction) silently fell back to static decks.
Root cause, found by probing the live API: the key is valid + active, but the
configured model "claude-sonnet-4-20250514" returns 404 not_found_error for this
account, so getAIClient()'s calls threw and hit the catch -> fallback. The
account's available Sonnet is 4.5; verified live that claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929
succeeds and the old ids 404. After the change Fluent generation flips from an
instant 2.5s static deck to a real multi-second AI generation.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical sandbox: green answer cells, auto-fit columns, scoring explainer
entries.plain: Technical sandbox now highlights answer cells in green so candidates know where to enter responses, auto-fits column widths, and explains scoring clearly on results.
entries.explanation:
- Spreadsheet engine: editable answer cells (incl. empty ones) now render
  with a light-green fill + dark-green text so the user sees exactly where
  input is required; columns auto-fit to content (column A / labels gets
  the most room, clamped); the helper line shows the green legend.
- Result page: a new ScoreMethodology panel adds (a) a plain-language
  narrative on the user's answers (overall tier + strongest/weakest
  pillar), (b) proficiency-level definitions, and (c) the exact scoring
  algorithm so admins know how the single "assessment complete" total is
  derived (weighted checkpoint pass-rate -> block % -> mean = overall ->
  tier thresholds), including the knowledge/hands-on blend when present.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical: register program clients first-class via the registry
entries.plain: Technical programs now register clients in the main platform system so client data is consistent across VIFM services.
entries.explanation:
Creating a technical program now registers its organization through the
shared registry (dual-write to organizations + ara_organizations, deduped
by name) instead of storing only a free-text name - so the client is a
real, cross-service platform client. The create form gains a client
picker (datalist of existing platform clients). Migration 00103 adds
technical_programs.organization_id; the FK link is best-effort and no-ops
cleanly until it is applied (client registration works regardless).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add sandbox technical assessment test-bank design spec
entries.plain: Designed the full hands-on technical assessment bank covering all 62 job functions with auto-graded exercises mapped to real-world scenarios.
entries.explanation:
Per-function design for the hands-on sandbox layer across all 62
technical functions (9 domains): 3 pillars, 3-5 auto-graded tasks each,
mapped to the four engines (spreadsheet / advanced_spreadsheet /
logic_input / sql) with weighted checkpoints, GCC scenarios, and real
framework references. Non-quantitative functions target their gradable
analytical slice with the limitation flagged. Build spec only; FP&A is
the live reference. Seed migrations follow per the build plan.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Combine MCQ knowledge section into voucher sandbox sitting
entries.plain: Technical assessments can now blend knowledge questions with hands-on tasks, with each section weighted separately but scoring together toward a verifiable credential.
entries.explanation:
The voucher-delivered technical assessment now optionally blends an MCQ
knowledge section with the hands-on sandbox. The voucher's MCQ % is a
SCORE WEIGHT (not an item count): each section scores 0-100, then
combined = mcq*(pct/100) + sandbox*(1-pct/100), with per-section floors
(50/50) and an overall bar (70). A passing combined sitting whose MCQ
section is assembled from the SME-approved bank issues a verifiable
technical_proficiency credential; indicative (AI-fallback) sections do
not. mcq_pct=0 keeps the historical sandbox-only behaviour.

Security + integrity (from adversarial review):
- keyed mcq_test stays server-side; stripped on the page load path and
  never serialized by the submit route (explicit allowlisted DTO on both
  fresh and already-submitted paths).
- atomic compare-and-set finalize + credential idempotency on source_id
  + client in-flight guard prevent double-issued credentials under a
  concurrent/double submit.
- reload after submit rehydrates the result panels + credential link;
  reload mid-MCQ keeps answers (sessionStorage) and the countdown
  (expires_at), with MCQ time budgeted into the timer.

Tolerant of migration 00085 not being applied (degrades to sandbox-only).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Assessment delivery: technical item types, intros, admin timers
entries.plain: Technical assessments now support more question types, include an introduction screen, and allow admins to set custom time limits per assessment.
entries.explanation: Technical assessment gains multi-select/scenario/true-false items across indicative + certified paths (migration 00082, type-aware bank assembly, SME console authoring). Add a 'Before you begin' intro to every assessment (technical, quiz, fluent, ARC, sandbox) via a shared component. Admin-configurable timers for quiz + fluent (migration 00083, keyed assessment_timers table + Settings UI).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Redesign tech-sandbox admin into domain cards + collapsible options
entries.plain: The technical sandbox admin page is now organized as domain cards that expand to show details, making it easier to navigate all job functions.
entries.explanation:
The competency framework was one long scroll (9 domains x all functions
expanded), pushing the issue/voucher tools far down the page. Redesign
for client demos:

- Framework is now a grid of 9 designed domain cards (icon + live/roadmap
  badge + function count), single-select: click a card to open a detail
  panel with that domain's functions; the live function keeps its pillars,
  skill blocks, and Preview button. Live domain opens by default.
- Each domain card + detail header now shows the professional frameworks
  it aligns to (IFRS/GAAP/SOCPA for Finance, etc.) via an editable
  DOMAIN_FRAMEWORKS map.
- Both distribution options (direct link / vouchers) are now collapsible
  cards, collapsed by default, so the page fits ~one screen.

New: collapsible-section.tsx. tsc clean; verified in the preview.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix tech-sandbox PDF: PASS/MISS marker overlapping checkpoint label
entries.plain: Fixed technical assessment PDF formatting so the PASS/MISS indicators no longer overlap with checkpoint labels.
entries.explanation:
The checkpoint marker cell had a fixed width of 12pt while the bold
PASS/MISS text is ~22pt wide, so it overflowed and overlapped the
label. Widen the marker to 26pt, top-align the row, and give the
label flex:1 so it wraps in the remaining width. Verified by
rendering the report PDF (rasterized page 1) - marker and label are
now clearly separated.

Also checks off the now-verified tech-sandbox tracker items in
PENDING-ACTIONS (migrations 00077-00079 applied, SANDBOX_DATABASE_URL
set, end-to-end delegate flow verified on production).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Tech sandbox: wire Resend result/link/code emails; fix admin 500
entries.plain: Technical assessment result emails and voucher redemption links now send correctly, and fixed a crash when admins email assessment links to candidates.
entries.explanation:
Email wiring:
- emailResults (with the PDF) fires from the submit route on a fresh
  completion via a new results-email helper (best-effort, never throws).
- Admin emailSandboxLinkAction ('Email to candidate' on the issue screen)
  and emailVoucherCodesAction ('Email N delegate(s)' on the generated batch
  + per-row 'Email' in the vouchers table) send one-click redeem links with
  code+name+email+company prefilled.
- Redeem page/form prefill name/email/company (not just code).

Admin 500 fix:
- /admin/tech-sandbox 500'd when migration 00079 was pending (missing
  technical_sandbox_vouchers.assigned_* columns) because the page loaders
  threw. Add isMissingSchemaError() and make listFunctions/loadBlocks/
  listVouchers tolerant of a pending migration (listVouchers retries
  without the assigned_* columns, then falls back to empty).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Technical sandbox: Resend email helpers (access link + results-with-PDF)
entries.plain: Techno assessment can now email candidates their access link and results with a PDF report using a reusable email system.
entries.explanation:
Reusable server-only email module (emailAccessLink, emailResults) on the shared
sendViaResend transport, ready to wire into submit + admin send actions now that
the viftraining.com domain is verified. Standalone module; not yet wired.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Technical assessment: framework showcase + one-page distribution + delegate import
entries.plain: Admins can now see all 9 skill domains and 62 functions in one place, preview a live assessment demo, and choose between sending direct links or voucher codes to candidates on the same page.
entries.explanation:
- Framework overview on /admin/tech-sandbox: all 9 domains and 62 functions,
  live ones (FP&A 1.7) expanded to pillars -> competencies with engine type, plus
  a 'Preview the sandbox' button (spins up a demo sitting, opens the runner) for
  client demos. Drill from breadth into the live one.
- Both distribution options now on the same page: Option 1 direct link per
  delegate, Option 2 voucher codes.
- Voucher delegate import (migration 00079 adds assigned_name/email): generate one
  NAMED single-use code per employee from a pasted 'Name, email' list or an
  uploaded CSV (Excel: copy columns or Save As CSV), plus the existing anonymous
  single-use and seat-pool modes. Output CSV = name,email,code,redeem_url ready to
  send. Existing-vouchers table shows the assignee.

tsc clean; npm run build succeeds. Requires applying 00079 to Supabase.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Repoint 'Technical Assessment' entry points to the new sandbox
entries.plain: The Technical Assessment menu now directs users to the new performance-based assessment system instead of the older version.
entries.explanation:
The MCQ runner (/ac/tech-assessment) and its admin pages are retired from the
live flow (Decision 5), but the entry links still pointed there. Repointed:
- Landing service tile -> /admin/tech-sandbox (+ description updated to the
  performance-based sandbox: live tasks, master-graded, banded, link or voucher).
- Portal sidebar Technical Assessment submenu -> Overview / Voucher codes /
  Model answers (new sandbox pages); added i18n keys techVouchers/techAnswers.
- Guided-start module menu + start resolver createRoute -> /admin/tech-sandbox.
Old /ac/tech-assessment + /admin/tech-assessment pages remain on disk (unlinked).
tsc clean; npm run build succeeds.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Technical sandbox: voucher system (client self-distribute) alongside direct links
entries.plain: Admins can now distribute assessments via voucher codes that candidates redeem themselves, alongside the existing direct-link option.
entries.explanation:
Two delegate-distribution options now available:
1. Direct link/email per delegate (existing): admin issues a token on /admin/tech-sandbox.
2. NEW voucher system (mirrors ARC): admin generates a batch of codes bound to one
   function + client - either single-use codes (one per delegate) or one shared
   seat-pool code. Client distributes them; each delegate self-redeems at the
   public /tech-sandbox/redeem (code + name + email + company), which atomically
   claims a seat and provisions a sitting, then drops them into the runner.

- Migration 00078: technical_sandbox_vouchers + redemptions + atomic
  technical_sandbox_voucher_claim / _release RPCs. Validated on scratch PG:
  seat exhaustion, expiry, disabled, and 20 concurrent claims on a 10-seat pool
  -> exactly 10 succeed; release floors at 0.
- vouchers.ts service (generate batch, redeem, list, enable/disable).
- Admin /admin/tech-sandbox/vouchers (generate, copy/CSV with redeem URLs, manage).
- Public /tech-sandbox/redeem (?code= prefill) -> /tech-sandbox/[token].

tsc clean; npm run build succeeds (both routes in the manifest).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Technical sandbox: printable PDF result report (download)
entries.plain: Candidates can now download a printable PDF report of their assessment results showing overall score, skill breakdown, and which checkpoints they passed or failed.
entries.explanation:
- tech-sandbox-report.tsx: React-PDF document (English-only, like the Fluent
  cert) with candidate/org header, overall score+band, per-pillar roll-up, and
  each skill block's score+band+framework with per-checkpoint PASS/MISS.
- getSessionReport(token): report-data loader for a submitted sitting.
- GET /api/tech-sandbox/[token]/report -> downloadable PDF (token-gated).
- 'Download PDF report' button on the results screen.

Independent of DNS. Email-on-completion will attach this same PDF once the Resend
domain is verified. tsc clean; npm run build succeeds.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Admin: Model Answers page (answer key, admin-only)
entries.plain: Admins now have a secure Model Answers page showing the correct answers, formulas, and grading criteria for each assessment question.
entries.explanation:
New /admin/tech-sandbox/answers (admin-guarded) shows, per active function and
skill block: model cell values + model formulas (spreadsheet), expected field
values (logic-input), the master SQL query, and the weighted checkpoints used to
grade/band. Linked from the Technical Sandbox admin page. getAnswerKey() loader
added to the service (server-only). tsc clean; build OK.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Results: show per-checkpoint pass/fail breakdown under each skill block
entries.plain: Candidates can now see exactly which individual checks they passed or failed for each skill block on their results screen.
entries.explanation:
Threads checkpointResults through scoring -> session score -> results screen so
the candidate/admin can see exactly which checks passed or failed (e.g. which
cell in the 3-statement model was off), bilingual labels. Doubles as the
development detail for the report. tsc clean; build OK.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Fix spreadsheet scoring 0: read via snapshot + register reader (refs don't survive next/dynamic)
entries.plain: Fixed a critical issue where spreadsheet answers scored zero even though candidates entered correct work - they now score correctly.
entries.explanation:
The 3-statement model computed correctly in-grid but scored 0 because the work
was never captured: next/dynamic does not forward React refs, so the runner's
sheetRef stayed null and readWork() never ran. Also Univer's FRange has no
getFormula().

- SpreadsheetEngine now exposes its reader via an onRegister(reader) callback
  prop instead of forwardRef/useImperativeHandle.
- readWork() reads from the workbook snapshot (getActiveWorkbook().save()),
  pulling both value (v) and formula (f) per editable cell reliably.
- Runner stores the reader in sheetReaderRef and keys the engine by block id so
  switching spreadsheet tasks remounts/re-registers cleanly.

tsc clean; npm run build succeeds.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Fix Univer blank grid: add defaultTheme + no-render diagnostic
entries.plain: Fixed the spreadsheet grid appearing blank by adding the missing design system theme and better error messages.
entries.explanation:
The grid rendered blank because createUniver was called without a theme, so the
design system produced no visible UI. Added theme: defaultTheme (from
@univerjs/presets). Also added a post-init check that surfaces a clear message if
the container still has no canvas after init, instead of a silent blank.

tsc clean; npm run build succeeds.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Fix Univer blank grid: add required name + sheetOrder; surface init errors
entries.plain: Fixed the spreadsheet grid appearing blank by adding required sheet configuration and now displays errors clearly if something goes wrong.
entries.explanation:
The spreadsheet rendered blank because createWorkbook was missing the required
name and sheetOrder fields, so Univer had no sheet to display. Added both
(sheetOrder: ['sheet1']). Also wrapped init in try/catch with a visible error
banner so any future load failure surfaces in-UI instead of a silent blank grid.

tsc clean; npm run build succeeds.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Technical sandbox: admin 'Test sandbox DB' health check
entries.plain: Admins can now run a one-click health check from the dashboard to verify the assessment database is connected and working.
entries.explanation:
One-click verification of SANDBOX_DATABASE_URL from /admin/tech-sandbox:
pingSandboxDb() opens a connection, does a temp-table insert/select/rollback
roundtrip and reports the server version, surfaced via an admin-guarded
checkSandboxDbAction and a button. Confirms the SQL sandbox wiring without
running a full assessment.

tsc clean; npm run build succeeds.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Technical sandbox: admin issue-assessment page + JD matcher
entries.plain: Admins can now issue assessments by picking a skill domain or pasting a job description to auto-match relevant skills, then generate a candidate link.
entries.explanation:
Admin flow to put the portal to work:
- /admin/tech-sandbox (admin-guarded): pick an active function or paste a JD to
  get a ranked, admin-confirmed shortlist, then create a token-accessed sitting
  and copy the candidate link.
- actions.ts: matchJdAction (keyword matcher over per-function descriptors) and
  createSandboxSessionAction (admin-gated session creation, invited_by stamped).
- service.ts: listFunctions(activeOnly) for the node-index picker and
  listFunctionDescriptors() for the JD matcher.

tsc clean; npm run build succeeds (admin + token routes in the manifest).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Technical sandbox: candidate runner (token flow) + engines + API
entries.plain: Candidates can now take the complete performance-based assessment with a timer, auto-saving, language toggle, and see their results broken down by skill.
entries.explanation:
End-to-end candidate experience for the performance-based assessment:
- /tech-sandbox/[token] page (server): loads session by token + public blueprint
  (answer key stripped) and renders the runner. Back affordance present (SOP).
- Runner: timed sitting across all active skill blocks, countdown + auto-submit
  on expiry, periodic + on-navigation autosave, bilingual EN/AR toggle (RTL),
  and a banded results view (overall + per-pillar roll-up + per-block bands).
- Engine components: logic-input (PVM), SQL editor (read-only), and the Univer
  (Apache-2.0) spreadsheet grid loaded client-only via next/dynamic with an
  imperative readWork() that extracts cell value + formula + array-formula flag.
- Token API routes: start / save (autosave) / submit (scores server-side,
  SQL via the sandboxed runner).
- Middleware: /tech-sandbox/* and /api/tech-sandbox/* bypass auth (token-gated),
  matching the ARA/Pre-Hire pattern.
- Deps: @univerjs/presets + preset-sheets-core, rxjs (peer), pg.

Gated: tsc --noEmit clean (0 errors) and 'npm run build' succeeds (route in the
manifest, Univer lazy-loaded). Runtime browser QA of the Univer grid pending.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Technical sandbox: server data-access + scoring service
entries.plain: The assessment system now securely stores candidate work, scores each skill block fairly, and keeps answer keys hidden from candidates.
entries.explanation:
Bridges the 00077 schema to the validators/scoring:
- getPublicBlueprint(): candidate-facing function -> pillars -> skill blocks,
  with master_solution and checkpoints STRIPPED (answer key never leaves server).
- createSession / getSessionByToken / startSession (stamps expiry from the sum of
  block time limits) / saveResponse (autosave upsert).
- submitSession(): scores every block from saved work (SQL blocks via the
  read-only runner), persists per-block score/band/checkpoint_results, rolls up to
  pillars + overall band, finalizes the sitting.

Project typechecks clean (tsc --noEmit, 0 errors).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Technical sandbox: read-only SQL runner with hash-match validation
entries.plain: The SQL assessment now runs safely in its own isolated database, prevents unsafe queries, and compares candidate results to the correct answer fairly.
entries.explanation:
Server-only SQL checkpoint executor for the FP&A 3.1 block, per Decision 12:
- Runs against a DEDICATED throwaway DB (SANDBOX_DATABASE_URL), never the app DB.
- isSafeSelect() guard: single SELECT/WITH only, no statement chaining, no
  DDL/DML keywords.
- Trusted schema+seed (rewritten to TEMP) and the candidate + master queries run
  in ONE always-rolled-back transaction with a 5s statement timeout.
- Canonical row hashing (sorted keys; rows sorted unless ordered=true) compares
  candidate vs master result sets.
- Adds pg@^8 dependency.

Verified on scratch Postgres: correct query matches master (4 rows); wrong
ORDER BY and count-vs-sum both fail; DROP TABLE and chained statements rejected
by the guard.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Technical sandbox: validators, scoring/banding, and pluggable JD matcher
entries.plain: Technical sandbox now correctly scores each skill block and produces an overall proficiency band, with a searchable job-description matcher for admins.
entries.explanation:
Core logic layer (pure, framework-free):
- validators.ts: weighted-checkpoint scoring per skill block. cell_value,
  logic_value, is_array_formula (genuine {=TABLE()}/array vs hardcoded), and
  sql_result_match (fed by the server-side SQL runner). Banded 60/85.
- scoring.ts: roll block scores up to pillars (counts by tier, no band) and to
  an overall function band, per Decision 6.
- jd-matcher.ts: pluggable JdMatcher interface + keyword-clustering matcher;
  returns a ranked shortlist for admin confirmation. Vector/hybrid drops in
  behind the same interface later.
- types.ts: engine payload / work / checkpoint / result types mirroring 00077.

Verified against the real FP&A seed: correct work -> 100% Advanced across all
four engines; broken balance check -> 70% Intermediate (delta fails); hardcoded
sensitivity cheat -> 50% Basic (array-formula check fails); empty -> Basic; JD
'3-statement model, forecasting, EBITDA, WACC' ranks FP&A 1.7 top at 0.875.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Technical sandbox portal: schema + 9-domain/62-function node index + FP&A 1.7 seed
entries.plain: Technical assessment now organizes 62 finance functions into 9 domains with a proper skill hierarchy, replacing the old multiple-choice module.
entries.explanation:
Performance-based replacement for the MCQ technical module per the Technical
Assessment Portal Master Blueprint. Model: Domain -> Function -> Pillar ->
Skill Block (the banded assessed unit, sandbox-delivered + checkpoint-scored).

- Migration 00077: 9 blueprint domains upserted; technical_functions extended
  with node_id / domain_key / node_status / keywords / descriptor and seeded
  with all 62 blueprint functions (FP&A 1.7 active, rest inactive lazy-load
  nodes). New tables: technical_pillars, technical_skill_blocks (engine_type +
  jsonb engine_config/master_solution/checkpoints + time limit), and the
  token-accessed technical_sandbox_sessions / technical_sandbox_responses.
- FP&A 1.7 fully seeded: 3 pillars, 4 skill blocks (spreadsheet 3-statement
  model with balance check, advanced spreadsheet sensitivity matrix, logic-input
  PVM variance, read-only SQL query) with internally-consistent master answers
  and weighted checkpoints.
- docs/technical-sandbox-portal.md: locked decisions + data model + build order.

Validated on a scratch Postgres 16: all 80 migrations apply cleanly; node index
counts correct; FP&A blocks/checkpoints present; SQL master query executes in a
rolled-back tx and returns the expected ordered totals.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): AP competency framework v2 - 6 categories / 19 competencies / 36 skills
entries.plain: Accounts Payable assessments now measure 19 competencies across 6 categories using professional industry standards, with full English and Arabic support.
entries.explanation:
Supersedes the v1 seed. Adds category_en/ar + reference columns to
technical_competencies; reseeds Accounts Payable as 6 categories, 19 competencies
(each with an authoritative reference: IOFM/COSO/ACFE/APQC/AICPA-CIMA/IFRS-GAAP),
36 bilingual sub-component skills, with the function blueprint aligned for roll-up.
Validated on scratch Postgres (19/6/36, 0 alignment gaps). Docs updated. Apply 00076.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: docs(tech): draft professional Accounts Payable competency framework (P2.3)
entries.plain: Created a professional Accounts Payable framework with six competencies and clear proficiency levels, awaiting final approval before use.
entries.explanation:
Function (job, not banded) -> Competency (assessed, banded Basic/Int/Adv) -> Skill
(measured by questions). 6 AP competencies with skills + Basic/Intermediate/Advanced
descriptors, scoring/banding, JD-customisation path, and references (APQC/IOFM/IMA).
Awaiting approval before seeding + question mapping.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: fix(tech): band per competency only, not domain/function (P2.3 model fix)
entries.plain: Fixed the assessment model to measure competencies only, not domains or functions, so results now show accurate proficiency levels.
entries.explanation:
Per correction: Domain (Finance) and Function (Accounts Payable) are groupings,
not assessed units - the competency is the assessed unit, measured by its skills.
Removed the incorrect overall function/domain Basic/Intermediate/Advanced band;
kept the per-competency banding. Tracker notes the competency-data dependency.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): 3-band proficiency (Basic/Intermediate/Advanced) breakdown on results (P2.3)
entries.plain: Results pages now show proficiency levels - Basic, Intermediate, or Advanced - broken down by competency for clearer feedback.
entries.explanation:
Adds proficiencyTier (Basic <60 / Intermediate 60-84 / Advanced >=85), an
additive layer over the existing 1-5 model. The technical results screen now
shows the overall band and a per-competency Basic/Intermediate/Advanced
breakdown (built on the competency tier from migration 00074). Bilingual labels.
PDF breakdown report to follow.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): wire competency tier into loaders, results, admin (Phase 2.2)
entries.plain: Competency proficiency levels are now visible throughout the system - in test reports, results pages, and admin dashboards.
entries.explanation:
Make the 00074 competency tier visible, additively + tolerantly (every
surface falls back to flat skills when a function has no competencies).

- Backbone: LocalizedTechFunction gains a competencies[] field; the
  function loaders (listTechnicalFunctions / getTechnicalFunctionByRef)
  batch-load technical_competencies + technical_competency_skills and
  attach them. Code-side fallback + un-seeded functions stay empty.
- Results (runner): new "By competency" breakdown above "By skill",
  aggregating the per-skill result into per-competency rows via a pure,
  server-free helper (aggregateByCompetency, with an "Other" bucket so
  totals reconcile). Renders only when the function has competencies.
- Admin: function detail page shows a read-only competency panel
  (competency name + its skill chips).
- EN/AR labels: tech.take.byCompetency, techFn.competencies.

Deferred (need runtime verification against the populated DB): JD-extractor
emitting a competency grouping + the admin write-path editor, and grouping
the runner picker chips by competency (entangled with mix & match).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: feat(tech): chunked AI generation, no silent placeholder fallback
entries.plain: Technical test generation now works reliably instead of silently using a default template, and gives clear error messages if it fails.
entries.explanation:
Generate function assessments with one small model call per skill
instead of one giant call per blueprint - the whole-test request
overran its output budget and proxy timeouts, silently falling back
to the placeholder deck. Generation failures now throw
TechGenerationError, which the route maps to an explicit 503; in
production the placeholder deck is never administered.

Also pluralize functionsCount/skillsCount picker labels via i18next
count keys (EN one/other, full Arabic plural forms).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): add Corporate Finance function to the Finance competency
entries.plain: Corporate Finance is now a proper assessment function so core finance skills like budgeting and valuation are available to select.
entries.explanation:
Finance held only FP&A + Management Accounting, and the core corporate-finance
skills (Capital Budgeting, Cost of Capital/WACC, Capital Structure, Working
Capital Management, Investment Appraisal, Financial Statement Analysis) lived
only in the now-retired broad-domain screener — orphaned from the function/
skill model. This seeds them as a proper Finance function so the competency
anchors a real area and those skills are pickable in the runner and mix & match.

- Migration 00073 seeds the standard 'corporate_finance' function (bilingual,
  ON CONFLICT (key) DO NOTHING)
- Mirrored in STANDARD_FUNCTIONS so the code fallback matches before the
  migration is applied
- Finance competency now has 3 functions (FP&A, Management Accounting,
  Corporate Finance)

Verified: tsc clean; runner assembles a 6-skill Corporate Finance test
covering exactly the six skills.

https://claude.ai/code/session_014ceigJNgNM4AtMUoWSPZRU

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): remove the broad-domain screener from the runner picker
entries.plain: Removed the outdated generic domain picker from the test runner so candidates only see the modern function and skill selection.
entries.explanation:
The legacy 'Or assess a broad domain instead' section offered the old
pick-a-domain → 8-generic-questions path alongside the function/skill model.
It's retired from the public runner: only the competency → function → skill
picker and the mix & match basket remain.

- Drops the secondary broad-domain card and the showDomains state from the
  runner client
- Keeps the 'domain' run code path intact (still used by org-assigned
  ?domain= locked URLs and for result-name mapping), so nothing breaks —
  only the public entry point is gone

Verified: tsc clean, runner compiles and serves 200.

https://claude.ai/code/session_014ceigJNgNM4AtMUoWSPZRU

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): skill-level mix & match — pick individual skills per function in the runner
entries.plain: Candidates can now pick and choose individual skills within a function instead of assessing whole functions only.
entries.explanation:
The combined-assessment basket previously added WHOLE functions. The picker
is now a proper menu process: open a competency → tap a function → its
skills appear → tick one, some, or all → move on to the next function or
competency → start one combined assessment over exactly the picked skills.

Runner:
- Function cards expand in place to a per-skill checklist (expanded card
  spans the grid row); 'Select all / Unselect all' shortcut and an
  'Assess this full function only' quick start (keeps the adaptive path)
- Collapsed cards badge how many of their skills are picked
- Basket bar lists contributing functions as removable chips with per-
  function skill counts, plus the picked skill names; start enables at 3+
  skills

API:
- action 'start' now accepts skills[] alongside functionKeys[]; the merged
  blueprint narrows to that selection, and only canonical blueprint skills
  are accepted (no arbitrary skill injection into assembly/generation)
- A single function with a skill subset is a valid combined run; the
  composite name/key only credit functions that actually contribute skills

Bilingual EN/AR labels. Verified: tsc clean, locale JSON valid; a 3-function
cross-competency pick of 5 skills returns one test covering exactly those
skills, and injected non-blueprint skills are rejected.

https://claude.ai/code/session_014ceigJNgNM4AtMUoWSPZRU

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): mix & match combined assessment in the front-end runner
entries.plain: Test takers can now combine multiple functions into one assessment - useful for roles spanning accounts payable, reporting, and other areas.
entries.explanation:
The runner (/ac/tech-assessment) previously only assessed one function per
sitting; composing a multi-function role was admin-only. Demos need the whole
client process on the front end, so the picker now supports ad-hoc combining:

- Every function card gets a +/✓ toggle; picks persist across competency
  drill-downs, so you can mix e.g. Accounts Receivable + Financial Reporting
  + a Data Analytics function
- A basket bar shows the picked functions and starts ONE combined sitting
  over their merged (deduped) skill blueprints — nothing persisted to the
  function library
- API: action 'start' accepts functionKeys[] (2+ refs); blueprints merge,
  per-skill draw scales down (~24-item cap), certified assembly is preferred
  when every merged skill clears the approved floor (skill-keyed banks are
  reused), else indicative AI; sessions/results bind to a composite
  'mix:<refs>' function_key (plain text column, no schema change)
- Bilingual EN/AR labels

Verified: tsc clean, locale JSON valid, combined AR + Financial Reporting run
returns one test covering all 12 merged skills with the composite title.

https://claude.ai/code/session_014ceigJNgNM4AtMUoWSPZRU

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): mix & match composer — build a custom role assessment from multiple functions
entries.plain: Admins can now build custom assessments mixing multiple finance functions and remove unwanted skills before assigning to candidates.
entries.explanation:
Clients often need one assessment for a role that spans several functions
(e.g. Accounts Receivable + Financial Reporting, or finance mixed with data
analytics). The admin Functions page now has a 'Compose a custom role' card:

- Pick any number of functions across all competencies (chip toggles,
  grouped by competency)
- Their skill blueprints merge into one deduped combined blueprint;
  skills shared by several functions are merged once and badged
- Untick any skill the client doesn't need; name + category auto-suggest
  from the picks and stay editable
- Saves through the existing createTechnicalFunctionAction as a custom
  function — instantly runnable via the runner link, and certifiable
  because item banks are keyed by skill, so approved pools for the
  underlying skills are reused automatically

Also generalizes the library badge from 'Custom (JD)' to 'Custom' since
composed functions share source='jd' without being JD-derived. Bilingual
(EN + AR) labels included. No schema change needed.

https://claude.ai/code/session_014ceigJNgNM4AtMUoWSPZRU

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): two-level competency picker (drill-down navigation)
entries.plain: The function picker now uses a simple two-step drill-down menu instead of one long scrolling list, in English and Arabic.
entries.explanation:
The runner picker listed every competency with all its functions on one long
scrolling screen. Now it's a drill-down: level 1 shows the 9 competency cards
(with a function count + chevron); selecting one opens level 2 with just that
competency's functions and a 'back to competencies' link. Bilingual EN/AR.

Build green; tsc clean.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: fix(tech): move competency taxonomy to a server-free module (fixes prod build)
entries.plain: Fixed a system build error caused by importing server code into the front-end, allowing production deployment to succeed.
entries.explanation:
The client picker components imported categoryRank from technical-function.ts,
which transitively imports supabase/server (next/headers) — illegal in client
components and broke 'next build' (the Render deploy failure):
  'You're importing a component that needs next/headers ... not supported'

Split the pure category constants/helpers (TECH_FUNCTION_CATEGORIES,
CATEGORY_ORDER, categoryRank, categoryLabel) into technical-categories.ts with
NO server imports. technical-function.ts re-exports them for server callers; the
runner + admin functions client now import categoryRank from the pure module.

Verified: 'npm run build' compiles cleanly with env present (full route table,
zero errors). tsc clean.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): one-click 'Draft all skills' button in the cert workbench
entries.plain: Techno certification authors can now draft all test questions for a skill group with one click instead of doing each skill individually.
entries.explanation:
Replaces the skill-by-skill drafting grind with a single button per function:
draftAllFunctionSkillItemsAction loops the function's skills server-side,
AI-drafts items for any skill still short of the target (idempotent — counts
existing draft/in_review/approved first), and reports how many landed.

- Runs in production where ANTHROPIC_API_KEY already lives, so the non-technical
  path is: open a function's cert page -> click 'Draft all skills' -> approve.
  No CLI, no .env, no terminal.
- Nothing auto-approved (hallucination guard intact). Button disabled when AI off.
- Bilingual EN/AR strings added.

tsc + JSON valid.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): expand technical functions to 9 competencies + 'Select a competency' picker
entries.plain: Technical assessments now organize jobs and skills into 9 major competency areas like Finance, Accounting, and Data Analytics for easier navigation.
entries.explanation:
Reframes the technical-assessment function library around 9 top-level
COMPETENCIES the runner now groups by, with functions/job-roles underneath:
Finance · Accounting · Banking · Investment · Treasury · Data Analytics ·
Business Intelligence · Artificial Intelligence · Human Resources.

- technical-function.ts: recategorize the 12 seeded functions onto competencies
  (reporting/tax/audit fold into Accounting; FP&A + Management Accounting become
  Finance) and add 35 new standard functions (Banking 6, Investment 6, Data
  Analytics 5, BI 5, AI 6, HR 7), each bilingual with 5-6 skills. New
  CATEGORY_ORDER + categoryRank() drive competency display order; CATEGORY_LABELS
  cover the 9 competencies (legacy labels retained for old JD-derived functions).
- migration 00060: recategorize existing rows + seed the 35 new functions
  (idempotent, ON CONFLICT (key) DO NOTHING).
- runner + admin functions library: group competency buckets in CATEGORY_ORDER.
- i18n (en/ar): picker title 'Choose your function' -> 'Select a competency',
  intro reworded to 'choose your competency area, then the function within it'.

Arabic strings are machine-generated and flagged for the native-speaker review
already on the production checklist. tsc passes clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add live adaptive (CAT) technical assessment runner
entries.plain: Technical assessments now adapt questions to each candidate's ability level, making tests shorter and more personalized while maintaining accuracy.
entries.explanation:
Wires the calibrated CAT engine into a real turn-based sitting. An
adaptive-ready function (calibrated approved pool >= ADAPTIVE_MIN_POOL) is
served one item at a time, selected by maximum Rasch information at the
running ability estimate; the run stops once the standard error hits target
(or the min/max-item bounds), yielding a theta-based proficiency + confidence
band. Shorter + ability-matched vs the fixed form.

- New /api/ac/tech-assessment/adaptive: start (build pool, hold key
  server-side, serve max-info first item) + answer (grade, re-estimate theta
  via estimateThetaRasch, SE via thetaStandardError, select next or finish).
  INDICATIVE (no credential) - the certified credential stays on the
  coverage-guaranteed fixed-form path. Session consumed only on finish.
- technical-function-bank.ts: buildAdaptivePool + adaptiveReadyRefs (one-query
  picker flag) + calibratedTotal/adaptiveReady on functionBankReadiness.
- Runner: auto-routes adaptive-ready functions to the turn-based flow with an
  "Adaptive" badge, one-item-at-a-time UI, progress + narrowing-band indicator.

Verified live: AP flagged ready -> 16 distinct items served -> SE narrowed
7.07 -> 0.62 -> theta-banded indicative result, no key leak, persisted.
No migration. tsc + next lint + EN/AR parity (3757) clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add confidence band + adaptive CAT/IRT to tech assessment
entries.plain: Test results now show a confidence range alongside the score, helping you understand whether the result is very reliable or less certain.
entries.explanation:
Phase-3 strand #2 (the last of the three).

Confidence band (ships on every result):
- src/lib/scoring/tech-reliability.ts: technicalConfidenceBand combines the
  binomial SE of the score with cross-skill disagreement + a small-sample
  inflation term, mapped onto the 1-5 scale via the same cut-points as the
  point estimate. Surfaces an indicative range (e.g. "Foundational-Expert")
  + an underpowered flag in the runner. Classical, so it works on every run
  (certified or indicative) with no calibration. Verified live: a 67% AP run
  -> Level 3, band 2-5, underpowered.

Adaptive CAT/IRT (calibrated groundwork, mirrors Fluent):
- Reuses the generic Rasch engine (src/lib/scoring/irt.ts) verbatim;
  src/lib/scoring/tech-cat.ts adds the difficulty prior, theta->proficiency
  band, and a maximum-information simulation. Verified: against an 80-item
  calibrated bank the adaptive flow reaches SE<=0.5 in 18 items while a
  same-size non-adaptive spread form misses it (SE 0.597).
- Migration 00060: irt_b / irt_se / calibrated_at on tech_assessment_items.
  calibrateFunctionBank() + a "Calibrate" button on the cert workbench +
  scripts/tech-calibrate-items.ts write each item's Rasch difficulty (from
  the p-value substrate, else the difficulty prior). Verified live: the
  calibrator writes irt_b once 00060 is applied.

tsc + next lint + EN/AR parity (3751) clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add item types + certified function banks to tech assessment
entries.plain: Technical assessments now include multiple question types and cognitive difficulty levels to better measure real job-relevant skills.
entries.explanation:
Two Phase-3 strands on the function-based technical assessment.

Richer item types (indicative path):
- Items are now single / multi-select / scenario, each tagged with a Bloom
  cognitive level (recall / apply / analyze). The function generator prompts
  for + parses the mix (~50/25/25); a generalized shuffle carries a correct
  SET; scoring grades multi all-or-nothing; the runner renders checkboxes,
  scenario context, and cognitive badges. Answer key (incl. correct_indices)
  is stripped server-side. Verified live: 24-item AP run = 12/6/6, all
  cognitive-tagged, keyLeak 0, scored 6x4 per skill.

Certified function banks (makes a function credential-eligible):
- Migration 00059: tech_assessment_items.domain_key nullable (function-skill
  items keyed by skill alone) + technical_function_cut_scores (per-function
  pass_pct + min_items_per_skill floor).
- Engine (technical-function-bank.ts): functionBankReadiness,
  getFunctionCutScore, buildCertifiedFunctionTest (assemble from approved
  per-skill items; null -> indicative), draftFunctionSkillItems. One approved
  pool per skill serves every function that lists it (standard or JD-derived).
- Route: function start tries the certified path first; a passing certified
  run issues a technical_proficiency credential titled by the function.
- Admin workbench at /admin/tech-assessment/functions/[ref]: per-skill
  readiness, AI-draft per skill, approve/reject/retire, cut-score editor.

Verified end-to-end on the live DB: approved AP bank -> certifiable ->
certified 12-item run (ai_generated false, key stripped) -> pass issues a
verifiable "VIFM Technical Proficiency - Accounts Payable" credential.
tsc + next lint + EN/AR parity (3746) clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add function-based technical assessment (library + JD import)
entries.plain: Technical assessments now test job-specific skills like Accounts Payable or Treasury instead of generic broad domain knowledge.
entries.explanation:
Technical competency is role/function-specific, not domain-wide, so the
assessable unit is now the FUNCTION (Accounts Payable, Treasury, Internal
Audit…) — a blueprint of the technical skills it requires — replacing the
old "pick a broad domain -> 8 generic questions".

Phase 1 — standard library + blueprinted assessment:
- Migration 00058: technical_functions (12 bilingual standard functions),
  programs.function_id, function_id/function_key on results+sessions,
  domain_key made nullable.
- generateFunctionAssessment(): ~4 items per blueprint skill (deep,
  per-skill), answer key held server-side, options shuffled.
- Runner leads with a function picker (grouped by category, EN/AR) and a
  per-skill result. Programmes scope to one function with a per-skill team
  rollup + function cohort PDF; tiers collapsed to function-team.

Phase 2 — JD import -> custom function:
- jd-technical-extractor: read a JD (text or PDF), match the shared skill
  library (reuse) and propose new skills; suggest name + category.
- /admin/tech-assessment/functions: import -> review -> create a custom
  (source=jd) bilingual function that flows into the runner + programmes.

Verified end-to-end on the live DB (deep AP/Payroll runs, session-path key
stripping, function-bound persistence, cohort PDF, Arabic RTL, JD->custom
function). tsc + next lint + EN/AR locale parity clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add standalone (ARA-style) technical certification programs
entries.plain: Organizations can now offer technical certification as a standalone program independent of talent-assessment engagements.
entries.explanation:
Sells technical certification on its own, independent of an AC engagement —
mirroring how ARA is packaged. Migration 00057 adds technical_programs (org +
department/division/enterprise tier), technical_program_domains, token-based
technical_program_participants, and program_id/participant_id on
tech_assessment_results/sessions.

- Admin UI at /admin/tech-assessment/programs: create a program (org + tier),
  pick in-scope domains (Certifiable/Indicative from bank readiness), add
  participants (each gets a token link), track per-participant per-domain
  status, and download a bilingual cohort PDF (reuses renderTechCohortHtml).
- Runner resolves ?token= to a participant: banner, locked domain, and threads
  programId/participantId/takerName through the API so the sitting binds to the
  program and a passing certified run credentials the participant.
- Sidebar "Programs" entry; bilingual (techProg namespace).

Verified end-to-end: create program → add participant → token link → Arabic
test → result binds to program/participant → cohort PDF (185KB). tsc + lint
clean; test data cleaned up.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Surface certified technical domains on the candidate report
entries.plain: Candidate reports now display their earned technical certifications with domain names and proficiency levels on the summary page.
entries.explanation:
The report data fetcher now loads a candidate's certified technical domains
(certified + passed-cut tech_assessment_results, via the service client since
that table is admin-only), with both EN/AR domain names. Both renderers show a
"Technical Certifications" block on the summary page — indigo badges reading
"<Domain> · Level N/5" (English React-PDF) and "<المجال> · المستوى N/5" (Arabic
HTML). Optional + non-fatal: omitted when the candidate holds no technical
credentials. Verified the fetcher query returns the bilingual cert data.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add technical certification cohort PDF
entries.plain: Managers can now download a bilingual cohort report showing which candidates passed technical certification by domain.
entries.explanation:
A "Cohort PDF" button on the engagement Technical Certification panel generates
a bilingual (EN/AR, RTL) report via Puppeteer: per-domain pass/cert rates with a
Certifiable/Indicative badge, and a certified roster (candidate · domain · level
· credential code). Reuses getEngagementTechProgram for data and the bundled-
Chromium harness from the ARA reports; the self-contained HTML template embeds
the Cairo Arabic web font. Admin-gated at /api/admin/engagements/[id]/technical-
cohort/pdf?lang=en|ar. Verified: valid 187KB PDF generated.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Guarantee bilingual tech bank so the certified path is always Arabic
entries.plain: Arabic-language candidates now see fully translated technical assessment questions instead of falling back to English.
entries.explanation:
Closes the caveat where an English-only approved item fell back to English on
an Arabic certified test. Three changes: (1) the AI draft flow now repairs any
item the model returned without valid Arabic before insert, so the bank is never
seeded English-only; (2) a backfillBankArabic() translator fills missing
question_ar/options_ar on any existing rows (legacy / manual / import),
preserving option order; (3) a "Fill missing Arabic" button in the SME console
runs it per domain. Verified end-to-end: an English-only item is translated to
Arabic (same option order, correct_index intact).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Serve technical-assessment test content in the taker's language
entries.plain: Technical assessment test questions now display in the candidate's chosen language instead of staying in English.
entries.explanation:
The chrome was translated but the items themselves stayed English, so an Arabic
taker couldn't read the test. Thread a `language` param from the runner through
the API to both builders: the certified path now serves question_ar/options_ar
(per-item English fallback so partially-translated banks don't mix languages),
and the indicative AI generator writes questions + options in Modern Standard
Arabic (keeping finance acronyms + the English skill tag). ui_language is stored
on the session + result instead of being hardcoded "en". Also localizes the
domain name in the test + result headers. Verified: Arabic items render.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add engagement-level technical certification program (paid org layer)
entries.plain: Engagement managers can now assign technical certification assessments to their cohort and track results by domain for each candidate.
entries.explanation:
An AC engagement can now run a technical certification program for its cohort.
Migration 00056 adds engagement_technical_domains (which domains are in scope).
A "Technical Certification" panel on the engagement detail lets an admin pick
in-scope domains (each flagged Certifiable vs Indicative from bank readiness),
see every candidate's latest result per domain (level / certified + verify link
/ below-cut), and copy a per-candidate per-domain assignment link into the
runner. Results + credentials already bind via candidate_id/engagement_id, so
a passing certified sitting issues the verifiable technical_proficiency
credential to that staff member. Bilingual (engTech namespace). Loader reads
admin-only data via the service client; tolerant of the table being absent.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Make the technical runner assignment-aware (bind to candidate/engagement)
entries.plain: Technical assessment runners now know which candidate and engagement they are testing for, binding results correctly to the right person.
entries.explanation:
The DB + API already accept candidate_id/engagement_id, but the client never
sent them, so run-for-candidate was a no-op for technical. The runner now reads
?candidateId/?engagementId/?domainKey, shows a "for {name}" banner, forwards the
ids in the start+score calls so results (and any earned credential) bind to the
candidate, and — when a domain is passed — auto-starts it and hides the picker.
Anonymous self-serve is unchanged when no params are present. Foundation for the
engagement-level technical certification layer.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Localize tech-assessment domain + skill names from the bilingual taxonomy
entries.plain: Arabic-language candidates now see job skills and domain names translated into Arabic instead of in English.
entries.explanation:
The runner imported the English-only code framework, so domain and skill names
stayed English on the Arabic runner. Migration 00055 adds technical_skills.name_ar
(50 skill translations; technical_domains already had name_ar). A new server-side
loader reads both bilingual tables via the service client (the runner is public,
the tables are RLS auth-only) and passes localized domains + an English→localized
skill-label map to the client. The picker, per-question skill badges, and the
per-skill result breakdown now follow the locale. Tolerant: falls back to the
English code framework when the tables/columns aren't present. Verified
end-to-end (start→answer→submit→result) in Arabic.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix tech-assessment crash: handle nested session test shape
entries.plain: Fixed a crash that prevented candidates from taking technical assessments on recently migrated systems.
entries.explanation:
The start endpoint returns the test nested as { session_id, test } when the
sessions table exists (the normal Tier-2 path on a migrated DB), but flat when
it doesn't. The client only handled the flat shape, so on a migrated DB
`test.items` was undefined and `allAnswered` threw "Cannot read properties of
undefined (reading 'length')" on render. Normalize both shapes (raw.test ?? raw),
refuse to enter the test phase on a non-OK/empty response, and guard the
allAnswered check with Array.isArray. Verified start→answer→submit→result
end-to-end with no crash.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Translate Technical Assessment module to Arabic (RTL)
entries.plain: Technical Assessment now works in Arabic with right-to-left text, making it accessible to Arabic-speaking candidates.
entries.explanation:
Adds the tech i18n namespace (128 bilingual keys) and converts all six
Technical Assessment surfaces — command dashboard, SME item-review console,
bridge editor, runner page, and runner client — to react-i18next. Fluent is
explicitly excluded from locale-aware routing so the English placement test
stays English/LTR.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Technical Assessment Command dashboard + parallel nav group
entries.plain: Technical Assessment gets its own dashboard in the admin area, showing the certification pipeline and team readiness at a glance.
entries.explanation:
Make Technical Assessment a parallel of the behavioural Assessment Center
in the sidebar: a group with Overview (new command dashboard) · Take
assessment (runner) · Item review (the SME console, moved to
/admin/tech-assessment/items).

The new /admin/tech-assessment dashboard reuses the AC's ProcessMap to
show the certification pipeline as a cycle — Item bank → SME review →
Cut-scores → Certifiable → Assessed → Credentials — with a readiness hub %
and a per-domain readiness+throughput table, powered by
getTechPipelineStats().

Each pipeline node deep-links to its section in the console (#draft /
#items / #cutscores / #readiness). A small HashScroll client helper does
the scrolling, since the admin content scrolls an inner overflow-auto
container rather than the window; targets carry scroll-mt-24.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add admin editor for the technical bridge + fix Badge nesting
entries.plain: Admins can now map technical skills to behavioural competencies with weighted importance levels, and a display bug has been fixed.
entries.explanation:
The /admin/tech-assessment console gains a per-domain "Behavioural bridge" card: add/remove competency mappings, set each weight (1 Supporting / 2 Strong / 3 Primary), and edit domain display names (the FK key stays immutable). Backed by admin-gated server actions (addBridge upserts on domain+competency, setBridgeWeight, removeBridge, updateDomainMeta) and service-role data helpers. Also fixes an invalid-HTML hydration error: a Badge (a div) nested inside a

in ReviewConsole, and the same pattern inside a

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Technical service tile + stronger landing headline
entries.plain: Technical Assessment is now featured as a sixth assessment tile on the platform homepage, making it easy for users to discover.
entries.explanation:
Hero headline becomes "Build the talent the future demands." A sixth
"Technical Assessment" tile (indigo tone-indigo, BadgeCheck) joins All
Services, linking to /ac/tech-assessment — giving the assessment runner a
discoverable home. Subhead count Five→Six (EN+AR).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add technical competency framework + assessment (third pillar)
entries.plain: Technical Assessment now grades candidates on real skills across finance and management domains, with answers kept secure on the server.
entries.explanation:
Introduce the third capability framework beside behavioural (AC) and
language (Fluent): technical proficiency across 10 finance & management
domains. A curated Domain->Skill taxonomy (technical-framework.ts), a
server-graded AI assessment (00052: sessions hold the answer key
server-side; results persist), and a runner at /ac/tech-assessment.

Integrity: the answer key never reaches the browser, grading is
server-side, and option order is randomized after generation so the
correct answer isn't always position A (LLM bias) — the test is not
gameable by picking one position. Indicative only; no credential is
issued until items are human-calibrated.

Folds onto the golden thread: a Technical Capabilities family on the
unified profile (unified-profile.ts) from assessment results (leveled
1-5) + Academy completions (evidence), surfaced on the candidate skills
page. All paths tolerant of 00052 not being applied.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent audit fixes: cohort + calibration IDOR (critical) + retake, retention, reports
entries.plain: English placement now prevents unauthorized access to test-taker names, emails, and scores - staff can only see their own organization's data.
entries.explanation:
Adversarial audit of the English-placement module (11 findings). All app-layer,
no migration.

Critical / security
- Cohort page had no staff gate and no org scoping - a service-role query
  returned every org's taker names, emails, and CEFR scores to any signed-in
  user. Now gated to staff; a client_manager is scoped to their own org
  (mirrors the report/certificate routes).
- Calibration page (QA console) likewise exposed cross-org names + verbatim
  writing responses + speaking transcripts - now staff-gated.
- submitHumanRating server action had no role check - now requireRole(staff),
  so a non-staff caller can't corrupt the calibration data.

Major
- Voucher link allowed unlimited retakes (a new result + CEFR credential each
  time). The take page and the score route now refuse once the redemption has
  a result - one seat, one placement.
- Retention purge now anonymises voucher-redeemer PII and sweeps expired
  sessions (keyed test + start IP), so no PII outlives the 2-year rule.
- Arabic taker name no longer prints as tofu: the report falls back to the
  email; the certificate routes to the Arabic (Puppeteer) renderer when the
  name is Arabic even on an EN request.

Minor
- Reliability band no longer counts writing when it wasn't administered
  (partial/Pre-Hire runs).
- Certificate relabels "Verification ID" -> "Result reference" (the printed id
  is the result row, not the publicly-verifiable credential code).
- Drop the IELTS/TOEFL equivalence claim from the hero (EN + AR).
- Bilingual framing on the token-delegate landing hero.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent review fixes: IELTS copy, word-target labels, coaching feedback
entries.plain: Fluent English test labels and coaching feedback have been refined for clarity.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Integrity pass: option shuffling, AI-likeness signal, org proctoring policy
