entries.plain: Fluent now shuffles answer options and checks for AI use, with clearer organisational proctor policies.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Report footers: reserve page space so notes never overlap content
entries.plain: Report footers now have proper spacing so notes no longer overlap the content above them.
entries.explanation: The Leadership Report's fixed footer is a ~3-line paragraph occupying ~66pt above the page edge, but the page only reserved 48pt - content rendered THROUGH the note (seen on section 3 pills). paddingBottom raised to 80. Swept every React-PDF report for the same defect: Fluent's single-line footer wraps to 2 lines at its width, sitting exactly at its 48pt reserve - raised to 58. All other reports have single-row footers with adequate clearance. Verified by rasterising the rendered PDF: footer now sits alone below the content.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent redeem: English-only (no EN/AR toggle); add bilingual flag to shared form
entries.plain: Fluent redemption page is now English-only, simplifying the user experience.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent speaking: never force typing on a working mic; keep listening
entries.plain: Fluent speaking test now stays listening through pauses instead of dropping candidates into typing mode unexpectedly.
entries.explanation:
Hardens the browser STT so the candidate is never dropped into the type
fallback while the mic works:
- Only an explicit "not-allowed" (permission denied) ends recognition. The
  previously-fatal "service-not-allowed" / "audio-capture" codes (which can
  fire transiently in the gap while a session restarts) are now recoverable -
  we just restart and keep listening.
- Restart through every silence/pause; only sustained pure silence or a dead-
  service loop (>=12 empty sessions) ends it, and then it delivers whatever was
  captured and stays in record mode (a gentle "tap Record again" note), never
  type-mode.
- Add a manual "Type instead" link in record mode so typing is always a choice,
  never forced.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent: keep mic listening through pauses; explain integrity score
entries.plain: Fluent speaking keeps the microphone active through thinking pauses, and integrity scores are now explained on results and reports.
entries.explanation:
- Speaking: browser STT now auto-restarts through silences until the
  candidate presses Stop, accumulating the transcript across restarts -
  a thinking pause no longer cuts them off or forces type-mode. Only a
  genuinely blocked mic / dead service ends it; transient hiccups show a
  gentle retry note and stay in record mode. Clearer EN/AR hints.
- Integrity signal: add a "How to read this signal" interpretation on the
  result view and the PDF report (lower is better; not a grade out of 100;
  bands Clean 0-14 / Minor 15-44 / Elevated 45-100; which band applies).
- Fix a pre-existing report crash (NaN% bar width) when a criterion is
  un-scored, which 500'd the whole PDF.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix Fluent integrity-signal crash on legacy rows
entries.plain: A crash affecting older Fluent test records on the cohort page and PDF reports has been fixed.
entries.explanation:
The cohort page and Full Report PDF trusted the persisted
integrity_flags.signal, but rows written before the signal gained a
reasons[] array stored an older shape. Reading sig.reasons.join /
.map on those rows threw, 500ing /ac/fluent/cohort and any affected
candidate's report PDF. Normalize at read time: use the stored signal
only when reasons[] is well-formed, else recompute from raw flags.
Found via end-to-end service testing.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent audit: enforce secure session grading + harden voucher (00165)
entries.plain: Fluent security has been strengthened to ensure test integrity and prevent voucher misuse.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire AI interview: ride out transient API overloads + scripted fallback
entries.plain: Pre-Hire AI interviews now handle temporary API slowdowns gracefully, so candidates can complete interviews even during busy periods.
entries.explanation:
The AI interview depends on the Anthropic API. Logs showed it returning HTTP
529 "overloaded_error" (a transient capacity throttle, x-should-retry:true)
during a candidate's session, which the code caught and turned into a looping
"Sorry - a brief hiccup" message - the interview could not progress.

Two fixes:
- client.ts: bump SDK maxRetries 2 -> 4 so transient 429/529 overload blips are
  absorbed before any feature falls back. Helps every AI path (CBI, Fluent, quiz).
- cbi-interviewer.ts: extract the deterministic interview into a PROGRESSIVE
  scriptedTurn() (Situation -> Task -> Action -> Result, keyed on answer count)
  and route BOTH the no-key branch and the API-error catch through it. A
  sustained outage now degrades to a real scripted STAR interview that advances
  and completes (transcript captured for scoring) instead of a stuck loop.
  Scoring already degrades gracefully to "human assessor should review".

Verified the dependency live: scripts/test-cbi-reliability.ts fired the exact
CBI call 20x (10 at maxRetries=2, 10 at 8) - 20/20 succeeded, confirming the
model/key are healthy and the earlier failure was a transient overload window.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent: default proctoring-link host to the public domain in production
entries.plain: Fluent proctoring links in PDF reports now point to the correct public website so they work reliably.
entries.explanation:
Harden the prior fix: if NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL / NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL are unset,
fall back to https://caliber.viftraining.com in production (not the internal
request origin) so the proctoring link baked into the report PDF is always
reachable. Local dev still uses the request origin.

Confirmed via Chrome on the live site: the proctoring-report endpoint itself
returns the PDF correctly for an authed admin - the failure was only the host
in the embedded link.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent: hide full report from the taker; fix proctoring-link origin
entries.plain: Test-takers no longer see the detailed placement report after finishing Fluent; only staff can access it, and the download link has been removed.
entries.explanation:
(2) The runner's completion screen offered the taker a "Download full report"
link to the detailed placement report. That report is a STAFF deliverable
(the route is already requireRole-gated, XP-13), so the link is removed - the
taker keeps only their own certificate. Drops the now-unused Award icon.

(3) The proctoring-report link embedded in the placement report was built from
new URL(req.url).origin. On Render behind Cloudflare that is the internal host,
so the link in the downloaded PDF was unreachable. It now uses the configured
public base (NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL / NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL) and only falls back to
the request origin in local dev. The proctoring report stays admin-gated by
design (it carries face snapshots).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent placement report: camera-proctoring attestation + link
entries.plain: When an English test was camera-monitored, the placement report now shows that fact and provides staff with a link to the full proctoring record.
entries.explanation:
When a sitting was camera-proctored, the placement report now shows a "Camera
proctoring" line ("camera-proctored with consent; a Proctoring & Integrity Report
is available to authorised staff") and a link to that report. Detected via
result.voucher_redemption_id -> redemption -> proctor_session (ref_id = the
redemption token, snapshot_count > 0). Best-effort + tolerant: non-proctored
results render unchanged (verified: report still 200/PDF, same size).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent voucher: server-enforced "Require camera proctoring" toggle
entries.plain: Administrators can now require camera proctoring for specific vouchers, ensuring test-takers are monitored when the voucher is set up that way.
entries.explanation:
Proctoring never activated via the voucher flow (the ?proctor=1 param wasn't on
the redeem link and wasn't carried into the test) - so a candidate redeeming a
voucher was never proctored, which is why the consent box never appeared.

Now it's a per-voucher flag (migration 00149: eng_fluent_vouchers.proctor_enabled,
off by default): a "Require camera proctoring" checkbox on the generate form +
a "Proctored" badge in the voucher table. The take page reads the redeemed
voucher's proctor_enabled and turns ProctorCapture on server-side (no strippable
URL param); the admin-run ?proctor=1 path still works.

Verified end-to-end on the preview: generate proctored voucher -> proctor_enabled
true -> redeem -> the test page shows the "Camera proctoring" consent box.
tsc + ESLint clean; 00149 applied; test data cleaned up.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent vouchers: give admins the complete redemption link, not just the code
entries.plain: Voucher admins can now copy the full redemption link to send to candidates, instead of just the code, making it easier for clients to distribute tests.
entries.explanation:
The voucher admin only surfaced the bare code. Now it builds the full link
(<origin>/ac/fluent/redeem?code=<CODE>) so an admin can send it to a client who
forwards it to the candidate: per-row "Link" button, "Copy all links" for a
batch, and the link shown for each just-generated code. The redeem page resolves
the voucher's client from the code and shows "English placement for <Client>"
(code pre-filled), so the link visibly recognizes the client; the result is
tagged to that client as before. Verified end-to-end on the preview.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Camera proctoring Phase 1: consent + snapshots + admin report
entries.plain: Camera proctoring is now available across Fluent and Pre-Hire assessments, capturing snapshots and creating a staff-only audit trail that is kept for 90 days.
entries.explanation:
Per-administration (OFF by default), 90-day retention. Generic across contexts
(fluent/prehire) via (context, ref_id).

- Migration 00147: proctor_sessions + proctor_snapshots (cascade) + private
  'proctor' Storage bucket + admin-only RLS.
- src/lib/proctor/access.ts: createSession / recordSnapshot / endSession /
  getSessionWithSnapshots (signed URLs) / purgeExpiredProctorData. Service-role,
  tolerant of un-applied migration.
- /api/proctor/{start,snapshot,end} (anonymous taker; middleware-bypassed) +
  /api/proctor/retention/cron (Bearer CRON_SECRET, mirrors ARA retention).
- ProctorCapture component: blocking consent gate -> getUserMedia -> downscaled
  JPEG snapshot every 15s -> upload; "Proctoring active" badge; sendBeacon end.
  Never blocks/fakes the test (no session => stores nothing).
- Wired into the Fluent runner (bare + voucher take pages) behind ?proctor=1.
- Admin: /admin/proctor (sessions list) + /admin/proctor/[id] (report: metadata,
  consent record, snapshot grid via signed URLs); linked from the Fluent cohort.
- .github/workflows/proctor-retention-purge.yml (daily 03:30 UTC, reuses CRON_SECRET).

tsc + ESLint clean; 00147 applied; start/snapshot/report/list/cron-gate verified
on the preview. Live webcam capture (getUserMedia) needs on-device verification.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent: course recommendations on the report (VIFM + pluggable partner source)
entries.plain: The Fluent placement report now recommends English development courses from VIFM and partner training providers matched to the test-taker's level.
entries.explanation:
Add an English-development recommendations section to the Fluent placement
report, fed by two sources via a new recommender (src/lib/recommender/english.ts):
- VIFM: real vifm_courses catalogue matched to communication-oriented
  competencies (keyword on the 41 names; no fabricated courses). Surfaced only
  when the candidate is below working proficiency (< C1).
- Partner: new partner_courses table (migration 00146) for SE Training Academy
  (and future partners), matched by CEFR band + the candidate's weakest skill.
  Stays empty/invisible until courses are added; recommender is tolerant of the
  table not existing.

Admin manager at /admin/fluent/partner-courses (add/show-hide/delete, CEFR-band
+ skill-focus tagging), linked from the Fluent cohort page. Report route computes
recs best-effort (never blocks the PDF). tsc + ESLint clean; 00146 applied.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent methodology brief: cover the productive skills (writing/speaking)
entries.plain: The Fluent methodology guide now explains how speaking and writing skills are measured and scored, closing gaps in the previous documentation.
entries.explanation:
The brief described only reading/listening and wrongly stated productive skills
weren't covered. Add the four-skill construct, a productive-skill scoring section
(7 writing criteria, 4 speaking criteria + Azure pronunciation blend 0.7/0.3,
self-consistency ensemble, prompt-injection sanitisation), human QWK calibration
(>=.70 target) under reliability, an administration-integrity section, productive
+ automated-scoring references, and honest productive-scoring limitations. v1.0 -> v1.1.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add VIFM Fluent client-pitch deck (PPTX + PDF)
entries.plain: A new 10-slide presentation deck is available to pitch the Fluent English placement solution to clients, covering methodology and deliverables.
entries.explanation:
10-slide, VIFM-branded client pitch for the Fluent English-placement solution -
what is measured (4 CEFR-aligned skills), the CEFR standard, methodology,
scoring rubric, calibration/integrity rigor, and deliverables. Editable
PowerPoint source + a shareable PDF render.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent report: integrity signal + receptive narratives + criterion defs
entries.plain: The Fluent placement report now includes an integrity advisory signal, skill definitions, and detailed writing feedback that were previously missing.
entries.explanation:
Three gaps the report had relative to the on-screen result:
- Integrity signal was shown post-test but absent from the report. The report
  route selected only `result`; it now also reads `integrity_flags`, derives the
  advisory signal (stored `signal`, else recomputed; null on a 00042-only DB),
  and renders an "Integrity signal (advisory)" section (tier + 0-100 + reasons +
  the never-affects-the-level caveat). Tone-coded clean/minor/elevated.
- Reading + Listening showed only a score. Each now has a narrative of the
  sub-skills it measures (gist / detail / inference / vocabulary-in-context /
  discourse-and-flow) and what the band reflects.
- Writing + Speaking showed criterion labels with no definitions. Each section
  now defines every criterion (7 writing, 4 speaking, + pronunciation when
  present). The redundant generic "What we measure" block is trimmed to the
  scale + indicative-placement note.

tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent TTS: require the redemption token (or staff) to serve audio
entries.plain: Test audio in Fluent now requires proof of a valid voucher redemption, preventing unauthorized access to listening materials even if a session ID is leaked.
entries.explanation:
Closes the one defense-in-depth note from the voucher-sandbox audit. The
listening-audio endpoint identified the session by its (unguessable) id alone.
Now: a voucher taker must also pass their redemption token (validated against
eng_fluent_voucher_redemptions), and the admin-run /ac/fluent flow (no token)
must be staff-authenticated - so a leaked or guessed session id is useless
without the caller's own credential. The runner already had the redemption
token; it now forwards it on the TTS URL. No schema change. tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Mobile: wrap the Fluent hero nav so it doesn't overflow on phones
entries.plain: The Fluent navigation buttons on mobile devices now wrap properly instead of overflowing off the screen on smaller phones.
entries.explanation:
The five hero pills (All Services / Cohort / Vouchers / Retention /
Calibration) sat in a non-wrapping flex row and overflowed at 375px. Add
flex-wrap so they stack onto the next line.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent FLU-1: flag a mid-test IP change in the integrity signal
entries.plain: If a test-taker's internet connection changes during the Fluent test, the system now flags this as a minor integrity concern in the advisory signal.
entries.explanation:
Captures the request IP at test START (stored on eng_fluent_sessions.start_ip,
migration 00142) and compares it at scoring; if both are known and differ, an
authoritative server-side ipChanged flag is merged into the (PDPL-safe) integrity
flags and the advisory signal gains +30 points (clears the "minor" bar on its
own, reads as elevated alongside other activity) plus the reason "IP address
changed during the test". Advisory only - never auto-fails (a legitimate network
switch also changes IP). Surfaces in the admin integrity view + the comprehensive
report. Best-effort + tolerant: a pending 00142 just means no IP detection.

NEEDS migration 00142 applied:
  ALTER TABLE eng_fluent_sessions ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS start_ip text;

tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent: comprehensive report PDF (4 skills + definitions + writing issues)
entries.plain: Test-takers now receive a comprehensive multi-page report showing all four skills, scoring details, writing issues with examples, and pronunciation feedback.
entries.explanation:
"Download report" was only a 1-page certificate. New comprehensive report:
- Per-skill breakdown: reading + listening (correct/total + CEFR), writing (all
  7 criteria as 1-5 bars + feedback), speaking (criteria + pronunciation +
  transcript + feedback), overall CEFR + confidence range.
- "What we measure" definitions block.
- FLU-4: the writing scorer now emits SPECIFIC issues (grammar / spelling /
  punctuation / vocabulary / etiquette / structure) each with the offending
  quote + a correction; rendered as a "Specific issues" section. Stored in the
  existing result jsonb, so it rides along - older results just show no issues.

New staff-gated route /api/ac/fluent/[resultId]/report (React-PDF). The runner's
staff download + the cohort row now offer "Full report" (the certificate stays
as a secondary CEFR cert). Verified live: report 200/valid PDF. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: XP-13 (Fluent): hide results from the taker; staff-only certificate
entries.plain: Fluent results are now hidden from test-takers and certificates become staff-only, preventing candidates from accessing reports they should not see.
entries.explanation:
Part 1 of the cross-portal "taker never sees results; admin downloads/sends"
change. Fluent specifically also closes a real leak: the certificate route was
public in middleware, so anyone with a result UUID could pull the PDF.

- Score route returns an isStaff flag (admin/consultant/assessor via
  getCurrentCaller) and only returns result_id to staff; stops emailing the
  taker their certificate.
- Runner shows the full result + download ONLY to staff; a delegate/anonymous
  taker now sees a "submitted, results shared with the organisation" thank-you.
- Certificate removed from the public middleware list AND self-gated with
  requireRole(admin/consultant/assessor) (defence-in-depth) - admin still
  downloads/sends from /ac/fluent/cohort.
- New shared isStaffCaller() helper in ara/auth-guards.

tsc + lint clean. Other portals (Cognitive, Persona, ARC personal) next; Technical already conforms.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent integrity: mirror widened capture to the Pre-Hire stage (CAL-FLU-601)
entries.plain: Pre-Hire assessments that include Fluent now capture the same detailed integrity signals (time away, paste attempts, events) as standalone Fluent tests.
entries.explanation:
The Pre-Hire Fluent stage had the same count-only proctor. Mirror the AC
capture: time-away DURATION (window blur/focus + visibilitychange, debounced,
deduped), paste CHAR-LENGTH (never content), events log. The submit route
computes the advisory integrity signal and persists it into the stage flags
jsonb for the recruiter/fairness view (never auto-fails). Completes CAL-FLU-601
across both Fluent surfaces.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent integrity: widen capture + advisory signal (CAL-FLU-601, AC surface)
entries.plain: Fluent now detects when test-takers look away or paste content, scoring integrity on a 0-100 scale to help identify cheating.
entries.explanation:
New pure src/lib/scoring/integrity.ts (computeIntegritySignal -> 0-100 +
clean/minor/elevated tier + reasons; back-compat with old {blurCount,
pasteCount} rows; PDPL-safe). The /ac/fluent runner now captures time-away
DURATION (window blur/focus + visibilitychange, 1.5s-debounced, dedup so a
tab switch isn't double-counted - catches 2nd-monitor/devtools that
document.hidden missed) and paste CHAR-LENGTH (never the pasted text) on the
writing + speaking-fallback boxes, with a time-stamped events log. The score
route computes the signal, folds it into the integrity_flags jsonb (no
migration), and returns it on the result. Surfaced as an advisory,
never-auto-fail panel on the candidate results screen and as a tiered signal
on the admin cohort. Proctor copy updated (EN/AR) to match what's captured.
Pre-Hire Fluent stage mirror follows next.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent: pre-expiry timer warnings + transcript flush + no-limit fix (CAL-FLU-605)
entries.plain: Fluent now warns test-takers 2 minutes and 1 minute before time runs out, and captures spoken answers before auto-submitting.
entries.explanation:
The countdown auto-submits on expiry but gave no warning and could drop an
in-flight recording. Now: an amber banner + aria-live announcement at ~2 min
and <1 min before expiry; on auto-submit any in-flight recording is stopped
first (browser STT streams interim text into the transcript, so the spoken
answer is captured and the mic released) rather than submitting mid-record.
Also fixes the latent coercion that forced a deliberate no-limit (timerMinutes
0) back to 15 - an explicit 0 now means no countdown/auto-submit; undefined
still defaults to 15. Bilingual EN/AR.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent: clear unanswered-questions prompt instead of a silent block (CAL-FLU-604)
entries.plain: Fluent now shows a clear message listing unanswered questions instead of silently blocking submission, with a jump-to-first button.
entries.explanation:
Submit was disabled with no feedback when items were incomplete, trapping
the taker. Now the button validates on click: if anything is incomplete it
reveals a clear summary (N unanswered + writing-too-short + speaking-needed),
rings + labels each unanswered reading/listening item, rings the writing/
speaking section, and offers a 'go to first unanswered' jump. The timer
auto-submit path still calls submit() directly (force-submits on expiry,
unchanged). Bilingual EN/AR. Also clears one pre-existing em dash.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire email: route through Resend so reports actually deliver (CAL-PRE-507)
entries.plain: Pre-Hire assessment reports now actually send to clients via email instead of being marked sent without delivering.
entries.explanation:
sendEmail/isEmailConfigured were Microsoft Graph-only, but production
sends via Resend - so deliverReport console-mock-'sent', emailed stayed
false, report_sent_at never stamped, and the UI showed 'not configured'
while nothing reached the client. Make sendEmail Resend-first (mirroring
src/lib/ara/email.ts) with attachment passthrough, and isEmailConfigured
= resendConfigured() || Graph. Also fixes Pre-Hire invitations + AC
Fluent result emails, which shared the same Graph-only path. Blast radius:
all AC templates now send via Resend in prod (intended - Resend is the
live verified sender); smoke-test one AC send after deploy. Also clears
4 pre-existing em dashes in the Pre-Hire email templates (SOP).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Landing/catalogue: CAL-101 level filter row, CAL-102/103 Fluent badges
entries.plain: Course catalogue now has a dedicated filter row for course level, and Fluent appears in Talent Management with clear badges for hiring and development.
entries.explanation:
CAL-101: course-level filters moved to their own labelled row in the /courses
catalogue (Vertical row + 'Course Level' row, divider between), new i18n key
filterLevelLabel (en/ar); filtering logic unchanged, mobile stacks.
CAL-102: 'For Selection' badge added to the AI English Placement (Fluent) card on
Talent Acquisition.
CAL-103: Fluent added to Talent Management as the development-mode counterpart of
the same engine, with a 'For Development' badge (Languages SVG icon, /ac/fluent).
Both reuse the existing dual-purpose card + badge system.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent: expand no-AI fallback deck to 10R/10L + strip stray control bytes
entries.plain: Fluent's backup test (when AI is unavailable) now includes the full 10 reading and 10 listening items to match the main assessment.
entries.explanation:
- The static fallback test (served only when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is absent) was
  3 reading / 2 listening, not matching the AI path's 10/10 shape. Expanded
  both to 10 items each with an A1->C2 CEFR difficulty ramp, so a no-key
  deployment still delivers the full-length placement.
- Removed three raw control bytes (a literal NUL + 0x1f + 0x7f) that had crept
  into the sanitizeResponse() regex character class; restored the intended
  escaped form /[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g. The NUL had been making git/ripgrep treat
  the file as binary.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent: 10R/10L, richer writing rubric, mandatory speaking, definitions
entries.plain: Fluent now tests 10 reading and 10 listening items with mandatory speaking, and grades writing on 7 criteria including tone and spelling.
entries.explanation:
- Test now generates 10 reading + 10 listening items (full A1-C2 ramp),
  1 writing + 1 speaking task; larger token budget + an >=8 reading
  fallback threshold.
- Writing rubric expanded from 4 to 7 criteria: adds Register
  (business-like tone), Etiquette (courtesy/politeness/cultural fit) and
  Mechanics (spelling/punctuation) alongside task/coherence/lexical/
  grammar. Threaded through the scorer prompt + parsing + ensemble +
  fallbacks + persisted criteria (jsonb, no migration) + the result bars
  (EN/AR labels).
- Speaking is now MANDATORY: submit is blocked until a spoken (or typed-
  fallback) response of >=3 words exists; the "optional" badge becomes a
  "required" badge.
- New bilingual "What we measure" definitions panel on the result
  (4 skills + every writing/speaking sub-criterion); the download button
  is relabelled "Download report".

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent: retention/cleanup admin (to ARC bar)
entries.plain: Fluent admins can now view and delete old results beyond the 2-year compliance window through a new retention management page.
entries.explanation:
Closes Fluent's lifecycle gap vs the ARC bar: a /ac/fluent/retention admin
page (linked from the runner nav) showing total vs past-window result
counts and a typed-confirmation purge of results older than the 2-year
compliance window (plus their calibration score-runs). No migration.
Reassessment is N/A for a placement test (re-test = a new voucher/run).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fluent: full standalone voucher model
entries.plain: Fluent now supports voucher-based access like other assessments, letting clients generate and distribute test codes to candidates.
entries.explanation:
Bring Fluent to the standalone template (client registration -> vouchers
-> org-scoped results):
- eng_fluent_vouchers + redemptions + claim RPC (migration 00104); results
  gain organization_id + voucher_redemption_id
- admin console /ac/fluent/vouchers: generate seat-pool batches tagged to a
  client (registered first-class via the shared registry), copy, disable
- public redeem /ac/fluent/redeem -> token-gated runner /ac/fluent/take/[token]
  (middleware-bypassed, no account); completion stamps the result with the
  client org
- cohort report gains a Client column + ?org filter chips (org-scoped results)
All paths tolerant: inert until 00104 is applied.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Add methodology briefs for AC, Fluent, Technical, Reflect; flip map cells
entries.plain: Published methodology documents for Fluent, Technical, AI Compass, and Reflect explaining how each assessment works and its research foundation.
entries.explanation:
Writes the four missing technical-manual / methodology documents and
turns the 'Technical manual / methodology doc' row on the Evidence &
Validity Map from red (missing) to green (documented):

- docs/AC-Methodology-Brief.md
- docs/Fluent-Methodology-Brief.md
- docs/Technical-Methodology-Brief.md
- docs/Reflect-Methodology-Brief.md

Each mirrors the existing ARC brief's structure (construct definitions,
item development, validity, reliability, reference frameworks, honest
limitations, update cadence) and is grounded in the actual schema and
the same curated bibliographies the per-construct suggesters use. The
briefs faithfully reflect each instrument's real validity state — they
document what exists and openly disclose the gaps (e.g. no CFA / DIF /
test-retest yet), rather than overclaiming.

matrix.ts: ac/fluent/technical/reflect cells in that row now point at
the new briefs (psy remains 'partial' — its doc is still a draft).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add mobile hamburger to /ac runner tools via shared layout
entries.plain: Mobile users can now open the navigation menu on assessment tools like Fluent via a hamburger button on their phone.
entries.explanation:
The standalone /ac/* tools (Fluent, Technical, Psychometrics, AI interview) had
no layout, so the platform left panel was unreachable on a phone there. Add an
ac/layout.tsx mounting the shared MobileSidebar (lg:hidden, so the desktop
runner is unchanged). Verified on a 375px viewport: hamburger opens the full
nav. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Update CLAUDE.md for browser-native Fluent transcription
entries.plain: Documentation updated to explain that Fluent uses your browser's microphone for speech recognition, not a server-based service.
entries.explanation:
Document that Fluent/Pre-Hire speaking is transcribed in-browser via the Web
Speech API by default (free, no server, no key), with server Whisper as the
no-Web-Speech fallback. Adds src/lib/speech/browser-stt.ts to the file map.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Transcribe Fluent speech in-browser; fix Reflect + dev recs
entries.plain: Fluent speaking tests now use your browser's built-in speech recognition instead of a server, so assessments work on all platforms.
entries.explanation:
Three review bugs from the live portal, plus a free, server-less transcription
path that fixes speaking on Render.

- B1/B1c: Fluent + Pre-Hire speaking transcription now runs in the candidate's
  browser via the free Web Speech API (src/lib/speech/browser-stt.ts) — no server
  STT, no API key, so it works on Render (no Python/ffmpeg/Whisper there). The
  recognised text streams in live and flows into Claude CEFR scoring. Server
  transcription stays as a fallback for browsers without Web Speech (e.g. Firefox):
  OpenAI Whisper API when OPENAI_API_KEY is set, else local faster-whisper in dev.
- B1b: drop the user-facing "type instead" choice; keep the silent auto-fallback
  to type-mode only when the mic is blocked or recognition fails.
- B2: the Reflect engagement wizard no longer silently creates an empty framework
  when AI extraction yields nothing — it rolls back and returns a clear error
  pointing to "Clone a library template" or "Build manually".
- B3: AC report development recommendations are synthesised from below-target
  competencies (with development tips) when none were explicitly recorded, so the
  report never renders an empty section.

tsc + next lint + EN/AR parity (3768) clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Keep Fluent English even under the Arabic locale cookie
entries.plain: Fluent assessment always displays in English regardless of your language preference setting, since it is an English placement test.
entries.explanation:
getServerT now takes an optional locale override; Fluent's three server
pages pin "en". Previously the client provider forced /ac/fluent to en/ltr
but the server-rendered hero + nav still followed the cookie, so the page
showed Arabic chrome around an English test. Server + client now agree:
Fluent is English-only, as required.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Drop stale "Assessment Center" links from standalone tools
entries.plain: Removed old links to Assessment Center from standalone tools, as they are now independent services rather than sub-sections.
entries.explanation:
The Assessment Center is now a peer service, not the hub, so the
standalone tools no longer link back to it as a parent. Removed the
header "Assessment Center" links (back-arrow on Fluent + Technical
Assessment, forward cross-link on Reflect + AR Compass) and the footer
links on Reflect/AR Compass — "All Services" is the single route to the
platform hub. Also dropped Reflect's stale "Scaffolding (M1)" status.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Promote technical taxonomy to DB + technical→behavioural bridge
entries.plain: Technical skills are now linked to core behavioural competencies with weighted strength, so assessments show how technical abilities support broader workplace skills.
entries.explanation:
Migration 00054 adds technical_domains + technical_skills (seeded from the
code framework) and turns the tech_assessment_* domain_key strings into
FKs (NOT VALID, so legacy rows can't fail the migration). The new
technical_domain_competencies table is the technical→behavioural bridge:
each domain declares which of the AC 38 it enables, with a 1–3 weight.

unified-profile now reads the bridge and, for a measured domain, adds an
"enables" CompetencySignal (kind/source "technical", indigo) onto each
mapped competency — so a technical result surfaces as a "↳ Finance · 4/5"
chip on the skills page, like Fluent's language enablers. Tolerant of
00054 not being applied.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add unified competency profile with Fluent Language Skills framework
entries.plain: All assessment results now feed into one unified skills profile showing how each test contributes to your core competencies, with language skills shown alongside behavioural ratings.
entries.explanation:
Introduce the competency-mapping engine (the "golden thread"): each
instrument's result is expressed in VIFM competencies, labelled by source
and relation. Fluent's Reading/Listening/Writing/Speaking are their own
Language Skills framework (CEFR) that ENABLES specific behavioural
competencies (not equals) — surfaced as a Language Skills family plus ↳
enabler chips on the candidate skills page, beside AC behavioural ratings.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Translate client portal + auth + public AC/courses to Arabic (RTL)
entries.plain: The entire client portal, login pages, and public course catalogue are now available in Arabic with right-to-left layout for Arabic-speaking users.
entries.explanation:
Completes Arabic/RTL across every remaining surface:
- Client portal: shell, dashboard, engagements + results, analytics
  (nine-box), reports, projects, solutions, templates
- Auth: login (incl. magic link), register, password reset
- Public: credential /verify page, /courses catalogue + quote request,
  /ac tools (AI interview, engagement tiers, roadmap), Fluent landing +
  cohort + calibration (the test-runner keeps its own ui_language)
- Shared ratings.* namespace (BARS + OAR recommendation labels) wired
  into wash-up, integration, client results, and the candidate report

Gate now covers all portals. ~960 new EN/AR keys (3251 total at parity).
tsc clean; production next build passes. Report pages that were
already bilingual were left untouched throughout.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Logica audit fixes: cohort IDOR (critical) + reports, timer, retention, i18n
entries.plain: Cognitive tests now prevent unauthorized access to test-taker names, emails, and scores - staff can only see their own organization's data.
entries.explanation:
Adversarial audit of the Cognitive/Psychometrics module (8 findings). All
app-layer, no migration.

Critical
- Cognitive cohort page had no staff gate and no org scoping - a service-role
  query returned every org's taker names, emails, and scores to any signed-in
  user. Now gated to staff; a client_manager is scoped to their own org
  (mirrors the report route + the Persona cohort fix).

Minor
- Retention purge now anonymises voucher-redemption PII and sweeps expired
  psy_sessions (keyed test + taker email), so no PII outlives the 2-year rule.
- Report no longer prints a "General mental ability (g)" composite for a scoped
  sitting: single-subtest suppresses it, a partial battery is relabelled
  "Reasoning average (partial battery)".
- Arabic taker names no longer render as tofu on the (Helvetica) report and
  bundle cover - they fall back to the email, then a generic label.
- Server-side time-limit backstop: the cognitive route stamps a deadline at
  start and rejects a grossly-late score (client auto-submit unchanged).
- Bilingual pre-test notice (item count, time limit, single-use, answers final,
  refresh loses progress) before the taker commits.
- Remaining English-only runner controls (subtitle, Begin, answered-count,
  Submit) now localise in Arabic mode.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Bundle combined report: one PDF for the whole sitting
entries.plain: Assessment results can now be downloaded as one combined PDF containing all test reports together instead of separate files.
entries.explanation: New /api/admin/bundle/[candidateId]/report merges an executive cover (bundle + candidate + org, per-service 'Sitting at a glance' summary, contents with computed page numbers) with the FULL standalone reports - Persona profile, Persona Leadership Report, Logica result - via pdf-lib, so the combined document can never drift from the individual ones. The Logica report data mapping is extracted to a shared builder (psy-report-data.ts) reused by both routes. Route is deliberately OUTSIDE the middleware-bypassed /api/bundle/ prefix: admin/staff always, client_manager only for their own org. Portal bundle roster gains a 'Combined report (PDF)' link per completed candidate; individual per-service reports remain available. Verified against the live Talent Director retest sitting (18-page merged PDF, cover summary + page numbers correct).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Remove leftover throwaway test script
entries.plain: Removed a temporary test file that was accidentally left in the system.
entries.explanation: scripts/_t8.mjs was a one-off local repro for the Logica score flow, accidentally committed. No secrets (reads .env.local at runtime).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Logica: scoped tests get real length + honest scope copy
entries.plain: Cognitive tests now adjust the number of questions based on how many subtests you select, so taking one subtest gives you more questions to answer fairly.
entries.explanation: Items-per-subtest now scales to the selection (1 subtest -> 10 items, 2 -> 6 each, 3 -> 5 each, 4 -> 4 each) instead of a flat 4/subtest that made a single-subtest sitting just 4 questions; the AI floor rises accordingly. The runner's header + intro card now name only the scoped subtests ('Inductive reasoning - an indicative...') instead of always listing all four. Verified: inductive-only start returns 10 inductive items; take page shows scoped copy only.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Logica take page: title + subtitle readable on the dark hero
entries.plain: The Cognitive test start page title and instructions are now clearly visible against the dark banner for better readability.
entries.explanation: The take page overlaps the runner's header onto its dark hero banner, so the navy 'Logica' title was invisible and the subtitle straddled the band edge. New onDark mode renders the title white + subtitle white/80, and the hero is deepened (pb-36 / -mt-32) so the full header sits on the dark band with the white intro card popping below. Verified in-browser on a live take link.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Logica: voucher takers no longer see 'Could not score' on successful submit
entries.plain: Fixed an issue where voucher users saw an error message even when their test was completed and scored successfully.
entries.explanation: XP-13 made the score route return result=null to non-staff takers (no score data over the wire), and the thank-you screen was written accordingly - but the submit guard still required d.result, so every voucher delegate's SUCCESSFUL submit was mislabelled 'Could not score.' and their retry hit the single-use guard ('already been submitted'). Scores were persisting the whole time. Success is now res.ok alone; canView requires staff + a result body. Verified as a real anonymous taker in-browser: inductive-scoped voucher -> begin -> answer 16 -> submit -> thank-you screen, no error, no score leak, result row persisted.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Logica: subtest-scoped vouchers (e.g. Inductive Reasoning only)
entries.plain: You can now issue vouchers for specific cognitive subtests only, so candidates complete just the reasoning type they need to demonstrate.
entries.explanation: Migration 00170 adds cognitive_vouchers.subtests text[] (NULL = full battery, CHECK-constrained to the four keys). Issue flow: subtest-scope chips on the voucher issuer (with ?subtests= prefill) + an 'Issue a voucher for this selection' link on the runner picker, right where only 'Begin assessment' was offered. Delegate flow: the take page locks the picker to the voucher's set, and the start API re-resolves the scope server-side from the redemption token so a tampered client can never widen it. Verified E2E on the live DB: an inductive-only voucher + a start request asking for all four returned an inductive-only test. All paths tolerant of 00170 not being applied.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Licensed Portal Preview: branded simulated tenant for pitches
entries.plain: A new preview mode lets you simulate how the platform looks for a prospective customer with their branding and selected features.
entries.explanation: Admin sidebar -> 'Licensed Preview' launcher: enter a prospect's org/sector/region/accent and open a full, navigable 'Caliber for {Org}' tenant - a Command Center plus a branded dashboard per licensed capability (AC, Techno, Logica, Persona, Fluent, AI Readiness, Reflect 360, Psychometrics, Pre-Hire, Academy, Credentials, Succession). Deterministic sample data from the org name; no DB writes, no real records. Admin-gated.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Voucher consolidation: shared core + redeem form + risk fixes
entries.plain: Fixed voucher security issues including ensuring all tests release seats properly and preventing personal data from appearing in shareable links.
entries.explanation:
Phase 0 (risk fixes):
- 00167: add eng_fluent_voucher_release_seat (Fluent was the only instrument
  with no seat-release - a failed redeem permanently burned a seat).
- Role Readiness redeem no longer trusts name/email from URL params (phishing
  vector); prefills from the voucher row server-side instead, dropping PII from
  the issued link (matches ARC/Fluent/Techno).

Phase 1 (shared core): src/lib/vouchers/ - codegen, types, descriptor registry
(all 7 engines), and core.redeemViaDescriptor (the single claim -> provision ->
compensate path). Cognitive/Logica redeem now delegates to it.

Phase 2 (shared UI): src/components/shared/voucher-redeem-form.tsx - one
bilingual EN/AR (RTL) redeem form. Fluent + Logica cut over to it (both gain the
language toggle they lacked).

Phase 4: Fluent redeem also delegates to the shared core.

Build + tsc verified green. Remaining services (Persona/Techno/Pre-Hire/RR/ARC)
are mechanical repeats of the same pattern - see docs/voucher-consolidation-plan.md.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Logica audit Batch 2: redeem PII-prefill + RTL, mid-test progress guard
entries.plain: Added safeguards to prevent phishing through voucher links, require right-to-left text for codes, and warn candidates if they try to leave a test mid-way.
entries.explanation:
- Redeem page: only the voucher code is honoured from the URL (name/email/company
  PII-prefill removed - phishing vector); code + email inputs pinned dir=ltr.
- Runner: beforeunload guard while a test is open, so a refresh/close/navigation
  warns before losing in-progress answers (answers live in state until submit).
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Logica audit Batch 1: session integrity, result-leakage, vouchers, generator
entries.plain: Strengthened security to prevent test scores being submitted twice, enforce session time limits, and hide detailed score data from unauthorized access.
entries.explanation:
- Cognitive score route: enforce session TTL server-side (expired -> 410);
  atomically claim the single-use session (consumed false->true) BEFORE scoring so
  a double/concurrent submit can't score twice; and a non-staff response now
  carries result=null (the detailed scales/bands no longer ship over the wire -
  UI-hiding alone left them inspectable). Client thank-you no longer depends on
  the result body.
- migration 00159: cognitive_voucher_release_seat + lock EXECUTE on the voucher
  RPCs to service_role + pinned search_path. redeemVoucher releases the claimed
  seat on redemption-insert failure.
- Cognitive item generator: reject AI items with DUPLICATE options (a graded MCQ
  must have distinct choices - caught live where the correct answer was repeated).
- Cohort report bands via the canonical cognitiveBand (was a divergent local
  bandFromPct with different thresholds).
migration 00159 applied + verified live. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Demo data: extend the one-click seed/purge to all 10 services
entries.plain: Demo data now populates all seven assessment services, so guided walkthroughs show realistic completed screens for every product.
entries.explanation:
Phase 1 covered AC / Pre-Hire / Fluent only. Refactor the seeder to a per-service
module registry (src/lib/demo/services/*) and add the remaining seven so the
guided demos walk populated screens for every service:

- Persona: 3 completed self-assessment sittings (standalone path; results+cohort)
- Logica: 3 cognitive psy_results (above/avg/below) for the cohort report
- Techno: 3 sandbox sittings (advanced/intermediate/basic) for the results list
- VIFM Academy: enrollments (1 completed + lesson checks, 1 in progress)
- AR Compass: 1 enterprise assessment + 5 respondents + 120 responses, scored
  to pillar + overall via the real recalculate path
- Reflect 360: engagement + 2 participants + 9 raters + 140 responses (1 scored)
- Succession Readiness: combined engagement + 3 candidates vs a target role

Registry order = dependencies before dependents (Academy reads AC candidates,
Readiness reads Persona/Reflect); purge iterates in reverse. Each module is
idempotent, best-effort, tolerant of an un-applied migration, and scoped to the
demo org / sentinel so the purge removes it.

Verified end-to-end on the live DB: Load -> all 10 populate (zero errors, ~23s)
-> Remove -> all removed incl. both org stores, status clean. tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Rename product brand Logical -> Logica (correction)
entries.plain: Corrected the cognitive assessment's official brand name from Logical to Logica across all customer-facing materials.
entries.explanation:
Follow-up to the prior Mentium->Logical rename: the brand is "Logica", not
"Logical". Scoped to the 24 brand files only (capital-L replace), so the ordinary
word "logical"/"Psychological" elsewhere (fluent definitions, psychometrics
proposal, seed tags) is untouched. Arabic transliteration لوجيكال -> لوجيكا. Slug
logical->logica + methodology doc renamed (Logica-Methodology-Brief.md) -> brief
serves at /api/methodology/logica/pdf. Display-only; no data/route migration.
tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Rename product brand Mentium -> Logical (display only)
entries.plain: Updated the cognitive assessment's brand name from Mentium to Logical in all customer-facing pages, emails, and reports.
entries.explanation:
Customer-facing brand rename across 24 files: "Mentium(R)" -> "Logical(R)" in the
landing tile, admin nav/dashboard, cognitive runner + cohort/voucher/retention
pages, portal-comparison sheet, evidence catalogue, emails, psychometric report,
and timer settings. Arabic transliteration "مينتيوم" -> "لوجيكال" in the 5
bilingual strings (placeholder, pending human review).

Methodology brief renamed (docs/Mentium-Methodology-Brief.md ->
Logical-Methodology-Brief.md) + registry entry (slug mentium->logical, service,
eyebrow, file, PDF filename) -> brief now serves at /api/methodology/logical/pdf.

Internal identifiers unchanged (routes /ac/cognitive, lib/cognitive,
lib/psychometrics, DB) - display-only rename, no data/route migration. tsc +
ESLint clean; zero Mentium/مينتيوم occurrences remain.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Rename the cognitive assessment from "Reason" to "Mentium"
entries.plain: Renamed the cognitive assessment from Reason to Mentium across all user-visible screens, emails, and reports.
entries.explanation:
Brand rename: every user-facing standalone "Reason" -> "Mentium" (Arabic
"الاستدلال"-as-name -> "مينتيوم") across the runner hero, sidebar, admin hub +
landing + compare cards (EN+AR), cohort/vouchers/retention/redeem pages, voucher
hub, delegate emails, psychometric report, evidence catalogue, timer label, and
i18n values. Applied via a word-boundary regex so the "reasoning" construct
descriptors (taglines, subtest names, "numerical/verbal/inductive/deductive
reasoning") are untouched; the 5 Arabic name-sites done surgically so the
generic الاستدلال (reasoning) descriptors stay.

Verified: tsc + JSON clean; 0 standalone "Reason" left; runner + sidebar show
"Mentium"; "reasoning" descriptors preserved. Internal routes (/ac/cognitive),
tables, and identifiers unchanged.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Rename the Cognitive instrument to "Reason" across all user-facing surfaces
entries.plain: Renamed the cognitive assessment to Reason across all user-facing pages, navigation, emails, and reports.
entries.explanation:
Branding: the cognitive-ability instrument is now "Reason" (Arabic: الاستدلال)
everywhere a user sees it - sidebar nav, the runner hero, landing card (EN+AR),
portal comparison, evidence page, voucher hub + voucher/cohort/retention/redeem
sub-pages, delegate emails, admin cohorts, the psychometric report PDF, settings
timer label, and the instrument display constant.

Deliberately left as-is: the /ac/cognitive route + DB kind='cognitive', internal
identifiers (CognitiveItem, COGNITIVE_SUBTESTS, functions, types), Bloom's
CognitiveLevel, the THINKING-domain "Cognitive & strategic" caption, the quiz
"Cognitive Challenge" badge, and academic citations (Carroll, Field) - none are
the instrument's display name.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: XP-13 (Cognitive): hide results from the taker; staff-only report
entries.plain: Test-takers no longer see their cognitive assessment scores; only staff can view and download detailed results.
entries.explanation:
Same pattern as Fluent. The score route returns isStaff and only hands result_id
to staff; the runner shows the full score breakdown + Download only to staff, and
a delegate/anonymous taker now sees a "submitted" thank-you. The report PDF is
removed from the public middleware list and self-gated with requireRole; admins
download/send from the cohort view.

tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: SDAIA demo: fix Reflect UUID bug, cognitive subtest picker, sidebar relabel
entries.plain: Fixed three issues: Reflect feedback now accepts unselected organizations, cognitive tests let users pick which reasoning skills to assess, and sidebar labels updated.
entries.explanation:
SD-5: Reflect engagement create no longer rejects an unselected client org
with the raw "Invalid UUID" token - schema coerces ""->null + friendly
messages, wizard passes organization_id || null, action backstops the message.

SD-4: cognitive runner can choose any 1-4 of numerical/verbal/inductive/
deductive subtests. framework.sanitizeSubtests + COGNITIVE_SUBTEST_KEYS;
generatePsyTest/aiCognitive/staticCognitive/assembleFromBank are subset-aware
(default all four, back-compat); start route sanitizes server-side; intro
picker (bilingual, >=1 required, optional admin lock). Scoring/result/PDF were
already scale-agnostic.

SD-8: sidebar instrumentGroup first leaf relabelled "Overview" -> "Take
assessment" (svcRunner key + PlayCircle) - fixes the stray Cognitive/Persona/
Fluent runner-as-Overview mislabel. Two-column IA already shipped.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Cognitive route: keep org linkage when 00137 is pending (review fix)
entries.plain: Cognitive assessment results now correctly link to organizations even when database updates are pending.
entries.explanation:
The result-link SELECT referenced project_label, so on a pre-00137 DB
the read errored, nulled the redemption, and skipped the whole org-link
block (dropping organization_id + voucher_redemption_id + result_id).
Revert the SELECT to org-only columns and read project_label in a
separate best-effort query, mirroring the Persona path.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add registered mark to the Cognitive/psychometric report title
entries.plain: The cognitive assessment's registered trademark symbol now appears in the assessment report title.
entries.explanation:
The instrument name (e.g. "Cognitive Ability") in the psychometric report
hero now carries the registered mark, completing the report-branding pass
for the Cognitive service (the shared framework constant stays unmarked so
the on-screen runner is unaffected).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: docs: correct stale psychometrics route refs in CLAUDE.md
entries.plain: Updated documentation to reflect the correct paths where the cognitive assessment actually runs.
entries.explanation:
The live cognitive runner is /ac/cognitive + /api/ac/cognitive; the
/ac/psychometrics page + /api/ac/psychometrics API documented here never
existed at those paths (the OCEAN personality runner was retired in favour
of Persona). Corrected so the docs match the running app.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona/Cognitive: post-review + live-verification hardening
entries.plain: Fixed critical bugs where personality and cognitive assessments were freezing or losing responses during forced-choice questions.
entries.explanation:
Fixes surfaced by a live browser walkthrough + an adversarial multi-agent
review of the session's changes:

- Persona runner (CRITICAL): chooseIps called the autosave side effect inside
  the setIpsChoices updater, firing startTransition during render ("Cannot call
  startTransition while rendering") and silently dropping the state commit, so
  forced-choice blocks never registered and Submit hung. Moved the side effect
  into the event handler.
- Persona autosave: replaced one-server-action-per-answer with a debounced,
  batched flush (buffer keyed by itemKey, ~700ms debounce, immediate flush on
  page-advance + submit). The old pattern fired 160+ server actions per test
  and a router-refresh cascade that buried the submit; now it is a handful of
  calls. Verified end-to-end on the live DB: 205 responses persisted
  (164 normative + 41 ipsative with answer_data), hiring fit = 82% Strong fit.
- Cognitive (HIGH): the inductive odd-one-out "3,5,7,9,11" had no defensible
  answer (all odd). Replaced with a perfect-squares odd-one-out (4,9,16,20,25).
  Gave each subtest a genuine easy->hard spread in the fallback deck.
- Cognitive: cognitiveNarrative now reads s.raw (matches the report route;
  raw == normalized for cognitive, so behaviour is unchanged).
- item-drafter: dropped the dead "abstract" fallback string (retired construct).
- persona-format: buildIpsativeBlocks skips a competency with no forward item
  rather than falling back to a reverse item (which would be mis-keyed).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Migration 00111: backfill cognitive inductive/deductive links by id
entries.plain: Restored missing skill linkages for inductive and deductive reasoning components in the cognitive assessment database.
entries.explanation:
00109 seeded the inductive/deductive construct_competency_links predicts-rows
via a JOIN on competencies.name, but the behavioural 38 were renamed to the
current scheme after 00066 (id ...004 is now "Critical Analysis", not
"Analytical Reasoning"), so the name-join matched zero rows - confirmed against
the live DB (abstract rows deleted, inductive/deductive rows absent). This
backfill inserts the four links by stable competency UUID (rename-proof),
aligned with framework.ts COGNITIVE_SUBTESTS. Idempotent; user applies.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Cognitive: split abstract into inductive + deductive subtests
entries.plain: Cognitive assessment now separately measures inductive reasoning (pattern recognition) and deductive reasoning (logical application) instead of combining them.
entries.explanation:
Replace the single "abstract reasoning" subtest with two scientifically
distinct CHC-grounded subtests: inductive (infer the rule from patterns -
fluid intelligence) and deductive (apply rules/premises to a valid
conclusion - logical validity). The cognitive instrument now measures
four subtests: numerical, verbal, inductive, deductive.

- framework.ts: 4 subtests with fuller definitions (definition_en/_ar) +
  cognitiveNarrative() score-band copy (strong/mid/developing, bilingual).
- generate.ts: static fallback deck + AI prompt + valid-scale set cover
  all four; min item count raised to 8.
- item-drafter.ts: SME-draft prompt gains inductive + deductive branches.
- Runner result + PDF report now show each subtest's fuller definition and
  a per-scale narrative.
- Swept "abstract" -> "inductive/deductive" across all cognitive UI copy
  (runner, redeem, cohort, landing, timer settings) and report methodology.
- Migration 00109 (write-only; user applies): retire the abstract
  construct_competency_links predicts rows, seed inductive + deductive
  rows; tolerant remap of any abstract psy_scales/psy_items/psy_norms.

IPIP Big-Five "abstract ideas" personality items deliberately untouched.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Cognitive to ARC bar: admin-set timer + cohort + retention
entries.plain: Cognitive assessment gains a timer that administrators can customize, plus cohort performance reports and data retention controls.
entries.explanation:
- Finish /ac/psychometrics -> /ac/cognitive repoints (landing, sidebar,
  admin readiness/psychometrics, guided start, resolver, report header).
- Admin-configurable timer (assessment_timers "cognitive") with countdown
  + auto-submit on the runner; "Cognitive ability" row in /admin/settings.
- Org-scoped cohort report at /ac/cognitive/cohort (g + per-subtest
  averages, band distribution, per-run report links, client filter).
- Retention admin at /ac/cognitive/retention (24-month purge of
  psy_results, typed confirmation, admin-gated).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Cognitive: standalone voucher model
entries.plain: Cognitive assessment now has its own independent voucher and seat-pool system, letting organizations manage access separately from other assessments.
entries.explanation:
Give the Cognitive Ability service its own independent voucher system
(mirrors Fluent), separate from any psychometrics umbrella:
- cognitive_vouchers + redemptions + claim RPC (migration 00105); psy_results
  gains organization_id + voucher_redemption_id
- admin console /ac/cognitive/vouchers: generate seat-pool batches tagged to a
  client (registered first-class via the shared registry), copy, disable
- public redeem /ac/cognitive/redeem -> token runner /ac/cognitive/take/[token];
  completion stamps the result with the client org
- middleware bypass for the delegate paths. Tolerant; inert until 00105 applied.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Make Cognitive its own service: /ac/psychometrics -> /ac/cognitive
entries.plain: Cognitive ability tests now have their own dedicated section in the platform instead of being grouped with other assessments, making it easier to find and use.
entries.explanation:
Per "everything is psychometric, so list them independently": the
Cognitive runner is no longer bundled under a "Psychometrics" umbrella.
- move /ac/psychometrics -> /ac/cognitive and /api/ac/psychometrics ->
  /api/ac/cognitive (runner + result-report PDF)
- retitle to "Cognitive Ability"; drop the embedded Persona card so the
  page is purely cognitive (Persona is its own service at /ac/persona)
- repoint landing, sidebar, guided-start, readiness, item-bank link, and
  the persona back-link; persona card now links straight to /ac/persona

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Landing + sidebar: dual-pillar service taxonomy
entries.plain: The main navigation now clearly separates talent acquisition tools (for hiring) from talent management tools (for development), with the same assessments listed in both places depending on how you want to use them.
entries.explanation:
Mirror the selection/development model across the landing and the portal
sidebar:
- Assessment Center, AI Readiness, Technical, Cognitive, Persona are
  dual-purpose: listed under both Talent Acquisition (For Selection ->
  fit-score) and Talent Management (For Development -> VIFM Academy),
  with the AC console shown as "Development Center" on the management side
- Dissolve the Psychometrics grouping; Cognitive/Persona are standalone
- Pillar-colored icons: blue = Acquisition, emerald = Management
- Subgrid the pillar headers so both tile columns align regardless of
  sub-heading wrap
- Sidebar lists every service under each pillar (item bank moved to Platform)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Surface Persona + Succession Readiness on the service map
entries.plain: Persona and Succession Readiness assessments are now visible as their own tiles on the main dashboard and sidebar, making them easier to locate and launch.
entries.explanation:
Make the three-service taxonomy real on the main landing and sidebar:
- Cognitive + Persona render as two tiles clustered under Psychometrics
  (Talent Acquisition); Persona is its own tile within Psychometrics.
- Succession Readiness becomes its own tile (amber) next to Reflect 360
  in Talent Management, with a new service home at /admin/readiness
  (the combined Persona-self + 360-others offering).
- Sidebar mirrors both groupings; readinessConfig relabelled to the
  scoring config under a Succession Readiness group.
- Drop stale Big Five copy from the landing (EN + AR); add tone-amber;
  #persona deep-link anchor.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persona: add engagement->candidate picker on the psychometric portal
entries.plain: When accessing Persona assessments, you can now pick a candidate from a list of engagements instead of needing to know their ID in advance.
entries.explanation:
Persona records against a specific candidate, so when /ac/psychometrics
is opened without ?candidateId, the Persona card now shows a picker:
choose an engagement (with its candidate count), then a candidate, then
Open Persona launches /candidate/behavioral/<candidateId>. The direct
?candidateId button path is unchanged; empty-state shown when no
candidates exist. Engagements + candidates loaded server-side.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Retire OCEAN Big Five; Persona is the behavioural instrument
entries.plain: Persona-a 38-competency behavioral assessment aligned with your 360-degree feedback tool-now replaces the older Big Five personality test across the platform.
entries.explanation:
The psychometric portal's behavioural instrument is now Persona - the
38-competency behavioural self-assessment on the same framework as the
Reflect 360 (self lens) - replacing the 5-trait OCEAN Big Five.

- /ac/psychometrics: Cognitive ability (unchanged) + a Persona launch
  card; OCEAN selector removed. API route is cognitive-only.
- Admin bank console lists cognitive only (personality dropped from
  INSTRUMENTS). Guided Start + platform landing point behavioural at
  Persona. Runner header + skills-page link rebranded "Persona" (EN/AR).
- Readiness unchanged: self from the behavioural self-assessment, others
  from the 360.
- 00095 deletes the OCEAN predicts/foundations rows from
  construct_competency_links (cognitive links untouched; shared psy_*
  tables retained for cognitive).

OCEAN removed from every product surface; dormant library internals left
in place (unreachable - no personality results produced, data cleared by
00095) to keep the build green. Full dead-code sweep is a deliberate
follow-up. tsc + readiness tests + next build all green.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add All Services link to the remaining service landings
entries.plain: Every assessment landing page now has a consistent button to return to the full services menu, making navigation faster and more predictable.
entries.explanation:
Every service landing now has a consistent "All Services" button back to
the launcher (/). The shared AllServicesLink was already on the admin/
assessor/candidate/client layouts and the Fluent/AR Compass/Reflect
heroes; this adds it to the three that were missing it — Psychometrics
(/ac/psychometrics), Courses (/courses), and the AI Conversational
Assessor prototype (/ac/ai-interview). Technical already had its own
inline pill. Verified live: all 10 service surfaces render the button.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Psychometrics Tier 2: bank console, norms, report PDF, IPIP-50
entries.plain: Cognitive assessments now include standardized scoring norms, allowing results to be compared fairly against larger reference groups instead of being preliminary estimates.
entries.explanation:
Promotes the indicative psychometrics runner toward a norm-referenced,
defensible instrument without changing the Tier-1 fallback.

Engine (calibration.ts + migration 00067): Cronbach's alpha, raw -> z ->
percentile/sten standardization, and the indicative<->calibrated tier
gate (>=8 approved items/scale, alpha >=.70, norm n >=200). applyNorms
ignores sub-threshold norms so pilot data never leaks premature
percentiles; a result is calibrated only when every scale is normed.

SME console (/admin/psychometrics): per-scale three-gate readiness with
a live tier badge, AI-draft into the bank, approve/retire/edit/delete
review lifecycle, manual authoring. bank.ts self-bootstraps the
instruments/scales on first write. Bank-driven assembly: generatePsyTest
prefers approved bank items (real psy_items uuids, so the response log
is calibratable) and falls back to Mini-IPIP / AI / static when thin.

Pilot norm-loader (norms.ts): computes per-scale {n,mean,sd} from
collected results as provisional norms; activates per scale at n>=200.

IPIP-50 (ipip50.ts): public-domain Big-Five markers, 10 items x 5 traits,
bilingual, one-click seed into the bank as approved -> clears the content
gate and serves the longer validated form.

Report PDF (/api/ac/psychometrics/[resultId]/report): per-scale band +
(calibrated) percentile + sten bar, response-validity statement, g
composite, tier framing; download button on the runner results.

Bugfix: the score route returned the un-normed result while persisting
the normed one, so the runner never reflected calibration. Now returns
the enriched result.

Verified live end-to-end (create -> render -> cleanup): indicative
report 10.6KB, calibrated report 15.5KB with correct percentile/sten;
all test rows + norms deleted after. tsc + lint clean; EN/AR parity held.
Psychometrics yields scores + a defensible report, not a credential.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Psychometrics to the All Services launcher
entries.plain: Cognitive tests are now included in the main platform launcher alongside other assessments, so you can access them from the central dashboard.
entries.explanation:
The sidebar and the platform launcher are separate lists; Psychometrics
was wired into the sidebar but not the launcher, so they drifted. Add it
to platform-landing.tsx as the seventh service: a fuchsia launcher-card
(BrainCircuit -> /ac/psychometrics) with bilingual EN/AR copy, update the
service count Six -> Seven (+ homepage meta description), and add the
tone-fuchsia hue to the launcher-card system.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add VIFM Psychometrics module (Foundations layer) + competency bridge
entries.plain: A new cognitive ability and personality module has been added as a foundational layer that helps predict behavioral competencies, with secure test delivery and admin-only result access.
entries.explanation:
Cognitive ability (numerical/verbal/abstract MCQ -> bands + g) and
Big-Five personality (public-domain Mini-IPIP, 20 items) as the
Foundations tier that PREDICTS the behavioural 38. Secure runner at
/ac/psychometrics mirroring Fluent/Technical: server-held keyed test in
psy_sessions (single-use), key stripped from the client, server-side
scoring, admin-only psy_results, service-role writes. Tier 1 is
indicative (no norms/IRT, no credential).

- Schema: migration 00065 (psy_instruments/scales/items/sessions/
  results/item_responses + RLS).
- Bridge: migration 00066 seeds construct_competency_links with
  predicts/foundations rows per subtest, g, and OCEAN trait;
  unified-profile surfaces them on the candidate skills page as
  per-competency predicts signals (with the "predicted, not measured"
  caveat).
- Sidebar "Psychometrics" link + Guided Start (certify -> ability) entry.
- Bilingual EN/AR; framework competency names aligned to the seeded 38.
- Docs: CLAUDE.md module section + proposal status banner.

Migrations 00065 + 00066 need applying.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add layered construct→competency bridge (00064) + psychometrics proposal
entries.plain: The system now uses a unified framework to link cognitive and personality measures to behavioral competencies, enabling a layered, integrated view of talent strengths.
entries.explanation:
Generalises the single-purpose technical→behavioural bridge into one typed link
table and lands the layered measurement model in code + docs.

- Migration 00064: construct_competency_links(source_kind, source_key,
  competency_id, relation ∈ {manifests, enables, predicts}, layer ∈ {foundations,
  attainments, competencies}, weight, validated, rationale), RLS like the other
  framework tables, seeded from technical_domain_competencies (nothing lost).
- unified-profile.ts: typed CompetencyRelation (manifests/enables/predicts) +
  CompetencyLayer + a relationMeta() renderer that flags a `predicts` chip as
  "predicted, not measured"; reads construct_competency_links for the technical
  path, falling back to the legacy table when 00064 isn't applied. Cognitive +
  personality plug in here as predicts/foundations links.
- docs/psychometrics-proposal.md: the layered model (spine + typed relations),
  the HiPo/Succession application, the reuse inventory, the professional-quality
  bar, phased plan, and technical-manual outline. CLAUDE.md documents 00064.

Migration 00064 already applied. tsc + next lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect 360 audit fixes: anonymity floor (2 critical) + IDOR, lifecycle, a11y
entries.plain: 360 feedback reports now hide feedback summaries when too few people respond, protecting respondent anonymity.
entries.explanation:
Adversarial audit of the 360 feedback module (12 findings). All app-layer, no
migration (the archived enum + archived_at column already exist).

Critical - anonymity de-anonymisation in the deliverable
- The pooled "Others" mean (overall + per-competency + per-behaviour) had no
  distinct-contributor floor, so a lone peer's exact average was published as
  "Others' view" while the per-group table dutifully hid it. Now withheld (and
  the combined overall too, to block inversion from self + counts) whenever it
  is carried by 1..min_n-1 distinct SENSITIVE (non-self, non-manager) raters.
- Per-behaviour verbatim comments were gated on the SCORER count, not the
  COMMENTER count, so a single peer comment among 3 scoring peers leaked. Now
  gated on distinct commenters per role.

Major
- loadReflectFrameworkForEngagement server action had no owner gate - a
  consultant could pull another's full framework by id. Now requireEngagementOwner.
- Strength/blind-spot rankings gated on a hardcoded >= 2 raters; now >= min_n.
- Rater field window never closed: requireRater now rejects writes past
  field_window_end, so a stale link can't overwrite reported aggregates.
- Reassessment guard checked "completed" but the enum value is "complete", so
  the primary year-on-year case was always blocked. Fixed the string.

Minor
- Participant PDF route now authorises before computing scoring, and the guard
  returns a uniform 403 for missing-vs-unauthorised (no existence oracle).
- Added an Archive action + button so the retention purge (which only matches
  archived rows) is reachable - it was a permanent no-op.
- Rater form now states the anonymity/aggregation promise before rating.
- Open-question saves register with the submit flush (no lost answers) and
  surface a retry on failure instead of failing silently.
- Gamified rating buttons are now a real radiogroup (role/aria-checked).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add DARE + EQ lens to Reflect report and AC engagement detail
entries.plain: Reflect 360 reports now include DARE and EQ lens analysis in both assessment center engagement summaries and full feedback reports.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect: instant rater selection + hide guided-demo pill on reports
entries.plain: Rater selection in Reflect 360 now highlights immediately when you click, and the guided-demo prompt no longer appears on reports or when printing.
entries.explanation:
- Rater form: the selection highlight now paints immediately on click. The
  save was being fired INSIDE the setAnswers updater, which entangled the
  visual commit with the server round-trip (a multi-second lag). State now
  updates purely and the save runs after, via an answersRef. Applies to both
  the standard and gamified forms.
- Guided demo: never render the launcher pill (or rail) on report routes - it
  was overlaying reports on screen and leaking into the printed PDF.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect 360: match cohort + individual reports to client slides
entries.plain: Reflect 360 reports for teams and individuals now match the client presentation slides exactly, including an executive summary and detailed contents page.
entries.explanation:
- Cohort report: add an "Executive summary" page mirroring the Insights
  slide one-to-one - left "At a glance" (top strengths, top dev areas,
  heatmap, % below/within/above, year-on-year delta with live headlines)
  and a dark "How the CHRO uses it - Three conversations" panel (CEO /
  L&D budget owner / line manager) with live data hooks; CHRO-briefing
  branding.
- Individual report: add a "What's in this report" 12-section contents
  page mirroring the per-leader slide (12 sections, bilingual,
  confidential to the leader and their VIFM coach).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect 360 audit: rater token integrity + scoring + a11y/back nav
entries.plain: Reflect 360 has been audited for rater token security, scoring accuracy, and navigation accessibility to ensure data integrity and user experience.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Deep-review fixes: HIGH RPC lockdown + MEDIUM email-delivered gating + LOW hardening
entries.plain: Critical security fixes prevent unauthorized users from tampering with seat allocations across different organizations and strengthen email verification controls.
entries.explanation:
HIGH (security) - migration 00152 (already applied live + verified): the
allocation seat RPCs (claim/release_allocation_seats) were SECURITY DEFINER,
trusted a caller-supplied p_org, and were PUBLIC-executable - a logged-in
client_manager could call them from the browser with another org's id and
tamper with that org's seat ledger. REVOKE EXECUTE FROM public/authenticated +
GRANT to service_role only; add the missing non-negative-count guard to release.
Verified: claim/release now auth=false, service_role=true.

MEDIUM - email mock-mode misreport: sendAraEmail/sendReflectEmail now return a
 flag (true only on a real send; console-mock returns ok:true but
delivered:false). ARC seat, Reflect seat, and Reflect resend now count emails
+ stamp invited_at only on , so 'sent N' is honest and a mock no
longer makes the resend sweep skip raters.

LOW: ARC orphan-respondent delete now checked (keeps seat charged if delete
fails) + server logging on swallowed errors; ARC pool voucher guards an empty
batch (no '?code=undefined' link); Reflect inviteReflect catch is created-aware
(no seat claw-back after participants commit); seat-actions admin orgParam
UUID-validated; portal service page pinned LTR (was RTL+English mixed).
Deferred: the /api default-deny middleware hardening (no current leak; risky to
bundle).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect: resend invitation email always uses an absolute link
entries.plain: Fixed invitation emails in Reflect 360 so they now contain working clickable links that raters can use from their inbox.
entries.explanation:
The resend email built its link from NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL only; if that env
is unset baseUrl was "" and the emailed /reflect/respond/<token> link was
relative (broken in an inbox). Fall back through NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL to the
production domain so the invitation always carries a clickable absolute URL.
(The in-app Copy-link already used window.location.origin and was correct.)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix Puppeteer PDF 500 on Render (ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR)
entries.plain: Fixed Reflect 360 and ARC PDF reports that were crashing on production servers - they now generate successfully.
entries.explanation:
Reflect/ARA PDF routes render by navigating Puppeteer to their own SSR
report page via new URL(req.url).origin. On Render the edge terminates TLS
and the app speaks HTTP on $PORT, so that origin comes back as
https://localhost:10000 - Puppeteer hits an HTTP port over HTTPS and every
report 500s with net::ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR.

Add selfOrigin(reqUrl) in pdf-browser.ts: downgrade the scheme to http for
loopback hosts only (public domains + local-dev http://localhost:3000 are
