untouched). Wire it into all four page.goto report routes (Reflect
participant + cohort + framework, ARA report).

Diagnosed live: GET /api/reflect/reports/<pid>/pdf -> 500
{"error":"net::ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR at https://localhost:10000/..."}.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect: per-rater invitation links + resend on engagement detail
entries.plain: Reflect 360 now shows each rater's personal invitation link on the engagement detail page so consultants can share links directly if email delivery is delayed.
entries.explanation:
Diagnosed a real engagement (Live, 2 raters): invitations were SENT via
Graph (email_log status=sent) but didn't reach the inbox - a deliverability
problem, with no in-app fallback to recover. Add a 'Rater invitations'
section listing each rater with a Copy-link button (built from
window.location.origin so it's always absolute) + a 'Resend pending'
control, so a consultant can hand a rater their private rating link
directly when email is delayed/spam-filed. Read-only consultant-owned data.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect: add a Launch button to the engagement detail page
entries.plain: Added a Launch button to Reflect 360 engagement detail pages so draft engagements can now go live without navigating back to the wizard.
entries.explanation:
Launch previously existed only in the wizard's final step, so a draft you
navigated away from could never go live (no invitations sent) - a likely reason
an engagement "does nothing". The detail page now shows a "Launch engagement"
button for draft engagements (confirm -> runs the existing launchReflectEngagement,
which guards >=1 participant + rater and invites all raters incl. the auto-
created self). Verified end-to-end: launched a draft from the detail page ->
status went live and the self-rater's invited_at was set.

tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect: auto-create the self-rater so every participant rates themselves
entries.plain: Reflect 360 now automatically creates a self-rating slot for each participant so every person rates themselves as required.
entries.explanation:
A 360 must include the participant's self-assessment, but adding a participant
never created a self-rater - "self" was a manual rater row the consultant had
to add by hand (and the default rater rows are manager/peer/direct-report), so
in the normal flow no self-rating ever existed and no self-invite was sent.

Fix:
- ensureSelfRatersForParticipants(): idempotent helper that creates a
  rater_role="self" row (email/name = the participant's) for any participant
  lacking one.
- Called when participants are added (bulkUpsertReflectParticipants) so the
  self-assessment exists immediately, and again at launch as a backfill for
  participants added before this change.
- bulkUpsertReflectRaters dedupes explicit "self" rows against the auto-created
  one so the self view is never double-counted.
- People-step tip updated: self is now automatic.

The existing invitation loop already emails self-raters, so the participant now
receives their self-assessment link on launch like any other rater.
tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect: fix gamified rater unable to submit despite "All cards done"
entries.plain: Fixed a bug in gamified Reflect 360 ratings where fast raters could not submit their assessment despite completing all cards.
entries.explanation:
Root cause: a double-advance race. Rating a card schedules an auto-advance
(220ms); if the rater also taps Next or rates quickly (mobile-first), `index`
jumps twice and skips the next card. That card stays unrated, but the rater
still reaches the end - where the "All cards done" badge is gated on
`index >= total`, not on `allRated` - so the badge shows while submit stays
disabled with "Rate every card."

Two fixes:
- Guard the auto-advance: only advance if still on the just-answered card
  (capture `from = index`; the timer no-ops if Next already moved past it), so
  no card is skipped.
- Recovery at the final step: when not all cards are rated, replace the "All
  cards done" badge with "N cards still need a rating" + a button that jumps to
  the first unrated card - the rater can never be stranded on a done-looking
  screen that won't submit.

tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Docs: note migrations continue through 00144
entries.plain: Updated documentation to note that system migrations have been completed through version 144.
entries.explanation:
The migration list in CLAUDE.md was illustrative to 00057; add a note that the
authoritative set continues through 00144 (Reflect 360, AP framework,
psychometrics, prehire/technical vouchers, ARC talent-lens) and that all are
applied on prod, with newer-column reads written migration-tolerant.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Assessment Center: three setup/run guards from the audit
entries.plain: Assessment Center now prevents engagements from launching with zero competencies or ratings, ensuring every assessment measures something meaningful.
entries.explanation:
Mirrors the Reflect "launch with nobody" hardening - stop an AC engagement
progressing through empty/meaningless states:

- OAR requires consensus: saveOarAction now refuses to save an overall rating
  until at least one consensus rating exists for the candidate (the OAR is the
  synthesis of the per-competency consensus, meaningless with none - also blocks
  the zero-competency -> zero-consensus -> OAR-anyway path).
- Activation requires content: updateEngagementStatusAction now refuses status
  -> 'active' unless the engagement has at least one competency AND one exercise,
  so an engagement edited/created down to empty can't go live and hand candidates
  a blank schedule with nothing to score.
- Wash-up needs observations: the wash-up page shows a clear "No observations
  recorded yet" state (with a link to assessor assignments) when competencies
  exist but no integration worksheet has been submitted, instead of a blank
  consensus grid that invites a baseless OAR.

tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect 360: guard empty launches + add-a-person form + no-raters banner
entries.plain: Reflect 360 now prevents launches with no participants or raters and adds an in-app form to add people without uploading a spreadsheet.
entries.explanation:
Audit found launched engagements with 0 participants/raters: the raters answer
the questions, so an engagement with none goes live but shows no assessment /
no questions. Three fixes:
- launchReflectEngagement now refuses to go live when participants=0 or
  raters=0, with a clear actionable message (was: silently flipped to live).
- Wizard step 4 gains an "Add a person" form (participant + their raters) so
  people can be added without crafting a CSV; reuses the proven
  bulkUpsertReflectParticipants/Raters actions. CSV import stays for bulk.
- Engagement detail shows a prominent "No raters yet - nobody can respond"
  banner when raterCount=0, pointing to the People step.

The rater respond form itself was verified working (renders all 20 question
cards with the frequency scale). tsc + ESLint clean; banner verified live.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Platform: portal comparison sheet (Talent Acquisition vs Talent Development)
entries.plain: Added a new comparison page showing how each VIFM portal works for talent selection versus development, helping you choose the right tool.
entries.explanation:
New /compare page under the platform: a bilingual on-page matrix of every portal
with its selection (Talent Acquisition) and development (Talent Management) lens
side by side - what it measures, how it runs, who takes it + scale, the reporting
& output (highlighted column), and any credential. Single-purpose portals
(Pre-Hire = selection only; Reflect 360 / Succession Readiness / Academy =
development only) flag the missing lens as not applicable. Lens badges reuse the
landing brand pair (light blue #5391D5 = acquisition, dark blue #111232 =
development). EN/AR with RTL + a back-to-platform link; reached from the landing
header and footer ("Compare portals").

Verified in-browser: table renders, N/A rows correct, RTL Arabic, lint + tsc clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix Puppeteer PDF 500s on Render (use @sparticuz/chromium in prod)
entries.plain: Fixed PDF generation failures for all VIFM assessments on production servers including Reflect 360, Pre-Hire, and ARC reports.
entries.explanation:
Every Puppeteer-rendered PDF (ARA reports, Reflect 360, Pre-Hire candidate
report + client email, technical programme/cohort) 500s in production with
"Could not find Chrome (ver. 147.x) ... /opt/render/.cache/puppeteer".

Root cause: `puppeteer.launch()` relies on the Chromium that the puppeteer
package downloads into the HOME cache at install time. Render persists
node_modules between build and runtime but NOT the home cache, so Chromium is
absent at runtime and the launch throws. (Confirmed live: the Pre-Hire report
endpoint returns HTTP 500 with that error; "Send to client" therefore never
emails - the PDF build fails before email is attempted, which is the real
cause of the "didn't receive the report" report.)

Fix: a shared launchPdfBrowser() (src/lib/reports/pdf-browser.ts) launches
bundled puppeteer Chromium in dev and @sparticuz/chromium@147 (already a
dependency, version-matched to the wanted Chrome 147) in production. Its
Chromium binary lives inside node_modules, so it survives the build->runtime
hand-off with no install step, env var, or Render dashboard change. Selected
when RENDER / NODE_ENV=production / USE_SPARTICUZ_CHROMIUM=1.

All 10 runtime launch sites now route through the helper. Arabic shaping is
unaffected - the Arabic PDFs load the Noto Naskh webfont and await fonts.ready,
so HarfBuzz shapes from the loaded font, not the Chromium build's system fonts.

Verified the dev (bundled) path returns a valid 162KB PDF through the helper;
tsc clean. Production verification to follow after Render auto-deploys.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: QA fixes: AC report release + Reflect IDOR + admin/CBI access control
entries.plain: Fixed three critical issues: Assessment Center candidate reports are now viewable, Reflect 360 access controls are enforced, and admin permissions work correctly.
entries.explanation:
Addresses the defects found in the end-to-end QA pass (verified against the
live code paths; routes re-checked live for no regression).

Critical 1 - AC candidate reports were never viewable in-app. The candidate
report viewer gates on a candidate_reports row with status='released', but
nothing ever wrote that table (the Share / Auto-Share buttons only toasted).
- Add admin-gated releaseReportAction + releaseAllReportsAction (update-or-
  insert candidate_reports -> released; best-effort candidate notification).
- Wire both Share buttons to call them. Candidates now see their report once
  released; clients likewise (RLS already allowed released-only reads).

Critical 2 - Reflect 360 IDOR. Every /reflect/consultant/* page fetched via
the service-role client using only the URL id, with no role/ownership check
and no layout guard, so any logged-in user could read any participant's
confidential 360 report / cohort / engagement / IDP.
- Add canAccessReflectEngagement / canAccessReflectParticipant guards
  (admin, or the owning consultant) and gate all six pages (report,
  cohort-report, engagement detail, framework-preview, preview-rater, idp).
- The Puppeteer PDF routes (already gated at the route level) now forward the
  requester's session cookies to Chromium so the now-gated SSR pages still
  render under the verified owner.

High 3 - admin surface has no role gate (chrome-only layout; middleware
enforces auth not role). Service-role admin pages leaked data.
- Gate /admin/courses/quotes (+ [id]) with an admin check (sales-lead PII).
- Add requireAdmin to the previously-ungated engagement mutation actions
  (createAssignment, addDemoAssessor [service-role!], removeCandidate,
  deleteAssignment, updateEngagementStatus) - the service-role one was a real
  hole; the rest are defense-in-depth atop RLS.

High 4 - CBI actions used service-role with no role/ownership gate, and
cbi_sessions RLS let any assessor touch any session.
- Add role gate (assessor/admin) + assignment-ownership check to
  persist/approve/discard actions; scope the assignment loader + gate the page.
- Migration 00112 (write-only; user applies): tighten cbi_sessions RLS to the
  owning assignment.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect 360: verifiable completion credential (to ARC bar)
entries.plain: Reflect 360 participants now receive a verifiable completion credential they can share, matching the credential feature in ARC.
entries.explanation:
Closes Reflect's only gap vs the ARC standalone bar - it now issues a
verifiable Reflect 360 completion credential to the participant when their
report is generated:
- issueReflect360Credential() (best-effort, idempotent per participant)
- wired into the participant-report PDF route after the report is persisted
- ai-readiness-style: reflect_360 added to the credential type, verify page,
  and certificate PDF labels (EN/AR)
- migration 00107 widens the credential_type CHECK. Inert until applied.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Reflect: register clients first-class via the shared registry
entries.plain: Reflect 360 client organizations now register in the shared registry so they can be reused across all VIFM services.
entries.explanation:
Inline org creation in the engagement wizard now dual-writes through
createClientOrganization (organizations + ara_organizations, createdBy
stamped) instead of writing ara_organizations only - so a Reflect-
registered client is reusable across every VIFM service, matching the ARC
standalone pattern.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add five open-ended questions to Reflect 360
entries.plain: Reflect 360 now includes five open-ended questions capturing strengths, development areas, and advice alongside the standard rating scales.
entries.explanation:
A shared OpenQuestionsBlock collects five qualitative questions (strengths,
development, a leadership/collaboration example, one piece of advice, anything
else), bilingual with self/other phrasing, autosaving on blur. Wired into both
the standard and gamified rater forms alongside Start/Stop/Continue. Migration
00101 adds the columns; the save action, loader, scoring aggregation and the
participant report's verbatim page all extend to the five. Anonymity threshold
applies identically. Verified end to end (rater form renders the five; report
renders the five sections + verbatims). Tolerant of 00101 not being applied.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Customer & Stakeholder Focus as the 9th competency cluster
entries.plain: Reflect 360 now includes Customer Focus as a new competency area, so behavioral assessments and 360 feedback measure the same nine skill categories.
entries.explanation:
Extends the canonical framework (now 9 clusters / 41 competencies) with
Customer Orientation, Stakeholder Management and Value Creation under the
RESULTS domain, so self (Persona), others (Reflect 360) and role profiles
can all evaluate them on one framework. Migration 00100 (cluster + 3
competencies + dev tips, deterministic UUIDs) plus the matching Persona
bank items (12 bilingual items). Migration applied + verified.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Wire Succession Readiness combined mode into the engagement UI
entries.plain: You can now link a Reflect 360 feedback survey to an Assessment Center evaluation to combine both leadership measurements into one readiness report.
entries.explanation:
The combined service had every engine piece but no way to assemble it.
Add a Succession Readiness setup panel on the AC engagement detail:
- assessment_mode toggle (standalone/combined) via setAssessmentModeAction
- "Link a Reflect 360" via linkReflectEngagementAction, which sets
  engagements.reflect_engagement_id, bridges reflect_participants.candidate_id
  by email/name, maps reflect_competencies.ac_competency_id by name, and
  suppresses the 360 self-rater on bridged participants (combined = Persona self)
- per-candidate status (role bound / Persona / 360 raters / readiness tier)
  with a per-candidate readiness-report link (was previously unreachable)
- migration 00099: engagements.reflect_engagement_id + reflect_participants.suppress_self

Tolerant of 00099 not being applied. Verified end to end on the demo
engagement: link bridges + 360 column populates + combined recompute.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Readiness Slices 2-4: 360 framework seed, report, behavioral runner
entries.plain: Reflect 360 now has a full feedback framework with observer questions and a dedicated readiness report page for each candidate.
entries.explanation:
Slice 2 - 38-competency 360 framework: 00092 seeds the Reflect template
mirroring the AC catalogue (each reflect_competency linked by
ac_competency_id; fixed a uuid LIKE that errors in Postgres -> c.id::text
LIKE). 00093 expands to 4 observer items per competency (152), self->
observer EN+AR from the item bank; source reverse-keyed items re-keyed
positive (Reflect scorer has no reverse handling).

Slice 3 - readiness report: per-candidate page at
/admin/engagements/[id]/readiness/[candidateId] (tier, weighted gap,
per-competency table, self-vs-Others, blind spots/hidden strengths,
bilingual, SVG icons) + a "Succession readiness" cohort panel on the
talent-map page (read-only compute; 9-box maths untouched).

Slice 4 - behavioral self-assessment runner: 00094 (sessions/responses/
per-competency rollup; idempotent/re-runnable - guarded CREATE TYPE +
IF NOT EXISTS + DROP/CREATE policies). behavioral-items.ts (152 first-
person items EN+AR, reverse flags, cluster-grouped), behavioral.ts
scoring (reverse 6-raw), candidate runner (autosave/resume) + entry on
the skills page. readiness-data.ts step 6 now reads combined-mode self
from behavioral_competency_scores; standalone keeps the 360 self.

Migrations dry-run verified against real Postgres (rolled back); 00092+
00093 idempotent (38 competencies, 152 behaviours), 00094 re-runs clean.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(reflect): consultant 'Preview rater experience' button — no-save gamified preview
entries.plain: Consultants can now preview the 360 feedback rater experience before sending it to actual raters, without creating test accounts or sending invitations.
entries.explanation:
Adds a preview-rater route that renders the gamified flow using the engagement's
real framework in no-save mode (synthetic rater, preview guards on all writes),
plus a button on the engagement page. Lets consultants eyeball the gamified
360 without creating raters or sending invites. tsc clean.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(reflect): opt-in gamified 360 rater experience
entries.plain: Reflect 360 now offers a mobile-friendly card-by-card feedback experience as an optional alternative to the standard grid layout.
entries.explanation:
Adds a per-engagement 'gamified_mode' toggle (consultant Step 1). When on,
raters get a one-card-at-a-time, mobile-first flow with progress + momentum
instead of the long Likert grid. Same items, same server actions, same
reflect_responses rows — scoring/reporting/anonymity unchanged. Existing flow
untouched when off.

- migration 00072: gamified_mode column
- GamifiedRaterForm component + branch in respond/[token]
- toggle wired through wizard/validation/create action + i18n (en/ar)
tsc --noEmit clean.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat: migrate competency framework to v2 (rename clusters/competencies by UUID + sync Reflect bridge + runtime name refs)
entries.plain: The competency framework has been updated and synced across all assessment services to ensure consistent skill definitions.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Guided Start wizard (additive front door) with inline create
entries.plain: A new guided wizard helps you choose the right assessment process in plain language, while keeping the original detailed setup pages available.
entries.explanation:
A new, fully additive entry point at /admin/start that helps an admin pick the
right process — without changing any existing module create-flow.

- Fork at the top: "Guide me" (the wizard) vs "Set it up myself" (a menu linking
  straight to the untouched per-module create pages).
- Wizard: 4 steps (goal -> details -> rigor -> recommendation) with a progress
  stepper, back/start-over, and one CTA per step. A pure, testable resolver
  (src/lib/start/resolver.ts) maps the selections to the diagnosed requirement +
  the process (registry is the single source of truth; new modules register one
  entry).
- Inline create (Full-B) reuses each module's OWN server action — no duplicated
  logic: Pre-Hire (createRequisitionAction -> lands in the requisition), AR Compass
  org (createAraAssessment -> drafts + redirects into the assessment), Reflect 360
  (createReflectEngagement, clones the seeded leadership framework -> lands in the
  engagement). Fluent + Technical launch their runners. AC engagement + personal-AI
  hand off to their own flows (AC genuinely needs its full competency/exercise/
  matrix builder, so an honest pre-warmed handoff rather than a fragile rebuild).
- Sidebar entry "Start an assessment" near the top of the admin nav.

v1 wizard copy is English literals (the admin portal renders EN-only at runtime;
the route-aware i18n only localizes /candidate + /ara/respond); full t() i18n is a
fast-follow. tsc + next lint + EN/AR locale parity (3784) clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add VIFM-branded user manuals for the six service portals
entries.plain: VIFM now provides illustrated user manuals for all six assessment services to help your team and candidates understand how to use each one.
entries.explanation:
Six PowerPoint manuals under docs/manuals/ — Assessment Center, Pre-Hire,
Fluent, Technical Assessment, Reflect 360 and AI Readiness. Each is
comprehensive (end-user + admin/operator), VIFM-branded, and visually
QA'd slide-by-slide. The PptxGenJS generator engine lives in .pptx-build/
(gitignored) — not part of the app.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Update sidebar logo tagline to Talent Intelligence Platform
entries.plain: The platform tagline now reads 'Talent Intelligence Platform' to reflect that it includes six different assessment services, not just Assessment Center.
entries.explanation:
The portal now spans Pre-Hire, Fluent, Assessment Center, Reflect 360,
AI Readiness, and Technical Certification, so the logo tagline "Assessment
Center" understated the platform. Scoped to the only showTagline usage.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Pre-Hire tile to the platform landing page
entries.plain: Pre-Hire screening is now visible as a fifth service tile on the platform home page, making all assessment options equally accessible.
entries.explanation:
Surface Pre-Hire as a fifth peer service on the launcher (own tone-rose
hue + UserSearch icon → /admin/prehire), bilingual copy + tooltip, and a
3-column desktop grid so all five tiles stay on one screen. Order the
tiles Pre-Hire → Fluent → Assessment Center → Reflect 360 → AI Readiness
and match the hero subheadline (EN + AR) to that order.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Translate the assessor portal + Reflect 360 console to Arabic (RTL)
entries.plain: The Reflect 360 platform and Assessment Center rater tools now support full Arabic translation and right-to-left display.
entries.explanation:
Assessor portal: shell/nav, dashboard, assignments, observation form,
integration worksheet, and the wash-up engine. Reflect 360 console:
consultant dashboard + engagement detail, the 5-step engagement wizard,
admin/templates, and the IDP editor. Report pages that were already
bilingual (participant report, cohort report, framework preview) were
left untouched. Gate enabled for /assessor, /client, and the Reflect
console routes. ~470 new EN/AR keys (2522 total at parity); tsc clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire audit fixes: required-stage completeness gate + retention, close guard, i18n/a11y
entries.plain: Pre-hire screening now correctly requires all mandatory stages to be completed before candidates can advance, even if those stages have no point value.
entries.explanation:
Adversarial audit of the pre-employment screening module (6 findings, 0
critical - the guardrails and token boundary held). All app-layer, no migration.

Major
- A required stage with weight 0 could be skipped entirely yet the candidate
  still scored "advance": the completeness gate only inspected weighted stages,
  and a not-taken stage read as passed=null (not a failure). Now any required
  stage that is unscored forces "incomplete" and nulls the composite - a
  required hurdle can no longer be silently skipped.

Minor
- Retention purge now deletes voucher-redemption PII (name/email/IP) for purged
  candidates - the FK is ON DELETE SET NULL, so it survived the candidate delete.
- quiz/fluent/cbi submit routes now reject a closed/archived requisition, for
  parity with the start routes (a stage begun while open could still be scored
  after close).
- Candidate apply flow is now bilingual EN/AR with a language toggle + RTL: the
  quiz (fully) and Fluent task instructions render the Arabic the backend
  already ships; consent/CBI chrome localised.
- Quiz answer options are now a real radiogroup (role=radio + aria-checked +
  aria-labelledby) so screen-reader users can perceive selection and grouping.
- Voluntary demographics card states explicitly it cannot influence the result
  (it is shown before the assessment for administrative reasons).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Wrap Pre-Hire voucher issuance in the 3-step wizard
entries.plain: Pre-Hire assessment codes can now be created and distributed in three simple steps, whether sending to clients or directly to job applicants.
entries.explanation: Role+label, deliver-to-client vs to-applicants, generate batch then email/copy to client, or upload/paste applicant emails and email each a link. Action now returns the codes for the client-batch email.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire audit Batch 2: apply-flow + admin UX/UI polish
entries.plain: Pre-Hire interface and workflows have been refined for a cleaner, more intuitive admin experience.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire audit B1.5: close client RLS leak + harden demo/fluent/cbi
entries.plain: Removed unused security rules that could have exposed candidate data - Pre-Hire screening is now more secure.
entries.explanation:
Migration 00163:
- D-5/D-6/D-7/D-1: drop the unused 'client' SELECT policies on
  prehire_requisitions/candidates/stage_results. RLS is row-level only
  and cannot hide demographic / decision / report_sent_to / detail
  columns; a codebase sweep confirmed every read goes through the
  service-role client + app-layer org checks, so these policies were
  pure latent attack surface. Fail-closed (zero rows for a client JWT).
- I-8: add prehire_requisitions.is_demo flag + backfill existing demos
  by title; candidate self-view of results/report now gates on the
  explicit flag, not a brittle title match.

Code:
- I-8: findCandidateByToken resolves is_demo tolerantly (flag wins,
  legacy title fallback only pre-migration); result/report/apply gate
  on it; startPrehireDemoAction stamps is_demo=true (best-effort).
- S-6: sanitize the client-supplied Fluent pronunciation score - gate
  on isAzureSpeechConfigured() (else discard) + clamp every sub-score
  to [0,100] so it can't inflate the speaking band.
- I-9: hard server-side cap on the CBI transcript - refuse answers
  beyond MAX_CANDIDATE_ANSWERS (no extra model call / DB write) and
  clamp each answer to 4000 chars.
- V-5: per-IP rate limit (10 / 15 min) on the public voucher-redeem
  action via a new reusable src/lib/security/rate-limit.ts.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire audit Batch 1: security + integrity hardening (migrations 00161-00162)
entries.plain: Fixed two security vulnerabilities: voucher codes are now locked down, and completed screening stages cannot be edited.
entries.explanation:
Voucher RPCs (00161): prehire_voucher_claim/release recreated with
SET search_path = public and EXECUTE locked to service_role only -
closes the search_path injection and privilege-escalation vectors
found in the audit (same pattern as 00157/00158/00159/00160).

Stage immutability (00162): BEFORE UPDATE trigger on
prehire_stage_results refuses writes once status='completed',
closing the TOCTOU double-submit race (mirrors candidate_quiz_attempts
in 00019). Adds normalized_score range CHECK [0,100], audit-log
DELETE guard (BEFORE DELETE trigger + FK ON DELETE SET NULL so
cascade can't silently erase the defensibility record), and an
explicit client_manager policy on prehire_audit_log.

Demographics isolation (00162): the client-portal RLS policy on
prehire_candidates previously granted SELECT on ALL columns,
including the voluntary demographic fields added in 00051. Replaced
with a scoped policy that excludes gender/age_band/nationality_group/
decision_reason/report_sent_to. Matching restriction on
prehire_stage_results to keep raw detail JSONB (transcripts, quiz
answer keys) out of client-role reads.

Consent enforcement: all six stage routes (quiz/start, quiz/submit,
fluent/start, fluent/submit, cbi/turn, cbi/submit) now gate on
ctx.candidate.consent_at != null and return HTTP 403 if missing -
server-side enforcement of the UAE PDPL / GDPR consent requirement
previously only enforced in the UI layer. Consent route made
idempotent (re-submission preserves the original timestamp).

Requisition status gate: findCandidateByToken now reads
requisition.status; all three start routes (quiz, fluent, cbi)
return HTTP 403 when status != 'open', preventing candidates from
submitting against closed/archived requisitions.

Answer-key hygiene: quiz/submit no longer stores correct_index in
the review object or in detail.questions after completion - only
{id, picked, isCorrect} is stored. The full keyed deck is available
server-side if a human review ever needs it but never lands in the
completed row.

CBI score bounds: bars_rating is clamped to [1,5] before normalization
and persistence, guarding against AI hallucination or prompt injection
returning an out-of-range value.

DEMO_REQ_TITLE: local duplicate constant in admin/prehire/actions.ts
removed; import from the canonical export in candidate-access.ts.

rescoreCandidate: update error is now checked and thrown rather than
silently discarded, so submit routes can surface a DB write failure.

PII prefill: redeem page no longer accepts name/email from URL params
(phishing vector fix from pre-audit scouting).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire CBI: cap interactive turn wait so a slow API never freezes the screen
entries.plain: The AI interview no longer freezes on slow API responses - it moves to the next question after 20 seconds.
entries.explanation:
Live verification of ca217a6 confirmed turns no longer hiccup-loop (they return
real, contextual questions), but during a sustained Anthropic 529 overload the
SDK retries pushed single turns to 30-45s+ - a frozen-looking spinner that reads
as "not working" to a candidate. Add a 20s wall-clock cap on the interactive
interviewer turn: if the LLM (incl. retries) exceeds it, abandon the in-flight
call and instantly serve the next scripted STAR question so the interview keeps
moving. Scoring is a one-shot end step and keeps the full retry budget (no cap).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire: edit a built requisition's stage plan
entries.plain: You can now edit which screening stages (quiz, English, AI interview) run after a requisition is created.
entries.explanation:
Adds an 'Edit stages' editor on the requisition detail page so a built
requisition's screening stages can be added/removed after creation
(Competency Quiz / English Fluent / AI Interview), with per-stage weight,
cut-score, and Fluent sub-skills - mirroring the create wizard. New
updateRequisitionStagesAction reuses the same stageEntrySchema +
receptive-skill rule, sets english_required from the Fluent stage, and
re-scores every candidate from the new plan (removed stage drops from the
composite; added stage shows incomplete until taken; collected results
kept). Non-editable stage kinds (e.g. assessment_center) are preserved.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire: quiz never dead-ends; speaking type-fallback word counter
entries.plain: Demo screening no longer fails - it now provides backup questions if the AI is unavailable, and speaking fallback counts words correctly.
entries.explanation:
Issue 1 - demo quiz stuck on 'Couldn't generate the assessment': the quiz
depended entirely on a live Claude call (generateQuizQuestions returns null
on no-key/quota/overload/timeout) and 503'd when it failed. Add
buildFallbackQuizDeck() - a deterministic deck grounded in the role's real
behavioural indicators (positive=>True, negative=>False) padded with
role-agnostic professional items - and serve it instead of the 503 so the
screening always proceeds.

Issue 3 - speaking type-fallback: when transcription fails the candidate is
told to type, but the same min-5-words gate applied with no visible counter,
so a short blob left Submit disabled with no explanation. Add a live word
counter ('N words / min 5 to submit') mirroring the Writing box.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire demo: show results on-screen + report download on completion
entries.plain: Demo candidates now see their results and can download a PDF report immediately after finishing.
entries.explanation:
Match the Techno demo: when a candidate finishes the self-serve DEMO
screening, the completion screen now shows the composite signal, the
advisory recommendation, per-stage scores, and a Download report (PDF)
button - instead of just 'thank you, go back elsewhere'.

- new token endpoints /api/prehire/[token]/result (JSON) + /report (PDF),
  both DEMO-ONLY (requisition title = 'Demo Screening (self-serve)'); a real
  screening returns 403 so the 'results go to the hiring team, not the
  candidate' guardrail holds
- apply-flow renders the result card (demo only); real flow unchanged
- scripts/seed-demo.ts: CLI runner for the demo seeder (reuses seedDemoData)

Verified on preview: composite 75/100 + stages render; report -> 200 PDF.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Techno + Pre-Hire: self-serve "start assessment" on the landing (demo)
entries.plain: Start buttons for Techno and Pre-Hire demo assessments are now front-and-centre on the landing page - no voucher needed.
entries.explanation:
Match the front-and-centre self-serve entry the other instruments (Persona,
Fluent, Logica, ARC) already have - no link or voucher needed.

- Techno: a "Start an assessment now" CTA on /admin/tech-sandbox links straight
  to the runner /ac/tech-assessment, which already supports anonymous sittings.
- Pre-Hire: a "Start demo screening" button on /admin/prehire calls a new
  startPrehireDemoAction that reuses the shared demo org, find-or-creates a
  "Demo Screening (self-serve)" requisition, mints a fresh anonymous candidate,
  and drops straight into the real apply flow (consent -> quiz -> Fluent -> CBI).
  Everything lands under the demo org, so the Demo-data purge removes it.

Verified on preview: Techno CTA -> runner; Pre-Hire button -> auto-provisions
-> apply flow with the consent gate. tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire: SME review -> Certify workflow (#3 of 3)
entries.plain: A VIFM assessor can now review and certify each candidate's AI-scored results before they are finalized.
entries.explanation:
The defensible tier above the raw AI report: a VIFM assessor reviews the
candidate's AI-scored responses and certifies the result.

- Migration 00145: prehire_candidates.certified_at / certified_by /
  certification_notes (tolerant; reads no-op pre-migration).
- Reviewer page /admin/prehire/[id]/candidate/[candidateId]/review: shows the
  per-stage AI scores, the CBI transcript + AI assessment, any Fluent
  writing/speaking, and a certify form (reviewer name + notes).
- certifyPrehireCandidateAction (admin-gated, audited as candidate_certified).
- getPrehireCertification() tolerant read; the per-candidate report (full +
  summary) now carries a green "Certified - SME-reviewed by <name> on <date>"
  banner once certified.
- Requisition shortlist: a "Review" action per candidate + a "Certified" badge.

Completes the three Pre-Hire outputs: summary sheet (d18eff2), AI detailed
report (145ebeb), SME-reviewed certified report (this). tsc + ESLint clean.
NOTE: migration 00145 still needs applying to the DB.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire: add a one-page summary-sheet output (#1 of 3)
entries.plain: Reports now include a one-page summary sheet for quick review, alongside the detailed report.
entries.explanation:
Adds a condensed at-a-glance sheet alongside the existing detailed report:
header, the composite + advisory band, a compact per-stage table, and the
guardrail line - no methodology / transcript / band table.

- renderPrehireSummaryHtml() in prehire-candidate-html.ts (self-contained,
  reuses the same labels/tones, EN + AR).
- buildPrehireCandidatePdf gains a mode: "full" | "summary" (default full);
  the report route honours ?view=summary; summary PDFs get a -summary- filename.
- A "Summary" trigger sits next to "Report" on each candidate row.

#2 (AI detailed report) already shipped (145ebeb). #3 (SME review + certify)
next. tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire report: show AI-interview transcript + drop false "assessor" claim
entries.plain: Reports now show the full AI-interview conversation and correctly state that results are AI-scored (not human-reviewed in this step).
entries.explanation:
The client report described the behavioural interview as "AI-led and reviewed by
a qualified assessor" - but in the Pre-Hire funnel the CBI is purely AI-scored
(cbi/submit just runs scoreCbiInterview; no human reviews it), and the client
had nothing to review since the transcript was never shown.

Fix (client reviews the responses - matches the "a person always decides"
guardrail):
- Corrected the stage definition to "An AI-led structured behavioural (STAR)
  interview, AI-scored. The full transcript and the AI's assessment are included
  below for your review." (EN + AR).
- The report now renders an "AI Interview - transcript & assessment" section:
  the full Q&A exchange, the AI rating + rationale + strengths/development areas,
  and an explicit "AI-generated; a human reviewer should validate it" note. The
  data (history + score) was already stored on prehire_stage_results.detail.

tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire: center the Candidate column in the shortlist
entries.plain: Candidate names in the shortlist are now center-aligned for a cleaner, more consistent look.
entries.explanation:
Match the now-centered AI signal / Composite columns: center the Candidate
TableHead + TableCell, and justify-center the inner name+badge row so the
"Not invited" chip sits with the name. Verified centered in preview.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire: center the AI signal column in the shortlist
entries.plain: AI signal scores in the shortlist are now center-aligned to match the other columns.
entries.explanation:
The AI signal header + recommendation-badge cells were left-aligned while the
Composite column beside them is centered. Add text-center to the AI signal
TableHead and TableCell so the column reads consistently. Verified in preview.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire: generate only the kept items + raise gen cap so the deck completes
entries.plain: Quiz generation now creates only the questions needed and doesn't time out - the full deck completes reliably.
entries.explanation:
Follow-up to the parallelization fix. Dev-preview timing showed the quiz still
returned a partial deck (1-3 questions) or 503: each per-competency call was
generating the generator's default 7 questions while the route keeps only ~2,
and the 35s cap then truncated the slow calls.

- quiz-generator: new optional `count` (default 7, so candidate-quiz + academy
  are unchanged); the prompt now asks for exactly `count` items and drops the
  rigid 7-item mix for small counts.
- Pre-Hire quiz/start: request `count: wanted` per competency (only what it
  keeps) and raise the per-call cap 35s -> 60s so the parallel calls finish.

Verified on the dev preview: full 7-question deck, HTTP 200, ~40s warm
(previously a 503 at ~74s). tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire: parallelize quiz generation so Start doesn't hang on "Preparing"
entries.plain: Quiz questions now generate in parallel instead of one-by-one, cutting wait time from 60-120 seconds to roughly 20-30 seconds.
entries.explanation:
The quiz-start route generated the deck by calling generateQuizQuestions once
per competency SEQUENTIALLY (up to 4 serial Claude round-trips). On production
(API key set) that is easily 60-120s, which the candidate sees as a Start button
stuck on "Preparing" - the assessment never appears to begin.

- Generate every competency's deck in parallel (Promise.all) - total time drops
  to roughly one round-trip instead of the sum of four.
- Timeout-guard each generation (35s via Promise.race); the Anthropic SDK's own
  default timeout is minutes, so a single stalled call could otherwise hang the
  whole request. A timed-out competency just contributes no items, and the
  synthetic fallback is bounded the same way - so the route always responds
  (deck or a clean 503) rather than spinning forever.

tsc + ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire vouchers: client-distributable codes tied to a requisition
entries.plain: Pre-Hire now supports shareable voucher codes - you can distribute them to candidates just like other assessments.
entries.explanation:
Adds Pre-Hire to the voucher model that ARC/Techno/Fluent/Mentium/Persona
already use, and into the shared /admin/vouchers hub (the fifth... now sixth tile).

- Migration 00144 (applied): prehire_vouchers + prehire_voucher_redemptions
  tables, atomic prehire_voucher_claim / prehire_voucher_release RPCs, admin-only
  RLS. A voucher references a requisition (ON DELETE CASCADE).
- Lib src/lib/prehire/vouchers.ts: generate batch (anonymous seat-pool or named
  delegates), redeem (atomic claim -> provision a prehire_candidate on the
  requisition with invited_at stamped -> audit redemption -> return the
  candidate access_token; releases the seat on failure), + hub list helpers.
- Public no-account redeem flow at /prehire/redeem (page + server action + form),
  middleware-bypassed (isPreHireRedeemRoute); forwards to /prehire/apply/[token].
- Admin hub: Pre-Hire tile (UserSearch icon) + create form (pick requisition,
  codes x seats, label, expiry) + voucher list with copy-link + disable.

Verified end-to-end on the live DB: created a voucher via the hub (org derived
from the requisition), redeemed it no-account, seat claimed (0->1), candidate
provisioned on the requisition, redemption audited, applicant landed in the
consent->quiz->fluent->cbi->demographics flow. Test artifacts removed. tsc +
ESLint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix "Invalid requisition" - Zod 4 strict .uuid() rejects seed UUIDs
entries.plain: Fixed a bug where Pre-Hire requisitions were incorrectly rejected with an 'Invalid requisition' error - the system now correctly accepts the platform's internal identifier format.
entries.explanation:
Root cause: the seed competencies (a0000001-...), role profiles
(00000001-aaaa-...), domains + clusters all carry SYNTHETIC UUIDs whose
version/variant nibbles are 0. Zod 4's z.string().uuid() validates those
nibbles per RFC, so it rejected every one - silently failing any payload that
carried a seed id. The prehire requisition action only surfaced top-level
formErrors, so a field-level uuid failure showed the useless "Invalid
requisition" fallback.

This was systemic, not just prehire: engagement creation (competency/exercise
ids), wash-up consensus, and assessor observation/rating all validate seed ids
the same way and were equally broken.

- Add lib/validations/ids.ts: shared `uuidish` (permissive UUID-shape regex).
- prehire actions: organization_id / role_profile_id / competency_ids ->
  uuidish; surface field errors (zodMessage) instead of the bare fallback.
- engagement.ts / washup.ts / assessor.ts: strict .uuid() -> uuidish for the
  seed-id fields (assessor.ts already had a local uuidShape for roleProfileId;
  completed it for competencyId/exerciseId).

Verified: creating a requisition with a seed role profile + 8 synthetic
competencies now succeeds (was "Invalid requisition" every time).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Sidebar: collapse instrument/screening portals to single links
entries.plain: Simplified the sidebar by collapsing Cognitive, Persona, Pre-Hire, and Fluent into single links per section, moving their sub-items to each portal's own top navigation.
entries.explanation:
- Cognitive, Persona, Pre-Hire and Fluent are now single sidebar links
  (per pillar) instead of nested sub-item groups; each portal's section
  links live on its own top bar
- Add Retention to the Pre-Hire portal layout's top nav (its only sub-item
  not already surfaced there)
- Remove the now-unused instrumentGroup helper + PlayCircle import

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire report: define Composite + each stage, rename "Aptitude" to Competency Quiz, drop em dashes
entries.plain: Updated Pre-Hire reports to define what the overall score means, explain what each stage measures, and renamed 'Aptitude' to 'Competency Quiz' for clarity.
entries.explanation:
Per client review of the Pre-Hire screening report:
- Composite now carries a one-line definition directly under the number
  (weighted average 0-100 of the stage scores; a screening signal, not a hiring
  decision).
- Each stage row gets a one-line definition of what it measures (quiz / fluent /
  cbi / assessment_center), bilingual EN + AR.
- The quiz stage is relabelled "Competency Quiz" (was "Aptitude & Knowledge"),
  matching the wizard / canonical labels - answers "why did you say aptitude".
- Removed em dashes and en-dash ranges from the report strings (EN + AR),
  the null placeholders, the methodology copy, the prehire i18n strings (EN+AR),
  the fairness page placeholders, and the adverse-impact age labels + comments.

Note: the composite shows 11.6 = 0.4*29 + 0.3*0 + 0.3*0 because English + CBI
were recorded as 0 (taken/abandoned), not left untaken; an untaken stage stays
null and reads "In progress" - the scoring logic is correct.

Verified: report renders EN+AR with definitions, no "Aptitude", zero em/en
dashes. tsc clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire quiz: competency-driven deck from a role-profile-prefilled set (CAL-PRE-502)
entries.plain: The Pre-Hire quiz now draws from job-specific competencies pre-filled from the role profile instead of a generic template, and hiring teams can customize which competencies to assess.
entries.explanation:
The quiz drew from one synthetic '{title} core competency' with no
behavioural grounding. Now a requisition carries an explicit competency
set (prehire_requisitions.competency_ids, 00138) pre-filled from the
role profile's competencies and editable (add/remove from the 38,
grouped by domain, with reset-to-default) in the wizard. The quiz route
resolves competency_ids -> role_profile_competencies -> synthetic
fallback, grounds each chosen competency with its behavioural indicators
+ dev tips + target (the G3 pattern), ranks by weight/priority, and
builds a ~7-item deck via top-4 weighted sampling (<=4 generator calls,
hard-capped at 7 - never 7xN). Answer-key strip + in-progress resume +
re-id'd unique question ids preserved (adversarial review: clean).
Tolerant of 00138 unapplied (create strips+retries; quiz falls back).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire Fluent: per-requisition CEFR sub-skill selection (CAL-PRE-503)
entries.plain: Hiring teams can now select which English language skills (reading, listening, writing, speaking) the Fluent stage assesses per job opening instead of always testing all four.
entries.explanation:
A requisition's Fluent stage can now run a subset of reading / listening
/ writing / speaking instead of always all four. stage_config.skills
(jsonb, no migration); omitted/empty = all four via resolveFluentSkills
(airtight back-compat). The start route filters the test to the selected
skills BEFORE the answer-key strip (key never leaks; full keyed test
stays server-side); resume reuses the stored test+skills so a mid-flight
requisition edit can't change the deck. Submit scores only selected
skills and excludes un-administered ones from the CEFR blend (WRITING_
NOT_ASSESSED sentinel, mirroring speaking). Wizard adds the sub-skill
picker; a receptive skill (reading|listening) is required - enforced
client-side AND server-side via a stageEntrySchema refine (review fix).
Report labels a reduced run 'partial placement'. The shared standalone
AC Fluent runner uses its own types and is unaffected (verified).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire: landing-page Add Client + advisory-signal legend & English clarifier (CAL-PRE-501, 510)
entries.plain: Added ability to create a new client organization directly from Pre-Hire, plus clearer in-product guidance on scoring bands and how the English requirement works.
entries.explanation:
CAL-PRE-501: reuse the shared CreateClientDialog on the Pre-Hire landing
hero + empty state so an org can be created without leaving the flow
(dual-writes via the registry; admin-gated; region/sector captured for
SDAIA saudi/government orgs).

CAL-PRE-510: add an in-product legend for the advisory-signal bands on
the shortlist (reusing the reco tones) + a header tooltip, and a wizard
clarifier that the English-required flag only toggles the Fluent stage
and carries no separate scoring weight. New EN/AR i18n keys (Arabic
placeholder, pending review). Column relabel (AI signal -> Advisory
signal) deferred to the naming decision. Also clears pre-existing em
dashes in both pages + the signalNote string (SOP).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire wizard: editable weights, English drives Fluent, inline client (CAL-PH-503/504/505)
entries.plain: Simplified Pre-Hire setup by letting teams adjust stage weights and scores in the wizard, tying the English requirement directly to the Fluent assessment, and creating clients inline.
entries.explanation:
CAL-PH-503: the English-required toggle now drives the Fluent stage -
one control includes the English assessment AND flags english_required,
replacing the redundant standalone English checkbox (matches the
guided-start flow's english_required = stages.fluent binding).
CAL-PH-505: per-stage weight + cut-score are editable in the wizard
(clamped on submit; weights still normalized at scoring time).
CAL-PH-504: inline '+ New client' creates the org via the shared
dual-write registry and selects it immediately - no pre-existing org
required. Also clears 4 pre-existing em dashes in actions.ts (SOP).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Show "Not invited" badge on Pre-Hire shortlist
entries.plain: Pre-Hire shortlist now shows an amber 'Not invited' badge next to candidates who were added but haven't been sent an assessment invitation yet.
entries.explanation:
Candidates are added without auto-sending an invite (invited_at stays null until
the admin invites), which made "added but never invited" easy to miss. The
shortlist now renders an amber "Not invited" badge next to the name whenever
invited_at is null; it clears once the candidate is invited. Bilingual EN/AR.

tsc + next lint + EN/AR locale parity (3783) clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Email Pre-Hire report to client with scoring methodology
entries.plain: Pre-Hire reports can now be emailed directly to the hiring client as PDF attachments with full scoring explanation, keeping candidate data secure.
entries.explanation:
VIFM runs the screening as a service and DELIVERS the report to the client (the
client decides). Replaces a brief, removed handoff-tracker experiment.

- Per-row "Send to client" + header "Send all reports"; the client recipient is
  stored on the requisition; rows show a "Sent <date>" marker. The per-candidate
  PDF is emailed as an attachment via the new prehire_client_report template
  (email.ts gains EmailAttachment support) — no public/token route, so PII stays
  in the inbox. The PDF is built by a shared buildPrehireCandidatePdf used by both
  the admin download route and the email action.
- The report now carries a bilingual "How this score is calculated" section
  (composite formula + the four advisory bands and their triggers) so the client
  understands the automatically-derived signal. Guardrail intact: a screening
  signal, never a hiring decision.
- Honest mock mode: with no Graph creds, sendEmail console-mocks and returns true;
  the action no longer falsely marks a report "Sent" — it warns and skips the
  stamp, and only real sends count in "Send all". A hint on the controls explains
  the disabled Send button until a client recipient is saved.
- Migrations: 00062 (handoff columns, superseded) + 00063 (drops them; adds
  prehire_requisitions.client_recipient_email + prehire_candidates.report_sent_at
  / report_sent_to).

tsc + next lint + EN/AR locale parity (3782) clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Rework Pre-Hire: drop assessor decision, add report + custom fields
entries.plain: Pre-Hire no longer shows an in-app hiring decision; instead VIFM generates a downloadable per-candidate screening report for the hiring team, and added recruiter notes fields.
entries.explanation:
VIFM is the assessor, not the client, so the in-app hiring "decision" is removed
and replaced with a downloadable per-candidate report; plus recruiter metadata.

- B4: remove the assessor Decision column + DecisionCell + the now-dead
  setPrehireDecisionAction. Add a per-candidate Pre-Hire screening report
  (Puppeteer PDF, bilingual EN/AR) at
  /api/admin/prehire/[id]/candidate/[candidateId]/report — under /api/admin (NOT
  the auth-bypassed /api/prehire) so candidate PII can't leak. It restates the
  advisory composite + per-stage scores with an explicit "screening signal, not
  a hiring decision" disclaimer.
- B5: custom_fields jsonb on prehire_candidates (migration 00061) — an optional
  Employee ID on Add Candidate, shown on the shortlist, the per-candidate report,
  and the JSON/CSV ATS export. Tolerant of 00061 not yet applied (best-effort
  separate reads/writes so a missing column can't empty those surfaces).

Needs migration 00061 applied. tsc + next lint + EN/AR parity (3768) clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix Fluent/Pre-Hire/AC/AR quick wins from review
entries.plain: Fixed six user experience issues: word counters now turn green when minimum is met, admin-only information is hidden from candidates, and invitations require deliberate action by the hiring team.
entries.explanation:
Six low-risk UX fixes from a stakeholder review of the live portal:
- Fluent + Pre-Hire writing word-count turns green once the minimum is met.
- Hide the per-task "Target B1" CEFR badge from candidates (admin-only signal).
- Pre-Hire: adding a candidate no longer auto-sends the invite; the admin sends
  per-row (or "Invite all uninvited"). invited_at stays null until sent.
- Pre-Hire: the voluntary equal-opportunity step now runs after consent and
  BEFORE the assessment (was shown only after completion).
- Admin dashboard "Add candidates" deep-links to an engagement detail (with the
  Add-Candidate dialog) instead of the engagements list (whose CTA read as
  "add a new project").
- AR Compass consultant assessment-create shows all four tiers (Department /
  Division / Enterprise / Personal); the Personal card routes to the consultant
  personal deep-dive, consistent with /ara/engage.

tsc + next lint + EN/AR locale parity (3763) clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Translate Pre-Hire module to Arabic (RTL)
entries.plain: Pre-Hire now fully supports Arabic with right-to-left layout on all hiring team screens including requisition list, wizard, shortlist, and candidate management.
entries.explanation:
Adds the prehire i18n namespace (142 bilingual keys) and converts all nine
recruiter surfaces — requisition list, create wizard, detail shortlist,
fairness & audit hub, and the add-candidate / decision / invite components —
to react-i18next + getServerT. The service shell now derives its dir from the
locale (was hardcoded LTR), so Pre-Hire renders right-to-left in Arabic.
Machine-facing ATS exports keep their English stage/recommendation labels.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Pre-Hire immersive shell + All Services nav across portals
entries.plain: Pre-Hire now has its own branded interface with a dedicated navigation area, and all assessment portals now include a link back to the main service launcher.
entries.explanation:
Give Pre-Hire its own standalone branded chrome: a rose prehire-hero on
the navy base + a dedicated layout, with the admin layout stepping aside
for /admin/prehire/* and a hero-led requisitions list. Add a shared
AllServicesLink to every role portal's top bar (top-right) and the
AR Compass / Reflect / Fluent hero navs, so every surface links back to
the launcher.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Document the Pre-Hire module in CLAUDE.md
entries.plain: Added comprehensive internal documentation of Pre-Hire routes, database structure, and how the module works.
entries.explanation:
Add migrations 00050/00051 to the list, the /prehire + /admin/prehire
route tree, the lib/prehire entry, and a dedicated "VIFM Pre-Hire"
section (positioning, guardrail, instruments, scoring, defensibility,
invites/export, DB tables, tolerance).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Complete Pre-Hire module: Fluent stage, invites/export, defensibility
entries.plain: Completed Pre-Hire screening module with full English language testing, automated invitation emails, secure ATS data export, and demographic tracking for hiring fairness.
entries.explanation:
Finishes the additive pre-employment screening module (Tasks 7-9):
- Full 4-skill Fluent apply stage (reading/listening/writing/speaking)
  with token-namespaced start/submit/transcribe/tts routes + a shared
  Whisper+Azure transcription helper.
- Invitation email (prehire_invitation template, auto-send + resend)
  and admin-gated JSON/CSV ATS export at /api/admin/prehire (kept off
  the public /api/prehire/ auth-bypass so it can't leak PII).
- Defensibility (migration 00051): voluntary demographic self-ID,
  human-decision capture, an immutable audit log, and adverse-impact
  (4/5ths) analysis on a /admin/prehire/[id]/fairness hub.
- The composite stays a screening signal that never auto-rejects.
- Document the module in CLAUDE.md.

Verified end-to-end on the live DB (pipeline + audit + demographics +
decision + export + adverse-impact + fairness render).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Pre-Hire CBI interview stage + multi-stage apply flow
entries.plain: Pre-Hire now includes a conversational interview stage that candidates complete in order after consent and a quiz, with their responses automatically scored and added to their application ranking.
entries.explanation:
Wire the conversational behavioural interview into the candidate flow:
turn/submit API routes (idempotent resume), a chat stage component, and
a quiz stage extracted from the old flow. Rewrite apply-flow as an
orchestrator that runs each configured interactive stage in order
(consent -> quiz -> cbi -> done). Verified end-to-end: BARS rating
normalises and rolls into the candidate composite.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Pre-Hire candidate flow: token apply + AI quiz stage
entries.plain: Job applicants can now access Pre-Hire screening via a private link, complete a consent form and an AI-generated skills quiz, and receive an instant score that determines their position on the recruiter shortlist.
entries.explanation:
Token-gated /prehire/apply/[token] flow for external applicants (middleware-
bypassed, identity from prehire_candidates.access_token): consent -> AI-generated
competency quiz -> submit. The quiz is generated via the existing generator, the
answer key is stripped before it reaches the browser, and scoring happens server-
side; the normalized score writes to prehire_stage_results and the candidate is
re-scored onto the recruiter shortlist. Adds candidate-access helper, consent +
quiz start/submit API routes, and a recruiter "invite link". Verified end-to-end
against the live DB. Fluent + CBI stages to follow.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add VIFM Pre-Hire module: schema + recruiter console
entries.plain: Pre-Hire is a new pre-employment screening product for clients - recruiters can create job openings, configure screening steps, invite candidates, and review ranked shortlists all from a new console.
entries.explanation:
New additive pre-employment screening layer (sell-to-clients). Migration 00050
adds prehire_requisitions/candidates/stage_results with org-scoped RLS, soft-
linking to the existing instruments (no duplication). Includes the composite
scoring engine (screening signal, never auto-reject), the recruiter console
(requisition create/config + ranked shortlist + add-candidate), a bilingual
"Pre-Hire" nav link, and the build blueprint. Verified end-to-end against the
live DB. Candidate-side flow (token apply + instrument wiring) to follow.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Landing: rename catalogue CTA button to Virginia Academy (EN + AR)
entries.plain: The button linking to our course catalogue is now labeled 'Virginia Academy' in English and Arabic.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add role/ownership guards to Reflect, Academy, credential routes
entries.plain: Fixed a security gap where any logged-in user could view other people's feedback reports and credentials by guessing a link - now only authorized assessors and course instructors can access those documents.
entries.explanation:
Audit found 11 routes gated only by middleware session-presence:
- Reflect participant/cohort/framework PDFs + needs-scheduling CSV were
  open to any authenticated user by UUID (cross-org PII leak). Now admin
  or owning consultant via guardReflectEngagementAccess.
- Academy enroll/lesson-start/save/complete used service-role writes that
  trusted any client-supplied id (IDOR + credential issuance). Now admin
  or owning candidate via guardAcademyCandidate, gated before AI cost.
- Credential PDF: now admin or owning candidate.
- Role-profiles export: now requireRole(["admin"]).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(auth): quick-login signs in on role select (no extra click)
entries.plain: Demo users can now sign in instantly by selecting their role from the dropdown, without needing an extra click on a login button.
entries.explanation:
Selecting a role in the demo dropdown now triggers signInWithPassword
immediately using the embedded DEMO_ROLES credentials. The Quick Login
button is kept as a re-trigger (re-selecting the same option does not
re-fire onChange). Inert while accounts don't exist / AUTH_ENABLED=false.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: harden: add candidate ownership guard on service-client page reads
entries.plain: Fixed a security gap where a logged-in candidate could view another candidate's records by changing the ID in the URL - now each candidate can only see their own credentials and course progress.
entries.explanation:
Pre-flip hardening (Bucket 1 / IDOR audit). Several candidate-facing
pages read by a URL/searchParams id through createServiceClient(), which
bypasses RLS. Today that is harmless (no real sessions), but once
AUTH_ENABLED flips to true any authenticated candidate could swap the id
in the URL and read another candidate's records.

Add requireCandidateAccess(candidateId) in src/lib/auth/candidate-access.ts
and call it before the data fetch on the affected pages:
  - candidate/credentials/[candidateId]
  - candidate/academy (searchParams.candidateId)
  - candidate/academy/[enrollmentId]            (keyed off enrollment.candidate_id)
  - candidate/academy/[enrollmentId]/lesson/[lessonKey]
  - candidate/skills/[candidateId]

The guard resolves ownership against candidates.profile_id (= auth.uid()),
not candidates.id. It is inert while AUTH_ENABLED is false (getCurrentCaller
returns the synthetic dev-admin), so dev behavior is unchanged; it enforces
admin-or-owner once auth is on.

Also narrow the skills page's eng_fluent_results read from "fetch up to 200
rows and filter in JS" to an indexed .eq(candidate_id).limit(1).

No behavior change in dev. Production build green; affected routes smoke-tested.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Group portal by Talent Acquisition / Talent Management + combined plan
entries.plain: The main platform menu now organizes services into two clear groups - Talent Acquisition and Talent Management - making it easier to find what you need.
entries.explanation:
Surface the two talent-lifecycle solution families consistently across the
front door, and let the manual launcher combine several services:

- Landing (All Services): two large vertical columns (Talent Acquisition /
  Talent Management) with big headings + stacked service cards; VIFM Academy
  added as a card; hero reframed to the platform level (emerald tone added).
- Sidebar: two collapsible pillar groups, with Assessment Center and Technical
  Assessment nested as sub-groups; Courses moved in as "VIFM Academy".
- Guided Start: goal step + "Set it up myself" menu clustered into the two
  pillars, pillar headings sized to match the landing.
- Combined setup plan: multi-select services in "Set it up myself" then a
  checklist tagged to one client, with new-tab deep links (client carried in
  the URL) and done-tracking.

Bilingual EN/AR throughout; locale parity holds. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Gate Academy cert on passing; add technical proficiency certification
entries.plain: Academy completion certificates are now only issued to candidates who pass every lesson's knowledge check, and certificates now display the candidate's average score.
entries.explanation:
Tier 1 (sellable completion certificate): the academy_completion
credential now requires PASSING every lesson's knowledge-check (best
score >= threshold), not merely attempting it, and stamps the average
score on the branded certificate. Enforced in lock-step across
complete.ts, /api/academy/complete, the course page, and the per-lesson
finish route so a "course complete" claim can never lack a credential.

Tier 2 (premium proficiency certification): SME-reviewed technical item
bank + per-domain cut-scores (migration 00053) behind a new
/admin/tech-assessment review console. A certified assessment is drawn
ONLY from human-approved items; clearing the documented cut-score issues
a verifiable technical_proficiency credential, otherwise the run stays
indicative with no credential. Sessions are single-use (no replay).
00053 also widens the vifm_credentials credential_type CHECK to admit
the new type. Honest limits: human-approved AI-drafted items, light
psychometrics (no IRT yet), secure delivery not invigilation.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fit the Academy front screen on one screen (no scroll)
entries.plain: The Academy front screen now fits in your browser window without scrolling, so you can see all available courses at a glance.
entries.explanation:
Drop the four Academy value cards (their message is already in the hero
sub-text), compact the hero, clamp each service tile's description to two
lines, and vertically-center the five service tiles in the remaining
space — so the front screen fills the viewport without overflowing or
scrolling.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Make the front screen an Academy-led landing
entries.plain: The platform now leads with VIFM Academy as the primary option, with diagnostic assessments available as a secondary path below.
entries.explanation:
Lead the launcher with VIFM Academy (hero + four value cards + Browse /
My-learning CTAs) and demote the five diagnostic services to a secondary
"Start with a diagnosis" section. Keep the left panel and bilingual
toggle; the front screen now scrolls as a full landing.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Translate the full candidate Assessment Center to Arabic
entries.plain: All candidate-facing pages in the Assessment Center - from welcome through reports to credentials - are now fully translated and displayed in Arabic when selected.
entries.explanation:
Wires every candidate-facing page to the i18n helpers (getServerT for
server pages, useTranslation for client components) and adds Arabic copy:
welcome + profile form, consent (incl. the UAE/Saudi PDPL + GDPR notice),
assessments, report, pathway, academy hub/course/lesson + components, and
the credentials wallet. Previously only 6 candidate files were wired, so
switching to Arabic flipped the layout RTL but left page text English.

Locale files reach full EN/AR parity (403 keys each, no orphans). DB
values (names, course/org titles, scores, enums) stay as data. Arabic is
MSA and should get a native-speaker review before go-live.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix 'Invalid competency list' on role-profile create/update
entries.plain: Fixed a bug that prevented recruiters from creating or updating role profiles with competency lists - the validation now accepts the correct format for pre-loaded skill IDs.
entries.explanation: Zod 4's strict .uuid() enforces RFC 4122 version/variant nibbles, so it rejects the 41 seeded competency ids (a0000001-..., version nibble 0) and the seeded role-profile org ids. Every role-profile create/update with competencies failed at validation with the generic fallback error. Switched competency_id + organization_id to the shared uuidish() shape check (same fix already applied in engagement/washup/assessor/prehire validators). Also fixed the identical latent bug in setCourseCompetencyTagsAction (course-to-AC-competency mapping editor).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC PDFs: two different reports for acquisition vs development (EN + AR)
entries.plain: AI Readiness reports now show two different versions - one for hiring decisions and one for development planning - so each use case gets the right information.
entries.explanation:
Threads the new development analysis through the PDF route and both renderers so
the downloaded report matches the on-screen split:

- Acquisition PDF (EN React-PDF + AR Puppeteer): leads with the Advance/Review/
  Hold advisory band + guardrail, adds AI risk flags + a freshness stamp around
  the existing verdict / calibration / interview probes.
- Development PDF: a "Your development plan" section - non-uses framing,
  strengths-first, constructive calibration, sequenced priorities (why-now +
  70-20-10 first step + AC competencies), reflect-&-commit, manager GROW guide,
  cadence - plus the course recommender. No verdict/risk/hire language.

Verified end-to-end: all four combinations (EN/AR x acquisition/development)
return a valid PDF (200, %PDF) via the live route.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Technical: seed 2.6 Learning & Development function (HR domain)
entries.plain: Learning and Development training analysis is now available in the platform, so you can assess skills gaps and plan training programs.
entries.explanation:
Stands up the L&D node in the sandbox portal (mirrors FP&A 1.7): refreshes
skills_en/_ar (drives the MCQ knowledge generator), descriptor + JD keywords;
seeds 3 pillars + 4 logic_input blocks (course-build estimation, TNA gap math,
training ROI/Phillips, LMS completion) with self-consistent hand-computed
arithmetic; flips node_status='active'. Delivery = high mcq_pct voucher for the
instructional-design judgment + the 4 blocks for quantitative competence.
Arithmetic + content need SME (Ahmad) sign-off before the delegate run.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Recommender: gap threshold + prefer AI courses for AI-readiness recs
entries.plain: AI Readiness recommendations now prioritize courses in AI and data skills over unrelated topics, fixing cases where irrelevant courses appeared as top matches.
entries.explanation:
Fixes a credibility issue where an AI Readiness result recommended
'The Art of Public Speaking' at fit 10/5.

A) AI-readiness recommenders (ARA personal snapshot + org pillars) now float
   courses in AI/data verticals (artificial_intelligence / analytics /
   business_intelligence) above generic ones that merely share a behavioural
   competency tag. AC + Reflect recommenders are unchanged - a communication
   course for a communication gap is correct there.
B) All recommenders now require a minimum meaningful gap (>= 0.5 on the 1-5
   scale) before recommending - a 0.1 shortfall no longer triggers a
   misleadingly-strong 'fit 10' push. Applied per-unit before cohort summing.

Verified against the live catalogue: an AI Collaboration gap now surfaces the
AI-Powered Business Communication course at #1 with all AI/data courses ahead
of public speaking / etiquette / storytelling; a 0.1 gap yields no recs.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: fix: course-page Back returns to the previous page (e.g. assessment scores) instead of always the courses list
entries.plain: The back button on course pages now returns you to where you came from instead of always going to the course list.
entries.explanation:
BackLink gains an opt-in history mode (router.back() with href fallback);
the admin course detail page now uses it with a generic 'Back' label.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat: show course fit on a 0-10 scale (one decimal, top=10) instead of % match
entries.plain: Course matches now display on a 0-10 scale instead of percentages, making it easier to see which courses fit best.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat: show course fit as 0-100% match (relative to top) instead of raw weighted sum
entries.plain: Course match scores now show as percentages relative to the best match, so you can easily compare how well each course fits.
entries.explanation:
Display-only change across all 5 render surfaces (web panel + EN/AR
personal-snapshot + EN/AR learning-plan). Raw total_score still drives
ranking and the High-fit badge; adds fitMatchPercent() helper.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Drop now-unused VifmVertical import from course detail
entries.plain: Technical cleanup removed unused code from the course detail page.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Translate course vertical labels (verticals.* namespace)
entries.plain: Course category names now appear in both English and Arabic across all platform pages, supporting a bilingual experience.
entries.explanation:
Adds a verticals.* i18n namespace (14 bilingual keys) and a shared
verticalLabel(t, key) helper that resolves a course vertical with the active
locale, falling back to the English const. Wires it across every locale-aware
surface: public /courses listing + detail + quote, candidate Academy, admin
courses list/duplicates/import/form, and the shared recommended-courses panel
(now an async server component). The English VIFM_VERTICAL_LABELS const stays
for the Object.keys enumerations and machine-facing exports/PDF. Academy rows
also localize the course level via coursesPublic.level.*.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Bundle composer: Persona competency scope + remove standalone-voucher footgun
entries.plain: The bundle builder no longer offers a confusing standalone Logica option and now lets you pick specific Persona competencies to include.
entries.explanation: (1) The composed-bundle card no longer offers 'Issue Logica vouchers for this scope' - a standalone Logica voucher runs ONLY Logica, which read as 'the bundle ends without Persona' in testing. The one-sitting invite is now the card's primary button ('Invite candidate - one sitting, all sections'). (2) Selecting Persona in the composer now opens a competency picker (41 chips grouped by cluster with select-all/clear + per-cluster toggles, full instrument default) - mirroring the Logica elements picker. A real subset persists as service_config.persona.competencyIds; the bundle sitting serves only those competencies and pins the scope on the behavioral session (00123) so the standard Persona report renders it as a scoped sitting; the portal bundle page shows the scoped competency chips. Verified E2E: a 6-competency + inductive bundle served 24 Persona statements (not 164), chained into 10 inductive questions, completed, with the scope pinned and exactly 6 competencies recorded.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal: bundles can now invite candidates (seat-metered)
entries.plain: Client managers can now invite candidates directly to custom bundles from the portal instead of having to request admin invitations.
entries.explanation: Role Readiness programmes could already issue invites from the portal, but composed bundles were admin-invite only. New 'Invite a candidate (one sitting)' panel on /portal/bundle/[id]: client_manager (own org; admin preview via ?org) creates the chained-sitting link directly. Commercially metered: each invite atomically draws ONE seat from EVERY bundled runnable service's allocation (a Persona + Logica bundle costs 1 Persona + 1 Logica seat), all-or-nothing with release on any failure; the panel states the cost inline and disables with a top-up hint when any bundled service has no seats left. Verified live as the Najm client manager: invite created the org-bound candidate and drew exactly one seat from each service (released in test cleanup).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Intelligence sheet: executive visual redesign
entries.plain: The insights dashboard now has a modern visual design with better charts, summaries, and status indicators for easier data reading.
entries.explanation:
Dark branded hero (dotted texture, org + generated date) with overlapping headline stat cards (icons + big numerals); per-service panels get an accent-tinted header with a distribution-model chip, server-rendered SVG donuts for seat utilisation + completion, proportional funnel bars, accent-toned result-mix chips, and initials-avatar completion rosters with PDF buttons. Persona cohort card gains a 1-5 gauge and interpretation-band-toned chips (band key threaded through portal-intel). Bundles render as cards with a completion donut + combined-report buttons. BackLink gains an optional className for dark surfaces. Zero client JS - all server-rendered.

Verified live as the Najm client manager: hero, 4 headline cards, all 7 service panels with donuts/funnels, Persona cohort 3.34/5 with band chips, Techno result mix, bundle section.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal: voucher ledger + Intelligence sheet
