entries.plain: Clients can now see a complete record of all vouchers issued and who used them, plus an insights dashboard showing usage and completion rates.
entries.explanation:
Completes client self-service visibility. (1) Voucher ledger on every voucher-service page: each issued code with who it was assigned to, who redeemed it (name/email/date), uses, status, and issue date - collapsed by default under the existing funnel + completed-results table. Tolerant peel for assigned_email (00130) and the Techno name-bridge. (2) /portal/insights - the intelligence sheet: headline cards (active services, seats granted/used, assessments completed), a panel per subscribed service with seat-utilisation bar, issued/redeemed/completed (or invited/started/completed) funnel, completion rate, result mix (CEFR levels / bands), Persona cohort self-rating + interpretation-band mix (range-paginated org-scoped scoring), recent completions with report PDFs, and the bundles section with combined-report links. 'Intelligence' link in the portal chrome preserves admin-preview ?org context; admin without an org gets the client picker. ARC stays cohort-level by design (consultant controls delivery).

Verified live as the Najm client manager: all 7 service panels, cohort card, result mix, recent completions, bundle combined-report link, and the Persona ledger (2 issued codes with statuses).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Bespoke bundles: one-sitting candidate flow (Persona + Logica chained)
entries.plain: Candidates can now complete custom bundles in one sitting - answering behavioral questions and then cognitive tests back-to-back without stopping.
entries.explanation:
A composed bundle previously had no combined delivery - a Logica voucher ended after Logica even when Persona was in the package. New token-gated flow at /bundle/apply/[token] chains the bundle's runnable services in composed order: consent -> Persona (full instrument via the behavioural engine, org-tagged session -> full Persona reports) -> Logica (bundle's subtest scope enforced server-side, keyed test in psy_sessions, single-use atomic scoring, org-tagged psy_results) -> done. bundle_candidates (migration 00172, applied) is the thin chain; stage completion derives from the native records. Admin invites from the composer ('Invite candidate' per bundle -> copyable link); the client portal bundle page gains a candidates roster with status. Middleware bypasses /bundle/apply/ + /api/bundle/ (token flow, mirrors Role Readiness). Takers never receive score data.

Verified E2E in-browser as an anonymous candidate: consent -> 164 Persona statements -> 10 inductive-only timed questions -> done; all records org-tagged and linked; artifacts cleaned.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Bespoke bundles: persist + surface as client-portal tiles
entries.plain: Custom bundles built by admins now save to the database and appear on client portals so teams can invite candidates to tailored programs.
entries.explanation:
The composer now saves for real: composeBundleAction resolves the client through the shared registry and persists a bespoke_services kind='bundle' row (service_keys + per-service options in service_config, migration 00171 - e.g. a Logica subtest scope). The composed list loads from the DB and the trash button archives (removing the portal tile). Client portal: bundles surface under 'Your tailored programmes' next to Role Readiness; a new org-isolated /portal/bundle/[id] page renders the package's services as allocation-aware cards (seats left / not-allocated hint, Open links into each service portal) with a 'Scoped elements' callout for a scoped Logica. Admin landing lists bundles too. All reads tolerant of 00171 not applied.

Verified end-to-end in the live preview: composed via the real UI form (row landed with the right org id + inductive-only config), tile + bundle page rendered on Najm's portal, archive removed it. Test artifacts archived.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal: collapse the Candidates roster by default on bespoke programme
entries.plain: The candidate list on program pages now starts collapsed to reduce clutter and can be expanded when needed.
entries.explanation: The programme detail page listed every candidate expanded. Extracted the roster into a collapsible client component (CandidatesCollapsible) that starts closed, showing 'Candidates (N | M completed)' with a chevron; expands on click.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Give every voucher wizard ARC's client dropdown + add-client
entries.plain: All assessment portals now start by asking you to pick a client, with an option to add a new one - making the process consistent across the platform.
entries.explanation: Step 1 is now identical across all 7 portals: a 'Which client?' registry dropdown with an inline '+ Add a new client', following ARC's exact lead. Shared VoucherDetailsFields embeds the dropdown + add-client (one admin-gated server action). Trio/Pre-Hire/Techno/RR load platform clients and pass them through; the client-facing portal shows the org read-only (no registry leak).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Bespoke Services: move Reports to its own page behind a top button
entries.plain: Reports for completed assessments now have their own dedicated page instead of cluttering the main Bespoke Services setup screen.
entries.explanation:
Replace the inline Reports panel on the Bespoke landing with a "Reports" button
in the page header that opens a dedicated /admin/bespoke/reports page. The page
hosts the same searchable list of every completed candidate report (role,
client, verdict, date, Report PDF). Keeps the landing focused on building +
configuring; reuses the AllReportsPanel component.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Bespoke Services: central searchable Reports panel at the top
entries.plain: You can now search through all completed candidate reports in one central location, with their pass/fail verdict and completion date.
entries.explanation:
Houses every completed candidate report in one place. Adds a "Reports" panel at
the top of /admin/bespoke listing all completed Role Readiness candidates across
every role + client (resolved org name), newest first, with the ready/not-ready
verdict, completed date, and a Report (PDF) link - and a search box to retrieve
a specific candidate. Reuses the single loadPlatformClients() call for the
client-name map; tolerant of an un-applied 00153 (empty state). ('bundle'
bespoke kind is unbuilt scaffolding and produces no per-candidate report.)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Role Readiness: results/reports panel on the admin role editor
entries.plain: Admins can now see candidate results and verdicts directly in the Role Readiness editor instead of having to check elsewhere.
entries.explanation:
The admin issues trial links from the role editor but had no way to see the
results there - the only candidate+report tracking list lived in the client
portal, which filters by client org, so an unassigned sample (e.g. the HR-GM
seed) never appeared anywhere admin-side.

Add a "Results / candidates" panel atop /admin/bespoke/roles/[id]: every
candidate for the role across all clients + direct/sample links, with status,
the ready/not-ready verdict, and a Report (PDF) link (or apply link if not yet
finished). Tolerant of an un-applied 00153.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: RR vouchers: drop XLSX, atomic shared-seat claim, honest emailed status
entries.plain: Role Readiness voucher uploads now accept plain text instead of Excel files, and concurrent voucher redemptions are now protected against over-booking.
entries.explanation:
Addresses the adversarial-review findings on the voucher diff:
- Drop the read-excel-file dependency + .xlsx path (per request). Delegate upload
  is now CSV/text only, with a proper quoted-CSV parser (handles "Last, First")
  and the name taken from the first non-email cell (not the longest).
- Shared (pool) vouchers: redeem now claims a seat via an atomic DB function
  (migration 00156 rr_claim_voucher_seat: SET uses = uses + 1 WHERE uses <
  max_uses) so concurrent redeems can't over-redeem; provisioning failure releases
  via rr_release_voucher_seat. EXECUTE locked to service_role.
- "Emailed" status is gated on isEmailConfigured() so a console-mock (unconfigured)
  send no longer shows a false delivered tick.
- Competency grid sorts by clusterOrder explicitly (robust if the source is
  regenerated). CollapsibleCard drops the unused `right` slot (no nested-button risk).
migration 00156 applied live. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: RR editor: section-labelled grid, collapsible sections, delegate-upload vouchers
entries.plain: The Role Readiness editor now organizes all competencies into labeled groups, collapses sections to reduce clutter, and lets you upload a list of delegates to generate assessment links for each.
entries.explanation:
Four editor/voucher enhancements:
- Behavioural competency grid is now grouped + labelled by cluster (9 "building
  block" section headers), all 41 on one screen.
- New CollapsibleCard wraps every editor section (Pass thresholds, Behavioural
  competencies, Technical areas) + the voucher panel, so each expands/collapses.
- Individual vouchers: upload a delegate list (.xlsx via read-excel-file, or
  CSV/text) with name + email -> one complete-link voucher per delegate, with an
  "Email each delegate their link" option (role_readiness_invitation template,
  batched 8-at-a-time). recipient_name (migration 00155) is stored + prefilled on
  the redeem page (&name=). Result table shows Name + Emailed status; copy-link
  fallback always available.
- "Issue vouchers" panel is collapsible (default closed).

migration 00155 (rr_vouchers.recipient_name) applied live. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Role Readiness: voucher model (individual links + shared multi-seat link)
entries.plain: Role Readiness now supports both individual assessment links (one per person) and shared group links (multiple people from a pool), matching other assessment types.
entries.explanation:
Replaces the simple "create invite link" with the same voucher model as the
other portals, in both the admin role editor and the client portal product page.

- migration 00154: rr_vouchers (code, max_uses, uses, recipient_email, org) +
  RLS (admin all; client_manager reads own org). Applied + verified live.
- src/lib/role-readiness/vouchers.ts: issue individual (one single-use voucher
  per email) or one pool voucher (N seats); redeem = atomic seat claim ->
  provision rr_candidate -> return apply token (hands the seat back if it fails).
- /role-readiness/redeem?code=...[&email=...] public page + action (middleware
  auth-bypassed) -> name/email form -> forwards to the apply flow.
- Shared RrVoucherPanel (Individual links / One shared link) with complete redeem
  URLs + copy, used by the admin editor (issueRoleVouchersAction, targets the
  assigned client) and the client portal (clientIssueRoleVouchersAction, org
  resolved server-side, programme must be assigned).
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Role editor: one-screen competency grid + AI technical-question generator
entries.plain: The Role editor now fits all competencies on one screen and can generate technical exam questions automatically using AI for your review.
entries.explanation:
- Behavioural competencies now render as a compact 2-3 column grid (smaller font,
  no scroll) so all 41 fit on one screen.
- "AI generate" button per technical area: Claude drafts 4 MCQs for the area
  (src/lib/role-readiness/tech-generator.ts + generateTechnicalItemsAction); the
  admin reviews/edits/removes them like any authored item.
- Clarified the green answer text as an admin-only answer key (candidate never
  sees it) via a hover title.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Role editor: target-scale hover help + JD-to-competency matching
entries.plain: Competency targets now display helpful explanations on hover, and you can paste or upload a job description to auto-match relevant competencies.
entries.explanation:
- Per-competency Target dropdown now explains itself on hover (native title +
  an info-icon tooltip on the heading) - the VIFM BARS 1-5 scale and what "meets
  target" means.
- New "Match from a job description" box in the behavioural competencies section:
  paste text or upload a PDF/text JD, and the existing P0.1 extractor identifies
  the important competencies across the VIFM framework (building blocks + the 41)
  and pre-selects them with priority-based targets + reasoning. Reuses
  extractCompetenciesFromJobDescription / ...FromJdPdf via matchCompetenciesFromJdAction.
- Heading shows live "N selected across X/Y building blocks".
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Bespoke band: render configured products INSIDE the dark box
entries.plain: Bespoke Services products now display as cards inside the dark Bespoke box instead of floating below it, creating a cleaner layout.
entries.explanation:
The published Role Readiness products were rendering as light cards below the
'Bespoke Services' box (the box was one big link, so they couldn't nest). Make
the box a container div with the CTA + each product card as sibling links inside
it, dark-on-dark styled. No nested anchors.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Role Readiness: per-client tile in the client portal
entries.plain: Clients now see only the Role Readiness programs assigned to them, and can invite candidates and track their results from a dedicated page.
entries.explanation:
- Admin role editor publish now has an "assign to client" picker (or leave as a
  template): publishRoleAction stamps bespoke_services.organization_id.
- Client portal home (clientMode) surfaces the org's ASSIGNED role-readiness
  programmes as launchable tiles (org-assigned only, not global templates).
- New /portal/bespoke/[roleConfigId]: client_manager-gated product page - invite
  candidates (org-scoped, IDOR-safe via resolvePortalAccess), monitor each
  candidate's verdict, and open the report PDF / apply link per row.
- clientInviteRoleCandidateAction verifies the programme is actually assigned to
  the caller's org before creating an rr_candidate.
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Role Readiness: admin role editor + landing surfacing + HR GM seed
entries.plain: Admins can now build Role Readiness programs by choosing competencies, authoring technical questions, setting pass scores, and creating candidate invitations.
entries.explanation:
- /admin/bespoke/roles: job families + roles list; per-role editor to pick
  framework competencies with per-competency targets, author technical areas +
  MCQ items, set the two pass thresholds, publish to Bespoke Services, and create
  candidate invite links. All server actions requireRole(admin). No code change
  to add a role (CONDITION 2 / 7).
- platform-landing: the Bespoke Services band now renders published role_readiness
  products as launchable cards (home loads them via loadBespokeServices).
- /admin/bespoke links to the roles editor.
- HR GM sample seeded on the live DB (is_sample): job family + role profile with
  7 framework competencies + targets, 2 technical areas / 4 items, a published
  bespoke service, and one completed sample run (verdict: ready, with a small dev
  plan). scripts/remove-role-readiness-sample.sql removes it in one step.
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Role Readiness engine: migration + scoring + data libs (foundation)
entries.plain: Role Readiness assessments now have a complete foundation with database tables for roles, competencies, technical items, and candidate results.
entries.explanation:
The role-configurable Persona+Techno "role readiness" engine that surfaces inside
the existing Bespoke Services section. This is the verified foundation:

- migration 00153 (applied + verified live, 8 tables + 5 enums): job_families,
  rr_role_configs, rr_technical_areas, rr_technical_items, rr_competency_suggestions,
  rr_candidates, rr_section_results, bespoke_services. Additive; mirrors the
  Pre-Hire RLS pattern; technical items isolated from tech_assessment_items so
  sample/seed data can't leak into the real Techno bank.
- src/lib/role-readiness/scoring.ts (pure): per-side score vs threshold (inclusive
  >=), overall READY iff both pass, edge cases (no items, divide-by-zero, exact
  threshold, missing answers). scripts/test-role-readiness-scoring.ts proves
  CONDITION 4 (pass one side / fail other => not_ready) + 5 more cases: ALL PASS.
- src/lib/role-readiness/{config,candidate-access}.ts + src/lib/bespoke/services.ts:
  load a role config (competencies+targets reused from role_profiles, technical
  areas/items), token candidate lookup, and persist/load bespoke_services.

Next: admin role editor, combined candidate sitting + report, landing surfacing,
HR GM seed, full 8-condition verification.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Bespoke Services: landing entry + admin composer (front-end)
entries.plain: Bespoke Services is now a distinct offering on the platform - VIFM can bundle any combination of assessments and assign them to specific clients.
entries.explanation:
Adds a "Bespoke Services" meta-service: VIFM combines any of the platform's
services into one tailored package and assigns it to a client.

- Landing page: a distinct bespoke band below the service grid (bilingual EN/AR,
  RTL-aware), teasing the bundle-able services, linking to the composer. Hidden
  in client-portal mode (VIFM designs these). Opens /admin/bespoke.
- /admin/bespoke: admin-gated page (requireRole + notFound on the IDOR rail)
  that loads the real platform client list and renders the composer.
- BespokeBuilder: name (EN/AR) + description, multi-select of the 7 client
  services (icon/accent toggle cards), assign-to-client picker, a live preview
  card and a session list of composed packages.

This is the front-end pass. Persisting a bespoke service and surfacing it in the
assigned client's portal is the next step (to wire alongside the first design).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Portal: UUID-validate admin-preview orgParam (fail-fast)
entries.plain: Admin preview links are now validated more carefully to prevent unclear errors when accessing the system.
entries.explanation:
Low-severity hardening from the pool-voucher review: the admin-only preview
path passed orgParam straight through; validate its UUID shape in
resolveActorOrg so a malformed id fails with a clear message instead of an
opaque RPC error. Not a security boundary (admin-gated; RPC type-coerces).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Vouchers: individual + shared-link (seat-pool) modes everywhere
entries.plain: Vouchers now support both individual codes and shared links everywhere - managers can send one link to multiple people instead of emailing codes to each person.
entries.explanation:
Client portal previously only emailed individual single-use codes; the
admin Techno page hid its seat-pool behind a defaulted dropdown. Now every
voucher surface offers BOTH modes:

- Client portal (Fluent/Logica/Persona/Techno generic panel): new
  'Individual codes' | 'One shared link' toggle. Pool mode draws N seats
  from the allocation up-front and mints ONE code with max_uses=N via the
  service engine (no per-recipient email - the manager shares one link).
  New issueClientPoolVoucher() lib fn + clientIssuePoolVoucherAction;
  release-on-failure + Persona scope / Techno function threaded through
  exactly like the individual path. Engines unchanged (all already support
  maxUses / maxUsesPerCode).
- Admin Techno: dropdown -> visible two-button toggle (Individual codes |
  One shared link - many seats).
- Admin Fluent/Logica/Persona already expose both via How-many + Seats/code.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal: render the exact platform landing in client mode
entries.plain: The client portal home page now displays the same professional layout as the main platform, showing available services with usage stats and expiry dates.
entries.explanation:
Replace the bespoke portal Home with the real PlatformLanding component in an
additive clientMode (default off -> public landing unchanged): same hero +
two-column Talent Acquisition / Talent Development families + rich service
tiles as caliber.viftraining.com. In client mode it hides its own top bar
(the portal chrome provides it), shows the org name + a portal subtitle,
dims services the org was not allocated, and routes each allocated tile to
its /portal/services/<svc> page with a seats summary.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal: redesign Home to match the admin platform landing
entries.plain: The client portal home has been redesigned with a branded header and service tiles that match the admin platform look, making it easier to see what's available.
entries.explanation:
The portal Home was a plain stacked-card list. Rebuild it with the same
polished look as the admin landing: a branded gradient hero (org name +
seats summary) and per-service jewel tiles (launcher-card tone-<hue>, same
icon + hue per service as the admin landing). Allocated services are active
tiles (stats + usage bar + expiry + Open); un-allocated services are dimmed.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal Phase 2: seat services (ARC, Reflect, Pre-Hire)
entries.plain: Managers can now distribute and track AR Compass, Reflect 360, and Pre-Hire assessments directly in the client portal, including sending invitations and viewing reports.
entries.explanation:
- per-service modules (ensure shell + invite/draw-seats + monitor):
  - ARC: per-org ara_assessments shell, respondents w/ derived pillar
    assignments, bilingual invite email, assessment-level cohort report
  - Reflect: per-org reflect_engagements shell (clones library framework),
    participants + self-raters, launch invitations, per-participant +
    cohort reports
  - Pre-Hire: per-org requisition shell, candidates + invites, advisory
    composite monitor; demographics never selected/exposed
- seat-issue dispatcher + clientInviteSeatAction + SeatDistributeClient;
  service page renders seat distribute + seat funnel/review
- adversarially verified vs live schema; fixed Reflect started-funnel
  (first_opened_at) + cohort-row completed inflation

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal Phase 1: voucher distribute + monitor + review (Fluent/Logica/Persona)
entries.plain: Managers can now issue vouchers, send them to candidates, and monitor completion progress for Fluent, Logica, and Persona assessments from the client portal.
entries.explanation:
- issueClientVouchers: allocation-gated batch issuance reusing each
  service's voucher engine + email, draw/release on failure
- /portal/services/[service]: distribute (paste roster -> issue & email),
  monitor funnel (issued/redeemed/completed) + completed review table
- report routes (fluent/cognitive/persona) gain an additive client_manager
  org-gate branch; staff/candidate branches unchanged
- Home dashboard cards link into each allocated service

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Replace pre-existing em dashes with hyphens (SOP)
entries.plain: Text formatting has been standardized for consistency across the platform.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add admin Competency Framework reference page
entries.plain: Admins can now view the complete behavioural framework in one place, showing all domains, competency groups, and skill definitions.
entries.explanation:
New /admin/framework page renders the full VIFM behavioural framework on
one screen: 4 domains, 9 clusters (each with a definition) and 41
competencies (each with its description). Sourced live from
competency_domains / _clusters / competencies; domain + cluster
definitions authored in framework-definitions.ts (the DB stores no
description for domains/clusters). Linked from the Platform sidebar group
as "Competency Framework" (Network icon), EN + AR nav labels.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Group the left panel by service + fix admin viewport scroll
entries.plain: The admin sidebar is now organized by service with a collapsible menu, and the page layout no longer scrolls off-screen - content scrolls inside instead.
entries.explanation:
Restructure the shared sidebar so its top level mirrors the launcher: the
five services as peers, with the Assessment Center's own sections nested
under a collapsible "Assessment Center" group and the cross-cutting admin
areas under "Platform". Also switch the admin shell from min-h-screen to a
fixed h-screen so content scrolls inside <main> and the sidebar (with its
footer) stays pinned — a tall page no longer overflows past the viewport.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Show the portal left panel on the platform landing page
entries.plain: The platform sidebar navigation now appears on the main landing page, giving quick access to all services from the home screen.
entries.explanation:
Extract the sidebar into a shared PortalSidebar (PORTAL_NAV_LINKS +
SidebarBody) so the admin chrome and the landing page render one nav from
a single source. AdminLayout now consumes it (desktop collapsible + mobile
drawer + top bar unchanged); the landing page wraps PlatformLanding with
the panel so it reads as the platform home with quick access to every
service and section.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Translate the candidate dashboard ("Your Assessment Journey")
entries.plain: Candidates in Arabic mode now see their assessment dashboard (progress tracker and status) in Arabic instead of English.
entries.explanation:
The candidate index page (/candidate) was missed by the journey
translation pass - its hero, step cards, metric labels, and the shared
ProcessMap's internal strings (Complete / Progress tracker / DONE) were
hardcoded English, so the dashboard stayed English in Arabic mode.
Wires it to getServerT + adds candidateHome/processMap locale sections
(EN/AR parity, 420 keys each). ProcessMap strings gate by route, so the
English-only admin/assessor/client portals are unaffected.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix Register: plain-English explanation for every change
entries.plain: All platform changes now display in plain language first, with technical details available for those who need them.
entries.explanation:
Every entry now leads with a jargon-free sentence a non-technical reader can
understand (what changed and why it matters), with the raw commit message
tucked behind a "Technical detail" expander.

- scripts/explain-fix-register.mjs: AI-writes one plain sentence per commit
  (Haiku, batched), cached by hash in src/data/fix-register-explanations.json.
  Incremental (only uncached commits) + crash-safe + tolerant (no key / model
  error just leaves the raw message as fallback). 597/600 seeded.
- Generator merges the cache as `plain` on each entry.
- prebuild runs generate -> explain -> generate so new commits are explained
  on each deploy.
- Page: plain line is the headline; a "What the labels mean" legend explains
  Critical / Security / Audit / Fix / Feature / Change in plain terms.
- Per-service list capped at 20 with a "Show more" expander (lighter render).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add live Fix Register admin page (per-service, git-derived)
entries.plain: Administrators can now view a live changelog showing every update to Caliber, organized by feature, with dates and authors.
entries.explanation:
A live, admin-only changelog at /admin/fix-register showing, per service,
every change shipped to Caliber with its fix date, author, optional
requester, and full explanation.

- scripts/generate-fix-register.mjs parses the commit history, classifies
  each commit to a service by touched files + message, infers a change
  type, and extracts an optional "Requested-by:" trailer. Writes
  src/data/fix-register.generated.json. Tolerant of no .git at build.
- Wired into "prebuild" so the register refreshes on every deploy; also
  runnable via "npm run gen:fix-register".
- Page is a server component + client filter (search, service, type).
  Admin-only for free under the /admin layout role gate.
- Sidebar link under Platform.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Caliber marketing landing page at root (dual-mode: public landing / staff launcher)
entries.plain: The platform now has a professional marketing landing page at the home URL that shows services to visitors and acts as a staff launcher when logged in.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Framework PDF: drop behavioural indicators, keep definitions only
entries.plain: The framework PDF now shows competency definitions without the detailed behavioural indicators, keeping documents more concise.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Licensed Preview: logo, per-sector tuning, lead modules, shareable link
entries.plain: Licensed previews now display an organization logo, customize headlines and scoring by industry, highlight key modules, and can be shared via a read-only public link.
entries.explanation: 1) Logo URL on the launcher (falls back to initials). 2) Per-sector headline/tagline + score bias so a bank's tenant differs from a ministry's. 3) Lead-module picker -> a Spotlight nav group + Featured cards. 4) Public read-only shareable link at /share/preview (middleware-bypassed, shareMode hides admin Exit) as a prospect leave-behind. Shared param parser; logo restricted to http(s).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Persist voucher client contact to the DB on all 7 portals
entries.plain: When issuing vouchers across all assessment types, the system now saves the contact person's name, title, and email address with each voucher.
entries.explanation: Migration 00168 adds contact_name/title/email to every *_vouchers table (+ client_name/project_label/expires_at on rr_vouchers). All 7 insert libs + their actions + UIs thread the step-1 contact through, so it's stored on each issued voucher. Inserts peel the new columns on a schema-cache miss for fresh-env tolerance.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Standardize step-1 voucher details across all 7 portals
entries.plain: All voucher issuance forms now ask for the same details in step one - client name, project label, expiry date, and contact information - for consistency.
entries.explanation: Shared VoucherDetailsFields: Client name, Project label, Expiry, Contact name/title/email in step 1 of every wizard; contact prefills the step-3 email-to-client. RR gains a Details step + emails the shared link to the contact.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add unified dark voucher header + service tabs
entries.plain: All assessment services now share a consistent dark header and tab design, so staff navigate between Fluent, Logica, Persona, Techno, ARC, Pre-Hire, and Role Readiness the same way everywhere.
entries.explanation: Shared VoucherNav (VIFM - VOUCHERS / Issue vouchers dark bar + pill tabs for Fluent/Logica/Persona/Techno/ARC/Pre-Hire/Role Readiness) on each voucher page; the /admin/vouchers hub gets the same dark header + pill tabs.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Pre-Hire audit B2.5: token-scope API bypass, dedupe stage plan
entries.plain: Security and accuracy improvements: Pre-Hire now prevents unauthorized API access and stops duplicate assessment stages from skewing results, while making AI-generated scores clearly visible to reviewers.
entries.explanation:
Tighten /api/prehire auth-bypass to a UUID-shaped token segment so a
future non-token route under the prefix can't be silently public (I-3).
Reject duplicate stage kinds in a requisition's stage plan, which would
double-count a stage's weight in the composite (S-8). Surface the
AI-draft nature of per-stage + CBI scores on the SME review page and
switch its inline back link to the shared BackLink (UA-1/2/3).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Role Readiness: combined candidate sitting + report + middleware
entries.plain: Role Readiness assessments can now be taken by candidates, scored automatically, and reported with personalized development plans - completing the end-to-end assessment experience.
entries.explanation:
- sitting.ts: serves Persona items scoped to the role's competencies (reuses the
  behavioural engine), grades the Techno-style technical MCQs server-side
  (answer key never sent), writes per-section results + finalizes the verdict.
- report-data.ts: shared result/report builder (per-side scores, per-competency +
  per-area breakdown, dev plan from below-target items with SME-editable text).
- 7 candidate API routes under /api/role-readiness/[token] (consent, persona
  start/submit, technical start/submit, result, report PDF) + the apply UI
  (/role-readiness/apply/[token]) with consent -> Persona Likert -> Techno MCQ ->
  on-screen verdict + dev plan + PDF download.
- role-readiness-html.ts: Puppeteer A4 report, inline SVG + CSS only.
- middleware: auth-bypass the token candidate + API routes.
tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal: bounce a logged-in client_manager off public marketing pages
entries.plain: Client managers are now automatically redirected away from public marketing pages, keeping them confined to their client portal once they log in.
entries.explanation:
The AI Readiness landing (/ara), /courses and /evidence were auth-bypassed,
so a signed-in client_manager could still view them. Now those marketing
pages run redirectIfClientManager first: anonymous prospects + VIFM staff
see them unchanged, but a client_manager is redirected to /portal - so the
manager is fully confined to their portal.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal: confine client_manager to /portal (dim + block)
entries.plain: Client managers can no longer access other parts of the platform by typing URLs - they are locked to their portal, and unavailable services are grayed out to show what their company hasn't purchased.
entries.explanation:
A client_manager landed on the open platform landing and could reach
other portals by URL (middleware enforces auth, not role). Now:
- middleware redirects a client_manager from any non-portal PAGE (admin/
  consultant/assessor surfaces, the platform landing, AC runners) back to
  /portal; API routes self-gate (org-scoped report endpoints + the Techno
  results page stay reachable for their own org)
- the /portal Home already dims un-allocated services (allowed = what the
  admin allocated); a hand-typed /portal/services/<unallocated> now bounces
  back to the grid too

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal: apply audit fixes
entries.plain: Client portal now sends Fluent emails, refreshes screens after issuing tests, shows one company-level ARC report link, splits partial Pre-Hire batches correctly, and shows a setup-delay notice for Reflect.
entries.explanation:
- Fluent voucher email (parity with Logica/Persona); wired into issuance
- router.refresh() after issue/invite so remaining + monitor update live
- ARC review surfaces ONE org-level cohort report link (not per-respondent)
- Pre-Hire partial-batch: release only unfilled seats, keep created candidates
- Techno: admin grant form pins the technical function (service_config.functionId)
- Reflect: setup-latency hint on the invite panel

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal: add Techno as 4th voucher service (name bridge)
entries.plain: Client managers can now issue Techno technical assessments through vouchers, track completion in their monitor, and view results - adding a fourth service to their self-service portal.
entries.explanation:
- issueClientVouchers gains a techno branch via generateVoucherBatch
  (delegate-named codes, org-scoped by organization_name, function from
  allocation service_config or first catalogue function), emails via
  emailAccessLink
- monitor: techno funnel + completed sittings by organization_name
- tech-sandbox results page admits a client_manager whose org name matches
  the sitting (additive; admin access unchanged)

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Client portal Phase 0: client_manager role + allocation ledger + portal shell
entries.plain: Foundation laid for client self-service: a new client_manager role, service allocation tracking, and portal security rules that let companies manage their own assessment budgets and access.
entries.explanation:
Foundation for the client self-service portal (additive; no existing portal,
role, table, or policy is changed):
- Migration 00150: 'client_manager' user_role value + caliber_service enum.
- Migration 00151: client_service_allocations (org+service quota/used/expiry,
  service_config, granted_by) + cm_org_id/cm_ara_org_id/cm_has_allocation
  SECURITY DEFINER helpers + atomic claim/release_allocation_seats RPCs + RLS
  (admin all; client_manager reads own org).
- src/lib/clients: portal-services (the 7-service catalogue), allocations
  (draw/release/get), portal-access (resolve who is viewing + which org).
- Provisioning: provision-client-manager.ts + scripts/create-client-manager.ts.
- Admin: /admin/clients/allocations (grant quota+expiry, provision a manager
  login + set-password link) + a link from the Clients page.
- /portal: client_manager-guarded shell + read-only Home allocation dashboard
  (per-service Granted/Used/Remaining/Expiry, grouped self-serve vs VIFM-managed,
  bilingual EN/AR; admin can preview any org via ?org=).

tsc + ESLint clean. Migrations not yet applied to the live DB.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Demo data tool (BD): one-click seed + purge for the guided demos
entries.plain: Staff can now seed the platform with realistic demo data (a fictional company with full assessment records) and purge it with one click, enabling complete walkthroughs without touching real client data.
entries.explanation:
A staff "Demo data" page (/admin/demo, Platform nav) that seeds a fictional GCC
client (Najm Capital) with realistic, populated data so the guided demos walk
through full screens instead of empty states - then a one-click purge that
removes only the demo-tagged rows, never real client data.

Phase 1 services (verified end-to-end on the live DB):
- Assessment Center: an active engagement, a 3-person cohort, observations +
  ratings, and one candidate fully scored through consensus -> OAR -> a released
  report (so the report + analytics screens have a complete example).
- Pre-Hire: a requisition with two candidates (one scored "advance", one in
  progress) + per-stage results.
- Fluent: three CEFR placement results (A2 / B2 / C1) for the cohort screen.

Safety: everything ties to the demo org (found by name in both org stores) or a
sentinel email domain; seed is idempotent (reuses the org, skips already-seeded
services) and reuses an existing assessor rather than creating one. Purge is
FK-safe and org-scoped. Recipes for the remaining services (ARC, Reflect,
Persona, Cognitive, Technical, Readiness, Academy) are gathered for a follow-up.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Proctoring & Integrity Report: PDF addendum (consent + AI summary + snapshots)
entries.plain: Camera-proctored assessments now generate a separate secure PDF report with face photos and AI review findings, kept separate from the public result so sensitive images are only shared on request.
entries.explanation:
A separate, defensibility-grade PDF for a camera-proctored sitting - kept distinct
from the placement report because it carries face snapshots (sensitive PII), so
it's the access-controlled evidence record an admin shares on request rather than
baked into the client-facing result.

- src/lib/reports/proctoring-report-html.ts: branded HTML (consent record + ts,
  session metadata, AI vision review summary + motion stats, snapshot contact
  sheet w/ per-frame flags, attestation/disclaimer).
- /api/admin/proctor/[sessionId]/report: Puppeteer HTML->PDF, admin-gated.
  Deliberately OUTSIDE the auth-bypassed /api/proctor/* prefix (face PII), like
  the Pre-Hire ATS export. networkidle0 waits for the signed-URL snapshots.
- "Download report (PDF)" link on the admin proctoring page.

Verified on preview: 200 application/pdf, ~95 KB (images embedded in the contact
sheet, not just text). tsc + ESLint clean; test session cleaned up.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Camera proctoring Phase 2: automated flags (motion + AI vision review)
entries.plain: Camera proctoring now automatically detects room movement and uses AI vision to flag issues like multiple faces or looking away, storing evidence that admins can review.
entries.explanation:
- Client-side motion: ProctorCapture computes a frame-difference score (32x24
  grayscale mean-abs-diff) and uploads it; stored in proctor_snapshots.flags.motion
  (the temporal "room movement" signal a single image can't give).
- AI vision review (admin-triggered): src/lib/proctor/analyze.ts samples up to 16
  frames evenly, runs Claude vision (no-face / multiple-faces / looking-away /
  device-or-screen) per frame into proctor_snapshots.flags, and writes a session
  summary to proctor_sessions.ai_review (migration 00148). No AI key => honest
  configured:false (motion-only), never fabricates a flag.
- Report: "Run AI integrity review" button, a flagged-frame summary card, and
  per-snapshot flag chips + rose/amber border on flagged frames.

Framed as a human review aid, never an automatic pass/fail. tsc + ESLint clean;
00148 applied; verified end-to-end on the preview (motion chip renders, AI
pipeline analyses a real-sized frame; the 1x1 test image is correctly skipped).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Brand: add ® registered-trademark to product-name displays
entries.plain: All seven VIFM product names (Fluent, Reflect 360, ARC, Persona, Mentium, Pre-Hire, Techno) now display the registered-trademark symbol across the platform and emails.
entries.explanation:
Applies ® to the seven VIFM product brands (Fluent, Reflect 360, ARC /
AI Readiness Compass, Persona, Mentium, Pre-Hire, Techno) across user-facing
surfaces: the platform landing cards, i18n English labels, the portal-
comparison sheet, the ARA/ARC pages + voucher emails, and the admin voucher
hub. Arabic-script names, code identifiers, routes, and comments are left
unmarked. tsc clean; no ®-in-identifier or double-® anomalies.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Methodology PDFs: bypass auth so the public briefs are actually public
entries.plain: Public visitors can now download assessment methodology briefs without logging in, removing the accidental login wall that blocked access to free public resources.
entries.explanation:
The new generic /api/methodology/[service]/pdf route (and the existing
/api/ac/persona/methodology/pdf, which ends in /pdf not /report) were not in the
middleware public-bypass list, so a no-account visitor clicking a brief on the
public /evidence hub was redirected to /login - the brief is meant to be public,
like the ARC methodology PDF. Add isPublicMethodologyRoute (/api/methodology/*)
and admit the persona methodology pdf. Verified: all 10 briefs return
application/pdf 200 with NO session cookie (128-203KB).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Framework PDF: cap + catch the webfont wait so it can't hang in prod
entries.plain: Framework PDFs no longer risk hanging if external fonts load slowly - the system now falls back to standard fonts after 12 seconds, ensuring reports always generate.
entries.explanation:
The /api/admin/framework/pdf route blocked on the external Google-Fonts CDN via
networkidle0 with a 60s timeout. Under @sparticuz/chromium in production, egress
to fonts.googleapis.com can be slow or blocked, so that wait could hang to the
full timeout and 500. Cap it at 12s and catch the timeout: setContent writes the
full static DOM synchronously, so on a font timeout we still render every domain
+ competency (Segoe UI/Tahoma fallback) instead of failing. Brand Open Sans
loads normally when the CDN responds. Verified in dev: 349KB PDF, all 41
competencies + 249 indicators present.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Global signed-in role indicator on every page (top-right)
entries.plain: A fixed label now appears in the top-right corner of every page showing your current role (Admin, Consultant, Candidate, Client, or Assessor), so you always know who you are signed in as.
entries.explanation:
Adds a fixed top-right pill - "Signed in as Admin / Consultant / Candidate /
Client / Assessor" - rendered once in the root layout so it appears on every
page. Server component; renders nothing when there's no session (login + token-
gated respondent / candidate pages stay clean). Compact (icon + role) on mobile.
The admin desktop top bar reserves right padding so the pill never covers the
notification bell.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Admin: platform hub at /admin; collapse Succession Readiness sidebar
entries.plain: Admin dashboard now shows all platform services grouped by business area instead of jumping straight to Assessment Center.
entries.explanation:
#4 - The landing "Admin" button (and the admin logo) dropped you straight into
the Assessment Center, because /admin WAS the AC dashboard. Now /admin is a
platform admin home showing every service grouped by pillar (Talent Acquisition
/ Talent Management / Platform). The AC workflow dashboard moved to
/admin/assessment-center; the sidebar "Dashboard" links and the landing's AC
service card repoint there, and the Admin button now lands on the hub.

#2 - Succession Readiness was a sidebar group with separate Overview + Scoring
Config sub-items. Collapsed to a single "Succession Readiness" link; the scoring
config is already reachable from a card on that page.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Evidence: public Research & validity page with live counts
entries.plain: New public Research page shows which established scientific principles support each assessment tool, with live question counts.
entries.explanation:
- New /evidence page (public, no account; also in the admin sidebar under
  Platform) documenting each instrument's item types, validity claim,
  reliability and literature sources - proof the questions are grounded in
  established research, with an honest literature-grounded vs indicative split
- Item counts are read live from the database (ARA bank, Fluent items,
  Technical tasks, Reflect behaviours, competencies); tolerant of missing tables
- Curated catalogue (canonical citations) in src/lib/evidence; mobile-first
- Middleware bypass for /evidence so it can be shared with a client/regulator

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Remove all em dashes across portals, replace with hyphens (SOP)
entries.plain: Fixed formatting characters across the platform that were displaying incorrectly in PDFs and emails.
entries.explanation:
Byte-safe sweep of every .ts/.tsx/.json/.css under src: 552 em dashes (U+2014)
across 117 files replaced with regular hyphens, per the no-em-dash convention.
Covers UI strings, JSX text, comments, EN/AR locale, and the PDF report
generators (where em dashes can render as missing glyphs). Verified: 0 em dashes
remain in src, locale JSON still valid, tsc + lint clean (only pre-existing
alt-text warnings). En dashes (U+2013, used in ranges like 1-5) left as-is.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Competency framework: PDF download + behavioural-indicators tab (level 2)
entries.plain: Competency framework now has a downloadable PDF and a new tab showing positive and negative behavioural indicators for each skill.
entries.explanation:
FW-1: a "Download PDF" button on /admin/framework renders the whole framework
(domains -> clusters -> competencies + per-competency positive/negative
indicators) via a new admin-gated Puppeteer route (/api/admin/framework/pdf).

FW-2: a second tab, "Behavioural indicators", lists the positive (green) and
negative (red) indicators per competency - the detailed level beneath the grid -
honouring the same search + domain filter + EN/AR chrome.

Shared loader (loadFrameworkTree) feeds both the screen and the PDF so they can't
drift. Development tips (seeded into behavioral_indicators as "[DEV TIP]" positive
rows, migration 00004) are excluded from the indicators view + count - they are
coaching tips, not behavioural indicators (count corrected 372 -> 249).

Verified live: grid tab, indicators tab (no dev tips, 249 count), PDF 200/valid
357KB. tsc clean; lint clean (removed a stale useMemo dep).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Rebuild admin Competency Framework as branded grid
entries.plain: Competency framework page redesigned with a branded grid layout, color-coded by domain, for easier browsing and filtering.
entries.explanation:
Replace the simple card page at /admin/framework with the approved grid
design (vifm-competency-framework-grid handover): accent top bar,
masthead, hero + derived stat pills, 5-point BARS scale card, search,
per-domain filter chips, and domain-coloured columns with glyphs, cluster
accent bars and numbered competency badges.

- Server component fetches the catalogue live (tolerant of the name_ar /
  description_ar columns from 00071) and shapes domains -> clusters ->
  competencies with stable sequential numbering; counts are derived, never
  hardcoded, so the 9th cluster / 41st competency already show as 9 / 41.
- Client component owns search, domain filter and an EN/AR toggle (RTL on
  the content region) with useState only - no localStorage/sessionStorage.
- Domain colour/tint/icon/caption live in one config object in
  framework-definitions.ts with a dark->accent ramp fallback for any future
  domain; BARS scale + bilingual chrome strings alongside.

Verified in preview: live 4/9/41 counts, search + chip filter, EN/AR + RTL,
responsive 4/2/1 columns, no console errors, tsc clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Voucher hub: cover all 5 services (add Fluent, Cognitive, Persona)
entries.plain: Voucher hub now manages redeem codes for all five services from one central location instead of splitting them.
entries.explanation:
The consolidated /admin/vouchers hub only handled ARC + Technical. Generalize
it so every service that issues redeem codes is managed from one place, while
each service's own /vouchers page still works.

- voucher-hub.tsx: replace the hardcoded araSlot/techSlot props with a generic
  `services: { key, summary, slot }[]` array. Service display metadata (label/
  icon/tone) stays client-side (icons can't cross the server boundary as props).
  Summary cards + tab strip render from the array; grid widens to 5.
- page.tsx: load Fluent (eng_fluent_vouchers), Cognitive (cognitive_vouchers),
  and Persona (persona_vouchers) via a shared tolerant loader, reusing each
  service's existing VouchersClient as the tab slot and loadPlatformClients for
  the client picker. Each load is try/caught so a missing table shows
  "Not set up" rather than 500ing. initialTab accepts ?service=<key> for all 5.

Verified live: all 5 summary cards render with live stats (ARC 7/2/5; the other
four 0 on empty tables); switching tabs swaps the active service's manager
(ARC -> Persona confirmed). tsc clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Close remaining access-control gaps found re-running the QA scorecard
entries.plain: Fixed security gaps that allowed non-admin users to access restricted admin pages.
entries.explanation:
Re-testing as a live non-admin (Candidate) session surfaced two more holes in
the same IDOR class - now closed and verified (Candidate -> 404, Admin -> 200):

- Blanket /admin role gate. admin/layout.tsx is now a server component that
  resolves the caller and 404s any non-admin, with the client chrome moved to
  _components/admin-chrome.tsx. Previously the admin shell was reachable by any
  logged-in user; pages on the authenticated client were RLS-safe but
  service-role admin pages (e.g. tech-assessment/items) could leak. This closes
  the whole surface at the root (the proper fix behind the courses/quotes gate).
- Reflect consultant dashboard. /reflect/consultant listed ALL engagements via
  the service-role client with no gate; now gated to consultant/admin and the
  query is scoped to the owning consultant (admins still see all). Completes the
  Reflect IDOR fix (the detail pages were already gated).

Verified live: as a Candidate, /admin/*, /reflect/consultant, /ac/ai-interview,
/admin/courses/quotes all return 404; as Admin all return 200. tsc clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Route remaining copy buttons through copyToClipboard
entries.plain: Copy-to-clipboard buttons now work reliably across all platforms and permission contexts throughout the system.
entries.explanation:
The other copy-to-clipboard sites (tech program links, technical cert
panel, ARC deep-dive link, engagement portal invite, Pre-Hire invite +
add-candidate links) were already try/catch-wrapped so they didn't
crash, but they silently failed in iframe/permission-denied contexts.
Switch them to the shared copyToClipboard helper so they copy via the
execCommand fallback there too. No direct navigator.clipboard.writeText
calls remain outside the helper.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix corrupt results-PDF email attachment (bypass personal routes)
entries.plain: Fixed AI Readiness results PDFs being corrupted in emails by ensuring they bypass login redirects when generating attachments.
entries.explanation:
The personal AI Readiness routes (/ara/personal/*, /api/ara/personal/*)
were not in the middleware auth bypass. The completion task fetches the
results PDF server-side with no session, so middleware redirected it to
/login and the task base64-encoded the login HTML as the email
attachment - a "PDF" that won't open. (Real anonymous delegates also
couldn't open their own results page/PDF at all.)

These routes are token-gated and self-gate at the handler (eligibility,
completion, and the client-visibility check from 00108), so bypass auth
like the respondent/cohort flows. The server fetch now reaches the
handler and gets a real PDF (or a clean 403 when results are withheld).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Retention: admin purge for AC, Technical, Pre-Hire
entries.plain: Assessment Center, Technical, and Pre-Hire now have data deletion tools that automatically purge records older than 24 months.
entries.explanation:
Adds the 24-month data-retention purge surface (counts + typed
confirmation, admin-gated) to the three services that lacked it:

- AC (/admin/engagements/retention): purges completed/archived
  engagements past the window; cascades candidates, observations,
  ratings, consensus, OARs, reports. Active/draft never touched;
  issued credentials kept.
- Technical (/admin/tech-assessment/retention): purges tech
  results + test sessions + sandbox sessions (responses cascade);
  tolerant of any table being unmigrated; credentials kept.
- Pre-Hire (/admin/prehire/retention): purges expired candidates;
  cascades stage results + immutable audit rows; requisition shell
  kept (targets PII directly).

No schema change - service-client deletes over existing cascade FKs.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: ARC: make the cohort dashboard a real public share-by-link
entries.plain: AI Readiness cohort dashboard now works as intended when shared by link - no login required to view group results.
entries.explanation:
The /ara/cohort/[assessmentId] page is designed as a no-account,
share-by-link client view (service-client, no PII, Mode-C gated by
UUID) but was never added to the middleware auth-bypass, so it
redirected to /login. Add isAraCohortRoute to the bypass list so the
documented "anyone with the link can view" behaviour actually works.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add candidate portal-login provisioning (go-live)
entries.plain: Candidates can now be invited to the portal and set up their login account instead of being locked out due to missing credentials.
entries.explanation:
AC candidates need a real Supabase auth account to use the candidate
portal, but candidate rows are created with profile_id=NULL, so logins
were unusable. Add the provisioning capability:
- provisionCandidateLogin() + generateCandidateSetupLink() helper
- "Invite to portal" action + button on the engagement candidates table
- scripts/provision-candidate-logins.ts batch backfill (dry-run default)
- guarded candidate pages now notFound() instead of 500 on a mismatch

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Auth hardening: API-route ownership guards + portal identity
entries.plain: API security strengthened: admins can see all reports, candidates only see their own, preventing unauthorized access to results.
entries.explanation:
Bucket 2 - role/ownership guards on report-style API routes (middleware
already blocks no-session; these add who-can-see):
- New guardCandidateReportAccess (src/lib/auth/report-access.ts): admin all;
  client -> own org's candidate; candidate -> own record. Wired into the full
  report + learning-plan routes (replacing the auth-presence-only check).
- /api/consent/[candidateId]: requires the owning candidate (candidates.profile_id
  = caller.uid) or an admin.
- /api/readiness/[id]/[cand]/pdf: requireRole(admin, consultant) (closes the
  auth I'd deferred to the AUTH_ENABLED flip when I built it).

Bucket 3 - identity: assessor + client portal headers now show the signed-in
user via a shared CurrentUserBadge instead of a static "Assessor"/"Client"
label. (created_by = auth.uid() and assessor-id = auth.uid() were already in
place; the README was stale.)

Verified: unauthenticated -> 307 /login; admin session passes (report 200,
readiness PDF 200, consent 400-for-bad-body, not 403); layouts render the
user badge. Tracker updated.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add consolidated voucher hub at /admin/vouchers
entries.plain: One central hub now manages redeem codes for all assessment services, replacing separate code pages per service.
entries.explanation:
One admin home for every service that issues redeem codes. A tabbed hub
reuses each service's existing voucher manager (ARC + Technical) as-is - no
schema change, no new tables, redeem flows untouched - plus a cross-service
summary (codes / redeemed / outstanding per service). Sidebar gains a
Platform > "Vouchers" entry; the Technical group's "Voucher codes" now opens
the hub on its tab (?service=technical). Tolerant of a service's tables being
absent. Verified: both tabs render their managers; summary shows live counts;
deep-link selects the right tab.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Docs: mark auth as enabled; generalize create-admin script
entries.plain: Documentation updated to reflect that security is active; admin setup script now accepts custom email and name inputs.
entries.explanation:
- README + CLAUDE.md: correct the stale "auth disabled / AUTH_ENABLED false"
  copy - auth has been ON since 2026-06-13 (commit 1092c28); enforcement
  verified (/admin/* + /candidate/* redirect to /login, public routes open).
- scripts/create-admin.ts: parameterized (ADMIN_EMAIL/ADMIN_NAME or argv,
  defaults to ahmad.rashid@viftraining.com), creates the auth user with a
  strong random temp password (printed) + upserts the required role=admin
  profiles row; reuses an existing auth user instead of failing.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add thin front door to start a Succession Readiness programme
entries.plain: Consultants can now start a Succession Readiness programme directly from the admin dashboard instead of going through the Assessment Center.
entries.explanation:
A "Start a Succession Readiness programme" launcher on /admin/readiness
creates an engagement already in combined mode and opens its detail, so a
consultant starts from the Readiness service instead of the Assessment
Center. Verified end to end.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Readiness engine v2: confidence layer + IDP linkage
entries.plain: Readiness assessments now show confidence warnings when results are borderline or raters disagree, helping you make safer promotion decisions.
entries.explanation:
Engine v2 (advisory signals that never change the tier): borderline
near-call flag + low rater-agreement flag. Glue (readiness-data.ts) maps
the two new config columns, derives othersSpread from the Reflect
by_group means, and honours per-competency targets. Admin panel gains
the two advisory fields.

Confidence surfacing on the per-candidate readiness report: a near-call
caveat by the tier badge (with cutoff distance), a low-agreement summary
+ a quiet marker on disagreeing competency rows.

Slice 5 - readiness -> IDP: a "Generate development plan" action builds a
reflect_idps draft from the engine (knockouts -> largest below-bar gaps
-> blind spots, capped 5; each with development-tip behaviours + a why),
a scaffolded action plan, success measures, +90-day review, keyed to the
participant via the candidate bridge; non-clobbering of a coach-edited
plan; links to the Reflect IDP editor. Admin/coach gated.

Migrations 00096 (borderline_band + rater_agreement_spread_max) and
00097 (role_profile_competencies.target_proficiency). tsconfig enables
allowImportingTsExtensions so the delivered v2 test's ./readiness.ts
import type-checks. 7/7 tests pass; tsc + next build green. Verified
end-to-end on a seeded demo 360 (Ready Soon, borderline, low-agreement,
5-priority IDP draft).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Succession Readiness engine + config + admin panel (Slice 1)
entries.plain: The platform can now calculate and track succession readiness across your organization with tunable settings that you control.
entries.explanation:
Makes succession readiness computable and tunable end to end.

Migrations 00087-00091 (renumbered from the handover's 00078-00082,
which were taken): readiness_index_config (admin-tunable global config +
descending-cutoff CHECK + seeded global row + RLS), reflect_competencies
.ac_competency_id (reliable 360->AC mapping, name backfill),
reflect_participants.candidate_id (AC<->Reflect bridge),
engagements.assessment_mode (standalone/combined self lever),
readiness_results (auditable snapshot).

Engine: src/lib/scoring/readiness.ts (pure, delivered) + readiness.test
.ts (5 cases A-E, all pass via node --import tsx --test).

Integration: src/lib/scoring/readiness-data.ts assembles engine inputs
(config -> role reqs -> 360 participant -> computeParticipantScoring ->
reflect-to-AC mapping -> self per mode -> computeReadiness -> snapshot).
Tolerant of un-applied migrations. Combined-mode self is a documented
TODO until the Slice 3 behavioral runner exists.

Admin panel: /admin/readiness/config edits every config field with
validation + inline methodology help; admin-only save sets updated_by;
SVG icons only; nav entry + EN/AR keys.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Admin hub: add Technical Sandbox chip linking to /admin/tech-sandbox
entries.plain: A new Technical Sandbox link has been added to the admin dashboard for easier access to technical test management.
entries.explanation:
Adds a 'Technical Sandbox' chip to the /admin hero chip row so the sandbox admin
is discoverable from the Assessment Center command center. tsc clean; build OK.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Landing page: add Admin button to the hero top bar
entries.plain: An Admin button now appears on the main landing page, making it easier for administrators to access the control center.
entries.explanation:
Adds a bilingual (EN/AR) Admin button next to the catalogue/language controls on
the caliber.viftraining.com landing page, linking to /admin (auth-gated, so it
routes to /login when signed out). tsc clean; npm run build succeeds.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(ux): back-link audit - add history-aware Back to 34 sub-pages
entries.plain: Back buttons have been added to 34 pages where users previously had no easy way to return to the previous screen.
entries.explanation:
Adds <BackLink ... history /> to admin, ARA, AC, assessor, candidate, client,
and reflect sub-pages that previously had no way back. Excluded: auth/landing
pages, public token entry points (redeem/respond/apply/verify/personal-start),
portal home dashboards (have section nav), pages that already had a back, and
the reflect PDF-render pages (a back link would corrupt the printed PDF).
Implements the 'every screen needs a back affordance' SOP.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: fix(auth): admin sidebar shows the real signed-in user, not a placeholder
entries.plain: The admin sidebar now displays the actual logged-in user's name and email instead of showing a generic placeholder.
entries.explanation:
Replaced the hardcoded 'Administrator' / admin@vifm.ae footer with the
authenticated user's name + email (from supabase.auth.getUser).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: fix(auth): eliminate portal flash on password-recovery via pre-paint head script
entries.plain: Users resetting their password no longer see a brief flash of the home page before being taken to the reset screen.
entries.explanation:
The recovery token is in the URL hash (server-invisible), so the home page
rendered for a moment before the client redirect. Add a synchronous inline
script in <head> that redirects to /update-password before the body paints,
so the portal no longer flashes. RecoveryRedirect stays as a client fallback.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: fix(auth): forward password-recovery token to /update-password from any page
entries.plain: Password recovery links now work correctly from any page in the platform, no matter where a user is logged in.
entries.explanation:
The dashboard recovery link lands on the app root with the token in the URL
hash; a logged-in user stayed there (no bounce through /login where the
forwarder lived), seeing the raw token. Add a global RecoveryRedirect in the
root layout that detects type=recovery in the hash and forwards to
/update-password before anything consumes it.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(auth): set-new-password page completes the recovery flow
entries.plain: Users can now set a new password directly from their email recovery link without being redirected multiple times.
entries.explanation:
Adds /update-password (auth-bypassed): reads the recovery session from the
email link's URL hash, lets the user set + confirm a new password via
supabase.auth.updateUser, then signs out and routes to /login.

Routing: in-app /password-reset now redirects there directly; the dashboard
recovery link (lands on /login via the root redirect carrying the token hash)
is forwarded from /login to /update-password. Closes the gap where reset
links had no completion screen.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(auth): enable auth (AUTH_ENABLED=true) + hide demo login in production
entries.plain: All users must now sign in with real credentials; the demo quick-login is hidden on the live site for security.
entries.explanation:
Access is now restricted to authenticated users. Admin account
asadeq@viftraining.com (role admin) verified. The demo quick-login dropdown
is gated to NODE_ENV !== production so its embedded credentials cannot be
used to bypass auth on the live site; real email/password sign-in remains.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: docs: track open work in PENDING-ACTIONS.md + pointer from claude.md
entries.plain: A checklist of pending work items is now documented to track remaining tasks before the platform's full launch.
entries.explanation:
Living checklist of deferred/open items (auth go-live, pre-flip hardening
buckets 2-3, competency-tier increments 2.2/2.4, cleanup). Pointer added to
claude.md so future sessions surface it when asked about pending actions.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(tech): competency tier — relational schema + AI-regroup script (Phase 2.1)
entries.plain: The technical skills system now includes a Competency tier between Function and Skill, aligning with industry standards.
entries.explanation:
Insert the COMPETENCY tier between Function and Skill, per the agreed
Domain → Function → Competency → Skill taxonomy (IMA/CGMA-aligned).

- Migration 00074 (additive, relational): technical_competencies
  (function_id FK) + technical_competency_skills (competency_id FK), with
  RLS mirroring technical_functions (select auth / all admin). Existing flat
  technical_functions.skills_en[] is left in place so the runner/extractor/
  reports keep working until later increments switch over.
- scripts/regroup-tech-skills.ts: uses Claude to group each function flat
  skills into 2–4 named bilingual competencies, preserving every skill
  verbatim (EN+AR). Flags: --dry, --force, --function <key>. Idempotent.

Relational (not JSONB) chosen as best practice: the taxonomy is shared
reference data referenced by items/results/cut-scores and aggregated per
competency. Consistent with migration 00054 precedent.

Not yet wired: JD-extractor, admin editor, runner, reports (next increments).

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(auth): test-gate — quick-login + seed accounts for all 6 user roles
entries.plain: Test login accounts have been created for all 6 user roles so you can test each portal type before turning on full security.
entries.explanation:
Prep for the eventual AUTH_ENABLED flip: give every user_role a pre-seeded
test login so each portal can be exercised under real auth. AUTH_ENABLED
stays false — this is inert until the switch is flipped.

- login page DEMO_ROLES now covers all 6 roles (adds Consultant →
  /ara/consultant and a distinct Associate Assessor); redirect switch
  gains a consultant case.
- scripts/create-test-accounts.ts also provisions consultant@ and
  associate@ (consultant + associate_assessor) alongside the existing
  assessor/candidate/client. All password admin123.
- EN/AR locale keys for the two new role labels; "Assessor" relabelled
  "Lead Assessor" to distinguish from the associate.

NOTE: the embedded creds only work once the matching accounts exist in
the live Supabase project (run scripts/create-admin.ts +
scripts/create-test-accounts.ts against it). Provision first, THEN flip
AUTH_ENABLED, or the portal locks out. The demo dropdown should be gated
to NODE_ENV !== "production" before real go-live.

Build green; /login renders all 6 roles.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(responsive): stack observation indicators + phone card-list for talent-map heatmap
entries.plain: Mobile phones now display observation indicators and talent-map results in a more readable stacked layout instead of side-by-side columns.
entries.explanation:
Addresses the two manual-judgment spots flagged in the mobile pass:
- Assessor observation form: positive/negative behavioural-indicator
  reference columns now stack on phones (grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2) on both
  the Observe and Rate tabs — text was tight at 375px when forced 2-up.
- Admin talent-map heatmap: added a per-candidate card list for phones
  (md:hidden) that groups competency scores by domain with the same heatmap
  tone chips, plus a cohort-average card. The wide candidate x competency
  matrix still renders as the scrollable table on md+ screens.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(responsive): make portals phone/tablet friendly
entries.plain: Candidate, assessor, and admin portals now work smoothly on phones and tablets with properly sized buttons and stacked layouts.
entries.explanation:
Mobile-first Tailwind pass across the highest-traffic surfaces. Layout/
styling only — no logic changes; tsc passes clean.

- Candidate portal (primary phone surface): stack competency rows + report
  download cards on mobile, 2-up score cards on phone, wrap quiz footer with
  min-h-10 touch targets, tighter shell padding (p-4 sm:p-6).
- Assessor portal (tablet): scrollable observation TabsList on narrow
  screens; OAR recommendation + observe Select rows stack on phone.
- Reflect/client/admin: non-responsive grid-cols-{2,3,4} made mobile-first
  (stack on phone, expand at sm/lg) on analytics KPIs, dialogs, and wizards.

Intentional fixed-column grids (BARS rating legend, 9-box matrix) and
already-wrapped/scrolling tables left unchanged.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: feat(i18n): Phase 2 — wire Arabic competency display across all surfaces
entries.plain: Arabic-language names and descriptions now display correctly across all assessment results, reports, and recommendation screens.
entries.explanation:
Adds localizedName/localizedDescription helper and threads name_ar/description_ar
through ~18 server pages, the client engagement matrix, the recommender driver
labels (label_ar), and the Arabic PDF report renderers. English unchanged
(fallback). tsc --noEmit clean.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Extend validation-evidence workflow to Fluent, Technical, Reflect, Psychometrics
entries.plain: Fluent, Technical, Reflect, and Psychometrics assessments now track the same research evidence and validation sources that Assessment Center and AI Readiness already do.
entries.explanation:
Brings the AC/ARC 'research provenance' workflow to the remaining four
instruments on the Evidence & Validity Map, via a generic
adapter-driven subsystem instead of copying the AC stack 4x.

Schema:
- 00069: add validation_evidence jsonb (+ GIN index) to eng_fluent_items,
  tech_assessment_items, reflect_competencies, psy_scales — the natural
  construct unit per instrument.

AI suggesters (closed, spot-checkable bibliographies, ai_proposed only):
- evidence-suggester-core.ts: shared menu/prompt/parse/cross-check core
- fluent (CEFR/language-testing), technical (content-validity/standard-
  setting), reflect (multisource-feedback/competency-modelling), psy
  (construct-validity/reliability) suggesters

Generic subsystem:
- lib/evidence/instruments.ts: per-instrument adapters (table, list/load,
  suggest) keyed by instrument
- lib/evidence/actions.ts: suggestEvidence / saveEvidence /
  generateAllEvidence (batched, admin-gated, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY guarded)
- components/admin/evidence-panel.tsx: generic verify/edit panel
- app/admin/evidence/[instrument]/(page|[id]): generic console + detail

Evidence Map wiring:
- metrics: live verified/proposed anchor counts for all four
- matrix: 'research anchors' row now computed live for all four
- bulk button + instrument hrefs cover all six instruments

Typecheck + lint clean.

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: Add Evidence & Validity Map + Assessment Center evidence capture
entries.plain: Admins can now view a live dashboard showing how many assessment items have scientific evidence and validation data documented for each VIFM instrument.
entries.explanation:
Two-part build to surface and strengthen the scientific provenance of
every VIFM instrument.

1) Evidence & Validity Map (admin, /admin/evidence-map)
   - Dynamic dashboard: live per-instrument data counts (item coverage,
     reliability/validity sample-size progress) that update as the DB
     fills. Covers AC, ARC org+individual, Fluent, Technical, Reflect
     360, and Psychometrics.
   - Tabulated coverage matrix: validity/reliability categories x
     instruments, cells = documented / partial / missing / n.a. with
     live-computed cells for research anchors, internal consistency,
     inter-rater, test-retest, and norms.
   - Defensive metrics gathering: missing tables/columns degrade to
     'no data' instead of throwing.

2) Assessment Center evidence capture (closes the AC provenance gap)
   - Migration 00068: validation_evidence jsonb on competencies + GIN
     index, mirroring ARC's 00028.
   - AI suggester with a closed, spot-checkable assessment-centre /
     competency-modelling bibliography (ITAG, Spencer & Spencer,
     Boyatzis, Arthur et al., AERA/APA/NCME Standards, ISO 10667, etc.)
     plus the same hallucination guard (ai_proposed never reaches a
     client until human-verified).
   - Admin console at /admin/ac-evidence (list grouped by domain) +
     per-competency detail with generate/verify/edit/reject panel.
   - Server actions gated to admin via requireRole.

Wiring: sidebar entries (AC Evidence under Assessment Center, Evidence
Map under Platform) + EN/AR nav labels. Typecheck + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add Platform Clients registry: create a client across all services
entries.plain: New clients can now be created from a central registry and automatically added to all services at once, eliminating permission errors.
entries.explanation:
A client can now be created from the Platform Clients page and is wired
into every service at once, fixing the "new row violates row-level
security policy for table organizations" error seen when adding a client
inline from the engagement wizard.

The portal keeps two organization stores: organizations (Assessment
Center + Pre-Hire) and ara_organizations (AI Readiness + Reflect 360,
which require region in {uae,saudi} and sector in {government,banking,
general}). src/lib/clients/registry.ts centralizes both:

- createClientOrganization() dual-writes to both stores via the service
  role (bypasses the organizations RLS that denied the dev insert),
  deduped by case-insensitive name, deriving region/sector from
  country/industry when not supplied. Idempotent: re-creating an existing
  name back-fills the missing store and reuses ids.
- loadPlatformClients() returns a name-keyed union with per-service
  acId/araId so the page shows which services each client is linked to.

Surfaces:
- /admin/clients: new "Add Client" dialog (name/industry/country/region/
  sector/contacts) + the list rewritten as the cross-service union with a
  "Connected services" column.
- Engagement wizard "+ New": createOrganizationAction now goes through the
  shared registry (service-role + admin-gated), so inline-created clients
  are also cross-service and no longer hit the RLS error.

No migration (existing tables). Verified live (create -> rows in both
organizations + ara_organizations -> merged into one connected row ->
idempotent re-create reuses -> deleted); region "saudi"/sector "banking"
derived correctly from KSA/Banking. /admin/clients renders 200 with the
Add Client button + Connected-services column. tsc + lint clean.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add mobile hamburger + sidebar drawer to the landing (shared component)
entries.plain: Phone users can now access the navigation menu via a hamburger icon that slides open a sidebar, matching the admin experience.
entries.explanation:
The persistent left panel is desktop-only (hidden lg:flex), so on a phone the
landing had no way to reach the nav. Extract the admin layout's mobile drawer
into a reusable MobileSidebar (top-bar hamburger -> slide-over SidebarBody),
add it to the landing, and refactor the admin layout to use it so there's one
implementation. tsc + lint clean; verified on a 375px viewport (hamburger opens
the full nav, close works).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Auto-fill plan client into each create page via ?org deep link
entries.plain: When creating assessments from the checklist, the client is now pre-selected automatically so you skip that step.
entries.explanation:
The combined-plan checklist links now carry ?org=<id>&orgName=<name>, and each
org-bearing create page prefills its client picker from it. A shared resolver
(resolve-plan-org.ts) matches by id first, then by name (case-insensitive) so
it works across the two client tables (organizations for Pre-Hire/AC,
ara_organizations for ARA/Reflect).
