- Pre-Hire: seeds the requisition form org state.
- Assessment Center: seeds the wizard reducer's organizationId.
- Reflect 360: seeds the wizard state's organization_id.
- AR Compass: server-rendered select defaultValue + stage-picker links carry
  the client through the stage choice.
- Runner pages (Fluent/Technical/Psychometrics/Academy) have no client, so the
  param is harmlessly ignored.

tsc + lint clean. Verified: ARA select renders selected (curl) and the Reflect
wizard org field is seeded (live DOM).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Redesign Guided Start as a landing page (ara-hero + launcher cards)
entries.plain: The Guided Start page now displays as a polished landing with visual cards and progress tracking to guide admins through setup.
entries.explanation:
Restyle /admin/start to the platform landing aesthetic: an ara-hero
aurora banner (eyebrow + accent-sweep headline + trust badges), the six
goals as per-tone launcher-card tiles, the manual-setup modules as
launcher tiles, radio-style option cards for context/rigor, a polished
progress stepper, and a gradient recommendation header. Reuses the
existing launcher-card / ara-hero system from the root launcher and adds
a tone-aware is-selected state. Wizard logic + inline create flows are
unchanged. Bilingual EN/AR (admin chrome renders EN at runtime).

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add inline AC engagement create to Guided Start wizard
entries.plain: You can now design Assessment Center competencies and exercises directly in the Guided Start wizard without leaving to another page.
entries.explanation:
Replaces the AC handoff with a full inline assessment-centre designer in the
wizard's recommendation step — the last handoff among the heavy modules.

- Inline competency selection (the 38, grouped by domain) with a "Quick-fill from
  role profile" dropdown, exercise selection, and the exercise x competency matrix
  grid with per-competency >=2 validation + an Auto-map button that fills a valid
  matrix (each competency -> 2 exercises) in one click.
- Reuses the module's own createEngagementAction (no duplicated logic — same Zod
  validation, RLS, atomic insert + rollback) and lands in /admin/engagements/[id].
- Start page loads the competency tree (flattened to domain-grouped), exercises,
  and role-profile competency sets; all best-effort so a missing table degrades
  gracefully. Registry marks the three AC processes (selection / development /
  succession) as inline.

The existing 5-step AC wizard is untouched (still the "set it up myself" path).
tsc + next lint clean; /admin/start renders.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Render exercise-type labels in Arabic across all UI
entries.plain: Exercise type labels throughout the platform now appear in Arabic when the system language is set to Arabic.
entries.explanation:
Replaces the hardcoded English EXERCISE_TYPE_LABELS const at every render
site (assessor assignment grid + observation header, engagement wizard
steps 3 & 5, candidate assessment schedule) with the translated
exercise.types.* map, so type badges follow the active locale. Drops a dead
EXERCISE_TYPE_LABELS import from the engagement detail. The const now backs
only EXERCISE_TYPES (enum key list); no UI reads it directly.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Translate Exercise library + detail to Arabic (RTL)
entries.plain: The exercise library and detail pages now display in full Arabic with right-to-left text layout.
entries.explanation:
Extends the exercise i18n namespace with the library-page and four-tab
detail-editor strings (reusing the existing exercise.types map for the type
badges) and converts both surfaces to react-i18next + getServerT.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix assessor write-path RLS rejections via service-role + role guard
entries.plain: Assessor observation and review actions now work correctly even when an admin or supervisor is making changes on behalf of the assessor.
entries.explanation:
The observation, integration, and wash-up server actions wrote through the
RLS-aware client, so they failed with "violates row-level security policy"
whenever the caller wasn't the owning assessor (admin oversight, dev/no-session,
mismatched assessor). Route their writes through the service client and add a
requireRole(["lead_assessor","associate_assessor","admin"]) gate at each entry
so removing RLS as the gate isn't an authz downgrade (synthetic admin in dev,
real check under auth=on). Mirrors the consent fix and the AC admin hardening.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Update auth README to match real login + RLS-aware reads
entries.plain: Documentation now accurately describes how login and data access work in the platform.
entries.explanation:
The doc claimed pages use createServiceClient() and login is a stub; both
are stale. Login does real signInWithPassword against demo accounts, pages
read via createClient(), and the gate is AUTH_ENABLED in config.ts - so you
must log in to see data in dev.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Revert candidate home to the RLS-aware Supabase client
entries.plain: Candidate home page data access is now secured properly so logged-in candidates only see their own assessments.
entries.explanation:
Restore createClient() (session-scoped) for the /candidate overview reads,
matching every other page. The earlier service-client switch showed data
without login but would surface the first-of-all candidates to an authed
candidate under RLS. The My Learning step, i18n, and ProcessMap hydration
fix are retained; data resolves once logged in.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Surface VIFM Academy as a step on the candidate journey
entries.plain: Candidates now see 'My Learning' as a journey step linking to training courses, and all journey links work correctly in the system.
entries.explanation:
- Add "My Learning" as the 5th process-map step on /candidate, linking to
  the Academy (bilingual EN/AR label + courses metric).
- Read the home overview via the service client so firstId resolves in dev
  (anon client returns nothing under RLS with auth off), making every
  journey link functional. TODO(auth) to scope to the authed candidate.
- Round ProcessMap node coordinates to fix a server/client hydration
  mismatch the 5-step (and latent 7-step) layout exposed.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Add All Services launcher link to admin sidebar
entries.plain: Admins can now click a link in the sidebar to jump to the platform launcher showing all assessment services.
entries.explanation:
Top sidebar entry linking to the platform launcher (/) where all four

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fix Register: survive Render shallow clone (deepen + entry guard)
entries.plain: The changelog now loads completely and accurately on all deployment platforms, including those that use shallow repository copies.
entries.explanation:
Render deploys shallow-clone at depth 1, so the prebuild git-log saw only
the HEAD commit and regenerated the register down to a single entry,
clobbering the committed 600-entry snapshot on the live site.

- readCommits() best-effort `git fetch --unshallow` to restore full history
  in CI (harmless/fast on an already-complete local clone).
- write step keeps the committed register when a parse yields fewer entries
  than the file already holds (belt-and-suspenders if unshallow is offline).

The report HTML set body color but no background. The new headless-Chromium
renderer prints a transparent body as BLACK when printBackground:true, so every
Role Readiness report opened as a solid black page. Force white on html+body.
Verified by rendering the PDF to an image (avg pixel ~rgb(250,250,250), full
content visible). Other Puppeteer reports navigate to SSR pages that already
carry a white background, so only this raw-HTML template was affected.

entries.plain: Subject-matter experts can now generate test questions for a single job function instead of having to create questions for an entire category at once.
entries.explanation:

Usage:

  npx tsx scripts/draft-function-items.ts --function corporate_finance --per 4

Verified: tsc clean; the filter resolves Corporate Finance's six skills.

https://claude.ai/code/session_014ceigJNgNM4AtMUoWSPZRU

entries.author: Claude
entries.title: fix(build): enable downlevelIteration (fixes prod build type error)
entries.plain: Fixed a production build error that prevented the system from deploying successfully to the live environment.
entries.explanation:
The Render build failed type-checking on scripts/evidence-anchor-run.ts (and
sibling evidence-* scripts) which spread a Map iterator:
  Type 'MapIterator<...>' can only be iterated through when using the
  '--downlevelIteration' flag or with a '--target' of 'es2015' or higher.

tsconfig had no target set (defaults low), so iterating Map/Set entries via
spread is rejected. These dev scripts arrived from other merged work and had
never been through a successful build. Enable downlevelIteration — surgical,
permissive, resolves every such case at once with no emit/behaviour change
(Next compiles via SWC; target only affects tsc type-checking here).

Verified: full 'npm run build' green with env present (compiles, type-checks,
generates all 76 pages).

entries.author: AimanSadeq

MCQ items into the certified function bank (status='draft', per-skill pool) so
an SME curates instead of authoring from scratch — the path to making the new
competency functions certifiable.

- Hallucination guard intact: nothing written as 'approved'; the indicative
  live-AI path is unaffected and already serves these functions.
- Idempotent: skips skills already at >= --per items (draft/in_review/approved);
  --force overrides.
- Flags: --dry, --new-only (the 6 new competencies), --category <c>, --per N
  (default 4), --limit N. Reuses the maintained draftFunctionSkillItems().
- dotenv override:true to dodge the harness empty-key injection (roadmap §1).

Imports verified under tsx; tsc clean. Runs where .env.local has SUPABASE +
ANTHROPIC keys (cannot run from the sandbox: no env + host not allowlisted).

entries.title: Add hover tooltips to the landing-page service tiles
entries.plain: Hovering over assessment tiles on the home page now shows helpful hints about what each assessment does.
entries.explanation:
Wrap each launcher tile in a Shadcn/Radix Tooltip with a concise bilingual
"best for" use-case hint per service (EN + AR). Portal-rendered, so the
landing's overflow-hidden doesn't clip it; no static layout change, so the
page still fits one screen.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Fit platform landing on one screen (no scroll)
entries.plain: Platform landing page now fits on a single screen without scrolling on most device sizes.
entries.explanation:
Make the launcher a fixed-height flex column (h-screen + overflow-hidden):
hero pinned top, service cards centered in the middle, footer flush bottom.
Tighten the hero (smaller headline, less padding) and the four cards
(smaller padding/icons, condensed text) so everything fits one viewport.
Verified 768-1066px: footer flush, no clipping, no scroll.

entries.author: AimanSadeq
entries.title: Sync launcher language choice with the shared locale cookie
entries.plain: Language choice now stays consistent when users switch between the home page and assessment portals.
entries.explanation:
The root launcher tracked its own localStorage flag only, so picking
Arabic there didn't carry into the cookie-gated portals/public pages.
choose() now also sets the vifm-locale cookie, and the launcher adopts
an existing cookie on mount - so the Arabic choice is consistent end to
end across the front door, portals, and public surfaces.
